paper_title: Simple integration of fast excitation and offset, delayed inhibition computes directional selectivity in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: ON moving bars (8 directions, 4 speeds), single-position bar flashes, two-step apparent motion
neural_activity: In vivo whole-cell recordings show depolarization to preferred direction motion and hyperpolarization to null direction motion; receptive field mapping reveals spatially offset excitatory (leading side) and inhibitory (trailing side) inputs; directional selectivity results from null-direction suppression without preferred-direction enhancement
ecological_context: Stimulus speeds tested cover a behaviorally relevant range; spatial offset between excitatory and inhibitory inputs (~6°) corresponds to angular separation between adjacent ommatidia
techniques_used: ['in vivo whole-cell electrophysiology', 'LED visual stimuli', 'computational modeling']
summary: T4 neurons exhibit directionally selective responses mediated by spatially offset fast excitatory and slow inhibitory inputs, resulting in null-direction suppression.

paper_title: Mushroom body output neurons encode valence and guide memory-based action selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson + red light); Inactivation (Shibire-ts1) during olfactory and visual conditioning (shock/sugar)
neural_activity: Not directly measured; optogenetic activation sufficient to induce attraction
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces attraction to red light. Inactivation impairs 2-hr aversive odor memory and aversive visual memory.
ecological_context: Memory-based action selection to avoid dangerous stimuli
summary: MBON-γ1pedc>α/β activation induces attraction, while its inactivation impairs short-term aversive memory for both odors and colors.

paper_title: Mushroom body output neurons encode valence and guide memory-based action selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation; Inactivation during visual and ethanol conditioning
neural_activity: Not directly measured; optogenetic activation sufficient to induce avoidance
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces avoidance of red light. Inactivation impairs appetitive visual memory and appetitive ethanol memory.
ecological_context: Memory-based action selection to approach rewarding stimuli
summary: These glutamatergic MBONs mediate avoidance when activated and are required for appetitive memory of visual cues and ethanol.

paper_title: Mushroom body output neurons encode valence and guide memory-based action selection in Drosophila
cell_type: M4/M6 Cluster Glutamatergic MBONs (MBON-γ5β′2a, MBON-β′2mp, MBON-β′2mp_bilateral)
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation; Inactivation during odor, visual, and ethanol conditioning
neural_activity: Not directly measured; optogenetic activation sufficient to induce avoidance
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces avoidance. Inactivation impairs 2-hr appetitive odor memory, appetitive visual memory, and appetitive ethanol memory.
ecological_context: Memory-based action selection to approach rewarding stimuli
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics (CsChrimson)', 'Thermogenetic silencing (Shibire-ts1)', 'T-maze olfactory conditioning', 'Visual conditioning', 'Ethanol odor conditioning']
summary: This cluster of glutamatergic MBONs mediates avoidance when activated and is required for multiple forms of appetitive memory.

paper_title: Mushroom body output neurons encode valence and guide memory-based action selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation; Inactivation during ethanol conditioning
neural_activity: Not directly measured; optogenetic activation sufficient to induce attraction
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces attraction. Inactivation impairs 24-hr appetitive ethanol memory and prevents the switch from aversive to appetitive memory after ethanol intoxication.
ecological_context: Memory-based action selection regarding ethanol intoxication outcomes
summary: MBON-α′2 activation induces attraction and is required for long-term appetitive ethanol memory and the switch from aversive to appetitive memory.

paper_title: Mushroom body output neurons encode valence and guide memory-based action selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation; Inactivation during odor/visual conditioning; dTrpA1 activation
neural_activity: Not directly measured; optogenetic activation induces attraction; dTrpA1 activation induces sleep promotion
behavioral_consequence: Inactivation impairs 2-hr appetitive odor and visual memory. dTrpA1 activation increases sleep duration.
ecological_context: Memory-guided approach and sleep-wake regulation
summary: MBON-γ2α′1 activation attracts flies and promotes sleep; inactivation impairs appetitive memory for odors and colors.

paper_title: Mushroom body output neurons encode valence and guide memory-based action selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: dTrpA1 activation
neural_activity: Not directly measured
behavioral_consequence: dTrpA1 activation increases sleep duration.
ecological_context: Sleep regulation and arousal state control
summary: Activation of these GABAergic MBONs promotes sleep.

paper_title: Mushroom body output neurons encode valence and guide memory-based action selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation; Inactivation during 24-hr odor conditioning
neural_activity: Not directly measured; optogenetic activation induces weak attraction
behavioral_consequence: Inactivation causes loss of retrieval of 24-hr aversive odor memory.
ecological_context: Long-term memory retrieval for aversive stimuli
summary: V2 cluster MBONs are required for the retrieval of long-term aversive odor memory and activation induces weak attraction.

paper_title: Afterimages drive a shared visual motion-reversal illusion in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: phase-shifted gratings interleaved with uniform frames (apparent-motion illusion); interleave luminance (dark vs light); random binary patterns
behavioral_consequence: silencing abolishes the direction reversal behavior
summary: Silencing direction-selective T4/T5 neurons abolishes the direction reversal observed in the apparent-motion illusion.

paper_title: Afterimages drive a shared visual motion-reversal illusion in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: phase-shifted gratings interleaved with uniform frames; interleave duration increases
neural_activity: invert their directional preference as interleave duration increases
summary: Recordings reveal that downstream wide-field neurons invert their directional preference as interleave duration increases.

paper_title: Afterimages drive a shared visual motion-reversal illusion in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: phase-shifted gratings interleaved with uniform frames; interleave luminance (dark vs light)
neural_activity: show signatures of an afterimage; emergence depends on interleave luminance
summary: Imaging neurons upstream of T4/T5 shows signatures of an afterimage, whose emergence depends on interleave luminance.

paper_title: Cross-species evidence for a developmental origin of adult hypersomnia with loss of synaptic adhesion molecules beat-Ia/CADM2
experimental_stimulus: Developmental knockdown or overexpression of beat-Ia using NPF-GAL4 driver
neural_activity: Knockdown reduces presynaptic synaptic density (Syt-EGFP puncta) specifically in the suboesophageal zone (SEZ); Overexpression increases presynaptic sites across brain regions including SEZ
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown increases total sleep duration, daytime/nighttime sleep, and proportion of long sleep bouts (>60 min); Overexpression decreases total and daytime sleep and reduces long sleep bouts
techniques_used: ['UAS-GAL4 RNAi', 'UAS-GAL4 Overexpression', 'Drosophila Activity Monitoring (DAM)', 'Confocal Microscopy (CD8::GFP, Syt-EGFP)']
summary: Developmental knockdown of beat-Ia in NPF cells disrupts synaptic elaboration in the SEZ and causes excessive sleep, while overexpression increases synapses and reduces sleep.

paper_title: Cross-species evidence for a developmental origin of adult hypersomnia with loss of synaptic adhesion molecules beat-Ia/CADM2
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (TrpA1), inhibition (Kir2.1), and NPFR knockdown
neural_activity: Identified via FlyWire connectome analysis as receiving input from descending NPF neurons in the SEZ; predicted to be GABAergic based on machine learning analysis of electron microscopy
behavioral_consequence: Activation reduces sleep; Inhibition increases sleep; NPFR knockdown increases sleep duration and long sleep bouts
summary: R10G06-labeled SEZ neurons receive NPF input and promote wakefulness when activated; inhibiting these cells or blocking NPF signaling increases sleep.

paper_title: Cross-species evidence for a developmental origin of adult hypersomnia with loss of synaptic adhesion molecules beat-Ia/CADM2
experimental_stimulus: Knockdown of beat-Ia using OK6-GAL4, OK371-GAL4, and D42-GAL4 drivers
behavioral_consequence: No increase in sleep duration compared to controls
summary: Knockdown of beat-Ia in motor neurons does not affect sleep duration, indicating the sleep phenotype is not due to motor neuron dysfunction.

paper_title: A pair of interneurons that confer positive real-time valence to sweet sensation in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) or silencing (Kir2.1, A1ACR); input from optogenetically stimulated Gr64f-expressing sweet GRNs
neural_activity: Robust calcium responses in Fox projections following Gr64f stimulation; optogenetic activation drives activity sufficient to override intrinsic taste valence
behavioral_consequence: Activation drives positional preference, proboscis extension reflex (PER), and increased food/water consumption; silencing impairs appetitive responses to sweet-GRN activation without abolishing baseline feeding; necessary for appetitive olfactory learning induced by natural sugar or sweet-GRN activation
ecological_context: Guides behaviors such as approach or avoidance, learning, and decision-making; crucial for survival; supports learned sugar responses; may be engaged under natural conditions when sweet input surpasses a threshold and internal state permits
techniques_used: ['optogenetics', 'positional preference assay', 'OptoPAD (feeding)', 'PER assay', 'associative learning assay', 'two-photon calcium imaging']
summary: Fox neurons are a pair of SEZ interneurons that assign positive real-time valence to sweet sensation, drive appetitive behaviors, and are necessary for appetitive learning, acting as a bottleneck transforming sweet sensation into reinforcing signals.

paper_title: A pair of interneurons that confer positive real-time valence to sweet sensation in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Activation leads to significant calcium responses in Fox neurons
behavioral_consequence: Increased food consumption and drives appetitive learning (dependent on Fox neurons)
ecological_context: Primary gustatory receptor neurons that project to the SEZ; sweet taste typically signals energy-rich nutrients and elicits attraction
summary: Gr64f-expressing sweet GRNs activate Fox neurons upon stimulation and drive increased food consumption and appetitive learning downstream of Fox.

paper_title: A pair of interneurons that confer positive real-time valence to sweet sensation in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation of Fox neurons; optogenetic silencing (A1ACR) of PAM-DANs
neural_activity: Calcium activation in MB compartments γ4, γ5, β2, and β′2 upon Fox stimulation
behavioral_consequence: Required for Fox-driven appetitive learning (blocking PAM-DANs abolishes learning) but not for Fox-driven feeding increases
ecological_context: Center for associative learning; innervated by dopaminergic neurons that mediate appetitive reinforcement
summary: PAM-DANs receive input from Fox neurons and are required for appetitive learning but not for Fox-driven feeding behaviors.

paper_title: A pair of interneurons that confer positive real-time valence to sweet sensation in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) and silencing (Kir2.1)
neural_activity: Connectome simulation shows silencing blocks PAM-DAN response to GRN stimulation
behavioral_consequence: Activation alone does not enhance feeding or generate appetitive memory; silencing abolishes Gr64f-induced feeding increase and learning
ecological_context: Major postsynaptic target of Fox that relays positive valence toward the learning center
summary: CB0233 neurons are downstream targets of Fox neurons that relay positive valence to the learning center; they are necessary for GRN-dependent feeding and learning but activation alone is insufficient.

paper_title: A pair of interneurons that confer positive real-time valence to sweet sensation in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Accounts for 10% of synaptic inputs to Fox neurons
behavioral_consequence: Increases food and water consumption and induces positional preference; does not generate appetitive olfactory learning when paired with odor
ecological_context: Third-order gustatory interneuron that regulates feeding behavior
summary: Fdg neurons regulate feeding behavior and positional preference but lack the capacity to drive appetitive learning, distinguishing them from Fox neurons.

paper_title: Connectomic mapping of pharyngeal and gut sensory circuits in adult Drosophila
cell_type: Internal sensory neurons (stomodeal nerve and pharyngeal sense organs)
summary: Identified multiple types of sensory axons in the stomodeal nerve and pharyngeal sense organs with distinct morphology; mapped downstream circuits revealing monosynaptic connections to motor neurons and endocrine cells.

paper_title: High-throughput tracking of freely moving Drosophila reveals variations in aggression and courtship behaviors
cell_type: pC1 neurons (doublesex+ cholinergic neurons labeled by R26E01-GAL4)
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation via TRPA1 at 30°C
neural_activity: Neurons thermogenetically activated; no baseline neural activity recorded
behavioral_consequence: Significantly elevated female aggression (head butts) in female-female pairs; no significant effect on male aggression (lunges) or courtship (UWEs) in male-male pairs
ecological_context: Aggression is used to secure food, territory, and mates; sex-specific interaction patterns reflect evolutionary strategies for resource access
techniques_used: ['TRPA1 thermogenetics', 'Kestrel high-throughput imaging', 'DeepLabCut pose estimation', 'Manual behavioral scoring']
summary: Thermogenetic activation of R26E01-labeled pC1 neurons replicates a known phenotype of heightened female aggression (head butts) without affecting male aggression or courtship behaviors.

paper_title: High-throughput tracking of freely moving Drosophila reveals variations in aggression and courtship behaviors
cell_type: R72A10-GAL4 labeled neurons (~40-45 central brain neurons, ~10-15 at AL-SOG junction)
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation via TRPA1 at 30°C
neural_activity: Neurons thermogenetically activated; no baseline neural activity recorded
behavioral_consequence: Robust increase in unilateral wing extensions (UWEs, courtship) in male-male pairs; no effect on male aggression (lunges) or female aggression (head butts)
ecological_context: Courtship behaviors like UWEs (courtship song) attract mates; courtship is reciprocal to aggression in securing mating partners
techniques_used: ['TRPA1 thermogenetics', 'Kestrel high-throughput imaging', 'DeepLabCut pose estimation', 'Immunostaining (mCD8::GFP, anti-Tdc2, anti-NC82)', 'NeuronBridge/connectomics analysis']
summary: Activation of R72A10-labeled neurons induces enhanced male courtship (UWEs) without affecting aggression; neurons are non-octopaminergic with a cluster at the AL-SOG junction projecting to SLP and AB.

paper_title: The Drosophila escape motor circuit shows differential vulnerability to aging linked to functional decay
cell_type: LC4 (Lobular Columnar type 4) visual projection neuron
experimental_stimulus: Light-off visual stimuli (20 ms duration), optogenetic activation via red-light flashes (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Electrophysiological recordings show Long Latency Response (LLR) generation dependent on LC4 to GF synaptic transmission. Immunohistochemistry reveals significant decrease in presynaptic active zone marker (BRP.S) density in old non-responders. Optogenetic activation shows decreasing response probability with age.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation induces escape behavior (jump + flight) in young flies, but probability declines drastically with age (nearly zero in >60 days). Synaptic transmission loss correlates with behavioral loss. Required for habituation of escape response (Shaker RNAi in LC4 abolishes habituation). Also vulnerable to starvation and oxidative stress.
ecological_context: Escape from potentially life-threatening visual stimuli (predator-like looming or light-off).
summary: LC4 neurons mediate visually induced escape and habituation. Their synaptic transmission to the Giant Fiber decays with age, stress, and starvation, correlating with loss of escape behavior.

paper_title: The Drosophila escape motor circuit shows differential vulnerability to aging linked to functional decay
cell_type: LPLC2 (Lobular Plate/Lobular Columnar type 2) visual projection neuron
experimental_stimulus: Light-off visual stimuli, optogenetic activation via red-light flashes (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Synaptic transmission to Giant Fiber remains robust to aging. Presynaptic active zone marker (BRP.S) intensity increases with age but does not decrease in old non-responders.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation reliably induces escape behavior (>80% probability) independent of age, including in old flies that have lost visually induced escape behavior.
ecological_context: Escape from potentially life-threatening visual stimuli (predator-like looming or light-off).
summary: LPLC2 neurons relay visual information to the Giant Fiber for escape. This circuit component is resilient to aging, stress, and starvation compared to LC4.

paper_title: The Drosophila escape motor circuit shows differential vulnerability to aging linked to functional decay
experimental_stimulus: Direct electrical stimulation, optogenetic activation via red-light flashes (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Action potential propagation measured as Short Latency Response (SLR) in DLM fibers. Dendritic total length stable, branch number decreases but not linked to functional decline. Postsynaptic markers (Dα7nAChR, Drep2) stable in old non-responders.
behavioral_consequence: Direct or optogenetic activation induces escape behavior (jump + flight) reliably at all ages, even in flies that no longer respond to visual stimuli. Downstream circuit function remains intact.
ecological_context: Fast initiation of escape response (jump followed by flight) to life-threatening stimuli.
summary: The Giant Fiber neuron initiates the escape motor command. While dendritic structure changes slightly with age, signal transmission and behavioral output via GF activation remain functional even in old non-responding flies.

paper_title: The Drosophila escape motor circuit shows differential vulnerability to aging linked to functional decay
experimental_stimulus: Light-off visual stimuli (20 ms duration)
neural_activity: Electroretinogram (ERG) recordings show receptor potential (RP) and lamina on/off transients. Amplitudes similar between young responders and old non-responders (except slight RP increase in old).
behavioral_consequence: Visual input function remains intact in old flies despite loss of escape behavior. Decline in escape is not caused by phototransduction or first-order synaptic transmission failure.
ecological_context: Detection of light-off stimuli that signal potential threats.
summary: Primary visual input neurons function normally in aging flies, indicating the behavioral deficit arises downstream in the central brain circuit.

paper_title: The Drosophila escape motor circuit shows differential vulnerability to aging linked to functional decay
experimental_stimulus: Direct electrical stimulation, Giant Fiber stimulation
neural_activity: Extracellular EMG recordings from DLM fibers and TTM show reliable muscle fiber firing. Direct stimulation of motoneurons is highly reliable up to 300 Hz.
behavioral_consequence: Motor output for jumping (TTM) and flight (DLM) remains functional throughout adult life, including in old non-responders.
ecological_context: Execution of jump and flight motor patterns during escape.
summary: Motoneurons and neuromuscular synapses are robust to aging and stress, maintaining the capacity for motor execution even when central processing fails.

paper_title: The Drosophila escape motor circuit shows differential vulnerability to aging linked to functional decay
experimental_stimulus: Genetic ablation (UAS-hid), Electrical stimulation of GF
neural_activity: SLR readout in DLM fibers. Ablation eliminates SLR in DLM in ~75% of animals.
behavioral_consequence: Confirms PSI role in transmitting signal from GF to DLM motoneurons for flight. Component remains functionally resilient to aging as SLR persists in old flies.
ecological_context: Integration of jump and flight commands for coordinated escape behavior.
summary: The PSI is a critical relay between the Giant Fiber and flight motoneurons. It is functionally robust to aging, unlike the LC4 to GF synapse.

paper_title: Differential modulation of feedforward inhibition reflects topographic organization in the olfactory system
experimental_stimulus: Odors (Benzaldehyde, Farnesol, 1-Octen-3-ol, 1-Hexanol, Apple Cider Vinegar, Orange Peel, Acetic Acid, Acetophenone); Endogenous 5-HT release via optogenetic activation of CSDns; Pharmacological application of 5-HT, 5-HT1A/1B agonist (Ipsapirone), 5-HT7 agonist (AS-19); 5-HT receptor RNAi knockdown.
neural_activity: 2-photon Ca2+ imaging in Antennal Lobe (AL) and Lateral Horn (LH). v-PNs exhibit heterogeneous serotonin receptor expression (all five 5-HT receptors, 1A, 1B, 2A, 2B, 7) with extensive co-expression. v-PNs expressing different receptors innervate distinct LH zones (e.g., 5-HT1A: ventral/medial; 5-HT7: dorsal/medial). Modulation is subset-specific: R24H08 v-PNs (express 5-HT1A/1B) show significant response reduction in AL with 5-HT1A/1B agonists but non-significant reduction with endogenous 5-HT in LH. R86G06 v-PNs (express 5-HT7) show significant response enhancement in LH with 5-HT and 5-HT7 agonists. Connectomic analysis shows v-PNs with different receptor profiles synapse on separate third-order LH neurons.
behavioral_consequence: None reported in this study. Discussion notes v-PNs are implicated in odor discrimination, valence coding, and habituation based on prior literature.
ecological_context: R24H08 v-PNs innervate glomeruli (DC3, VL2a) responding to plant-derived odors associated with reproductive success and project to ventral LH (pheromone processing). Benzaldehyde is repellent to flies. 1-Octen-3-ol is a fungal semiochemical. v-PNs modulate distinct components of odor representations.
techniques_used: ['2-photon Ca2+ imaging', 'Optogenetics', 'Pharmacology', 'RNAi', 'Hybridization Chain Reaction (HCR)', 'Multi-color Flip-Out (MCFO)', 'Photoactivatable GFP (PA-GFP)', 'Connectomics (Hemibrain)']
summary: Ventral Projection Neurons (v-PNs) express heterogeneous serotonin receptor combinations and project to distinct Lateral Horn zones. Serotonin differentially modulates v-PN subsets (R24H08 vs R86G06) with distinct downstream targets, allowing nuanced regulation of specific odor scenes rather than uniform population modulation.

paper_title: Differential modulation of feedforward inhibition reflects topographic organization in the olfactory system
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (Red LED).
neural_activity: Release endogenous serotonin in the Antennal Lobe and Lateral Horn. Activation significantly enhances odor-evoked Ca2+ responses in R86G06 v-PNs (5-HT7 expressing) and causes slight non-significant reduction in R24H08 v-PN responses in the LH.
behavioral_consequence: None reported in this study.
ecological_context: Sole synaptic source of serotonin within the Antennal Lobe and Lateral Horn. Interact with principal neuron classes to impact olfactory processing and behavior.
summary: CSDns release serotonin that differentially modulates v-PN subsets (enhancing 5-HT7 expressing R86G06 v-PNs and suppressing 5-HT1A/1B expressing R24H08 v-PNs), enabling context-dependent regulation of olfactory processing.

paper_title: Differential dopaminergic modulation of the antennal lobe of Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Dopamine (1 mM), Forskolin (40 µM), Nicotine (100 µM), Odors (Isoamyl-acetate, 3-Octanol, 4-methylcyclohexanol)
neural_activity: Dopamine application increased cAMP levels (G-Flamp1) but did not increase Ca2+ levels (GCaMP7b). Nicotine application increased GRAB-DA2 signals indicating dopamine release near ORN terminals. Odor pulses did not elicit measurable GRAB-DA2 signals.
behavioral_consequence: None tested
ecological_context: Not explicitly tested in this study
summary: ORNs express DopEcR and Dop1R1. Exogenous dopamine increases cAMP but not Ca2+ in ORNs. Nicotine induces dopamine release near ORNs, but odors do not.

paper_title: Differential dopaminergic modulation of the antennal lobe of Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Dopamine (1 mM), Odors (Isoamyl-acetate, 3-Octanol, 4-methylcyclohexanol)
neural_activity: Dopamine application increased cAMP and Ca2+ levels. Dopamine enhanced odor responses in glomeruli DL3, DL1, and DL5 (integral) and peak responses in DL1 and DL5. Dopamine reduced temporal sharpness in DL1, DL5, and DM3. Knockdown of TH in OK371-Gal4 neurons reduced DL1 odor response and increased sharpness.
behavioral_consequence: None tested
ecological_context: Not explicitly tested in this study
summary: ePNs express all four DopRs. Dopamine application differentially enhances odor-evoked Ca2+ responses and reduces response sharpness in specific glomeruli. Endogenous dopamine from OK371-Gal4 neurons modulates these responses.

paper_title: Differential dopaminergic modulation of the antennal lobe of Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Dopamine (1 mM), Nicotine (100 µM), Odors
neural_activity: Dopamine application increased cAMP levels but did not increase Ca2+ levels. Nicotine application increased GRAB-DA2 signals indicating dopamine release near iLN processes. Odor pulses did not elicit measurable GRAB-DA2 signals.
behavioral_consequence: None tested
ecological_context: Not explicitly tested in this study
summary: iLNs express DopEcR, Dop1R1, and Dop2R. Exogenous dopamine increases cAMP but not Ca2+. Nicotine induces dopamine release near iLNs, but odors do not.

paper_title: Differential dopaminergic modulation of the antennal lobe of Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Nicotine (100 µM), ATP (1 mM via P2X2 activation)
neural_activity: SIFa, DNp32, and TH-Gal4/OK371-Gal4 labeled neurons were identified as TH+ and innervating the AL. Activation of TH-Gal4 or OK371-Gal4 neurons (P2X2) increased GRAB-DA2 signals in the AL. Activation of Vmat-Gal4 (CSD neuron) did not significantly affect GRAB-DA2 signals. Nicotine application increased GRAB-DA2 signals in the AL.
behavioral_consequence: None tested
ecological_context: Not explicitly tested in this study
summary: Multiple TH+ cell types (SIFa, DNp32, CSD) innervate the AL. Activation of TH-Gal4 and OK371-Gal4 neurons releases dopamine in the AL, while CSD neuron activation did not.

paper_title: Differential dopaminergic modulation of the antennal lobe of Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: ATP (1 mM via P2X2 activation), Odors
neural_activity: OK371-Gal4 labels ~3 TH+ vLNs per lobe in the ventrolateral cluster. Activation of these neurons increased GRAB-DA2 signals in the AL. Knockdown of TH in these neurons reduced DL1 ePN responses to IAA and increased temporal sharpness.
behavioral_consequence: None tested
ecological_context: Not explicitly tested in this study
summary: A subset of glutamatergic vLNs (OK371-Gal4) express TH. Activation of these neurons releases dopamine in the AL, and reducing their dopamine synthesis alters ePN odor responses.

paper_title: Sensory processing reformats odor coding around valence and dynamics
experimental_stimulus: 12 odors including ACV, benzaldehyde, ethyl acetate, acetic acid, and neutralized ACV
neural_activity: In vivo 2-photon calcium imaging (GCaMP6f) revealed responses organized by valence; some PNs had positive covariance with behavioral preference (attraction), others negative (aversion). Responses were mostly sustained with little adaptation.
behavioral_consequence: Population activity vectors correlated with innate behavioral valence (preference index). No direct neuronal activation/inactivation tested.
ecological_context: Innate odor valence drives feeding, mating, and avoiding danger.
summary: PNs encode odor identity and valence, with connectivity shaping downstream LHN input.

paper_title: Sensory processing reformats odor coding around valence and dynamics
experimental_stimulus: 12 odors, including ACV
neural_activity: Strongest positive valence association of any PN. Major source of cascaded local inhibition onto LHNs via Local Inhibitory LHNs (LHLNs).
behavioral_consequence: Correlates with odor attraction in behavior assays.
ecological_context: Supports attraction behaviors (e.g., feeding).
summary: DM1 PNs encode strong positive valence and exert widespread inhibition on LHNs with distinct valence.

paper_title: Sensory processing reformats odor coding around valence and dynamics
experimental_stimulus: 12 odors, ACV variants (acid vs ester)
neural_activity: Acid-responsive glomeruli. Respond robustly to ACV. Provide input to appetitive LHNs.
behavioral_consequence: Correlates with positive valence coding in downstream LHNs.
ecological_context: Senses acidic components of natural odor mixtures.
summary: DP1m PNs respond to acid components and drive ACV-selective LHN responses.

paper_title: Sensory processing reformats odor coding around valence and dynamics
experimental_stimulus: 12 odors, ACV variants, 2s vs 10s odor pulses
neural_activity: GCaMP6f imaging showed LHNs reformat PN activity into segregated valence channels (Channel 1: appetitive, Channel 2: aversive). Dynamics split into sustained and transient types. GABA blockade altered tuning in specific types.
behavioral_consequence: Activity correlates with innate odor valence. No direct neuronal activation/inactivation tested.
ecological_context: Valence segregation supports innate behavioral responses; dynamics allow regulation of different motor programs.
summary: LHNs transform combinatorial PN codes into segregated valence channels and orthogonal temporal dynamics.

paper_title: Sensory processing reformats odor coding around valence and dynamics
experimental_stimulus: ACV, acetic acid, neutralized ACV, ester mixtures
neural_activity: Two-photon imaging and patch-clamp recordings. Sustained adaptation. Linear integration of acid (DP1m) and ester (DM1/DM4) PN inputs; mixture responses matched linear sum.
behavioral_consequence: Correlates with positive valence (attraction).
ecological_context: Feeding response to natural odor mixtures (e.g., vinegar).
summary: Appetitive LHNs integrate acid and ester components linearly to generate sustained ACV responses.

paper_title: Sensory processing reformats odor coding around valence and dynamics
experimental_stimulus: ACV, other odors
neural_activity: Two-photon imaging. Strongly adapting (transient response to odor onset, returns to baseline).
behavioral_consequence: Correlates with positive valence.
ecological_context: Enables detection of odor onset (motion) for rapid response.
summary: Appetitive LHNs encoding transient dynamics to signal odor onset.

paper_title: Sensory processing reformats odor coding around valence and dynamics
experimental_stimulus: ACV, other odors
neural_activity: Two-photon imaging and patch-clamp. Transient response to ACV suppressed by local inhibition. Patch-clamp showed depolarization followed by hyperpolarization upon ACV. GABA blockade increased response persistence.
behavioral_consequence: Correlates with negative valence when inhibition active; inhibition supports this coding.
ecological_context: May signal specific aversive or inhibitory cues within complex environments.
summary: DP1m-dominant LHNs with unbalanced inhibition generate transient ACV responses.

paper_title: Sensory processing reformats odor coding around valence and dynamics
experimental_stimulus: Odors
neural_activity: Imaged from glomerulus DP1l (exception to PN imaging). Responses measured to confirm acid sensing.
behavioral_consequence: Not directly tested for behavior.
ecological_context: Primary sensory input for olfactory processing.
summary: ORNs provide initial odor representation; DP1l ORNs tested as acid sensors.

paper_title: Ascending nociceptive pathways drive rapid escape and sustained avoidance in adult Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Thermal nociceptive stimuli (>40°C)
neural_activity: Activated (Calcium imaging from axons)
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation induces rapid escape and sustained place avoidance
summary: Abdominal md neurons are activated by thermal nociceptive stimuli (>40°C) and optogenetic activation induces rapid escape and sustained place avoidance.

paper_title: Ascending nociceptive pathways drive rapid escape and sustained avoidance in adult Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Synaptic input from abdominal md axons
neural_activity: Strongest synaptic connections with md axons (Connectomic reconstruction)
behavioral_consequence: Two classes mediate rapid escape responses; a third class supports sustained avoidance
summary: Ascending interneurons form strong synaptic connections with md axons; two classes mediate rapid escape and a third supports sustained avoidance.

paper_title: A neural circuit for context-dependent multimodal signaling in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson, 625 nm light)
neural_activity: Induces persistent courtship state; activates recurrent neural network (RNN) downstream; intrinsic slow decay of activity (5-10 s)
behavioral_consequence: Reliably induces vibrations (abdominal quivering) persistently (tens of seconds after activation); drives song; induces locomotor arrest (stops the male); suppresses song in courting males; sexual satiation reduces persistence of evoked signals
ecological_context: Coordinates multimodal signaling with locomotion; stationarity ensures effective transmission of substrate-borne vibrations to the female

paper_title: A neural circuit for context-dependent multimodal signaling in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson, 625 nm light)
neural_activity: Processes auditory and visual cues; direct connection to descending neuron pIP10
behavioral_consequence: Reliably drives song (sine and pulse modes); induces vibrations at activation offset (5-10 s); suppresses vibrations in courting males; sexual satiation reduces persistence of offset vibrations
ecological_context: Signal choice based on distance and locomotion; singing is favored when male/female are closer or moving

paper_title: A neural circuit for context-dependent multimodal signaling in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Descending neuron receiving input from pC2l
behavioral_consequence: Drives song (sine and pulse); does not drive vibrations
ecological_context: Part of the central brain song pathway driving ventral nerve cord song production

paper_title: A neural circuit for context-dependent multimodal signaling in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Descending neuron involved in song production
behavioral_consequence: Drives song; does not drive vibrations
ecological_context: Part of the central brain song pathway

paper_title: A neural circuit for context-dependent multimodal signaling in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic inactivation (GtACR1, 525 nm light)
neural_activity: Silencing of all glutamatergic motor neurons
behavioral_consequence: Stops female locomotion; increases male vibration probability by 30% in courting males
ecological_context: Female stationarity is necessary and sufficient to drive male vibrations; stationarity improves vibration transmission via substrate contact

paper_title: A neural circuit for context-dependent multimodal signaling in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson, 625 nm light)
neural_activity: Drives walking behavior
behavioral_consequence: Accelerates female locomotion; nearly abolishes male vibrations in courting males
ecological_context: Walking interferes with the transmission and detection of substrate-borne vibrations

paper_title: Synaptic targets of circadian clock neurons influence core clock parameters
experimental_stimulus: Genetic manipulation (Tetanus toxin, hid, NaChBac, Shibirets); Environmental conditions (Constant Darkness, LD at 25°C, LD at 18°C)
neural_activity: Nonclock neurons (no PER nuclear signal); Identified as main postsynaptic partners of sLNvs (connectomics, BAcTrace); Predicted glutamatergic (FlyWire); Low Hr38-Tomato signal
behavioral_consequence: Silencing (Tetanus toxin/hid at 25°C) shortened free-running period in DD; reduced daytime activity and increased daytime sleep in LD (25°C and 18°C); No effect on eclosion rhythms. Hyperexcitation (NaChBac) lengthened free-running period; reduced daytime activity; No change in sleep. Silencing (Shibirets at 29°C) lengthened free-running period.
ecological_context: Relevant for activity organization under cold, winter-like days (18°C LD cycles)
techniques_used: ['Connectomics (Hemibrain, FlyWire)', 'Split-GAL4 driver generation', 'BAcTrace', 'Immunohistochemistry (PER, GFP, PDF)', 'Locomotor activity monitoring (DAM2)', 'Eclosion monitoring']
summary: SLP316 neurons are nonclock postsynaptic partners of sLNvs; genetic silencing at 25°C shortens free-running period and increases daytime sleep, while hyperexcitation lengthens period.

paper_title: Synaptic targets of circadian clock neurons influence core clock parameters
experimental_stimulus: Connectomic analysis, BAcTrace (retrograde tracing)
neural_activity: Identified as main presynaptic inputs to SLP316 neurons
summary: sLNvs form strong synaptic connections with SLP316 neurons, confirmed via BAcTrace and connectomic analysis.

paper_title: SIFa peptidergic neurons orchestrate the internal states and energy balance of male Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Social isolation (single-reared), sexual experience (mating), ATP application (via P2X2 for activation), Temperature shift (29°C via TrpA1)
neural_activity: TRIC transcriptional reporter showed altered Ca2+ activity in PI region after social isolation/experience. GCaMP6 showed Ca2+ influx upon sNPF activation. GRAB sensors detected dopamine release upon SIFa activation. Immunostaining showed reduced presynaptic terminals (syt.eGFP) in VNC and brain for singly reared/mated males compared to grouped.
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown eliminated both Longer Mating Duration (LMD) and Shorter Mating Duration (SMD). Activation (TrpA1/29°C) induced SMD and increased food intake (EX-Q/Blue-Dye). Inactivation (shits) reduced food intake. Dopaminergic transmission affected sleep. Courtship index unchanged by acute activation.
ecological_context: Regulates interval timing in mating based on social context (rivals vs sexually saturated condition) and integrates internal states for energy balance (feeding).
techniques_used: ['RNAi knockdown', 'Thermogenetics (TrpA1, shits)', 'Calcium imaging (GCaMP6, TRIC)', 'GRAB sensors', 'Mating duration assay', 'Food intake assays (EX-Q, Blue-Dye)', 'Immunostaining']
summary: SIFa neurons regulate mating duration (LMD/SMD) and food intake based on social experience and internal states, releasing dopamine and glutamate, with synaptic plasticity observed after mating or isolation.

paper_title: SIFa peptidergic neurons orchestrate the internal states and energy balance of male Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Dopamine binding (Tango system), ATP application (via SIFa activation)
neural_activity: Dop1R1-Tango system confirmed activation by dopamine. GRAB sensors detected DA signals in these cells upon SIFa neuron stimulation.
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown of SIFaR disrupted both LMD and SMD. Knockdown of VGlut receptors in these cells disrupted SMD. Dop1R1-RNAi selectively influenced SMD.
ecological_context: Receives SIFa signals from the brain to modulate interval timing behaviors in the VNC context.
summary: SIFaR-expressing neurons in the VNC receive dopaminergic and glutamatergic input from SIFa neurons to regulate mating duration behaviors.

paper_title: SIFa peptidergic neurons orchestrate the internal states and energy balance of male Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: ATP application (via P2X2 system to depolarize neurons)
neural_activity: Activation elicited strong Ca2+ responses in SIFa neurons (recorded with GCaMP6). GRASP assays showed robust synapses in PI tract and LAL area.
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown of sNPF-R in SIFa cells disrupted SMD. Knockdown of sNPF in SIFaR cells disrupted LMD and SMD. Synaptic signals increased in PI region upon isolation/experience.
ecological_context: Part of a feedback loop with SIFa neurons to create internal states for interval timing behaviors based on social environment.
summary: sNPF neurons activate SIFa neurons and form a feedback loop to modulate internal states required for context-dependent mating duration.

paper_title: Visual circuitry for distance estimation in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Gap-crossing assay (visual corridors, gaps of varying sizes); visual motion cues during locomotion
neural_activity: Primary local motion detectors (characterized as requiring direction selectivity for motion parallax); calcium activity not directly recorded in this paper but identified as motion detectors
behavioral_consequence: Silencing (shibirets) shifted gap-crossing frequency curve toward smaller gap sizes (flies overestimated gap size, reduced crossing of large gaps). Constitutive activation (dTRPA1) shifted curve toward larger gap sizes (flies underestimated gap size, crossed larger gaps). Required for visual distance estimation.
ecological_context: Gap-crossing behavior during free locomotion in the environment; inference of three-dimensional structure from two-dimensional retinal images.
summary: T4 and T5 local motion detectors are required for visual distance estimation during gap crossing; silencing causes flies to avoid large gaps, while activation allows crossing of larger gaps.

paper_title: Visual circuitry for distance estimation in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Gap-crossing assay (silencing screen); Panoramic visual stimuli (moving bars on moving checkerboard backgrounds, varying speeds/directions)
neural_activity: Calcium responses recorded via in vivo two-photon microscopy. Not direction-selective for object or background individually. Exhibits relative-direction selectivity (suppressed when bar and background move in same direction, especially similar speeds). Tuned to the ratio of bar and background velocities (motion parallax signal). Tuning organized during active locomotion.
behavioral_consequence: Silencing (shibirets) shifted gap-crossing frequency curve toward larger gaps (modest effect compared to T4/T5).
ecological_context: Distance estimation during gap crossing; encoding relative distance between objects and backgrounds using motion parallax cues.
techniques_used: ['High-throughput gap-crossing assay', 'shibirets (silencing)', 'in vivo two-photon calcium imaging']
summary: LC15 visual feature detectors encode motion parallax signals via relative velocity ratios and are necessary for normal gap-crossing behavior; silencing alters distance perception.

paper_title: Visual circuitry for distance estimation in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Gap-crossing assay (silencing screen)
neural_activity: GABAergic medullar neuron providing feedback to peripheral visual neurons (disrupts T4/T5 direction selectivity)
behavioral_consequence: Silencing shifted gap-crossing frequency curve toward larger gaps (similar to LC15, modest effect)
ecological_context: Gap-crossing behavior during locomotion
summary: Silencing of C3 neurons alters gap-crossing behavior, shifting the curve toward larger gaps, consistent with a role in visual distance estimation.

paper_title: Visual circuitry for distance estimation in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Gap-crossing assay (silencing screen)
neural_activity: Not characterized in detail
behavioral_consequence: Silencing shifted gap-crossing frequency curve toward smaller gaps (statistically barely significant)
ecological_context: Gap-crossing behavior during locomotion
summary: LC20 silencing showed a preliminary effect on gap-crossing behavior toward smaller gaps, though results were barely significant and not pursued further.

paper_title: Type-specific molecular signaling architectures and synaptic plasticity of Drosophila olfactory sensory neurons
experimental_stimulus: Geosmin (natural ligand; not experimentally stimulated in this study)
neural_activity: Not electrophysiologically recorded in this study. Measured molecular composition: High Unc13A levels, low Unc13B and Syd-1 levels at active zones (AZs); Fewer AZs per neuron (EM/Confocal); Small dendritic area occupied by ORs (STED). No increase in AZ number upon Cac knockdown (Confocal).
behavioral_consequence: Not experimentally tested in this study. Paper states activation of target DA2 glomerulus elicits aversive behaviour (citing Stensmyr et al., 2012).
ecological_context: Detects geosmin, an odorant signalling ecologically harmful microorganisms.
summary: Or56a OSNs detect geosmin and show high Unc13A levels and fewer active zones compared to Or22a OSNs. They lack homeostatic plasticity (no AZ increase) following Cac knockdown.

paper_title: Type-specific molecular signaling architectures and synaptic plasticity of Drosophila olfactory sensory neurons
experimental_stimulus: Food odours (natural ligand; not experimentally stimulated in this study)
neural_activity: Not electrophysiologically recorded in this study. Measured molecular composition: Lower Unc13A levels, higher Unc13B and Syd-1 levels at active zones (AZs); More AZs per neuron (EM/Confocal); Larger dendritic area occupied by ORs (STED). Increased AZ number and Unc13A puncta upon Cac knockdown (Confocal).
behavioral_consequence: Not experimentally tested in this study.
ecological_context: Detects conventional food odours.
summary: Or22a OSNs detect food odours and show lower Unc13A levels and more active zones compared to Or56a OSNs. They display homeostatic plasticity (AZ increase) following Cac knockdown.

paper_title: Connectomics Reveals a Feed-Forward Swallowing Circuit Driving Protein Appetite
experimental_stimulus: Yeast deprivation (protein deprivation)
neural_activity: Not directly measured; activity modulation by amino acid state previously established
behavioral_consequence: Silencing using IR76b-Gal4 > Kir2.1 led to a drastic decrease in total yeast sips, reduced length of feeding bursts, and increased inter-burst intervals in yeast-deprived flies.
ecological_context: Regulation of protein appetite and nutrient-specific feeding bursts during amino acid deprivation.
summary: IR76b GRNs are required for yeast appetite; silencing reduces total sips and feeding burst length in protein-deprived flies.

paper_title: Connectomics Reveals a Feed-Forward Swallowing Circuit Driving Protein Appetite
experimental_stimulus: Yeast feeding
neural_activity: Not directly measured; morphologically and functionally distinct populations identified (claw vs dorsal)
behavioral_consequence: Silencing tpGRNs (R57F03-Gal4) decreased yeast feeding burst length in yeast-deprived flies but not fully fed flies. Did not affect inter-burst intervals.
ecological_context: Modulation of feeding burst length specifically in response to nutrient state changes (yeast deprivation).
summary: Taste peg GRNs are necessary for sustaining yeast feeding bursts in nutrient-deprived flies but dispensable in fully fed flies.

paper_title: Connectomics Reveals a Feed-Forward Swallowing Circuit Driving Protein Appetite
experimental_stimulus: Yeast and Sucrose feeding
neural_activity: Not directly measured; identified as part of PER hub via connectomics
behavioral_consequence: Silencing had no effect on yeast feeding burst length, total sips, or inter-burst intervals. Small effect on sucrose inter-burst intervals.
ecological_context: Proboscis extension reflex (PER) initiation; dispensable for sustaining protein-specific feeding bursts.
summary: Rattle neurons (PER hub) are dispensable for modulating the length of yeast feeding bursts in freely behaving flies.

paper_title: Connectomics Reveals a Feed-Forward Swallowing Circuit Driving Protein Appetite
experimental_stimulus: Yeast feeding (yeast-deprived and fully fed)
neural_activity: Not directly measured; 2nd order dtpGRN-specific neuron in swallowing hub
behavioral_consequence: Silencing led to a slight (almost significant) increase in yeast feeding burst length in deprived flies and a significant increase in fully fed flies.
ecological_context: Downstream component of the nutrient state-dependent feeding circuit.
summary: Silencing the downstream partner CB0041 increased yeast feeding burst length, suggesting inhibitory regulation of burst duration.

paper_title: Connectomics Reveals a Feed-Forward Swallowing Circuit Driving Protein Appetite
experimental_stimulus: Yeast (amino acid-deprived vs fully fed), Sucrose (carbohydrate-deprived vs fully fed)
neural_activity: Not directly measured; 3rd order neuron with strong effective connectivity to swallowing motor neurons
behavioral_consequence: Silencing significantly reduced yeast feeding burst length in amino acid-deprived flies but not in fully fed flies. No effect on sucrose burst length. Thermogenetic activation increased ingestion volume (cibarial area).
ecological_context: Facilitates protein-specific long feeding bursts when amino acid-starved; regulates ingestion efficiency.
summary: Sustain neurons are necessary for sustaining yeast feeding bursts in a protein state-dependent manner and control ingestion volume per pumping event.

paper_title: Connectomics Reveals a Feed-Forward Swallowing Circuit Driving Protein Appetite
experimental_stimulus: Yeast and Sucrose feeding
neural_activity: Not directly measured; motor neuron receiving inputs from Sustain
behavioral_consequence: Silencing led to shorter feeding bursts for both yeast and sucrose. Total sips and inter-burst intervals unaffected.
ecological_context: Controls ingestion volume and rate; essential for sustaining feeding bursts regardless of nutrient quality.
summary: MN11D activity is necessary for sustaining feeding bursts on both protein and carbohydrate substrates, linking Sustain to ingestion efficiency.

paper_title: Anti-diuretic hormone ITP signals via a guanylate cyclase receptor to modulate systemic homeostasis in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Desiccation stress (dehydration)
neural_activity: Reduced ITPa immunofluorescence indicates increased peptide release into circulation
behavioral_consequence: Adult-specific overexpression increases desiccation tolerance and body water content; knockdown of receptor Gyc76C in renal tubules reduces desiccation tolerance
ecological_context: Survival during dehydration and osmotic stress
summary: L-NSCITP neurons release ITPa during desiccation. Overexpression enhances desiccation tolerance and water retention, mediated via Gyc76C in renal tubules.

paper_title: Anti-diuretic hormone ITP signals via a guanylate cyclase receptor to modulate systemic homeostasis in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Desiccation stress
neural_activity: Reduced ITPa immunofluorescence indicates peptide release during desiccation
behavioral_consequence: ITPa overexpression did not show drastic differences in locomotor activity under normal or desiccating conditions
ecological_context: Circadian clock-associated behaviors during desiccation
summary: 5th-LNv and LNdITP neurons release ITPa during desiccation, though overexpression did not significantly alter locomotor activity under tested conditions.

paper_title: Anti-diuretic hormone ITP signals via a guanylate cyclase receptor to modulate systemic homeostasis in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Anatomical mapping (no specific stimulus tested)
neural_activity: Co-express ITPa and ITPL1; innervate hindgut and rectum
behavioral_consequence: None explicitly tested; anatomical mapping shows innervation of rectal pad
ecological_context: Hindgut and rectal innervation for excretion/osmoregulation
summary: iag neurons are anatomically identified as ITPa/ITPL1-expressing neurons innervating the rectum, suggesting a role in hindgut physiology.

paper_title: Anti-diuretic hormone ITP signals via a guanylate cyclase receptor to modulate systemic homeostasis in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Recombinant ITPa application (ex vivo); Gyc76C knockdown
neural_activity: Gyc76C knockdown abolishes ITPa-mediated inhibition of fluid secretion
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown reduces desiccation tolerance, increases chill-coma recovery time, and increases survival under salt stress
ecological_context: Osmotic and ionic homeostasis
summary: Gyc76C in principal cells mediates ITPa-induced inhibition of renal tubule secretion, influencing desiccation tolerance and salt stress survival.

paper_title: Anti-diuretic hormone ITP signals via a guanylate cyclase receptor to modulate systemic homeostasis in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Recombinant ITPa application (ex vivo); Gyc76C knockdown
neural_activity: Gyc76C knockdown abolishes ITPa-mediated inhibition of fluid secretion
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown reduces desiccation tolerance, increases chill-coma recovery time, and decreases survival under salt stress
ecological_context: Osmotic and ionic homeostasis
summary: Gyc76C in stellate cells mediates ITPa-induced inhibition of renal tubule secretion, influencing desiccation tolerance and salt stress survival.

paper_title: Anti-diuretic hormone ITP signals via a guanylate cyclase receptor to modulate systemic homeostasis in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Gyc76C knockdown (loss of function)
neural_activity: N/A
behavioral_consequence: Reduced starvation tolerance, reduced glucose and lipid levels, increased feeding, and altered food preference (yeast over sucrose)
ecological_context: Metabolic homeostasis and energy storage
summary: Gyc76C signaling in fat body regulates metabolic homeostasis; knockdown reduces starvation tolerance and alters feeding behavior and energy stores.

paper_title: Social experience shapes fighting strategies in Drosophila
behavioral_consequence: Required for lunging aggression behavior
ecological_context: Social isolation generally increases lunging aggression; lunging is a frequent and low-intensity combat behavior
summary: Or67d olfactory receptor neurons are required for lunging aggression behavior.

paper_title: Social experience shapes fighting strategies in Drosophila
behavioral_consequence: Required for tussling aggression behavior
ecological_context: Social enrichment heightens tussling behavior; tussling is less frequent but more forceful than lunging
summary: Or47b olfactory receptor neurons are required for tussling aggression behavior.

paper_title: Social experience shapes fighting strategies in Drosophila
behavioral_consequence: Specifically promote tussling
ecological_context: Shifting from lunging to tussling in socially enriched males is accompanied with better territory control and mating success
summary: Three pairs of central pC1 neurons specifically promote tussling behavior.

paper_title: Neuron-to-glia signaling drives critical period experience-dependent synapse pruning
experimental_stimulus: 24-hour exposure to ethyl butyrate (EB) odorant (5-25% in oil) during 0-1 days post-eclosion (critical period)
neural_activity: Experience-dependent externalization of phosphatidylserine (PS) on synaptic membranes in VM7 glomeruli; significant reduction in OSN synaptic innervation volume (pruning) dependent on PS synthase and Subdued scramblase
ecological_context: Critical periods enable early-life synaptic connectivity optimization whereby initial sensory experience remodels circuits to adapt juveniles to the odorant environment
techniques_used: ['Confocal microscopy', 'Genetic knockdown (RNAi)', 'Genetic overexpression', 'Innervation volume quantification', 'Fluorescence intensity measurement (LactC1C2::GFP, MApHS)']
summary: Or42a OSNs exposed to EB odorant during the critical period exhibit experience-dependent PS externalization and synaptic pruning in VM7 glomeruli; this process requires neuronal PS synthase and Subdued scramblase activity.

paper_title: Neuron-to-glia signaling drives critical period experience-dependent synapse pruning
experimental_stimulus: Experience-dependent signaling from activated OSNs during critical period (induced by EB exposure)
neural_activity: Infiltration of VM7 synaptic glomeruli; phagocytosis of synapses mediated by Draper receptor binding to externalized PS; infiltration and pruning driven by glial insulin receptor (InR) signaling
ecological_context: Glial phagocytosis orchestrates targeted synaptic glomeruli pruning to sculpt olfactory circuit connectivity dependent on early-life critical period sensory environment
techniques_used: ['Confocal microscopy', 'Genetic knockdown (repo-Gal4 InR RNAi)', 'Genetic activation (repo-Gal4 InR-CA)', 'Fluorescence intensity measurement (repo-RFP)']
summary: Astrocyte-like glia infiltrate and phagocytose OSN synapses in VM7 glomeruli in response to experience; this process requires the Draper receptor and glial insulin receptor (InR) signaling.

paper_title: Molecular gradients shape synaptic specificity of a visuomotor transformation
experimental_stimulus: Looming stimuli (dark radially expanding motion) at various elevations (dorsal to ventral); moving dark bars in 24 orientations
neural_activity: Dorsal LPLC2 neurons form more synapses with the Giant Fibre (GF) than ventral neurons (EM connectomics). Dorsal LPLC2 neurons respond to both upwards and downwards motion, while ventral neurons show little response to downwards motion (two-photon calcium imaging). Exhibit dorsoventral gradients in cell recognition molecules Dpr13 and Beat-VI (scRNA-seq, HCR-FISH).
behavioral_consequence: Flies exhibit higher percentages of short-mode escape takeoffs in response to dorsal looming stimuli compared to ventral. Knockdown of Beat-VI in LPLC2 reduces dorsal sensitivity to downward motion, flattening directional tuning. Overexpression of Dpr13 flattens the short-mode takeoff gradient across elevations.
ecological_context: Escape behaviour to stimuli approaching from the dorsal visual field, facilitating rapid response to looming threats.
techniques_used: ['electron microscopy connectomics', 'two-photon calcium imaging', 'scRNA-seq', 'HCR-FISH', 'high-throughput takeoff assay (FlyPEZ)', 'genetic manipulation (RNAi, overexpression)']
summary: LPLC2 neurons detect looming motion and exhibit dorsoventral molecular and synaptic gradients that drive preferential escape responses to dorsal stimuli.

paper_title: Molecular gradients shape synaptic specificity of a visuomotor transformation
experimental_stimulus: Looming stimuli at various elevations
neural_activity: In vivo whole-cell patch-clamp recordings show larger membrane depolarization in response to dorsal looming compared to ventral looming. Receives graded synaptic inputs from LPLC2 axons (connectomics). Expresses DIP-ε binding partner for Dpr13.
behavioral_consequence: Drives short-mode escape takeoffs. Loss of DIP-ε in GF significantly reduces short-mode takeoff percentages across all elevations and eliminates the dorsoventral gradient in escape behavior.
ecological_context: Executes rapid escape responses (takeoffs) to avoid looming objects.
techniques_used: ['in vivo whole-cell patch-clamp electrophysiology', 'high-throughput takeoff assay (FlyPEZ)', 'genetic manipulation (RNAi, null alleles)']
summary: The Giant Fibre mediates rapid escape takeoffs, with response magnitude biased by dorsal looming inputs via graded connectivity with LPLC2 neurons.

paper_title: Molecular gradients shape synaptic specificity of a visuomotor transformation
experimental_stimulus: Moving dark edges in upwards and downwards directions
neural_activity: Form antiparallel dorsoventral synaptic gradients onto LPLC2 dendrites in LoP4 layers (FlyWire connectome). Beat-VI on LPLC2 and Side-II on T4/T5 regulate these specific inputs.
behavioral_consequence: Regulate LPLC2 directional tuning. Removal of Side-II from T4/T5 neurons phenocopies Beat-VI knockdown in LPLC2, reducing downward motion sensitivity in dorsal LPLC2 neurons.
ecological_context: Motion detection component of the looming stimulus processing.
summary: T4d/T5d neurons provide graded downward motion inputs to LPLC2 dendrites, regulated by Beat-VI/Side-II interactions, shaping LPLC2 directional tuning.

paper_title: Dual roles of Drosophila reward-encoding dopamine neurons in regulating innate and learned behaviors
experimental_stimulus: Activation of the entire population of reward-encoding DANs; Activation of subsets of DANs
behavioral_consequence: Activation of the entire population drives innate aversion in feeding, locomotion, and spatial preference assays. These neurons also confer positive value onto associated cues to drive future learned attraction. Activating subsets reveals that effects on innate and learned behaviors are dissociable.
summary: Reward-encoding DANs regulate innate aversion (feeding, locomotion, spatial preference) and learned attraction to cues. Activating the entire population drives both innate aversion and learned attraction, while activating subsets dissociates these effects.

paper_title: A competitive disinhibitory network for robust optic flow processing in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Binocular asymmetric horizontal motion (rotational/yaw), binocular symmetric horizontal motion (translational-like), monocular stimuli
neural_activity: Increased calcium responses to binocular asymmetric optic flow; enhanced selectivity to rotational vs. translational stimuli compared to HS cells; small response to contralateral back-to-front visual motion.
behavioral_consequence: Unilateral silencing (Kir2.1) resulted in contralateral path deviation during forward walking runs; bilateral or no silencing showed no bias.
ecological_context: Steering control during high-speed walking; course control in exploratory flies.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging', 'Behavioral walking arena', 'Genetic silencing (Kir2.1)', 'Electron microscopy (EM)']
summary: DNp15 neurons exhibit enhanced selectivity to rotational optic flow compared to HS cells and are necessary for steering control during high-speed walking, as unilateral silencing biases walking direction.

paper_title: A competitive disinhibitory network for robust optic flow processing in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Binocular asymmetric, binocular symmetric, and translational optic flow
neural_activity: Enhanced selectivity to binocular symmetric motion compared to translational; responses reduced and selectivity skewed towards zero when GABA receptors (Rdl) were disrupted; minimal responses to non-preferred directions.
behavioral_consequence: Unilateral silencing (Kir2.1) induced biases during forward runs; bilateral silencing did not affect forward run course.
ecological_context: Monitoring motor state and steering in the presence of angular drifts during high-speed walking; course control.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging', 'GABA receptor disruption (Rdl-FlpStop)', 'Behavioral walking arena', 'Genetic silencing (Kir2.1)', 'Computational modeling']
summary: bIPS interneurons enhance selectivity to binocular symmetric optic flow via GABAergic inhibition; unilateral silencing biases steering during high-speed walking, supporting robust optic flow processing.

paper_title: A competitive disinhibitory network for robust optic flow processing in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Binocular asymmetric, binocular symmetric, translational, and velocity-tuned motion
neural_activity: More sensitive to slower speeds; minimal or negative calcium signals to pitch and roll; responded to progressive stimuli but minimal to contralateral regressive motion.
behavioral_consequence: Not directly tested in this study.
ecological_context: Part of the central network supporting steering control and optic flow processing.
summary: uLPTCrn interneurons show distinct velocity tuning and minimal response to non-horizontal optic flow, contributing to the intermediate layer of the optic flow network.

paper_title: A competitive disinhibitory network for robust optic flow processing in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Binocular asymmetric, binocular symmetric, translational, and velocity-tuned motion
neural_activity: More sensitive to faster than slower speeds (contrast to H2 cells); responded to both contralateral regressive and progressive motion despite no direct HS input.
behavioral_consequence: Not directly tested in this study.
ecological_context: Part of the central network supporting steering control and optic flow processing.
summary: H2rn interneurons exhibit velocity tuning distinct from H2 cells and receive inputs that convey visual information indirectly to the network.

paper_title: A competitive disinhibitory network for robust optic flow processing in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Binocular asymmetric, binocular symmetric, translational, and monocular optic flow
neural_activity: Responded to both rotational and translational optic flow; relatively weak selectivity to rotational vs. translational compared to downstream partners.
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this study (referenced as driving rotations in prior work).
ecological_context: Course control and steering during high-speed translation.
summary: HS and H2 cells form the input layer of the network, responding to horizontal optic flow and providing convergent input to central interneurons.

paper_title: A competitive disinhibitory network for robust optic flow processing in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Horizontal, pitch, and roll optic flow
neural_activity: Identified via EM connectivity; form a distinct network from HS-H2.
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this study.
ecological_context: Gaze stabilization and course control.
summary: VS cells form a distinct central network with parallel operation to the HS-H2 network, projecting to different descending and neck motor outputs.

paper_title: Specialized parallel pathways for adaptive control of visual object pursuit
experimental_stimulus: Small moving objects ('motion pulses') on visual panorama; Optogenetic arousal (P1 stimulation); Variations in forward velocity
neural_activity: Responds best to objects near visual midline; Direction-selective (prefers ipsiversive motion); GABAergic (inhibitory); Depolarizes and increases firing rate with arousal; Visual responses increase with forward velocity (graded, nearly linear)
behavioral_consequence: Direct depolarization causes ipsilateral turning and increases forward velocity; Suppression decreases steering toward visual object during pursuit; Suppression blunts forward acceleration when object crosses midline (open-loop)
ecological_context: Courtship pursuit where aroused males chase a running female
techniques_used: ['Patch-clamp electrophysiology', 'Optogenetics', 'Genetic suppression (Kir2.1)', 'Behavioral assays (spherical treadmill, virtual reality)']
summary: AOTU019 cells detect objects near the midline, are direction-selective, and increase steering gain during arousal and fast running. Depolarization drives turning and forward velocity, while suppression impairs precise steering.

paper_title: Specialized parallel pathways for adaptive control of visual object pursuit
experimental_stimulus: Small moving objects ('motion pulses') on visual panorama; Optogenetic arousal (P1 stimulation)
neural_activity: Responds best to objects far from midline (lateral visual field); Less direction-selective than AOTU019; Cholinergic (excitatory); Increases firing rate with arousal (less than AOTU019)
behavioral_consequence: Direct depolarization causes ipsilateral turning
ecological_context: Courtship pursuit where aroused males chase a running female
summary: AOTU025 cells detect objects far from the midline and are excitatory. Activation drives ipsilateral turning, contributing to coarse steering toward the midline.

paper_title: Specialized parallel pathways for adaptive control of visual object pursuit
experimental_stimulus: Small moving objects ('motion pulses')
neural_activity: Right-left firing rate asymmetry predicted by combined activity of AOTU019 and AOTU025
behavioral_consequence: Visual responses predict rotational velocity
ecological_context: Steering control during visual object pursuit
summary: DNa02 activity asymmetry reflects AOTU input and predicts rotational velocity during visual pursuit.

paper_title: Cell type-specific driver lines targeting the Drosophila central complex and their use to investigate neuropeptide expression and sleep regulation
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTrpA1) and Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Activation via split-GAL4 drivers (SS54903, SS53683, SS63959) comprising eight intrinsic FB columnar neurons
behavioral_consequence: Decreased sleep duration and increased P(Wake) in males and females. Phenotypes more pronounced for nighttime sleep in males. No increase in activity measures (beam counts/pixel movements).
ecological_context: Sleep regulation and activity control within the central complex.
summary: hDeltaF neurons are strongly wake-promoting; activation decreases sleep duration and increases P(Wake) without increasing general activity.

paper_title: Cell type-specific driver lines targeting the Drosophila central complex and their use to investigate neuropeptide expression and sleep regulation
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTrpA1) and Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Activation via split-GAL4 lines (SS02232, SS54295) comprising 22 columnar neurons linking EB, PB and noduli
behavioral_consequence: Decreases sleep duration and increases P(Wake) in males; wake-promoting in females (SS02232). Optogenetic activation suppresses sleep and increases P(Wake) in both sexes.
ecological_context: Activity regulation and sleep homeostasis involving EB-PB-Noduli circuitry.
summary: PEN_b activation is wake-promoting, decreasing sleep duration and increasing wake probability in both sexes.

paper_title: Cell type-specific driver lines targeting the Drosophila central complex and their use to investigate neuropeptide expression and sleep regulation
cell_type: PFGs (Protocerebral Bridge Fan-shaped Body Glutamatergic neurons)
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTrpA1) and Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson); EASI-FISH
neural_activity: Express tyramine (Tdc2 positive, Tbh negative) as neurotransmitter. Activation via split-GAL4 lines (SS52547, SS62596, SS52590, SS20046).
behavioral_consequence: Sexually dimorphic sleep behavior. Optogenetic activation increased sleep in females; mild increase in one male line. P(Doze) consistently higher in males and females. Thermogenetics showed increased sleep in two lines, decreased in one (SS20046).
ecological_context: Sex-specific sleep regulation and activity control.
summary: PFGs use tyramine as a neurotransmitter and show sex-specific sleep-promoting effects upon activation, increasing P(Doze).

paper_title: Cell type-specific driver lines targeting the Drosophila central complex and their use to investigate neuropeptide expression and sleep regulation
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTrpA1) and Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Activation via split-GAL4 lines (SS00026 and others).
behavioral_consequence: Decreases sleep and increases P(Wake) on Day 2. Thermogenetic activation decreased sleep in one line.
ecological_context: Sleep regulation within the central complex.
summary: EL neuron activation decreases sleep duration and increases wake probability.

paper_title: Cell type-specific driver lines targeting the Drosophila central complex and their use to investigate neuropeptide expression and sleep regulation
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTrpA1) and Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Activation via split-GAL4 lines (SS59455, SS63089, SS54676, SS59410). Highly peptidergic intrinsic columnar neurons.
behavioral_consequence: Inconsistent results across lines. One line (SS59410) wake-promoting in both sexes. Two lines (SS59455, SS63089) sleep-promoting in males. Optogenetic results varied similarly. Authors could not make an overall conclusion.
ecological_context: Potential role in sleep regulation, though experimental outcomes were not consistent.
summary: hDeltaK activation showed inconsistent effects on sleep across different driver lines, with some wake-promoting and others sleep-promoting in males.

paper_title: Cell type-specific driver lines targeting the Drosophila central complex and their use to investigate neuropeptide expression and sleep regulation
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTrpA1) and Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Activation via split-GAL4 lines (SS74918, SS74944). Connects central clock outputs to the CX.
behavioral_consequence: Strongly wake-promoting in both sexes. Decreased sleep duration, increased P(Wake), decreased P(Doze).
ecological_context: Links circadian clock outputs to the central complex to regulate wakefulness.
summary: SMP368 activation strongly suppresses sleep and increases wake probability, suggesting a role in clock-to-CX communication.

paper_title: Cell type-specific driver lines targeting the Drosophila central complex and their use to investigate neuropeptide expression and sleep regulation
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTrpA1) and Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Activation via split-GAL4 line (SS79089). Part of a potential pathway from clock outputs to the CX.
behavioral_consequence: Suppresses sleep when activated in both males and females. Increases P(Wake) and decreases P(Doze).
ecological_context: Part of a pathway connecting circadian clock outputs to the CX.
summary: SMP531 activation suppresses sleep and increases wake probability, linking clock outputs to the CX.

paper_title: Cell type-specific driver lines targeting the Drosophila central complex and their use to investigate neuropeptide expression and sleep regulation
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTrpA1)
neural_activity: Activation via five split-GAL4 lines utilizing R84C10 hemidriver, representing different dorsal FB tangential cell types.
behavioral_consequence: Mixed effects: some lines sleep promoting and others wake promoting. No detectable VNC expression.
ecological_context: Dorsal fan-shaped body involvement in sleep/activity regulation.
summary: Combinations of dFB cell types show mixed sleep-promoting or wake-promoting effects upon thermogenetic activation.

paper_title: Cell type-specific driver lines targeting the Drosophila central complex and their use to investigate neuropeptide expression and sleep regulation
experimental_stimulus: EASI-FISH on split-GAL4 marked brains; RNA profiling
neural_activity: 51 of 85 CX cell types expressed at least one neuropeptide; 21 expressed multiple neuropeptides. All neuropeptide-expressing cell types also expressed a small neurotransmitter. Examples: PFGs express tyramine; FB4K expresses acetylcholine and glutamate.
behavioral_consequence: No direct behavioral testing for the general survey; specific subtypes linked to sleep via activation screen.
ecological_context: Highly peptidergic region of the brain involved in navigation and activity regulation.
summary: Survey of 80+ CX cell types shows more than half express a neuropeptide, and all neuropeptide-expressing cells also express a small molecule neurotransmitter.

paper_title: Activity of a descending neuron associated with visually elicited flight saccades in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Visual looming stimuli (expanding dark circles on bright background), moving square, dimming control, and optogenetic red light activation
neural_activity: Whole-cell patch-clamp recordings showed state-dependent spiking: graded depolarization to ipsilateral looming at rest, action potentials during flight, and hyperpolarization to contralateral looming. Sustained activity after stimulus end predicted saccade execution.
behavioral_consequence: Activity correlated with changes in wing stroke amplitude (saccades) in tethered flight. Optogenetic activation initiated rapid free-flight saccades (increased angular velocity, heading change up to 180 degrees).
ecological_context: Mediates evasive turns during flight in response to approaching threats (predators).
techniques_used: ['Whole-cell patch-clamp electrophysiology', 'Visual stimulation (LED panels)', 'Wing kinematics recording', 'Optogenetics (csChrimson)', '3D free-flight tracking']
summary: DNp03 neurons exhibit state-dependent spiking in response to looming during flight, correlate with evasive saccades, and optogenetic activation triggers rapid turns.

paper_title: Activity of a descending neuron associated with visually elicited flight saccades in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic red light activation
neural_activity: Expression of csChrimson in DNaX neurons.
behavioral_consequence: Bilateral activation increased angular velocity and caused change in flight direction (turns), similar to optogenetically elicited saccades.
ecological_context: Associated with escape saccades during free flight.
summary: DNaX neurons, when activated optogenetically, initiate rapid flight turns similar to DNp03 activation.

paper_title: Social state alters vision using three circuit mechanisms in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (TrpA) or optogenetic activation (Chrimson); visual stimulation in norpA mutants
neural_activity: Excitatory input to TuTuA_1 (electrophysiology/calcium imaging); direct inputs to AOTU cells and IB112 (connectome)
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces prolonged aggressive behavior in females; aggression is abolished in norpA visual mutants
ecological_context: Female-female aggression; requires visual cues to locate conspecifics
summary: aIPg neurons drive female aggression in a vision-dependent manner, utilizing multiple downstream circuit mechanisms including TuTuA neurons and IB112.

paper_title: Social state alters vision using three circuit mechanisms in Drosophila
