neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging in noduli and patch-clamp electrophysiology show mirror-symmetric tuning to angular velocity (increased activity for preferred turn direction, decreased for non-preferred). Two-color calcium imaging in ellipsoid body shows P-EN activity bumps lead E-PG bumps during turns. Conjunctively tuned to heading and rotation in visual closed-loop.
behavioral_consequence: Direct behavioral activation not tested. shiTS temperature-sensitive block of P-EN synaptic output reduced E-PG bump amplitude and turn-tracking reliability, but fly walking behavior (forward velocity, rotational speed) remained similar to controls.
ecological_context: Updating heading representation in darkness using self-motion cues for navigation and path integration.
summary: P-EN neurons encode angular velocity with mirror-symmetric tuning and their activity leads E-PG heading activity in the ellipsoid body; P-EN synaptic input is required for maintaining E-PG bump strength and stability.

paper_title: Angular velocity integration in a fly heading circuit
experimental_stimulus: Head-fixed walking in darkness; visual closed-loop stripe rotation
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging in ellipsoid body shows localized activity bump tracking fly heading. In protocerebral bridge, P-EN bumps lag E-PG bumps. E-PG population vector average (PVA) tracks fly heading accurately even in darkness.
behavioral_consequence: E-PG bump tracks heading in darkness. Blocking P-EN synaptic output (shiTS) reduced E-PG bump amplitude and reliability in tracking turns, though activity still tracked turns.
ecological_context: Maintaining internal compass representation for spatial navigation in the absence of visual cues.
summary: E-PG neurons maintain a localized heading representation bump in the ellipsoid body that tracks heading in darkness and depends on P-EN synaptic input for stability and amplitude.

paper_title: Role of GABAergic Inhibition in Shaping Odor-Evoked Spatiotemporal Patterns in the Drosophila Antennal Lobe
experimental_stimulus: Local iontophoresis of GABA; odor stimuli (pentyl acetate, methyl salicylate, benzaldehyde, ethyl 3-hydroxybutyrate, p-cresol, 2-heptanone, 1-hexanol, trans-2-hexenal, 1-octen-3-ol); pharmacological antagonists (Picrotoxin, CGP54626)
neural_activity: GABA consistently inhibited spontaneous firing (reduced from 2.3 to 0 spikes/s). GABA-gated hyperpolarization was blocked by Picrotoxin (GABAA-type) but not CGP54626 (GABAB-type). LNs were broadly tuned to odors, showing specific odor preferences in response magnitude and latency. Morphology showed wide arborization throughout antennal lobe glomeruli but with nonuniform innervation density and occasional bypassed glomeruli.
summary: GABAergic LNs inhibit via GABAA receptors, display broad odor tuning with heterogeneous preferences, and exhibit wide but nonuniform morphological arborization in the antennal lobe.

paper_title: Role of GABAergic Inhibition in Shaping Odor-Evoked Spatiotemporal Patterns in the Drosophila Antennal Lobe
experimental_stimulus: Local iontophoresis of GABA; odor stimuli (pentyl acetate, methyl salicylate, etc.); pharmacological antagonists (Picrotoxin, CGP54626)
neural_activity: GABA hyperpolarized PNs via two conductances: GABAA (fast, Picrotoxin-sensitive) and GABAB (slow, CGP54626-sensitive). Picrotoxin increased spikes in the first 500 ms of odor response. CGP54626 blocked late inhibitory epochs (1.5-2.5 s). Blocking GABAB receptors decreased the coefficient of variation of PN responses, reducing the divergence of odor representations.
summary: PNs receive GABAergic inhibition via GABAA and GABAB receptors; GABAB-mediated inhibition shapes late odor-evoked epochs and increases response diversity among the PN population.

paper_title: Neural dynamics for working memory and evidence integration during olfactory navigation in Drosophila
cell_type: VT062617-Gal4 labeled Fan-Shaped Body local neurons
experimental_stimulus: Closed-loop virtual odor navigation (0.5% apple cider vinegar), wind direction control, virtual turbulent plume simulation, single odor pulses in freely walking wind tunnel
neural_activity: Localized bump of calcium activity in Fan-Shaped Body (FB). Activity ramps up with successive odor encounters (integration). Persists after odor offset for variable intervals (~7.8s time constant). Activity position remains stable during heading changes (allocentric), not tracking heading.
behavioral_consequence: Bump presence correlates with straight walking and maintaining goal direction after odor loss. Optogenetic silencing (GtACR1) impairs persistence of upwind heading after odor loss, reducing upwind walking probability in the 10s post-odor period. Split-Gal4 for hΔK (dominant type) shows similar reduction.
ecological_context: Navigating turbulent odor plumes where sensory cues are intermittent. Simulations show the measured persistence time constant optimizes navigation in a turbulent boundary layer plume.
techniques_used: ['2-photon calcium imaging', 'Optogenetics (GtACR1)', 'Closed-loop virtual reality', 'Freely walking wind tunnel', 'Computational modeling']
summary: VT062617 FB neurons exhibit odor-evoked activity that integrates over time and persists after odor loss, correlating with maintained goal-directed walking. Silencing these neurons impairs persistent upwind navigation after odor offset, suggesting a role in short-term directional memory for turbulent plume navigation.

paper_title: Neural dynamics for working memory and evidence integration during olfactory navigation in Drosophila
cell_type: 52G12-Gal4 labeled ventral Fan-Shaped Body local neurons
experimental_stimulus: Virtual turbulent plume navigation, closed-loop wind/odor pulses
neural_activity: Transient responses to odor and wind onset. No gradual ramping or persistence observed. Narrower integration filter compared to VT062617. Activity correlates with turning (positive angular speed filter) rather than straight walking.
behavioral_consequence: No specific silencing experiments performed. Trial-by-trial activity correlates with upwind displacement similarly to VT062617.
ecological_context: Part of the FB navigation center. Distinct coding features compared to VT062617 suggest specialized roles within the FB.
summary: 52G12 neurons show transient odor responses and correlate with turning behavior, contrasting with VT062617 neurons which integrate odor and persist to support straight goal-directed walking.

paper_title: Distinct circuit motifs evaluate opposing innate values of odors.
cell_type: Lateral Horn Neurons (LHNs) - Attractive and Aversive
experimental_stimulus: 16 odors with varying innate values (Value Index)
neural_activity: Distinct sets of LHNs represent opposing innate values in a graded manner. Attractive LHNs respond nearly exclusively to attractive odors with amplitude linearly related to positive value; Aversive LHNs respond to aversive odors. Attractive and aversive LHNs are spatially clustered.
behavioral_consequence: Population activity in LHNs predicts odor value more accurately than in Mushroom Body or Antennal Lobe. Blockade of local inhibition reduces LHNs tuned to attractive odors and increases those tuned to aversive odors.
ecological_context: Computation of innate hedonic values associated with food attraction (positive value), predator avoidance or poisonous substances (negative value), and pheromones.
techniques_used: ['volumetric two-photon Ca2+ imaging', 'optogenetic cell labeling (PA-GFP)', 'pharmacological blockade (picrotoxin, CGP54626)', 'spiking network modeling']
summary: LHNs encode innate odor values with attractive and aversive subtypes spatially segregated. Local inhibition is required to shape attractive odor tuning via gain control and thresholding.

paper_title: Distinct circuit motifs evaluate opposing innate values of odors.
experimental_stimulus: Odors (attractive, neutral, aversive)
neural_activity: GABAergic inhibitory neurons responding to various odors. Suppression of activity was achieved via GtACR1.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic suppression significantly biased olfactory behavior toward aversion, reducing time spent in attractive odor plumes (e.g., 2-pentanone).
ecological_context: Recognition of positive, innate values of odors required for attraction behaviors (food, mating).
summary: Local GABAergic LHNs (AV4 type) are required for the computation and recognition of positive, innate odor values; their suppression biases behavior toward aversion.

paper_title: Distinct circuit motifs evaluate opposing innate values of odors.
experimental_stimulus: Odors
neural_activity: Activity measured in Mushroom Body lobes via Ca2+ imaging. Responses abolished by laser transection or chemogenetic silencing.
behavioral_consequence: Laser transection or chemogenetic silencing (Ort) of KCs did not alter odor tuning in LHNs or compromise the accuracy of odor value decoding in the Lateral Horn.
ecological_context: Context suggests Lateral Horn value computation is independent of Mushroom Body input, supporting innate rather than learned processing.
summary: Mushroom Body input is not required for innate odor value representation in the Lateral Horn, as silencing Kenyon Cells does not affect LHN coding.

paper_title: Drosophila learn to prefer immobile spherical objects through repeated physical interaction
experimental_stimulus: Visual pinwheel guidance directing flies to interact with mobile versus immobile spherical objects
behavioral_consequence: Silencing specific subtypes (hΔA, hΔJ, hΔM, hΔK, hΔI) impaired preference for immobile over mobile balls. Silencing hΔM reduced ball-pull behavior. Silencing hΔJ increased ball-walk behavior. Silencing hΔC increased jumping from mobile balls. Silencing hΔK, hΔC, hΔE, hΔL, hΔJ increased fidelity of following visual guidance cues.
ecological_context: Object exploration allows evaluation of environmental affordances (shelter, elevation) and assessment of object stability for safety or benefit.
summary: Silencing specific hΔ neuron subtypes in the fan-shaped body impairs preference for immobile objects and alters interaction motifs like ball-pull and ball-walk.

paper_title: Drosophila learn to prefer immobile spherical objects through repeated physical interaction
experimental_stimulus: Visual pinwheel guidance directing flies to interact with mobile versus immobile spherical objects
behavioral_consequence: Silencing had no effect on the flies' ability to develop a preference for immobile objects.
summary: Silencing EPG neurons did not affect the preference for immobile objects.

paper_title: A temporally restricted function of the dopamine receptor Dop1R2 during memory formation
experimental_stimulus: Aversive olfactory conditioning (3-octanol/4-methyl-cyclohexanol odors paired with electric shock) and appetitive olfactory conditioning (odors paired with sucrose)
neural_activity: Localization of Dop1R2::HA confirmed via immunostaining; no neural activity (firing/calcium) measured
behavioral_consequence: Conditional knock-out of Dop1R2 did not impair Short-Term Memory (0h), 2-hour memory, or 24-hour Long-Term Memory (aversive or appetitive). Sensory responses to odor, shock, and sugar were normal.
summary: Dop1R2 is not required for memory formation in the Mushroom Body γ-lobe, as knock-out does not affect STM, 2h, or 24h memory performance.

paper_title: A temporally restricted function of the dopamine receptor Dop1R2 during memory formation
cell_type: Mushroom Body neurons (α/β and α’/β’ lobes)
experimental_stimulus: Aversive olfactory conditioning (3-octanol/4-methyl-cyclohexanol odors paired with electric shock) and appetitive olfactory conditioning (odors paired with sucrose)
neural_activity: Localization of Dop1R2::HA confirmed via immunostaining; no neural activity (firing/calcium) measured
behavioral_consequence: Conditional knock-out of Dop1R2 impaired 2-hour memory and 24-hour Long-Term Memory for both aversive and appetitive conditioning. Short-Term Memory (0h) remained intact. Sensory responses to odor, shock, and sugar were normal.
techniques_used: ['CRISPR-Cas9 conditional knock-out', 'Gal4/UAS system (c739-Gal4 for α/β, c305a-Gal4 for α’/β’)', 'Immunostaining (Confocal microscopy)', 'Olfactory Conditioning (T-maze)']
summary: Dop1R2 is required in the Mushroom Body α/β and α’/β’ lobes for later memory forms (2h and 24h) of both aversive and appetitive conditioning, but is dispensable for Short-Term Memory.

paper_title: Dopamine neurons that inform Drosophila olfactory memory have distinct, acute functions driving attraction and aversion
experimental_stimulus: Red light optogenetic activation (CsChrimson); Green light optogenetic inhibition (ACR1); Dopamine depletion (TH-RNAi, 3-IY); Neurotransmitter knockdowns (vGlut, Tbh, TH)
neural_activity: Activation drives valence; dopamine, glutamate, and octopamine all contribute to function (partial dependence on each)
behavioral_consequence: Strong attraction to light (positive valence). Flies slow down or stop in the light zone. Dopamine depletion reduces valence by ~46%. Combined depletion of dopamine, glutamate, and octopamine virtually ablates valence. Olfactory memory circuits (KCs, ORNs) are dispensable for this valence.
ecological_context: Indicates the role of this cluster in controlling ongoing locomotor behavior.
summary: Broad PAM DAN activation drives attraction and reduces walking speed. Function depends on dopamine, glutamate, and octopamine but not olfactory sensory circuits.

paper_title: Dopamine neurons that inform Drosophila olfactory memory have distinct, acute functions driving attraction and aversion
experimental_stimulus: Red light optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Activation of a restricted subset of PAM DANs
behavioral_consequence: Avoidance of light at high intensity (negative valence).
ecological_context: Not explicitly described.
summary: Restricted PAM DAN activation drives aversion and avoidance behavior.

paper_title: Dopamine neurons that inform Drosophila olfactory memory have distinct, acute functions driving attraction and aversion
experimental_stimulus: Red light activation; Green light inhibition (ACR1); Dopamine depletion (TH-RNAi); Glutamate depletion (vGlut-RNAi)
neural_activity: Activation and inhibition both drive behavior; dopamine and glutamate required for valence
behavioral_consequence: Activation drives attraction. Inhibition drives avoidance, indicating preexisting neural activity. Dopamine and glutamate knockdowns reduce but do not abolish valence.
ecological_context: Indicates the role of this cluster in controlling ongoing locomotor behavior.
summary: PAM-β DANs drive attraction upon activation and avoidance upon inhibition, relying partially on dopamine and glutamate.

paper_title: Dopamine neurons that inform Drosophila olfactory memory have distinct, acute functions driving attraction and aversion
experimental_stimulus: Red light optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Activation of MBONs projecting to γ lobe zones
behavioral_consequence: Strong aversion (avoidance). Flies turn away at the light-dark boundary. Valence is determined by choice/turning rather than speed differences.
ecological_context: Not explicitly described.
summary: MBON activation drives aversion and turning behavior, distinct from DAN-mediated speed control.

paper_title: Dopamine neurons that inform Drosophila olfactory memory have distinct, acute functions driving attraction and aversion
experimental_stimulus: Green light optogenetic inhibition (ACR1)
neural_activity: Inhibition of KC activity
behavioral_consequence: Required for olfactory memory formation (shock-odor conditioning). Dispensable for PAM DAN-mediated acute optogenetic valence.
ecological_context: Not explicitly described for acute valence.
summary: KCs are essential for associative learning but dispensable for acute DAN-mediated attraction.

paper_title: Dopamine neurons that inform Drosophila olfactory memory have distinct, acute functions driving attraction and aversion
experimental_stimulus: Green light optogenetic inhibition (Orco>ACR1)
neural_activity: Inhibition of ORN activity
behavioral_consequence: Little impact on attraction to PAM DAN activation.
ecological_context: Not explicitly described.
summary: Primary olfactory sensory input is not required for acute PAM DAN-mediated attraction.

paper_title: Dopamine-mediated interactions between short- and long-term memory dynamics
experimental_stimulus: Electric shock, sucrose, and odorants (innately attractive/repulsive)
neural_activity: Voltage imaging showed shock increases spiking; sucrose decreases spiking. Odors bi-directionally modulate spiking according to behavioral preference. During learning, these neurons control short-term memory formation.
behavioral_consequence: Associated with initial conditioning bouts controlling short-term memory formation; diminished inhibitory feedback from MBON-γ1pedc>α/β to these neurons allows regulation of memory dynamics.
ecological_context: Processing innate valences of threats (shock) and food (sucrose) in dynamic environments to promote survival.
summary: These PPL1-DANs bi-directionally encode innate and learnt valences and control short-term memory formation during associative conditioning.

paper_title: Dopamine-mediated interactions between short- and long-term memory dynamics
experimental_stimulus: Electric shock, odorants (CS+, CS-), paired odor-shock presentations
neural_activity: Voltage imaging revealed encoding of net innate plus learnt valence. CS+-evoked responses evolved across training bouts (decrease to increase to decrease). Attractive odor reduced shock-evoked spiking; repulsive odor enhanced it.
behavioral_consequence: Gates long-term memory formation. Blocking neurotransmitter release or feedback inhibition impairs memory at 3-hr but not 5-min.
ecological_context: Enables flexible learning by integrating innate and learnt valences for reliable associations in changing environments.
summary: These PPL1-DANs encode net valence signals that regulate long-term memory formation via feedback interactions with MBONs.

paper_title: Dopamine-mediated interactions between short- and long-term memory dynamics
experimental_stimulus: Odorants
neural_activity: Voltage imaging showed mainly excitations to odors. Unaffected by associative conditioning (no CS+ vs CS- bias change).
behavioral_consequence: Does not participate in the measured valence integration or memory gating mechanisms described for other DANs.
ecological_context: Unknown specific function in this study, distinct from other PPL1-DANs.
summary: PPL1-α'3 exhibits excitatory responses to odors and is unaffected by associative conditioning protocols.

paper_title: Dopamine-mediated interactions between short- and long-term memory dynamics
experimental_stimulus: Odors (CS+, CS-), associative conditioning
neural_activity: Voltage imaging showed decreased CS+-evoked responses after training, returning to near-baseline values ~1 hr later. Provides inhibitory feedback to PPL1-DANs.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation during conditioning impairs 3-hr memory. Plasticity supports short-term memory regulation.
ecological_context: Rapid adjustment of fly behavior based on recent associations.
summary: These MBONs exhibit short-lasting plasticity (<1 hr) and provide feedback to DANs to regulate memory formation.

paper_title: Dopamine-mediated interactions between short- and long-term memory dynamics
experimental_stimulus: Odors (CS+, CS-), associative conditioning (attractive/repulsive)
neural_activity: Voltage imaging showed depressed CS+-evoked responses persisting >24 hr. Plasticity depends on innate odor valence. Spontaneous spiking rose after training.
behavioral_consequence: Blocking neurotransmitter release impairs memory at 3-hr but not 5-min, verifying selective importance for long-lasting memory. Plasticity dynamics depend on innate valence of odors.
ecological_context: Reserves long-lasting memories for reliable, frequently encountered associations.
summary: MBON-α3 exhibits long-lasting plasticity (>24 hr) required for long-term memory retention, modulated by innate odor valence.

paper_title: New genetic tools for mushroom body output neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: 627 nm LED light (optogenetic activation), paired with odor A in olfactory learning assay; 10 s LED for kinematics; 30 s continuous LED for preference assay
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation via CsChrimson
behavioral_consequence: Induced robust aversive memory; low walking speed during LED ON; orientation toward upwind after LED OFF; avoidance of illuminated quadrants
ecological_context: Relevant for olfactory navigation and associative learning
summary: MBON21 activation via CsChrimson induces robust aversive memory, reduces walking speed during light, promotes upwind orientation after stimulus, and drives avoidance of illuminated quadrants.

paper_title: New genetic tools for mushroom body output neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: 627 nm LED light (optogenetic activation), paired with odor in olfactory learning assay; 10 s LED for kinematics; 30 s continuous LED for preference assay
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation via CsChrimson
behavioral_consequence: Induced aversive memory; increased angular motion at onset of activation; no change in walking speed or upwind orientation; avoidance of illuminated quadrants
ecological_context: Relevant for olfactory navigation and associative learning
summary: MBON29 activation via CsChrimson induces aversive memory, increases angular turning at activation onset, and drives avoidance of illuminated quadrants.

paper_title: New genetic tools for mushroom body output neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: 627 nm LED light (optogenetic activation), paired with odor in olfactory learning assay; 30 s continuous LED for preference assay
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation via CsChrimson
behavioral_consequence: Induced appetitive memory; attraction to illuminated quadrants (observed at first test period)
ecological_context: Relevant for olfactory navigation and associative learning
summary: MBON33 activation via CsChrimson induces appetitive memory and drives attraction to illuminated quadrants.

paper_title: New genetic tools for mushroom body output neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: 627 nm LED light (optogenetic activation) for 10 s
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation via CsChrimson
behavioral_consequence: Low walking speed during LED ON; orientation toward upwind after LED OFF
ecological_context: Relevant for olfactory navigation
summary: MB011B (MBON01/03/04) activation via CsChrimson reduces walking speed and promotes upwind orientation after stimulus.

paper_title: New genetic tools for mushroom body output neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: 627 nm LED light (optogenetic activation)
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation via CsChrimson
behavioral_consequence: Increased angular motion at onset of activation (similar to MBON29)
ecological_context: Relevant for olfactory navigation
summary: MBON26 activation via CsChrimson increases angular turning at stimulus onset.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for transforming learned olfactory valences into wind-oriented movement
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson, 627 nm LED, 10 s); odor presentation (pentyl acetate, ethyl lactate, OCT, MCH); optogenetic appetitive conditioning (pairing odor with reward DANs)
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging showed MBON-α3 activation evokes calcium increase (excitation); MBON-α1 activation suppresses MBON-α3 effect (inhibition). Whole-cell recordings showed MBON-α3 photostimulation evokes strong excitation (3/11 neurons); MBON-α1 photostimulation evokes inhibitory responses (4/17 neurons). After appetitive conditioning, UpWiNs displayed increased excitatory response to subsequent exposures to CS+ odor but not CS- odor.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation promotes robust upwind locomotion (increased distance from center against airflow) in starved flies. Behavior depends on airflow rate and direction. Activation biases turning direction toward upwind and increases forward walking speed. Increases probability of returning to location where activation was terminated. Inactivation (TNT or shibire-ts) impairs memory-driven upwind locomotion and reduces preference to CS+ odor in binary choice during retrieval.
ecological_context: Wind direction provides a critical cue for olfactory navigation in natural environments where odorants are propagated by the stream of airflow. UpWiN activation may promote a series of cast-surge-cast reactions when flies navigate intermittent plumes of reward-predicting odors.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for transforming learned olfactory valences into wind-oriented movement
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic stimulation during training and recording; optogenetic activation of DANs projecting to α1 compartment during training
neural_activity: Electrophysiology showed glutamatergic MBON-α1 evokes inhibitory responses in UpWiNs. Displays reduced odor response to an odor associated with activation of DANs in α1 after memory formation.
behavioral_consequence: Memories in the α1 compartment promote steering and walking upwind in response to the CS+ odor. Reduced upwind locomotion in response to CS- odor compared to controls. Required for retrieval of long-lasting appetitive memory.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for transforming learned olfactory valences into wind-oriented movement
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic stimulation during training and recording; optogenetic activation of DANs projecting to α3 compartment during training
neural_activity: Electrophysiology showed cholinergic MBON-α3 evokes strong excitation in UpWiNs. Increases response to an odor associated with sugar reward and decreases response to punishment-associated odors.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation promotes significant upwind locomotion. Memories in the α3 compartment (via DAN activation) reduce upwind locomotion in response to CS- odor.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for transforming learned olfactory valences into wind-oriented movement
cell_type: Dopaminergic Neurons (DANs) projecting to specific MB compartments
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson, LED) paired with odor (pentyl acetate or ethyl lactate) for γ5β′2a, γ4, β1β2, or α1 compartments
neural_activity: Optogenetic activations promoted local release of dopamine in the targeted MB compartments.
behavioral_consequence: DAN activation in α1 and β1β2 compartments promoted steering and walking upwind in response to CS+ odor. DAN activation in γ5β′2a and γ4 compartments failed to promote significant upwind movement. All promoted strong preference to CS+ odor in binary choice.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for transforming learned olfactory valences into wind-oriented movement
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson, LED)
neural_activity: Not explicitly measured in this paper.
behavioral_consequence: Activation modulated forward walking speed and promoted flies that already faced upwind to maintain that orientation. Did not cause directional turning toward the upwind direction.

paper_title: Dopaminergic neurons dynamically update sensory values during olfactory maneuver.
experimental_stimulus: 27 odors with varying innate values; optogenetic activation of sweet/bitter gustatory receptor neurons (GRNs); optogenetic inhibition during olfactory maneuver
neural_activity: Encode innate odor value (attractive to aversive) across compartments; linearly integrate olfactory and gustatory value signals; responses depend on odor value identity
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic inhibition of PAM-b02p DANs biases flight toward aversion; inhibition of PPL1-a3 DANs biases flight toward less aversion; drives value-dependent steering in virtual olfactory environment
ecological_context: Continuously update sensory values during olfactory maneuver (nonassociative manner) to guide exploration; adaptive for enhancing attraction to attractive odors or escaping aversive ones
techniques_used: ['Two-photon Ca2+ imaging', 'Optogenetics (Chrimson, GtACR1)', 'Virtual flight arena', 'Connectome-based network simulation']
summary: DANs encode innate odor values and integrate multisensory inputs, dynamically updating these values during olfactory maneuver to drive value-dependent behavioral steering.

paper_title: Dopaminergic neurons dynamically update sensory values during olfactory maneuver.
experimental_stimulus: Repetitive application of attractive or aversive odors
neural_activity: Exhibit value-dependent plasticity (depression of responses) in a dopamine-dependent manner; depression is reversible and blocked by D1 antagonist SCH 23390
behavioral_consequence: Not directly manipulated, but plasticity underlies the value updates that bias behavior via DAN modulation
ecological_context: Store the current value of odors as the animal explores the environment
summary: Repetitive odor application induces dopamine-dependent plasticity in MBONs, allowing the storage of updated odor values.

paper_title: Dopaminergic neurons dynamically update sensory values during olfactory maneuver.
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (Chrimson) of Gr64f (sweet) or Gr66a (bitter) GRNs
neural_activity: Activation produces calcium responses in DANs resembling responses to natural tastants (sucrose/quinine)
behavioral_consequence: Not directly tested
ecological_context: Used to probe how DANs integrate multisensory values (olfaction and gustation) during exploration
summary: Optogenetic activation of sweet/bitter GRNs mimics tastant responses in DANs, enabling the study of multisensory value integration.

paper_title: Flexible specificity of memory in Drosophila depends on a comparison between choices
experimental_stimulus: Odors (pentyl acetate, butyl acetate, ethyl lactate) presented as single pulses or rapid transitions
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (opGCaMP6f). Responses to similar odors strongly depressed after optogenetic reinforcement. Responses to odor transitions were distinct based on order (A-A' vs A'-A), with higher contrast in A-A' transitions post-learning.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation drives upwind attraction. Optogenetic reinforcement in associated DANs enables hard discrimination and generalization. Flies attracted to unpaired odor A' only when it immediately followed paired odor A in a sequence.
ecological_context: Flexible categorization of odors when encountering odor boundaries in complex environments where cues are sequential.
techniques_used: ['two-photon calcium imaging', 'optogenetics', 'behavioral arenas (quadrant choice, odor attraction)']
summary: MBON-γ2α'1 activity reflects a temporal comparison of odors during transitions, enabling flexible discrimination and generalization that drives attraction behavior.

paper_title: Flexible specificity of memory in Drosophila depends on a comparison between choices
experimental_stimulus: Odor transitions (pentyl acetate, butyl acetate)
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (GCaMP6f). Responses to second odor in transition were depressed for both orders after shock training. No significant contrast at transition point.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic reinforcement in associated DANs supports easy discrimination and generalization but not hard discrimination.
ecological_context: N/A
summary: MBON-α3 shows uniform depression for similar odors during transitions, supporting generalization but not fine discrimination.

paper_title: Flexible specificity of memory in Drosophila depends on a comparison between choices
experimental_stimulus: Odors (pentyl acetate, butyl acetate, ethyl lactate) presented as single pulses or transitions
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (GCaMP6f). Similar odors elicited overlapping response patterns. Decoders could distinguish odors. Transition-evoked changes in KC representations did not account for MBON transition effects.
behavioral_consequence: No direct behavioral manipulation of KCs; plasticity induced via DANs.
ecological_context: N/A
summary: KC subtypes exhibit overlapping responses to similar odors but do not show sequence-specific modulation during transitions that explains MBON discrimination.

paper_title: Flexible specificity of memory in Drosophila depends on a comparison between choices
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation paired with odor
neural_activity: Plasticity induction (no direct activity measurement reported).
behavioral_consequence: Reinforcement supports hard discrimination and generalization performance.
ecological_context: N/A
summary: Optogenetic reinforcement in DAN PPL1-γ2α'1 drives aversive learning sufficient for flexible odor categorization.

paper_title: Flexible specificity of memory in Drosophila depends on a comparison between choices
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation paired with odor
neural_activity: Plasticity induction (no direct activity measurement reported).
behavioral_consequence: Reinforcement supports easy discrimination and generalization but not hard discrimination.
ecological_context: N/A
summary: Optogenetic reinforcement in DAN PPL1-α3 drives aversive learning but supports weak discrimination of similar odors.

paper_title: Global change in brain state during spontaneous and forced walk in Drosophila is composed of combined activity patterns of different neuron classes
experimental_stimulus: Spontaneous and forced walking; turning
neural_activity: Increased global brain activity in most regions during walk compared to rest
behavioral_consequence: Activity correlates with walk bouts; functional manipulation not performed in this study
ecological_context: Proprioceptive information during movement
techniques_used: ['Fast whole brain light field imaging (LFM)', 'GCaMP calcium imaging']
summary: Glutamatergic neurons show increased global activity during walk compared to rest.

neural_activity: Increased global brain activity in most regions during walk compared to rest

paper_title: Global change in brain state during spontaneous and forced walk in Drosophila is composed of combined activity patterns of different neuron classes
experimental_stimulus: Spontaneous and forced walking; turning
neural_activity: Increased activity during walk globally. Specific components (e.g., EB-DA, PPM2-LW) show lateralized activity during turning. Some components (M-Omega, posterior slope) activate before spontaneous walk onset.
behavioral_consequence: Activity correlates with walk and turn direction; pre-walk activity observed only in spontaneous walk; functional manipulation not performed in this study
ecological_context: Potential role in initiating walk; modulation of motor state
techniques_used: ['Fast whole brain light field imaging (LFM)', 'GCaMP calcium imaging', 'PCA/ICA analysis']
summary: Dopaminergic neurons are broadly activated during walk, with specific subtypes showing lateralized turning activity or pre-onset activation during spontaneous walk.

paper_title: Global change in brain state during spontaneous and forced walk in Drosophila is composed of combined activity patterns of different neuron classes
experimental_stimulus: Spontaneous and forced walking
neural_activity: Increased activity during walk; OL-PENP component strongly correlated with walk
behavioral_consequence: Activity correlates with walk bouts; functional manipulation not performed in this study
ecological_context: Modulation of motor state
techniques_used: ['Fast whole brain light field imaging (LFM)', 'GCaMP calcium imaging']
summary: Octopaminergic neurons show increased activity during walk, with specific components like OL-PENP strongly correlated.

paper_title: Global change in brain state during spontaneous and forced walk in Drosophila is composed of combined activity patterns of different neuron classes
experimental_stimulus: Spontaneous and forced walking
neural_activity: Less globally activated than dopamine/octopamine. Specific region (AVLPshell) inhibited during walk. Other AVLP regions activated at onset
behavioral_consequence: Activity correlates with walk (inhibition in specific areas); functional manipulation not performed in this study
ecological_context: Sensorimotor loop modulation; AVLP receives input from leg proprioception
techniques_used: ['Fast whole brain light field imaging (LFM)', 'GCaMP calcium imaging']
summary: Serotonergic neurons show heterogeneous responses: some regions inhibited (AVLPshell) while others activated during walk.

paper_title: Global change in brain state during spontaneous and forced walk in Drosophila is composed of combined activity patterns of different neuron classes
experimental_stimulus: Spontaneous and forced turning
neural_activity: Lateralized activity during turning (differential activation ipsilateral vs contralateral)
behavioral_consequence: Activity correlates with turn direction; functional manipulation not performed in this study
ecological_context: Turning control during navigation
techniques_used: ['Fast whole brain light field imaging (LFM)', 'PCA/ICA component analysis']
summary: PPM2-LW component shows lateralized activity correlated with turning direction.

paper_title: Global change in brain state during spontaneous and forced walk in Drosophila is composed of combined activity patterns of different neuron classes
experimental_stimulus: Spontaneous and forced walking
neural_activity: Activated at walk onset; not activated before onset
behavioral_consequence: Activity correlates with walk onset; functional manipulation not performed in this study
ecological_context: Walking initiation/maintenance
techniques_used: ['Fast whole brain light field imaging (LFM)', 'PCA/ICA component analysis']
summary: EB-DA component activates at walk onset, consistent with walking-related activity.

paper_title: Global change in brain state during spontaneous and forced walk in Drosophila is composed of combined activity patterns of different neuron classes
experimental_stimulus: Spontaneous and forced walking
neural_activity: Significantly inhibited during walk
behavioral_consequence: Activity correlates with walk (inhibition); functional manipulation not performed in this study
ecological_context: AVLP receives input from leg proprioception; potential sensorimotor loop
techniques_used: ['Fast whole brain light field imaging (LFM)', 'PCA/ICA component analysis']
summary: AVLPshell component is inhibited during walk, suggesting serotonergic modulation in this region.

paper_title: Global change in brain state during spontaneous and forced walk in Drosophila is composed of combined activity patterns of different neuron classes
experimental_stimulus: Spontaneous walking transitions
neural_activity: Activity increases significantly before walk onset in spontaneous walk; delayed in forced walk
behavioral_consequence: Activity correlates with spontaneous walk initiation; functional manipulation not performed in this study
ecological_context: Potential role in initiating walk
techniques_used: ['Fast whole brain light field imaging (LFM)', 'PCA/ICA component analysis']
summary: Posterior slope components show activity preceding spontaneous walk onset, suggesting a role in walk initiation.

paper_title: Local 5-HT signaling bi-directionally regulates the coincidence time window for associative learning.
experimental_stimulus: Odor (CS), electric shock (US), optogenetic light activation
neural_activity: Releases serotonin (5-HT) heterogeneously in MB γ lobe compartments in response to physiological stimuli (odor/shock); releases 5-HT homogeneously upon optogenetic activation. Activity is activated by acetylcholine (ACh) from Kenyon Cells (KCs) via nicotinic ACh receptors. 5-HT release is abolished by silencing DPM (Kir2.1) or blocking KC input.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation or increasing extracellular 5-HT prolongs the coincidence time window for olfactory learning (e.g., from 16.9s to 25.2s or 44.1s). Silencing DPM or reducing 5-HT synthesis (Trhn−/−) shortens the coincidence window (e.g., to 10.8s or 10.4s). 5-HT from DPM helps bridge the CS-US temporal gap in trace conditioning.
ecological_context: In a natural environment, flies do not experience precisely controlled stimuli; learning must adapt to changing CS/US regimens. The serotonergic modulation helps flies successfully extract meaningful cause and effect between environmental events.
techniques_used: ['GRAB5-HT1.0 sensor imaging', 'Two-photon microscopy', 'Optogenetics (CsChrimson, Kir2.1)', 'T-maze behavioral assay', 'Pharmacology (fluoxetine)']
summary: The single serotonergic DPM neuron releases 5-HT to inhibit Kenyon Cells, bi-directionally regulating the coincidence time window for associative learning. This circuit helps bridge temporal gaps between stimuli in natural learning contexts.

paper_title: Local 5-HT signaling bi-directionally regulates the coincidence time window for associative learning.
experimental_stimulus: Odor (CS), electric shock (US), optogenetic light activation, ACh application
neural_activity: Release acetylcholine (ACh) in response to odor and shock, measured by ACh3.0 sensor. Release ACh to activate DPM neurons. Receive inhibitory 5-HT feedback from DPM which suppresses both tonic and phasic ACh release and tonic cAMP levels via 5-HT1A receptors. Show synaptic depression in γ1-γ3 compartments upon odor-shock pairing.
behavioral_consequence: Synaptic depression in KCs correlates with aversive olfactory memory formation. The coincidence time window for inducing synaptic depression matches the behavioral learning window. Genetic reduction of 5-HT1A receptors in KCs shortens the coincidence time window.
ecological_context: MB compartments exhibit intrinsically distinct lengths of the coincidence time window proportionally gated by heterogeneous 5-HT signals, allowing for different temporal processing capabilities within the learning center.
techniques_used: ['ACh3.0 sensor imaging', 'G-Flamp1 (cAMP) sensor imaging', 'Two-photon microscopy', 'Optogenetics (CsChrimson, Kir2.1)', 'Chemogenetics (hM4Di)', 'T-maze behavioral assay']
summary: Kenyon Cells release ACh to activate DPM neurons and receive inhibitory 5-HT feedback that regulates their intrinsic coincidence time windows and synaptic plasticity, directly impacting olfactory learning performance.

paper_title: Rewarding Capacity of Optogenetically Activating a Giant GABAergic Central-Brain Interneuron in Larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (ChR2XXL, Chrimson) via blue/red light; odor presentation (AM, OCT); Fructose reward (control condition)
neural_activity: GABAergic (confirmed by immunostaining); Optogenetic activation reduces intracellular Ca2+ levels in Kenyon cells (GCaMP6m imaging); Presynaptic in calyx, postsynaptic in calyx and lobes (EM/chemical tagging); Regional synaptic polarity changes during metamorphosis
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation acts as a rewarding signal, establishing appetitive associative odor memory (positive memory score); Silencing (GtACR1) leads to aversive memory; Activation during training abolishes odor-fructose memory; Activation during testing terminates search behavior; Naive odor preference unaffected
ecological_context: Larvae naturally live on overripe fruit; Odor-taste learning helps larvae find food sources (fructose reward associated with odor)
summary: The APL neuron is a giant GABAergic interneuron whose optogenetic activation exerts a rewarding effect, establishing appetitive odor memory and terminating search behavior, likely via dopaminergic pathways.

paper_title: Rewarding Capacity of Optogenetically Activating a Giant GABAergic Central-Brain Interneuron in Larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: APL optogenetic activation; Carbamylcholine (cholinergic stimulation)
neural_activity: Intracellular Ca2+ levels reduced upon APL optogenetic activation (GCaMP6m imaging); High Ca2+ induced by carbamylcholine reduced by simultaneous APL activation
behavioral_consequence: Underlie the odor-specific associative memory established by APL activation (implied by circuit function)
ecological_context: Part of the mushroom body memory center involved in sensory integration and memory
summary: KCs receive inhibitory input from APL; APL activation reduces KC Ca2+ levels, consistent with a role in sparsening sensory representations.

paper_title: Rewarding Capacity of Optogenetically Activating a Giant GABAergic Central-Brain Interneuron in Larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Ablation (reaper gene); Systemic dopamine synthesis inhibition (3IY); Dopamine precursor administration (L-DOPA)
neural_activity: Not directly recorded in this study
behavioral_consequence: Ablation or dopamine synthesis inhibition impairs odor-APL reward learning; Memory scores reduced compared to controls; L-DOPA rescues the impairment
ecological_context: Dopaminergic neurons convey reward signals in larval Drosophila for associative learning
summary: The rewarding effect of APL activation depends on functional dopaminergic pPAM neurons, as their ablation or dopamine synthesis inhibition impairs the learned memory.

paper_title: Rewarding Capacity of Optogenetically Activating a Giant GABAergic Central-Brain Interneuron in Larval Drosophila
cell_type: Calyx Mushroom Body Output Neurons (MBONa1, MBONa2)
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (ChR2XXL); Optogenetic silencing (GtACR1)
neural_activity: Not directly recorded
behavioral_consequence: Manipulating activity (activation or silencing) of calyx MBONs individually or together has no reinforcing or punishing effect on odor memory
ecological_context: MBONs promote approach or avoidance behavior; tested for potential role in APL-mediated reward
summary: Manipulation of calyx MBONs does not replicate the reinforcing effect of APL activation, suggesting the rewarding effect does not involve direct APL-to-MBONa1/a2 connections.

paper_title: Reward expectations direct learning and drive operant matching in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation using red light (617 nm, 500 ms flash) triggered upon entry into the reward zone of a chosen odor
neural_activity: Used as a reward signal; optogenetic activation capable of inducing cue-reward associations
behavioral_consequence: Flies learn to prefer the rewarded odor over unrewarded odors; flies perform operant matching (choice ratio proportional to reward ratio) in dynamic foraging tasks
ecological_context: Foraging animals must use decision-making strategies that dynamically account for uncertainty in the world; natural environments often require accumulating rewards from unreliable predictors
summary: Gr64f-expressing sugar sensory neurons were used to deliver optogenetic reward, enabling flies to learn cue-reward associations and perform operant matching in dynamic environments.

paper_title: Reward expectations direct learning and drive operant matching in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation using red light (617 nm, 500 ms flash) triggered upon entry into the reward zone, replacing natural reward delivery
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation used to override natural reward signal; natural activity implied to incorporate reward expectation
behavioral_consequence: Flies showed reduced plateau performance; flies failed to follow the operant matching law (undermatching) in dynamic foraging tasks compared to sugar neuron reward
ecological_context: Reward expectation is required for successful foraging strategies in unpredictable environments; DAN activity likely represents this expectation in the mushroom body
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics (58E02-Gal4 driving UAS-CsChrimson-mVenus)', 'Y-arena behavioral tracking', 'Logistic regression modeling', 'Mathematical modeling (MB-inspired model)']
summary: Direct optogenetic activation of PAM DANs as reward disrupts operant matching and reduces learning performance, suggesting natural DAN activity incorporates reward expectation in the mushroom body.

paper_title: Loss of the Schizophrenia-Linked Furin Protein from Drosophila Mushroom Body Neurons Results in Antipsychotic-Reversible Habituation Deficits
experimental_stimulus: 15 recurrent footshocks (45 V DC)
behavioral_consequence: Attenuation of Furin1 resulted in defective habituation (failure to reduce shock avoidance). Transgenic expression of Drosophila or human Furin1 fully rescued the deficit. Acute oral delivery of haloperidol (10 nM) or clozapine (2 nM) reversed the habituation defect.
summary: Furin1 activity in adult alpha'/beta' MBNs is necessary and sufficient for normal footshock habituation; deficits caused by Furin1 loss are reversible by genetic rescue or specific antipsychotics.

paper_title: Loss of the Schizophrenia-Linked Furin Protein from Drosophila Mushroom Body Neurons Results in Antipsychotic-Reversible Habituation Deficits
experimental_stimulus: 15 recurrent footshocks (45 V DC)
behavioral_consequence: Attenuation of Furin1 precipitated defective habituation. Synaptic silencing with shits resulted in deficient habituation. Transgenic Furin1 expression did not reverse the deficit in the context of alpha'/beta' mutation. Acute oral delivery of haloperidol (2 nM) reversed the defect, but clozapine and risperidone did not.
summary: Furin1 activity in adult gamma MBNs is necessary but not sufficient for footshock habituation; neurotransmission from these neurons is required, but pharmacological rescue differs from alpha'/beta' neurons.

paper_title: Loss of the Schizophrenia-Linked Furin Protein from Drosophila Mushroom Body Neurons Results in Antipsychotic-Reversible Habituation Deficits
experimental_stimulus: 15 recurrent footshocks (45 V DC)
behavioral_consequence: Attenuation of Furin1 in adult alpha/beta MBNs did not result in habituation defects; flies habituated normally.
summary: Furin1 attenuation in adult alpha/beta MBNs does not impair footshock habituation.

paper_title: Loss of the Schizophrenia-Linked Furin Protein from Drosophila Mushroom Body Neurons Results in Antipsychotic-Reversible Habituation Deficits
experimental_stimulus: 15 recurrent footshocks (45 V DC)
behavioral_consequence: Attenuation of Furin1 using MB247-Gal4 or dnc-Gal4 resulted in deficient habituation to footshocks. Robust habituation deficit on Furin1 loss throughout MBs was fully rescued only with 10 nM haloperidol.
summary: Furin1 activity across all adult MBNs is required for footshock habituation; pharmacological reversal depends on the specific neuronal population affected.

paper_title: Dopaminergic neurons dynamically update sensory values during navigation
experimental_stimulus: 27 different odors (attractive and aversive), optogenetic activation of sweet (Gr64f) or bitter (Gr66a) gustatory receptor neurons, simultaneous olfactory and gustatory stimuli
neural_activity: Encoded innate value of odors with compartment-specific tuning; specific compartments preferentially responded to attractive (e.g., β'2) or aversive (e.g., γ2/α'1) odors. Integrated olfactory and gustatory values linearly. Activity in β'2 compartments (PAM) correlated with attractive value; activity in γ2/α'1 compartments (PPL1) correlated with aversive value.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic inhibition of PAM-β'2p DANs during closed-loop navigation biased flies to avoid odors more strongly. Inhibition of PPL1-γ2α'1 DANs had little behavioral effect.
ecological_context: Dynamic updating of sensory values during olfactory navigation; relevant for multimodal integration during feeding in natural environments where animals sample sensory cues with innate values.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging', 'Optogenetics (GtACR1 for inhibition, Chrimson for GRN activation)', 'Closed-loop flight simulator']
summary: DANs encode innate odor value across compartments and integrate multisensory values; their activity modulates olfactory navigation behavior in a value-dependent manner.

paper_title: Dopaminergic neurons dynamically update sensory values during navigation
experimental_stimulus: Repetitive application of attractive (2-pentanone) or aversive (2-methylphenol) odors
neural_activity: Induced compartment- and value-dependent plasticity (depression). Attractive odors caused stronger depression in MBON-β'2mp; aversive odors caused stronger depression in MBON-γ2α'1. Plasticity required dopamine receptor activity (blocked by SCH 23390).
behavioral_consequence: Plasticity biases behavior to the side determined by MBON identity (approach/avoidance); DAN inhibition relaxes depression in MBONs, biasing behavior towards avoidance.
ecological_context: Continuous updating of odor values during navigation to shape sensory exploration and adaptive behavior.
summary: MBONs undergo value-dependent plasticity induced by repetitive odor exposure in a dopamine-dependent manner, modulating navigation behavior.

paper_title: Dopaminergic neurons dynamically update sensory values during navigation
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation of Chrimson-expressing Gr64f or Gr66a GRNs
neural_activity: Activation mimics sucrose (Gr64f) or quinine (Gr66a) responses in DANs. Responses to bitter GRN activation were well correlated with aversive odor responses across compartments.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation replaced tastant application to test multisensory integration in DANs.
ecological_context: Represents gustatory value input for integration with olfactory cues during feeding.
summary: Optogenetic activation of sweet or bitter GRNs mimics tastant responses in DANs, allowing study of multisensory value integration.

paper_title: A dopamine-gated learning circuit underpins reproductive state-dependent odor preference in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: Putrescine odor (polyamine)
neural_activity: Detect polyamine odor; synaptic output depressed by SPR/MIP signaling upon mating.
behavioral_consequence: Blocking synaptic output *during mating* did not reduce mated female attraction to putrescine. Mated females show increased attraction to higher polyamine concentrations compared to virgins.
ecological_context: Polyamines are nutrients in food that increase female egg production; preference shift optimizes nutrient intake post-mating.
techniques_used: ['T-maze group assay', 'T-maze single fly assay', 'Synaptic inhibition (Shibirets1)']
summary: Polyamine-detecting OSNs mediate attraction to putrescine; their synaptic output modulation upon mating contributes to the preference switch, but their activity during mating is not strictly required for the lasting behavioral change.

paper_title: A dopamine-gated learning circuit underpins reproductive state-dependent odor preference in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: cVA (pheromone) and general odorants
neural_activity: Detect pheromones (cVA) via ORCO/Or67d pathway.
behavioral_consequence: Blocking synaptic output of ORCO- or OR67d-expressing OSNs *during mating* significantly reduced mated female attraction to putrescine. Pheromone detection during mating is required for the preference switch.
ecological_context: cVA is transferred during mating and serves as a contextual sensory signal linking mating experience to odor preference.
techniques_used: ['T-maze group assay', 'T-maze single fly assay', 'Synaptic inhibition (Shibirets1)', 'ORCO mutant analysis']
summary: Pheromone-detecting OSNs provide a critical sensory context during mating; blocking their output during mating prevents the lasting increase in polyamine preference.

paper_title: A dopamine-gated learning circuit underpins reproductive state-dependent odor preference in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: Mating context, Putrescine odor
neural_activity: Synaptic output required for behavior; no global brain activity change detected with mating.
behavioral_consequence: Inhibiting KC output *during mating* completely prevented the increase in polyamine attraction. Inhibiting KC output at test reduced choice of putrescine. KC output required for both induction and expression of preference.
ecological_context: Mushroom body involved in learning and memory; integrates mating experience to modulate reproductive state-dependent behavior.
summary: KC output in the mushroom body is necessary during mating to induce the lasting change in polyamine preference and influences the expression of the choice.

paper_title: A dopamine-gated learning circuit underpins reproductive state-dependent odor preference in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: Mating experience, Putrescine, cVA
neural_activity: Activation mimics mating state; response to cVA modulated by mating state (state-dependent).
behavioral_consequence: Activation in virgins increased putrescine preference to mated levels. Activation during mating reduced mated attraction. Inhibition at test reduced mated attraction. Inhibition during mating had no effect.
ecological_context: Conveys mating-related sensory experience to the mushroom body, acting as a dopamine-gated signal for reproductive state-dependent learning.
summary: PAM-β'1 DANs are sufficient to induce mated-like polyamine preference in virgins and regulate the expression of attraction in mated females.

paper_title: A dopamine-gated learning circuit underpins reproductive state-dependent odor preference in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: Mating context, Putrescine
neural_activity: Activity modulates attraction state-dependently.
behavioral_consequence: Activation reduced mated female attraction. Inhibition increased virgin attraction to putrescine. Suppresses expression of attraction in virgins.
ecological_context: Provides a brake on attraction in virgin females, released or modulated by mating state.
summary: PPL1-DANs repress the expression of polyamine attraction in virgin females, regulating the state-dependent difference in behavior.

paper_title: A dopamine-gated learning circuit underpins reproductive state-dependent odor preference in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: cVA, Putrescine, Water
neural_activity: Responds to cVA but not putrescine; no lasting change in odor response 3-5 days after mating.
behavioral_consequence: Inhibition *during mating* prevented the switch to mated preference. Activation in virgins increased preference to mated levels. Bidirectional modulation of behavior.
ecological_context: Integrates mating context and pheromone cues to drive long-lasting changes in odor preference.
techniques_used: ['T-maze single fly assay', '2-photon calcium imaging', 'Optogenetics (dTrpA1)', 'Synaptic inhibition (Shibirets1)']
summary: MBON-β'1 activity is required to induce mating-induced preference changes and bidirectionally modulates polyamine attraction.

paper_title: A dopamine-gated learning circuit underpins reproductive state-dependent odor preference in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: Putrescine, cVA, Water
neural_activity: Responds to putrescine and cVA; no lasting change in odor response after mating.
behavioral_consequence: Activation at test reduced mated attraction. Inhibition at test increased virgin attraction. Not required for induction of change during mating.
ecological_context: Involved in the expression of odor preference, analogous to learned behavior expression circuits.
techniques_used: ['T-maze single fly assay', '2-photon calcium imaging', 'Optogenetics (dTrpA1)', 'Synaptic inhibition (Shibirets1)']
summary: MBON-β'2 output is necessary for the expression of polyamine attraction in mated females but is insufficient to induce the mating-induced behavioral switch.

paper_title: A dopamine-gated learning circuit underpins reproductive state-dependent odor preference in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: Putrescine, Water
neural_activity: Responds significantly stronger to putrescine in mated females compared to virgins (lasting modulation).
behavioral_consequence: Inhibition at test reduced mated female attraction to putrescine. Required for expression of preference.
ecological_context: Modulation by mating links innate olfactory processing to reproductive state-dependent choice.
summary: AD1b2 LHONs show lasting mating-induced modulation of odor response and are required for the expression of polyamine attraction.

paper_title: A dopamine-gated learning circuit underpins reproductive state-dependent odor preference in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: cVA (via DA1 glomerulus)
neural_activity: Receives input from DA1 glomerulus (cVA-detecting OSNs).
behavioral_consequence: Inhibition at test reduced attraction to putrescine in mated females.
ecological_context: Integrates pheromone information (cVA) with odor preference circuits.
summary: aSP-g neurons contribute to the expression of polyamine attraction in mated females, linking pheromone detection to food preference.

paper_title: Multisensory learning binds modality-specific neurons into a cross-modal memory engram
experimental_stimulus: Multisensory training (Color + Odor); Odor testing (CS+ vs CS-)
neural_activity: In naïve flies, odors evoke hyperpolarization. After multisensory appetitive training, CS+ odor evokes depolarization (excitation) in γ5 axon segments. After multisensory aversive training, CS+ odor evokes excitation in γ1 and γ5 segments.
behavioral_consequence: Blocking γd output during testing abolished visual memory enhancement in the congruent protocol. Blocking γd output impaired odor-only memory retrieval in multisensory-trained flies but not in unisensory olfactory-trained flies.
ecological_context: Multisensory experiences guide behavior; integration improves object recognition and memory performance.
summary: γd KCs, normally visually selective, become odor-responsive in specific lobe compartments after multisensory learning. This recruitment is required for enhanced odor memory retrieval following multisensory training.

paper_title: Multisensory learning binds modality-specific neurons into a cross-modal memory engram
experimental_stimulus: Multisensory training (Color + Odor)
behavioral_consequence: Blocking αβp output during testing abolished the visual enhancement of performance in the congruent protocol.
ecological_context: Multisensory experiences guide behavior; integration improves object recognition and memory performance.
summary: αβp KCs are required for visual memory enhancement in the multisensory congruent protocol.

paper_title: Multisensory learning binds modality-specific neurons into a cross-modal memory engram
experimental_stimulus: Multisensory training (Color + Odor)
neural_activity: Connectome analysis shows DPM branches bridge γm and γd KC axons in specific compartments. Bath application of 5-HT evoked excitatory responses in γd KCs.
behavioral_consequence: Blocking DPM output during acquisition or retrieval impaired multisensory training enhancement of odor memory. No effect on unisensory olfactory memory.
ecological_context: Multisensory experiences guide behavior; integration improves object recognition and memory performance.
techniques_used: ['EM Connectome analysis', 'UAS-Shits1 (blocking)', '5-HT bath application with voltage imaging']
summary: DPM neurons form a serotonergic excitatory bridge between olfactory and visual KC streams, enabling cross-modal memory binding.

paper_title: Multisensory learning binds modality-specific neurons into a cross-modal memory engram
experimental_stimulus: Multisensory training (Color + Odor)
neural_activity: Receives inputs from dopaminergic neurons (DANs) in specific compartments.
behavioral_consequence: Adult-restricted RNAi knockdown of Dop2R in APL neurons abolished the multisensory enhancement of odor memory performance. Mild defect in unisensory odor memory.
ecological_context: Multisensory experiences guide behavior; integration improves object recognition and memory performance.
summary: Dop2R-mediated inhibition in APL neurons regulates the recruitment of γd KCs into the olfactory memory engram during multisensory learning.

paper_title: Consolidation of Sleep-Dependent Appetitive Memory Is Mediated by a Sweet-Sensing Circuit
experimental_stimulus: Sweet taste (arabinose, sucralose) vs. tasteless nutritive substance (sorbitol); feeding state (fed vs. starved)
neural_activity: Synaptic transmission blocked using UAS-shibirets1 (restrictive temperature); depolarization using UAS-TrpA1 (30°C)
behavioral_consequence: Silencing in fed flies switches memory to sleep-independent (memory intact after sleep deprivation). Activation in starved flies switches memory to sleep-dependent (memory impaired by sleep deprivation). Controls show sleep-dependent memory when fed and sleep-independent when starved.
ecological_context: Sweet taste predicts food availability; starvation induces foraging behavior. Memory consolidation switches based on survival needs (foraging vs. feeding).
techniques_used: ['UAS-Gal4 system', 'Temperature-sensitive shibirets1 (silencing)', 'UAS-TrpA1 (activation)', 'Appetitive conditioning assay', 'Sleep monitoring (Drosophila Activity Monitors)', 'Memory test (T-maze)']
summary: Gr64f+ GRNs mediate sweet taste signals required for sleep-dependent memory consolidation in fed flies; silencing these neurons induces sleep-independent memory in fed flies, while activation induces sleep-dependent memory in starved flies.

paper_title: Consolidation of Sleep-Dependent Appetitive Memory Is Mediated by a Sweet-Sensing Circuit
experimental_stimulus: Feeding state (food vial) vs. starvation; Post-training maintenance on sweet sugars (arabinose, sucralose) vs. tasteless nutrient (sorbitol)
neural_activity: Calcium increase measured via CaLexA imaging (GFP signal); higher activity in fed flies compared to starved flies; higher activity in sweet sugar conditions compared to sorbitol
behavioral_consequence: Required for long-term memory consolidation in fed flies. Silencing (shibirets1) impairs memory in fed flies but has no effect in starved flies. Mediates sleep-dependent memory.
ecological_context: Mediates memory consolidation when flies are fed and food is available, requiring sleep for consolidation.
summary: PAM-b'2mp DANs show feeding-dependent calcium increases and are required for sleep-dependent memory consolidation in fed flies, but not in starved flies.

paper_title: Consolidation of Sleep-Dependent Appetitive Memory Is Mediated by a Sweet-Sensing Circuit
experimental_stimulus: Feeding state (food vial) vs. starvation; Post-training maintenance on tasteless nutrient (sorbitol) vs. sweet sugars
neural_activity: Calcium increase measured via CaLexA imaging (GFP signal); higher activity in starved flies compared to fed flies; higher activity in sorbitol conditions compared to sweet sugars
behavioral_consequence: Required for long-term memory consolidation in starved flies. Silencing (shibirets1) impairs memory in starved flies but has no effect in fed flies. Mediates sleep-independent memory.
ecological_context: Mediates memory consolidation when flies are starved (foraging state), allowing memory formation without sleep.
summary: PAM-a1 DANs show starvation-dependent calcium increases and are required for sleep-independent memory consolidation in starved flies, but not in fed flies.

paper_title: Consolidation of Sleep-Dependent Appetitive Memory Is Mediated by a Sweet-Sensing Circuit
experimental_stimulus: Appetitive training (odor-sugar association)
neural_activity: Synaptic transmission blocked using UAS-shibirets1
behavioral_consequence: Silencing the PAM cluster impairs long-term memory consolidation in both fed and starved flies
ecological_context: Reward signaling is generally required for memory consolidation regardless of state, though specific subsets mediate state-dependent memory.
summary: Silencing the PAM dopamine neuron cluster impairs long-term memory consolidation in both fed and starved flies, indicating general reward signaling requirement.

paper_title: Single-cell transcriptome profiles of Drosophila fruitless-expressing neurons from both sexes
experimental_stimulus: Temperature shift from 19°C to 25°C (activates TrpA1 channel)
neural_activity: Activation of Clk856 ∩ fru P1 neurons via TrpA1 channel (inferred from temperature shift)
behavioral_consequence: Males: Little daytime activity (sleep-promoting role); Shortened circadian period (mean 22.8 hr). Females: Robust early afternoon activity (sleep-reducing role); Lengthened circadian period (mean 24.6 hr). Strongest period strength in activated flies.
ecological_context: Likely directs dimorphism in daytime sleep (male daytime siesta) and circadian period regulation in natural light/dark cycles.
techniques_used: ['scRNA-seq', 'Immunostaining (Per, Myc)', 'Genetic intersectional approach (Clk856-Gal4 ∩ fru P1-FLP)', 'TrpA1 activation', 'Drosophila Activity Monitor (DAM) assay']
summary: Clk856 ∩ fru P1 circadian neurons were identified via scRNA-seq. Functional activation via TrpA1 showed sexually dimorphic effects on locomotor activity and circadian period, with males exhibiting sleep-promoting and period-shortening effects, and females exhibiting sleep-reducing and period-lengthening effects.

paper_title: Single-cell transcriptome profiles of Drosophila fruitless-expressing neurons from both sexes
experimental_stimulus: None (Spatial validation)
neural_activity: None
behavioral_consequence: None tested
ecological_context: None described
summary: Cluster 107 identified as female-specific CCHa2-expressing neurons in VNC and brain; no male CCHa2 ∩ fru P1 neurons found in VNC.

paper_title: Single-cell transcriptome profiles of Drosophila fruitless-expressing neurons from both sexes

summary: Male-specific cluster 68 identified; subclustering resolved VNC (abdominal ganglion) and brain (pC1/pC2, SN, SLN) populations. Male-specific TN1/TN2 neurons proposed in VNC.

paper_title: Single-cell transcriptome profiles of Drosophila fruitless-expressing neurons from both sexes
cell_type: fru P1 Mushroom Body Kenyon Cells (KCs)
experimental_stimulus: None
neural_activity: None
behavioral_consequence: None tested

techniques_used: ['scRNA-seq', 'Marker gene analysis (ey, Dop1R2, trio, Fas2, sNPF)']
summary: Annotated αβ and γ KC subtypes based on marker genes (e.g., trio for γ KCs). Subclustering revealed 13 subclusters with learning and memory gene expression signatures.

paper_title: Single-cell transcriptome profiles of Drosophila fruitless-expressing neurons from both sexes
experimental_stimulus: None
neural_activity: None
behavioral_consequence: None tested
ecological_context: None described
summary: 62% cholinergic, 19% GABAergic, 14% glutamatergic. Sexual dimorphisms in morphology observed (e.g., male-specific tritocerebral loop in ChAT neurons; mAL neurons in VGAT neurons).

paper_title: Selective integration of diverse taste inputs within a single taste modality
cell_type: Bitter-sensing cells (bitter neurons in different taste organs)
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation of subsets within each organ
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation elicited broad and highly overlapping effects; transsynaptic tracing revealed connections to overlapping downstream pathways with biased connectivity
behavioral_consequence: Elicited broad and highly overlapping behavioral effects; observed behavioral differences indicating biased convergence
summary: Optogenetic activation of subsets of bitter-sensing cells in different taste organs elicited broad and overlapping behavioral effects with observed differences, and transsynaptic tracing revealed biased connectivity to overlapping downstream pathways.

paper_title: Selective integration of diverse taste inputs within a single taste modality
cell_type: Second-order bitter neuron (projecting to the higher brain)
experimental_stimulus: Investigation of taste processing and input integration from multiple organs
neural_activity: Integrates input from multiple organs
behavioral_consequence: Regulates specific taste-related behaviors
summary: A specific type of second-order bitter neuron projecting to the higher brain integrates input from multiple organs and regulates specific taste-related behaviors, with downstream circuits traced.

paper_title: Pairing-Dependent Plasticity in a Dissected Fly Brain Is Input-Specific and Requires Synaptic CaMKII Enrichment and Nighttime Sleep
experimental_stimulus: Electrical stimulation of Antennal Lobe (odor pathway) and Ascending Fibers of Ventral Nerve Cord (punishment pathway) in dissected brains; paired vs. unpaired induction; Odor + Electric Shock in intact flies
neural_activity: Ex vivo calcium imaging (GCaMP6f) showed potentiation (enhancement) of calcium responses in a93 compartment after paired induction; suppression after unpaired induction; plasticity blocked by sleep deprivation and rescued by rebound sleep
behavioral_consequence: CaMKII Udel mutation (reducing KC CaMKII levels) impaired immediate memory performance in aversive olfactory learning assays compared to WT; CaMKII levels specifically decreased in synaptic regions
ecological_context: Associated with aversive olfactory associative memory formation and short-term memory maintenance
techniques_used: ['Ex vivo calcium imaging', 'Intact behavioral learning assays', 'Genetic manipulation (CaMKII Udel)', 'Sleep deprivation/recovery']
summary: a9b'9 Kenyon Cells exhibit pairing-dependent plasticity (PDP) with potentiation in a93 compartment following paired CS/US stimulation; this plasticity requires CaMKII and is blocked by sleep deprivation but rescued by rebound sleep, correlating with behavioral aversive memory performance.

paper_title: Pairing-Dependent Plasticity in a Dissected Fly Brain Is Input-Specific and Requires Synaptic CaMKII Enrichment and Nighttime Sleep
experimental_stimulus: Electrical stimulation of Antennal Lobe (CS) paired with US pathway activation in dissected brains
neural_activity: Ex vivo calcium imaging (20xGCaMP6f, MB027B split-GAL4) showed suppression of calcium responses in a93 dendrites after paired induction
ecological_context: MBONs bias behavior to aversion through mushroom body output
summary: a93 MBONs show suppression PDP in dendrites following artificial aversive learning induction, aligning with in vivo results of aversive memory circuit biasing behavior.

paper_title: Pairing-Dependent Plasticity in a Dissected Fly Brain Is Input-Specific and Requires Synaptic CaMKII Enrichment and Nighttime Sleep
experimental_stimulus: Electrical stimulation of Ascending Fibers of Ventral Nerve Cord (US pathway); ATP perfusion to activate P2X2 channels expressed in PPL1
neural_activity: Dopamine release detected via GRABDA2m sensor in KCs in response to US pathway activation alone (AFV stimulation); release did not require CS+US coincidence; robust in a93, weaker in a3
ecological_context: Encoding negative prediction errors and aversive reinforcement information to the MB
summary: PPL1 dopaminergic neurons release dopamine in response to US pathway activation alone, carrying aversive reinforcement information to the MB without requiring CS+US coincidence for release.

paper_title: Pairing-Dependent Plasticity in a Dissected Fly Brain Is Input-Specific and Requires Synaptic CaMKII Enrichment and Nighttime Sleep
experimental_stimulus: Electrical stimulation of Antennal Lobe via suction electrode
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (GCaMP6f, GH146-GAL4) showed activation of distinct subsets of projection neurons based on electrode placement; AL stimulation recruits a93 KCs at low intensity and a3 KCs at higher intensity
ecological_context: Olfactory information transmission to the mushroom body
summary: Antennal Lobe stimulation activates distinct subsets of projection neurons; AL input intensity determines recruitment of a93 vs a3 Kenyon cell compartments in the memory circuit.

paper_title: Different dopaminergic neurons signal absolute and relative aversive value in the Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Electric shocks of varying intensity (0V, 15V, 30V, 45V, 60V, 90V)
neural_activity: Calcium transients (GCaMP6m) increased significantly with shock intensity above 30V, correlating linearly with voltage
behavioral_consequence: Required for both absolute aversive learning (0V vs 60V) and relative aversive learning (60V vs 30V); thermogenetic inhibition impaired both
summary: PPL1-g1ped DANs respond linearly to shock intensity and are required for absolute and relative aversive value learning.

paper_title: Different dopaminergic neurons signal absolute and relative aversive value in the Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Electric shocks of varying intensity (0V, 15V, 30V, 45V, 60V, 90V)
neural_activity: Calcium transients increased above 30V but plateaued between 30V and 90V
behavioral_consequence: Required for relative aversive learning (60V vs 30V); thermogenetic inhibition impaired relative choice but not absolute choice
summary: PPL1-g2a'1 DANs show a plateau in response to high shock intensity and are specifically required for relative aversive value learning.

paper_title: Different dopaminergic neurons signal absolute and relative aversive value in the Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Electric shocks of varying intensity (0V, 15V, 30V, 45V, 60V, 90V)
neural_activity: No significant correlation between calcium response and shock intensity
behavioral_consequence: Required for relative aversive learning (60V vs 30V); thermogenetic inhibition impaired relative choice but not absolute choice
summary: PPL1-a2a'2 DANs do not show intensity-dependent calcium responses but are required for relative aversive value learning.

paper_title: Different dopaminergic neurons signal absolute and relative aversive value in the Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Sequential odor-shock training (Odor X-0V, Odor Y-60V, Odor Z-30V)
neural_activity: Calcium transients increased during the last odor Z+30V association when preceded by Y+60V, compared to mock control
behavioral_consequence: Required for relative aversive learning; optogenetic inhibition during the last association (Z+30V) impaired relative choice
techniques_used: ['two-photon calcium imaging', 'behavioral T-maze assay', 'thermogenetic inhibition (Shits1)', 'optogenetic inhibition (GtACR1)']
summary: PAM-b'2ag5n DANs signal relative aversive value ('better than') during the final association of a sequential aversive experience.

paper_title: Different dopaminergic neurons signal absolute and relative aversive value in the Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Odor (CS+ paired with shock, CS- unpaired)
neural_activity: Odor-evoked responses (CS+) significantly depressed after training with 30V or higher shock; depression correlated with shock intensity
behavioral_consequence: Plasticity at KC-MBON junction correlates with learning-driven plasticity driven by PPL1-g1ped DANs
summary: MBON-g1ped>ab shows intensity-dependent plasticity in CS+ response depression following aversive conditioning.

paper_title: Different dopaminergic neurons signal absolute and relative aversive value in the Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Odor (CS+ paired with shock, CS- unpaired); Relative training (X, Y, Z)
neural_activity: CS- odor responses increased after training with 45V or higher; response to Z+30V was higher than Y+60V during relative training
behavioral_consequence: Provides excitatory input to PAM-b'2ag5n DANs to drive 'better than' signal for relative value
