summary: MBON-g2a'1 shows intensity-dependent plasticity in CS- response and provides input for relative aversive value coding.

paper_title: Different dopaminergic neurons signal absolute and relative aversive value in the Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Odor (CS+ paired with shock, CS- unpaired)
neural_activity: No learning-induced plasticity in odor responses observed after training
behavioral_consequence: No correlation with PPL1-a2a'2 DAN shock responses
summary: MBON-a2sc does not exhibit learning-induced plasticity in odor responses despite PPL1-a2a'2 DAN involvement in relative learning.

paper_title: Different dopaminergic neurons signal absolute and relative aversive value in the Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Odor during relative training (X, Y, Z)
neural_activity: No significant difference in odor-evoked responses compared to mock training; no difference in subtractive analysis (Z-Y)
behavioral_consequence: Receives synaptic input from PAM-b'2ag5n DANs but did not show differential response during relative learning
summary: MBON-g5b'2a showed no significant plasticity or differential response during relative aversive learning protocol.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learned and innate valences in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Ethyl acetate odor; Optogenetic activation of Kenyon cells (KCs); Optogenetic activation/inactivation
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (GCaMP6f) showed excitation by ethyl acetate via Lateral Horn (LH) pathway; mixed excitatory/inhibitory responses to KC activation
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation repressed turning (approach-like); inactivation increased turning; aversive learning depressed response to conditioned odor
ecological_context: Navigating towards innately attractive odors (food sources) in odor gradients
techniques_used: ['Calcium imaging', 'Optogenetics (CsChrimson, GtACR2, Kir2.1)', 'Behavioral analysis (turn rate, navigation)', 'Electron microscopy']
summary: MBON-m1 receives excitatory inputs from LH and balanced inputs from MB, is activated by attractive odors, and optogenetic activation represses turning to promote approach.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learned and innate valences in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Ethyl acetate odor; Optogenetic activation of KCs; Optogenetic activation/inactivation
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (GCaMP6f) showed excitation by ethyl acetate via LH pathway; mixed excitatory/inhibitory responses to KC activation
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation repressed turning (approach-like); inactivation increased turning; constitutive silencing impaired navigation in odor gradients
ecological_context: Navigating towards innately attractive odors (food sources) in odor gradients
techniques_used: ['Calcium imaging', 'Optogenetics (CsChrimson, GtACR2, Kir2.1)', 'Behavioral analysis (turn rate, navigation)', 'Electron microscopy']
summary: CN-33 integrates LH and MB inputs, is excited by attractive odors, and optogenetic activation represses turning to promote approach.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learned and innate valences in the insect brain
cell_type: Positive-valence MBONs (e.g., MBON-a1, b1/b2, d1, g1/g2, m1, e1)
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: N/A (activity controlled by optogenetics)
behavioral_consequence: Activation repressed turning and promoted crawling (approach-like behavior)
ecological_context: Approach behavior towards appetitive stimuli
summary: Activation of positive-valence MBONs represses turning and promotes approach behavior in Drosophila larvae.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learned and innate valences in the insect brain
cell_type: Negative-valence MBONs (e.g., MBON-a2, h1/h2, i1, k1, e2)
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: N/A (activity controlled by optogenetics)
behavioral_consequence: Activation promoted turning and repressed crawling (avoidance-like behavior)
ecological_context: Avoidance behavior towards aversive stimuli
summary: Activation of negative-valence MBONs promotes turning and suppresses crawling, driving avoidance behavior.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learned and innate valences in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation of ORN42b (with KCs silenced)
neural_activity: N/A (downstream activity inferred)
behavioral_consequence: Activation repressed turning, sufficient to mediate innate odor attraction even without functional MB
ecological_context: Innate attraction to ethyl acetate during navigation
summary: Optogenetic activation of ORN42b and downstream LH neurons represses turning, encoding innate positive valence independent of the mushroom body.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learned and innate valences in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation; Silencing (Kir2.1, TNTe)
neural_activity: Silencing confirmed by lack of odor memory; Calcium imaging in downstream neurons (MBON-m1, CN-33)
behavioral_consequence: Silencing reduced efficiency of innate odor navigation in gradients
ecological_context: Learning and modulation of innate navigation behavior
summary: Silencing KCs impairs the efficiency of innate odor navigation, while KC activation provides modulatory inputs to convergence neurons.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learned and innate valences in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: N/A
behavioral_consequence: Activation paired with odor induced aversive learning (switch from approach to avoidance)
ecological_context: Nociception and aversive conditioning
summary: Optogenetic activation of Basin interneurons serves as a punishment signal to induce aversive associative learning.

paper_title: Compartment specific regulation of sleep by mushroom body requires GABA and dopaminergic signaling
cell_type: PAM-Dopaminergic Neurons (PAM-DANs) projecting to γ5, γ4, β′2 compartments
experimental_stimulus: ATP bath application (via P2X2 channel); GABA bath application; Temperature shift (21°C permissive, 29°C restrictive for dTrpA1 activation or Shibirets1 inactivation); Carbamazepine (CBZ) feeding
neural_activity: ATP application induced robust intracellular Ca2+ increases (GCaMP6m imaging); GABA application suppressed Ca2+ increases; GABA-induced suppression required Rdl receptor expression
behavioral_consequence: Thermogenetic activation suppressed sleep (induced wakefulness); Thermogenetic inactivation (Shits1) increased sleep or blocked CBZ-induced wakefulness; Rdl knockdown reduced total sleep and bout length; Sleep suppression by activation was blocked in DopR1 and DopR2 mutant backgrounds
ecological_context: These circuits mediate satiety, novelty, caffeine-induced arousal, punishment, and reward-associated experiences, balancing sleep, wakefulness and arousal
techniques_used: ['Calcium imaging (GCaMP6m)', 'Drosophila Activity Monitoring (DAM)', 'Thermogenetics (dTrpA1, Shibirets1)', 'RNAi', 'Pharmacology (CBZ, ATP, GABA)']
summary: PAM-DANs projecting to specific MB compartments are wake-promoting; their excitability is inhibited by GABA via Rdl receptors, and their wake-promoting effects require DopR1 and DopR2 signaling downstream.

paper_title: Compartment specific regulation of sleep by mushroom body requires GABA and dopaminergic signaling
cell_type: Kenyon Cells (KCs) (α′/β′ and all KCs in γ5/β′2 compartments)
experimental_stimulus: Knockdown of DopR1 and DopR2 receptors using UAS-RNAi lines (MB010B, MB107B)
neural_activity: Not explicitly measured
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown of DopR1 or DopR2 increased total sleep duration and average sleep bout length; decreased sleep latency
ecological_context: These circuits mediate satiety, novelty, caffeine-induced arousal, punishment, and reward-associated experiences, balancing sleep, wakefulness and arousal
summary: DopR1 and DopR2 signaling in KCs is required for wakefulness; knockdown increases sleep duration and bout length.

paper_title: Compartment specific regulation of sleep by mushroom body requires GABA and dopaminergic signaling
experimental_stimulus: Knockdown of DopR1 and DopR2 receptors using UAS-RNAi lines (MB011B)
neural_activity: Not explicitly measured
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown of DopR1 or DopR2 increased total sleep duration and average sleep bout length
ecological_context: These circuits mediate satiety, novelty, caffeine-induced arousal, punishment, and reward-associated experiences, balancing sleep, wakefulness and arousal
summary: DopR1 and DopR2 signaling in γ5/β′2 MBONs is required for wakefulness; knockdown increases sleep duration and bout length.

paper_title: The anterior paired lateral neuron normalizes odour-evoked activity at the mushroom body calyx
experimental_stimulus: Odours (4-methylcyclohexanol, 3-octanol, δ-Decalactone, Pentyl acetate, Methyl Palmitate, Farnesol)
neural_activity: Odour-evoked calcium transients in the calyx. Response scales with PN input strength (stronger odours elicit stronger response). Activation is regionalized around PN input distribution.
ecological_context: Navigate through a complex and variable environment; identify and memorize discrete but similar environmental inputs.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon in vivo calcium imaging (GCaMP6m)', 'Electron microscopy (EM) connectomics (Hemibrain/FAFB datasets)']
summary: APL neurons respond to odours with calcium transients in the calyx that scale with input strength and are spatially localized to activated PN regions, suggesting a role in normalizing input.

paper_title: The anterior paired lateral neuron normalizes odour-evoked activity at the mushroom body calyx
experimental_stimulus: Odours (4-methylcyclohexanol, 3-octanol, δ-Decalactone)
neural_activity: Calcium transients in antennal lobe glomeruli and MB calyx boutons. Activity strength varies with odour (e.g., Oct > Mch). Number of active boutons does not change between odours, but response levels do.
ecological_context: Transmit olfactory information from antennal lobe to higher brain centers.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon in vivo calcium imaging (GH146-GAL4, NP225-GAL4; GCaMP6m, Syp::GCaMP3)']
summary: PNs display odour-dependent variability in calcium transient strength in both antennal lobe and calyx boutons, which correlates with APL activation levels.

paper_title: The anterior paired lateral neuron normalizes odour-evoked activity at the mushroom body calyx
experimental_stimulus: Odours (4-methylcyclohexanol, 3-octanol, δ-Decalactone, Pentyl acetate, Methyl Palmitate, Farnesol)
neural_activity: Calcium transients in KC claws (dendritic terminals). When APL is active, average response magnitude is homogeneous across odours. When APL output is silenced (TNT), response becomes variable and mirrors PN bouton activity.
ecological_context: Support pattern separation for stimuli discrimination and associative memory formation.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon in vivo calcium imaging (MB247-homer::GCaMP3)', 'APL silencing (UAS-TeTx, tubP-GAL80ts)']
summary: KC claw responses are normalized by APL inhibition, maintaining homogeneous odour-evoked activity levels across different odours; silencing APL increases response variability.

paper_title: Associative learning drives longitudinally graded presynaptic plasticity of neurotransmitter release along axonal compartments
cell_type: Kenyon Cells (KCs) in γ lobe compartments (γ1-γ5)
experimental_stimulus: Odors (ethyl butyrate as CS+, isoamyl acetate as CS-) presented before and after appetitive (sucrose) or aversive (electric shock) conditioning
neural_activity: Acetylcholine (ACh) release measured via GRAB-ACh sensor imaging; showed compartment-specific plasticity (potentiation in γ1, mixed in γ2/γ3, depression in γ5 following appetitive learning; depression in γ2/γ3 following aversive learning)
behavioral_consequence: Conditional knockdown of Cacophony (Cac) in KCs impaired appetitive learning performance in T-maze odor choice assays; knockdown of IP3R caused olfactory adaptation (reduced responsivity to repeated odors)
techniques_used: ['in vivo ACh imaging (GRAB-ACh)', 'RNAi knockdown (Cac, IP3R)', 'T-maze behavioral assay']
summary: KC ACh release in γ lobe compartments undergoes bidirectional, compartment-specific plasticity (potentiation or depression) depending on learning valence (appetitive vs aversive); Cac is required for potentiation and reward learning, while IP3R prevents odor adaptation.

paper_title: Associative learning drives longitudinally graded presynaptic plasticity of neurotransmitter release along axonal compartments
cell_type: Mushroom Body Output Neurons (MBONs) - γ1pedc>α/β, γ2α′1, γ3/γ3β′1, γ5β′2a
experimental_stimulus: Odors (ethyl butyrate as CS+, isoamyl acetate as CS-) presented before and after appetitive conditioning
neural_activity: Calcium (Ca2+) responses measured via GCaMP6f imaging; plasticity mirrored KC ACh changes (e.g., γ3/γ3β′1 showed CS+ potentiation, γ5β′2a showed decreased CS+ response)
behavioral_consequence: Blocking synaptic output from γ3/γ3β′1 MBONs significantly impaired appetitive short-term memory in T-maze choice assays; blocking γ2α′1 MBONs did not significantly impair performance
techniques_used: ['in vivo Ca2+ imaging (GCaMP6f)', 'Shibirets temperature-sensitive silencing', 'T-maze behavioral assay']
summary: MBON Ca2+ responses exhibit learning-induced plasticity coherent with KC ACh changes; γ3/γ3β′1 MBON output is necessary for normal appetitive short-term memory and decision-making.

paper_title: A locomotor neural circuit persists and functions similarly in larvae and adult Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Red light (30 s duration)
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation via CsChrimson::mVenus
behavioral_consequence: Near-complete arrest of forward locomotion, increased probability of pausing, reduced distance traveled. Did not cause general paralysis; small body movements (grooming) preserved.
ecological_context: Pausing between antagonistic behaviors (forward vs backward locomotion) during food search or response to noxious stimuli.
summary: Pair1 neurons persist from larva to adult, maintaining molecular markers (Hb, Scr, Bcd). Optogenetic activation in adults arrests forward locomotion, similar to larval function.

paper_title: A locomotor neural circuit persists and functions similarly in larvae and adult Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Not tested for behavior in this study
neural_activity: Synaptic contact with Pair1 (t-GRASP signal)
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this study
ecological_context: Part of the circuit coordinating forward and backward locomotion transitions.
techniques_used: ['t-GRASP (Targeted GFP Reconstitution Across Synaptic Partners)', 'Confocal microscopy']
summary: MDN neurons persist from larva to adult and are re-established as synaptic partners with Pair1 at mid-pupal stages (48 hr APF).

paper_title: A locomotor neural circuit persists and functions similarly in larvae and adult Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: N/A
neural_activity: N/A
behavioral_consequence: Regulates forward crawling in larvae
ecological_context: Forward locomotion in larvae
summary: A27h interneurons undergo apoptosis during pupal stages and are absent in adults.

paper_title: A neural circuit for wind-guided olfactory navigation
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (Chrimson + Red light), Odor (1% vinegar)
neural_activity: Activation drives robust navigation behaviors; silencing (TNT) abolishes upwind orientation and search responses
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation increases upwind velocity and OFF curvature (search behavior); silencing abolishes these responses to odor
ecological_context: Locating food sources in natural environments where odors are transported by wind in turbulent plumes
summary: ORNs (orco+ and IR8a+) are required for upwind orientation and search; optogenetic activation mimics odor-evoked upwind navigation.

paper_title: A neural circuit for wind-guided olfactory navigation
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (Chrimson + Red light), Wind and Odor stimuli from 5 directions
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (dendritic processes): Responds to vinegar but shows no tuning for wind direction
behavioral_consequence: Activation drives robust upwind movement and OFF curvature (search)
ecological_context: Processing innate olfactory information to guide navigation in windy environments
summary: LHONs (e.g., AD1b2 cluster) promote upwind movement and encode odor independent of wind direction.

paper_title: A neural circuit for wind-guided olfactory navigation
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (Chrimson + Red light), Wind and Odor stimuli from 5 directions
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (axonal processes): Responds to vinegar but shows no tuning for wind direction; Electrophysiology shows no wind direction encoding in MB compartments
behavioral_consequence: Attraction-promoting MBONs (e.g., MB052B, MB077B, MB082C) drive upwind movement; Aversion-promoting MBONs (MB434B) drive downwind; others (MB112C, MB011B) drive straightening
ecological_context: Integrating learned and innate olfactory valence for navigation towards or away from food sources
summary: MBONs encode odor presence independent of wind direction; specific subsets drive upwind, downwind, or straightening behaviors.

paper_title: A neural circuit for wind-guided olfactory navigation
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (Chrimson + Red light), Wind and Odor stimuli from 5 directions
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (FB layers): Respond to attractive odor (vinegar) but show no directional tuning for wind; Trans-synaptic tracing shows MB/LH connectivity
behavioral_consequence: Activation drives persistent upwind movement (more persistent than MB/LH); Silencing individual lines does not block odor-evoked upwind movement
ecological_context: Conveying non-spatial odor value signals to the navigation center (Fan-shaped Body)
summary: FB tangential inputs encode odor independent of wind direction and drive persistent upwind movement when activated.

paper_title: A neural circuit for wind-guided olfactory navigation
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (Chrimson, SPARC, split-GAL4), Odor + Wind stimuli
neural_activity: 2-photon Calcium imaging (dorsal FB): Exhibit odor-gated, wind direction-tuned activity (bump of activity) unique to each fly's coordinates
behavioral_consequence: Sparse activation promotes navigation in a reproducible direction; Broad activation causes unstable reorientation (turns); Silencing impairs persistent upwind orientation during odor
ecological_context: Integrating odor and wind cues to specify a goal heading for navigation towards an odor source
summary: hΔC neurons encode an odor-gated wind direction signal; they are required for sustained upwind orientation and can specify reproducible headings.

paper_title: A neural circuit for wind-guided olfactory navigation
experimental_stimulus: Wind direction stimuli (manifold), Odor stimuli
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (Fig 2F): Encode wind direction (ventral layers PFNa, p, m); Little sensitivity to odor stimuli
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation screen found no lines labeling PFNs generated significant upwind movement
ecological_context: Encoding self-motion and environmental flow (wind direction) for orientation
summary: PFNs encode wind direction but not odor; activation of PFN lines did not drive upwind movement in this screen.

paper_title: A neuronal ensemble encoding adaptive choice during sensory conflict in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation/inhibition during two-choice food decision (50 mM sucrose vs 500 mM sucrose + 1 mM quinine); Foreleg application of sweet (50 mM sucrose), bittersweet (500 mM sucrose + 1 mM quinine), and water stimuli during calcium imaging
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (GCaMP6f/tdTomato) showed FBl6 neurons were strongly inhibited by the taste of the rejected food choice (e.g., inhibited by bittersweet if fly chose sweet). Optogenetic manipulation did not induce feeding in fed flies or prevent feeding in food-deprived flies
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic inhibition shifted preference towards bittersweet (increased proportion of flies consuming bittersweet); optogenetic activation had no effect on preference
ecological_context: Enables adaptive feeding decisions when foraging on decomposing fruits, balancing obtaining nutrition with avoiding toxins
summary: FBl6 neurons encode final behavioral food choice by inhibiting in response to the rejected food stimulus. Inhibiting FBl6 shifts preference towards bittersweet food.

paper_title: A neuronal ensemble encoding adaptive choice during sensory conflict in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation/inhibition during two-choice food decision (50 mM sucrose vs 500 mM sucrose + 1 mM quinine)
neural_activity: Not directly recorded in this study
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation suppressed feeding in food-deprived flies; inhibition shifted preference towards bittersweet
ecological_context: Relays metabolic state information to higher brain regions to influence hunger-dependent food intake
summary: Lk neurons modulate hunger state and food intake; activation suppresses feeding while inhibition biases choice towards higher calorie bittersweet food.

paper_title: A neuronal ensemble encoding adaptive choice during sensory conflict in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation/inhibition during two-choice food decision
neural_activity: Not directly recorded in this study
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation shifted preference towards sweet; inhibition shifted preference towards bittersweet
ecological_context: Conveys food identity information for decision making in the central brain
summary: AstA neurons influence the balance between sweet and bittersweet choices, with activation favoring sweet food.

paper_title: A neuronal ensemble encoding adaptive choice during sensory conflict in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation/inhibition during two-choice food decision
neural_activity: Not directly recorded in this study
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation shifted preference towards sweet
ecological_context: Conveys food valence information for decision making
summary: NPF neuron activation biases flies towards sweet food choices during sensory conflict.

paper_title: A neuronal ensemble encoding adaptive choice during sensory conflict in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation/inhibition during two-choice food decision
neural_activity: Not directly recorded in this study
behavioral_consequence: Activation had no significant effect; inhibition shifted preference towards bittersweet
ecological_context: Senses sugars and conveys food identity information to FBl6
summary: DH44 neuron inhibition biases choice towards bittersweet food, while activation does not significantly alter preference.

paper_title: A neuronal ensemble encoding adaptive choice during sensory conflict in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation/inhibition during two-choice food decision
neural_activity: Not directly recorded in this study
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation of these subsets shifted preference towards bittersweet
ecological_context: Involved in aversive memory, taste conditioning, and long-term memory influencing food choice
summary: Activation of specific dopaminergic subsets biases flies towards bittersweet food choices.

paper_title: A neuronal ensemble encoding adaptive choice during sensory conflict in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation/inhibition during two-choice food decision
neural_activity: Not directly recorded in this study
behavioral_consequence: Did not affect food preference
ecological_context: Higher-order brain region tested for involvement in decision making
summary: Acute manipulation of mushroom body neurons did not alter food preference in the decision task.

paper_title: A neuronal ensemble encoding adaptive choice during sensory conflict in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Acute optogenetic inhibition / Chronic inhibition (tetanus toxin) during two-choice food decision
neural_activity: Not directly recorded in this study
behavioral_consequence: Did not change food preference
ecological_context: Tested for potential role in relaying taste information to fan-shaped body
summary: Inhibition of TPN3 taste projection neurons did not affect food choice, suggesting taste information may reach FBl6 through other pathways.

paper_title: A neurogenetic mechanism of experience-dependent suppression of aggression
cell_type: Octopaminergic/tyraminergic (OA/TA) neurons (subpopulation specifically labeled by nvy)
experimental_stimulus: Social experience (comparison of socially experienced vs. socially naïve flies; various contexts including escape or courtship transitions)
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown of nvy in OA/TA neurons increased aggression; Overexpression of nvy suppressed spontaneous aggression in socially naïve flies; Loss-of-function nvy mutant exhibited persistent aggression under various contexts where wild-type flies transition to escape or courtship behaviors; Subpopulation of OA/TA cells specifically labeled by nvy is required for social-experience-dependent suppression of aggression
ecological_context: Excessive aggression can be detrimental to animal fitness; Social experiences among conspecific individuals reduce aggression
techniques_used: ['Genetic manipulation (loss-of-function mutation, overexpression, knockdown)', 'Cell-type-specific transcriptomics', 'Aggression behavior assessment']
summary: A subpopulation of OA/TA neurons labeled by nvy is required for social-experience-dependent suppression of aggression. Knockdown of nvy in these neurons increases aggression, while overexpression suppresses spontaneous aggression in naïve flies.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for steering control in walking Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Spontaneous walking on spherical treadmill; fictive odor (Orco-LexA); fictive heat (Gr28b.d-LexA); compass manipulation (P-EN1 ATP microstimulation)
neural_activity: Whole-cell recordings showed firing rate increases correlate with rotational and sideways velocity; filters are monophasic (sustained output); lower gain than DNa02; right-left firing rate difference correlates with steering
behavioral_consequence: Predicts sustained, low-gain steering; unilateral optogenetic activation causes ipsilateral steering bias; bilateral silencing reduces sideways speed and translational velocity; alters leg kinematics (reduced inner back leg stance prolongation); decreases step frequency and increases leg swing distance
ecological_context: Navigation toward remembered targets or objects in the local environment during walking
techniques_used: ['Whole-cell electrophysiology', 'Optogenetics (ReaChR activation, GtACR1 silencing)', 'Spherical treadmill tracking', 'Arena walking tracking', 'EM reconstruction']
summary: DNa01 descending neurons correlate with sustained, low-gain steering during walking; their activity predicts rotational and sideways velocity changes, and their silencing reduces sideways speed and alters leg kinematics.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for steering control in walking Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Spontaneous walking on spherical treadmill; fictive odor (Orco-LexA); fictive heat (Gr28b.d-LexA); compass manipulation (P-EN1 ATP microstimulation)
neural_activity: Whole-cell recordings showed firing rate increases correlate with rotational and sideways velocity; filters are biphasic (transient output); high gain compared to DNa02; right-left firing rate difference linearly relates to rotational velocity; active before compass-compensatory turns; encodes laterality of stimulus-evoked action
behavioral_consequence: Predicts transient, high-gain steering; unilateral optogenetic activation causes ipsilateral steering bias; bilateral silencing alters leg kinematics (inner back leg stance) but rotational speed is compensated; predicts magnitude and direction of compass-directed and stimulus-directed turns
ecological_context: Navigation toward remembered targets or objects in the local environment during walking
techniques_used: ['Whole-cell electrophysiology', 'Optogenetics (ReaChR activation, GtACR1 silencing)', 'Spherical treadmill tracking', 'Arena walking tracking', 'EM reconstruction', 'Two-photon calcium imaging']
summary: DNa02 descending neurons correlate with transient, high-gain steering; their activity predicts steering magnitude/direction for both compass-directed and stimulus-directed navigation, and their silencing affects leg kinematics.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for steering control in walking Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: P-EN1 microstimulation (ATP puff) to shift compass heading
neural_activity: EM reconstruction shows PFL3 neurons receive disynaptic input from E-PG compass neurons and project to DNa02; connectivity transforms compass ring attractor into point attractor dynamics
behavioral_consequence: Computational model shows PFL3 wiring generates stable heading direction regardless of initial heading; FB input to PFL3 can shift stable heading policy
ecological_context: Maintains heading direction relative to spatial memory goals during navigation
summary: PFL3 neurons provide disynaptic input from compass neurons to DNa02; their specific wiring transforms compass signals into stable steering drives for memory-directed navigation.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for steering control in walking Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Walking in darkness; P-EN1 microstimulation (ATP puff)
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (GCaMP6f) shows a single bump of activity rotating as fly steers; bump jumps after P-EN1 stimulation
behavioral_consequence: Bump position constitutes working memory of heading; compensatory behavioral turns return bump to initial location after P-EN1 stimulation
ecological_context: Spatial memory navigation toward distant targets
summary: E-PG neurons encode fly heading via a bump of activity; this bump serves as a working memory of heading direction that drives compensatory steering when perturbed.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for steering control in walking Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: ATP puff (P2X2 activation)
neural_activity: Microstimulation causes bump jump in E-PG ensemble
behavioral_consequence: Elicits compensatory steering maneuver to return compass bump to initial location
ecological_context: Relays angular velocity to compass neurons for heading estimation
summary: P-EN1 neurons relay angular velocity to compass neurons; their microstimulation causes compass bump jumps that drive compensatory steering behavior.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for steering control in walking Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual object stimuli (inferred from EM and connectivity)
neural_activity: EM reconstruction identifies LC9 cells projecting directly to DNa02 from lobula
behavioral_consequence: Part of pathway for visual object-directed steering
ecological_context: Steering toward visual objects in the local environment
summary: LC9 neurons project to DNa02 from the lobula and likely participate in visual object-directed steering pathways.

paper_title: Neural circuit mechanisms for steering control in walking Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Fictive odor (Orco-LexA/CsChrimson); Fictive heat (Gr28b.d-LexA/CsChrimson)
neural_activity: DNa02 firing rate increases ipsilateral to attractive odor; higher firing contralateral to aversive heat
behavioral_consequence: Drives steering toward attractive stimuli and away from aversive stimuli; bypasses compass system
ecological_context: Stimulus-directed navigation toward objects in the local environment
summary: Sensory pathways bypassing the compass drive DNa02 activity to control steering toward attractive or away from aversive local stimuli.

paper_title: Identification of Dopaminergic Neurons That Can Both Establish Associative Memory and Acutely Terminate Its Behavioral Expression
experimental_stimulus: Blue light (optogenetic activation), odor (n-amyl acetate) presented paired or unpaired, sugar reward (2 mol/L D-fructose)
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation via ChR2-XXL induced by blue light
behavioral_consequence: Paired training with activation established appetitive associative memory (increased odor preference); unpaired training established aversive associative memory (decreased odor preference). Activation during recall test acutely terminated memory-based search behavior (odor preference returned to baseline). Activation had no effect on innate odor preference. Activation modulated microbehavior (head cast rate and direction) during memory search, and terminated these modulations during recall.
ecological_context: Mediates the transition from exploration (searching for food) to exploitation (stopping search once food is found), allowing adaptive resource utilization.
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics (ChR2-XXL, blue light)', 'Behavioral tracking (odor preference assays)', 'Head cast rate analysis', 'Immunohistochemistry', 'Electron microscopy-based dendrogram analysis']
summary: DAN-i1 neurons, when optogenetically activated, can both establish associative memory of opposite valence (appetitive or aversive) depending on training contingency, and acutely terminate the behavioral expression of that memory during recall, without affecting innate behavior. This supports an exploration-exploitation transition strategy.

paper_title: Circuits that encode and guide alcohol-associated preference
experimental_stimulus: Intoxicating ethanol vapor paired with odor (acquisition); Odor cues alone (retrieval)
neural_activity: 2-photon calcium imaging (GCaMP6m) showed population-level calcium dynamics increased with prolonged alcohol exposure compared to odor only
behavioral_consequence: Thermogenetic inactivation (shibirets1) during acquisition or retrieval significantly reduced alcohol-associated preference; inactivation during consolidation had no effect
ecological_context: Used as a model for substance use disorders; alcohol memories are more inflexible than natural reward memories
summary: Population level PAM dopaminergic activity is required during acquisition and retrieval of alcohol-associated preference, as shown by calcium imaging and thermogenetic inactivation.

paper_title: Circuits that encode and guide alcohol-associated preference
experimental_stimulus: Odor cues associated with alcohol (retrieval)
neural_activity: Inactivation during retrieval reduced preference; identified as postsynaptic to b`2mp MBON
behavioral_consequence: Thermogenetic inactivation during retrieval significantly reduced alcohol-associated preference
ecological_context: Part of circuit for enduring, inflexible alcohol memories (addiction model)
summary: Sparse subset of PAM dopaminergic neurons (b`2a) are required for memory expression during retrieval.

paper_title: Circuits that encode and guide alcohol-associated preference
experimental_stimulus: Odor cues associated with alcohol (retrieval)
neural_activity: Inactivation during retrieval reduced preference; RNAi of D1/D2 receptors in these neurons did not affect preference
behavioral_consequence: Thermogenetic inactivation during retrieval significantly reduced alcohol-associated preference; inactivation during acquisition did not
ecological_context: Part of circuit for enduring, inflexible alcohol memories (addiction model)
summary: Glutamatergic b2 b`2a MBON is required for expression of alcohol-associated preference during retrieval.

paper_title: Circuits that encode and guide alcohol-associated preference
experimental_stimulus: Overnight consolidation period (sleep)
neural_activity: Inactivation during consolidation enhanced preference; identified as postsynaptic to b`2a dopamine neurons
behavioral_consequence: Inactivation during consolidation significantly increased alcohol-associated preference; inactivation during acquisition or retrieval did not
ecological_context: Implicated in arousal; regulates consolidation of enduring alcohol memories
summary: Glutamatergic b`2mp MBON is required for memory consolidation; its inactivation during sleep enhances alcohol preference.

paper_title: Circuits that encode and guide alcohol-associated preference
experimental_stimulus: Odor cues associated with alcohol (retrieval)
neural_activity: Inactivation during retrieval reduced preference; converges on b`2mp and FSB
behavioral_consequence: Thermogenetic inactivation during retrieval significantly reduced alcohol-associated preference; inactivation during acquisition did not
ecological_context: Part of circuit for enduring, inflexible alcohol memories (addiction model)
summary: Cholinergic a`2 MBON is required for expression of alcohol-associated preference during retrieval.

paper_title: Circuits that encode and guide alcohol-associated preference
experimental_stimulus: MBON activity convergence (a`2, b`2mp, b2 b`2a)
neural_activity: Identified as postsynaptic targets of a`2, b`2mp, and b2 b`2a MBONs via trans-synaptic tracing
behavioral_consequence: No direct behavioral manipulation reported; identified as a region of convergence for MB output activity
ecological_context: Implicated in arousal and integration of naïve and learned responses
summary: Dorsal FSB layers 4-6 receive convergent input from memory-related MB output neurons.

paper_title: A Circuit Encoding Absolute Cold Temperature in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Cold temperature steps (stable conditions below 25°C)
neural_activity: Sustained firing (non-adapting) that scales with absolute temperature below 25°C; calcium levels do not return to baseline for duration of cold step
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation increases sleep (especially morning); Genetic silencing prevents sleep restructuring in cold conditions (prevents evening sleep reduction and morning sleep advancement)
ecological_context: Adapting sleep and activity specifically to cold conditions on the timescale of hours to days
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging', 'Two-photon guided patch-clamp electrophysiology', 'Optogenetics (CsChrimson)', 'Genetic silencing (Tetanus toxin light chain)']
summary: TPN-IIs encode absolute cold temperature with sustained firing below 25°C and inhibit DN1a neurons to regulate sleep patterns in cold conditions.

paper_title: A Circuit Encoding Absolute Cold Temperature in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Cold temperature steps, Light exposure, PDF application, sLNv activation (ATP/P2X2)
neural_activity: Persistently silenced by cold temperature (inhibited by TPN-IIs); Excited by light and sLNv signaling (PDF); Clock-regulated firing rhythms (higher in morning ZT0-4 vs night ZT20-24)
behavioral_consequence: Genetic silencing mimics cold conditions (advanced siesta onset); Silencing prevents cold-induced sleep restructuring in the evening
ecological_context: Circadian clock network; Regulation of morning activity and night-to-day sleep/wake transition
techniques_used: ['Patch-clamp electrophysiology', 'Immunohistochemistry', 'Genetic silencing (Tetanus toxin light chain)', 'Circadian behavioral assays (DAMS)']
summary: DN1a neurons are directly inhibited by cold via TPN-IIs and integrate light/circadian signals to regulate sleep and activity timing.

paper_title: A Circuit Encoding Absolute Cold Temperature in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Cooling temperature steps
neural_activity: Respond to cooling with activity that scales with stimulus intensity; show both transient and persistent components
ecological_context: Peripheral thermosensory input to brain thermosensory map
summary: Arista TRNs provide sensory input to the cold glomerulus of the PAL and synapse onto TPN-IIs.

paper_title: A Circuit Encoding Absolute Cold Temperature in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Cooling temperature steps (cold range)
neural_activity: Exclusively respond to cold temperatures (below 25°C), not just cooling
ecological_context: Peripheral thermosensory input to brain thermosensory map
summary: Sacculus chamber I neurons innervate the cold glomerulus and selectively respond to absolute cold temperatures.

paper_title: A Circuit Encoding Absolute Cold Temperature in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Cooling temperature steps
neural_activity: Show persistent calcium signals both above and below 25°C
ecological_context: Internal temperature receptor on surface of antennal nerve
summary: ACc neurons are an unusual internal cold receptor that innervates the cold glomerulus and connects to TPN-IIs.

paper_title: A Circuit Encoding Absolute Cold Temperature in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: ATP (via P2X2 activation), Pigment Dispersing Factor (PDF) application
neural_activity: Artificial activation drives increased firing in DN1a neurons and can overcome cold inhibition
ecological_context: Core component of clock circuit; suggested role in morning activity regulation
summary: sLNvs provide excitatory drive to DN1as via PDF, helping overcome cold inhibition and regulating sleep timing.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learnt and innate valences in the fly brain
experimental_stimulus: Ethyl acetate (innately attractive odor), optogenetic activation of Kenyon cells, aversive training (odor paired with optogenetic activation of Basin interneurons)
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (GCaMP6f) showed excitation by ethyl acetate via lateral horn pathway. Net mushroom body drive was zero in naive intact animals. Response to conditioned odor significantly decreased after aversive learning.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation represses turning and promotes crawling (odor approach). Optogenetic inactivation (GtACR2) increases turning. Constitutive silencing (Kir2.1) impairs innate approach navigation in an odor gradient.
ecological_context: Mediates innate attraction to volatile molecules and allows switching to learnt avoidance when an attractive odor is paired with punishment.
techniques_used: ['Calcium imaging', 'Optogenetics (activation, inactivation, silencing)', 'Microfluidics', 'Electron microscopy', 'Behavioral tracking (turning, crawling, navigation index)']
summary: MBON-m1 is a convergence neuron excited by ethyl acetate via the lateral horn pathway that promotes approach behavior; its conditioned odor response is depressed by aversive learning.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learnt and innate valences in the fly brain
experimental_stimulus: Ethyl acetate, optogenetic activation of Kenyon cells (in vivo and brain explants)
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (GCaMP6f) showed excitation by ethyl acetate via lateral horn pathway. Net mushroom body drive was variable (excitatory, inhibitory, or zero) across individuals in naive state.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation represses turning and promotes crawling (odor approach). Optogenetic inactivation increases turning. Constitutive silencing (Kir2.1) abolishes attraction to ethyl acetate.
ecological_context: Integrates learnt and innate valences to compute predicted value associated with approaching or avoiding an odor.
summary: CN-33 is a convergence neuron that receives input from lateral horn and mushroom body output neurons; its activation promotes approach behavior and is required for innate odor attraction.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learnt and innate valences in the fly brain
cell_type: Approach-promoting MBONs (e.g., MBON-a1, b1/b2, d1, e1, g1/g2)
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) with red light
neural_activity: Activity induced by red light activation of CsChrimson expressed in specific MBON types.
behavioral_consequence: Activation represses turning and promotes crawling compared to controls. Activation during odor presentation would repress turning to allow approach.
ecological_context: Promoting approach behavior towards innately attractive or learned attractive odors.
summary: Activation of approach-promoting MBONs represses turning and promotes crawling, driving approach behavior.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learnt and innate valences in the fly brain
cell_type: Avoidance-promoting MBONs (e.g., MBON-a2, h1/h2, i1, k1, e2)
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) with red light
neural_activity: Activity induced by red light activation of CsChrimson expressed in specific MBON types.
behavioral_consequence: Activation promotes turning and represses crawling compared to controls. Activation during odor presentation would promote turning to result in odor avoidance.
ecological_context: Promoting avoidance behavior towards innately repulsive or learned aversive odors.
summary: Activation of avoidance-promoting MBONs promotes turning and represses crawling, driving avoidance behavior.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learnt and innate valences in the fly brain
cell_type: Lateral Horn Neurons (LHNs, downstream of PN42a/b)
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation of ORN42b, ethyl acetate presentation (in KC-silenced animals)
neural_activity: Confirmed functional excitatory input to MBON-m1 and CN-33 via calcium imaging in animals with silenced Kenyon cells.
behavioral_consequence: Mediate innate odor approach by repressing turning when odor concentration increases (crawling towards odor source).
ecological_context: Support innate odor attraction behavior independent of a functional mushroom body pathway.
summary: LHNs downstream of PN42b mediate innate odor attraction by repressing turning in response to increased odor concentration and provide functional input to convergence neurons.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learnt and innate valences in the fly brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson), Silencing (Kir2.1), Odor-sugar training (memory test)
neural_activity: Silencing verified by lack of odor memory. Optogenetic activation evoked variable responses in downstream neurons.
behavioral_consequence: Silencing results in less efficient navigation in an ethyl acetate gradient (specifically impaired turning in response to decrease in odor concentration).
ecological_context: Contributes to aspects of innate odor navigation to improve accuracy or efficiency, particularly when navigating away from attractive odor sources.
summary: Silencing Kenyon cells impairs navigation efficiency in odor gradients, suggesting the mushroom body contributes to aspects of innate odor navigation.

paper_title: Circuits for integrating learnt and innate valences in the fly brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson)
neural_activity: Activity induced by red light activation in naive animals.
behavioral_consequence: Increase in activity decreases turning; decrease in activity increases turning. Promotes approach of innately attractive odors.
ecological_context: Encode innately attractive odors such as ethyl acetate to drive innate approach behavior.
summary: Optogenetic activation of ORN42b decreases turning, promoting approach behavior consistent with innate attraction to ethyl acetate.

paper_title: Recurrent architecture for adaptive regulation of learning in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation paired with odor (CS); Optogenetic activation of nociceptive (MD IV) and mechanosensory (Chordotonal) neurons
neural_activity: Reliable calcium transients (GCaMP6f) in response to at least two types of fictive punishments; tuning differs between DANs
behavioral_consequence: Activation paired with odor establishes aversive memory for paired odors; Innate activation evokes turning avoidance (mechanosensory) or fast crawling/rolling escape (nociceptive/Basins)
ecological_context: Larvae sense multiple types of innately aversive somatosensory stimuli that evoke distinct types of innate avoidance and escape responses; supports adaptive behavior in changing environments
summary: Vertical lobe DANs (f1, g1, d1) are activated by somatosensory punishment and optogenetic activation induces aversive memory, while distinct sensory inputs evoke specific innate escape responses.

paper_title: Recurrent architecture for adaptive regulation of learning in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation paired with odor (CS)
neural_activity: Identified as modulatory neurons innervating the medial lobe
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces appetitive memory for paired odors
ecological_context: Learning associations between sensory cues and rewards (appetitive) in changing environments
summary: Medial lobe DANs (h1, i1, k1) activation induces appetitive memory for paired odors, contrasting with vertical lobe DANs.

paper_title: Recurrent architecture for adaptive regulation of learning in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation paired with odor (CS)
neural_activity: Cholinergic (excitatory); Presynaptic to DAN-f1 in EM reconstruction
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces aversive memory for paired odors, similar to postsynaptic DAN-f1
ecological_context: Feedback pathways from MBONs regulate modulatory neuron activity to support learning
summary: FB2N-19 is a cholinergic feedback neuron targeting DAN-f1; its activation induces aversive memory.

paper_title: Recurrent architecture for adaptive regulation of learning in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation paired with odor (CS)
neural_activity: GABAergic (inhibitory); Presynaptic to DAN-d1 in EM reconstruction
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces appetitive memory for paired odors (opposite to postsynaptic DAN-d1 valence)
ecological_context: Feedback motifs link distinct memory systems (aversive and appetitive)
summary: FAN-7 is a GABAergic feedback neuron targeting DAN-d1; its activation induces appetitive memory, opposite to DAN-d1.

paper_title: Recurrent architecture for adaptive regulation of learning in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation paired with odor (CS)
neural_activity: Glutamatergic (likely inhibitory); Presynaptic to DAN-f1 in EM reconstruction
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces appetitive memory for paired odors (opposite to postsynaptic DANs)
ecological_context: Indirect feedback pathways from MBONs to modulatory neurons increase connectivity between memory systems
summary: FB2N-18 and FB2N-11 are glutamatergic feedback neurons targeting DAN-f1; activation induces appetitive memory.

paper_title: Recurrent architecture for adaptive regulation of learning in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation
neural_activity: MBON-i1 activation evokes long-latency inhibitory responses in DAN-i1; MBON-m1 activation evokes excitatory responses in DAN-i1
behavioral_consequence: Indirectly regulate learning by influencing modulatory neuron activity; activity represents learnt valences
ecological_context: Prior memories represented by MBON activity can strongly influence modulatory neuron activity
summary: MBON-i1 provides inhibitory feedback to DAN-i1, while MBON-m1 provides excitatory feedback, demonstrating convergent feedback from compartments of opposite valence.

paper_title: Recurrent architecture for adaptive regulation of learning in the insect brain
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (fictive punishment)
neural_activity: Evoke calcium transients in specific DANs; distinct innate escape responses
behavioral_consequence: Activation paired with odor induces aversive memory; Chordotonal evokes turning avoidance; MD IV/Basins evoke fast crawling/rolling escape
ecological_context: Innate avoidance and escape responses to somatosensory stimuli
summary: Somatosensory neurons transduce different punishments (vibration, nociception) into distinct innate escape responses and aversive memories via specific DANs.

paper_title: Recurrent architecture for adaptive regulation of learning in the insect brain
cell_type: DAN-c1, OAN-a1, OAN-e1, MBIN-e1, MBIN-e2, MBIN-l1, OAN-g1
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation paired with odor (CS)
neural_activity: DAN-c1, OAN-e1, MBIN-e2 not significantly activated by fictive punishments
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces neither appetitive nor aversive short-term memory
ecological_context: Defines a third class of compartments in larval MB distinct from aversive and appetitive memory compartments
summary: Several modulatory neurons (c1, OAN-a1, e1, etc.) do not induce short-term memory when activated and do not respond significantly to fictive punishments, defining a distinct functional class.

paper_title: Connectomics Analysis Reveals First-, Second-, and Third-Order Thermosensory and Hygrosensory Neurons in the Adult Drosophila Brain
summary: Reconstructed all putative VP sensory neurons in FAFB volume. Identified seven distinct glomeruli (VP1d, VP1l, VP1m, VP2, VP3, VP4, VP5). Receptor expression (Ir40a, Ir68a, Ir21a, Gr28b.d) mapped via IHC; modalities (e.g., dry, humid, heating, cooling) inferred from expression but awaiting physiological confirmation.

paper_title: Connectomics Analysis Reveals First-, Second-, and Third-Order Thermosensory and Hygrosensory Neurons in the Adult Drosophila Brain
summary: Reconstructed 88 VP PNs (38 morphological types) on the right-hand side. Identified diverse targets including lACA, CA, LH, SEZ, and VC5. Mapped connectivity to third-order neurons; 23 novel VP PN types identified.

paper_title: Connectomics Analysis Reveals First-, Second-, and Third-Order Thermosensory and Hygrosensory Neurons in the Adult Drosophila Brain
summary: Presented first connectome of lACA. Identified Kenyon Cells (KCa'b', KCg) and other targets (DN1a, DN1-l, DNp44). DN1a neurons linked to circadian clock circuitry via accessory medulla. DNp44 descending neuron identified with strong VP4 PN input. Connectivity mapped to LH, SMP, and antlers.

paper_title: Presynaptic developmental plasticity allows robust sparse wiring of the Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: odor inputs (olfactory stimuli)
neural_activity: sparse odor responses preserved in mushroom bodies with reduced cellular repertoires; Kenyon cells produce fixed distributions of dendritic claws
behavioral_consequence: not tested
ecological_context: not discussed
techniques_used: ['cell ratio manipulation', 'developmental imaging of dendritic claws']
summary: Kenyon cells of the Drosophila mushroom body produce fixed distributions of dendritic claws while presynaptic processes are plastic during development. Sparse odor responses are preserved in mushroom bodies with reduced cellular repertoires, suggesting developmental specification of convergence ratio allows functional robustness. Postsynaptic Kenyon cells set the convergence ratio with pre- and post-synaptic cell manipulation.

paper_title: A neural mechanism for deprivation state-specific expression of relevant memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Osmolality changes (270 to 370 mOsm), dehydration (8h water deprivation), starvation (20h)
neural_activity: Increased Ca2+ signal (GCaMP6m and TRIC reporter) in response to high osmolality and thirst; signal increased after 20h starvation compared to sated flies; decreased when osmolality returned to 270 mOsm
behavioral_consequence: Silencing (Kir2.1/RNAi) impaired 6h water memory in thirsty flies and 6h sugar memory in hungry flies; activation (TrpA1) promoted 6h water memory in water-sated flies and 6h sugar memory in food-sated flies; knockdown impaired innate attraction to high humidity
ecological_context: Activity correlates with internal thirst and extreme hunger states to regulate memory-related circuitry
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging', 'TRIC reporter', 'Optogenetics (TrpA1)', 'Silencing (Kir2.1, RNAi)', 'Olfactory conditioning', 'Humidity preference assay']
summary: LHLK neurons are osmosensitive and activated by thirst and extreme hunger; their release of LK neuropeptide gates both thirst-dependent water memory and hunger-dependent sugar memory expression.

paper_title: A neural mechanism for deprivation state-specific expression of relevant memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Osmolality changes (270 to 370 mOsm)
neural_activity: Decreased Ca2+ signal (GCaMP6m) in response to high osmolality
behavioral_consequence: Not experimentally tested in this study
ecological_context: Contrasts with LHLK neuron activity in response to osmolality
summary: SELK neurons show decreased activity with increased hemolymph osmolality, distinct from LHLK neurons.

paper_title: A neural mechanism for deprivation state-specific expression of relevant memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Synthetic LK peptide, dNPF peptide, starvation (20h)
neural_activity: LK application decreased Ca2+ signals; starvation and dNPF application increased Ca2+ signals; dNPF neutralized LK-induced reduction
behavioral_consequence: Activation impaired 6h water memory in thirsty flies; blocking promoted 6h water memory in water-sated flies; Lkr or NPFR knockdown impaired water memory state-specificity
ecological_context: Integrates thirst (LK) and hunger (dNPF) signals to gate water memory expression
summary: PAM-β'2a DANs are inhibited by LK to promote water memory and activated by hunger signals (dNPF) to prevent inappropriate water memory expression.

paper_title: A neural mechanism for deprivation state-specific expression of relevant memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Synthetic LK peptide, 5-HT, starvation (20h)
neural_activity: LK application decreased Ca2+ signals; starvation and 5-HT application increased Ca2+ signals; 5-HT reduced LK-induced inhibition
behavioral_consequence: Activation impaired 6h water memory in thirsty flies; combined blocking with PAM-β'2a promoted water memory in water-sated flies; Lkr or 5HT1B knockdown impaired water memory state-specificity
ecological_context: Integrates thirst (LK) and hunger (5-HT) signals to gate water memory expression alongside PAM-β'2a DANs
summary: PPL1-γ2α'1 DANs are inhibited by LK and activated by hunger signals (5-HT), working with PAM-β'2a DANs to regulate water memory expression.

paper_title: A neural mechanism for deprivation state-specific expression of relevant memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Synthetic LK peptide
neural_activity: LK application increased Ca2+ signals in a dose-dependent manner
behavioral_consequence: Blocking impaired 6h sugar memory in hungry flies; activation promoted 6h sugar memory in fed flies; Lkr knockdown impaired sugar memory in hungry flies
ecological_context: Facilitates hunger-dependent sugar memory expression
summary: PAM-β'2mp DANs are activated by LK to facilitate hunger-dependent sugar memory expression, contrasting with the inhibition of PAM-β'2a and PPL1-γ2α'1 DANs.

paper_title: A neural mechanism for deprivation state-specific expression of relevant memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Heat activation (TrpA1)
neural_activity: Not directly measured in this study
behavioral_consequence: Activation impaired 6h water memory in thirsty flies
ecological_context: Hunger signal that competes with thirst signals to prevent inappropriate water memory expression
summary: Activation of dNPF neurons impairs water memory expression in thirsty flies, indicating a role in hunger-driven behavioral prioritization.

paper_title: A neural mechanism for deprivation state-specific expression of relevant memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Heat activation (TrpA1)
neural_activity: Not directly measured in this study
behavioral_consequence: Activation impaired 6h water memory in thirsty flies
ecological_context: Hunger signal that competes with thirst signals to prevent inappropriate water memory expression
summary: Activation of specific 5-HT neurons impairs water memory expression in thirsty flies, functioning as a hunger signal competing with LK.

paper_title: Nitric oxide acts as a cotransmitter in a subset of dopaminergic neurons to diversify memory dynamics
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson-mVenus, 627 nm red light) paired with odor (3-octanol or 4-methylcyclohexanol); also activated by natural punishment stimuli (electric shock, noxious temperature, bitter taste)
neural_activity: NOS immunoreactivity observed in axonal terminals within the γ1pedc compartment of the mushroom body; RNA-Seq identified NOS1 expression; calcium imaging in postsynaptic MBONs showed prolonged increase in calcium levels upon DAN activation in TH mutant background, suppressed by NOS inhibitor L-NNA
behavioral_consequence: In wild-type flies, induces negative-valence (aversive) olfactory memory; in TH mutant flies (dopamine-deficient), induces positive-valence (appetitive) memory (valence inversion); NOS inhibition or knockdown blocks inverted memory in TH mutants and prolongs retention of negative memory in wild-type flies; facilitates faster memory updating during reversal learning
ecological_context: Supports survival in a dynamically changing world by enabling prediction of odor value based only on recent events; involved in processing natural punishment (e.g., heat, shock)
summary: PPL1-γ1pedc DANs express NOS and release nitric oxide as a cotransmitter. In wild-type flies they induce aversive memory, but in dopamine-deficient flies they induce appetitive memory. NOS signaling shortens memory retention and facilitates rapid memory updating.

paper_title: Nitric oxide acts as a cotransmitter in a subset of dopaminergic neurons to diversify memory dynamics
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson-mVenus) paired with odor
neural_activity: NOS immunoreactivity observed in terminals within the γ5 compartment; RNA-Seq identified NOS1 expression
behavioral_consequence: In wild-type flies, induces positive-valence (appetitive) olfactory memory; in TH mutant flies, induces negative-valence (aversive) memory (valence inversion); NOS inhibition blocks inverted memory
ecological_context: Involved in reward signaling (as cited in the paper context of DAN subsets)
summary: PAM-γ5/PAM-β'2a DANs express NOS and induce appetitive memory in wild-type flies, but aversive memory in dopamine-deficient flies, indicating NO acts antagonistically to dopamine.

paper_title: Nitric oxide acts as a cotransmitter in a subset of dopaminergic neurons to diversify memory dynamics
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation paired with odor
neural_activity: Low NOS transcript levels compared to PPL1-γ1pedc
behavioral_consequence: Induces negative-valence memory in both wild-type and TH mutant flies (no valence inversion observed); NOS inhibition does not affect memory
ecological_context: Part of parallel memory systems with distinct dynamics
summary: PPL1-γ2α'1 DANs do not exhibit NOS-dependent valence inversion, suggesting they use a different cotransmitter or mechanism compared to PPL1-γ1pedc.

paper_title: Nitric oxide acts as a cotransmitter in a subset of dopaminergic neurons to diversify memory dynamics
experimental_stimulus: Odor presentation; Optogenetic activation of paired DANs
neural_activity: Express soluble guanylate cyclase (sGC) subunits Gycα99B and Gycβ100B; Gycβ100B-EGFP fusion protein broadly expressed in MB lobes
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown of Gycβ100B in KCs abolishes NO-dependent memory (positive valence in TH mutants) without affecting dopamine-dependent memory (negative valence with L-DOPA rescue); KC activity is required for odor-specific memory
ecological_context: Represent sensory stimuli via sparse activity in mushroom body parallel axonal fibers for olfactory learning
summary: Kenyon cells express sGC required for NO-dependent memory. Knockdown of Gycβ100B in KCs blocks NO-dependent memory while sparing dopamine-dependent memory.

paper_title: Nitric oxide acts as a cotransmitter in a subset of dopaminergic neurons to diversify memory dynamics
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation of PPL1-γ1pedc DANs
neural_activity: Calcium imaging showed prolonged increase in calcium levels in response to DAN activation in TH-mutant background, suppressed by L-NNA
behavioral_consequence: Activation of individual MBONs can cause behavioral attraction or repulsion depending on compartment (cited from Aso et al., 2014b)
ecological_context: Use population code to govern behavior based on learned odor valence
summary: MBON-γ1pedc>α/β shows calcium response to PPL1-γ1pedc activation dependent on NOS signaling, linking DAN activity to output neuron modulation.

paper_title: Neurogenetic dissection of the Drosophila lateral horn reveals major outputs, diverse behavioural functions, and interactions with the mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (red light, Chrimson)
neural_activity: Cholinergic output neuron
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation drives aversion/avoidance behavior in a four-quadrant assay
ecological_context: Suggested to mediate ethologically-relevant innate avoidance behavior
summary: AV1a1 LHONs are cholinergic and optogenetic activation drives aversion behavior.

paper_title: Neurogenetic dissection of the Drosophila lateral horn reveals major outputs, diverse behavioural functions, and interactions with the mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (red light, Chrimson)
neural_activity: Glutamatergic local interneuron
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation drives aversion behavior
ecological_context: Part of the circuit mediating innate olfactory responses
summary: PV4a1:5 LHLNs are glutamatergic and optogenetic activation drives aversion behavior; they synaptically connect to AV1a1 dendrites.

paper_title: Neurogenetic dissection of the Drosophila lateral horn reveals major outputs, diverse behavioural functions, and interactions with the mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (red light, Chrimson)
neural_activity: Cholinergic output neuron integrating MBON input (axoaxonic and axodendritic)
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation drives approach/attraction behavior
ecological_context: Mediates innate approach behavior
summary: AD1b2 LHONs integrate MBON input and optogenetic activation drives approach behavior.

paper_title: Neurogenetic dissection of the Drosophila lateral horn reveals major outputs, diverse behavioural functions, and interactions with the mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (red light, Chrimson)
neural_activity: Output neuron
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation drives an increase in forward locomotion
ecological_context: Modulates locomotor programs during olfactory sensation
summary: AV6a1 LHON activation drives an increase in forward locomotion.

paper_title: Neurogenetic dissection of the Drosophila lateral horn reveals major outputs, diverse behavioural functions, and interactions with the mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation during flight
neural_activity: Bilateral cholinergic output neuron projecting to SLP
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation results in increased yaw turning via modulation of wingbeat amplitudes
ecological_context: Modulates flight maneuvers during olfactory sensation
summary: AD3b1 LHON activation increases yaw turning during flight.

paper_title: Neurogenetic dissection of the Drosophila lateral horn reveals major outputs, diverse behavioural functions, and interactions with the mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation during flight
neural_activity: Glutamatergic output neuron projecting to central complex
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation results in a substantial and persistent decrease in wingbeat frequency (flight speed)
ecological_context: Modulates flight speed, particularly with visual stimulation
summary: PV5k1 LHON activation decreases flight speed (wingbeat frequency).

paper_title: Neurogenetic dissection of the Drosophila lateral horn reveals major outputs, diverse behavioural functions, and interactions with the mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation during flight
neural_activity: Cholinergic output neuron integrating MBON input
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation leads to persistent increased wingbeat thrust (power)
ecological_context: Modulates flight power during olfactory sensation
summary: PD2a1/b1 LHON activation increases wingbeat thrust (power) during flight.

paper_title: Neurogenetic dissection of the Drosophila lateral horn reveals major outputs, diverse behavioural functions, and interactions with the mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: N/A (Anatomical/Physiological characterization)
neural_activity: Polarity (LHON, LHLN, LHIN) and neurotransmitters (ChAT, GABA, VGlut) determined
behavioral_consequence: N/A (Resource generation)
ecological_context: Provides genetic access to dissect innate olfactory circuits
summary: 82 LH cell types were mapped for polarity and neurotransmitter identity, providing a resource for functional dissection.

paper_title: Neuronal reactivation during post-learning sleep consolidates long-term memory in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Courtship training with mated females (6 hr for LTM; 1 hr for STM); Training with virgin females (control); Naive males.
neural_activity: Increased luminescence signal (Lola-LUC reporter) between 7-10 hr after onset of training with mated females in freely behaving males. Increased calcium levels (GCaMP6s) upon optogenetic activation of vFB neurons in explant brains.
behavioral_consequence: Silencing (shits) between 7-10 hr post-training abolished long-term memory (LTM). Artificial activation (csChrimson) between 5-7 hr after 1 hr training consolidated short-term memory (STM) to LTM. Required for memory acquisition (DopR1 mutants failed to sleep enhance or form LTM). Activation in naive males did not induce courtship suppression.
ecological_context: Courtship conditioning where males learn to avoid mated females after repeated rejections. Memory lasts for at least a day. Sleep enhancement occurs after learning experiences.
summary: DAN-aSP13 neurons show increased activity between 7-10 hr after LTM-inducing courtship training. Their reactivation during this window is necessary and sufficient for consolidating STM to LTM, mediated by vFB neuron input.

paper_title: Neuronal reactivation during post-learning sleep consolidates long-term memory in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson); Thermogenetic activation (TrpA1).
neural_activity: Induced sleep. Elicited excitatory calcium responses in DAN-aSP13 neurons (explant).
behavioral_consequence: Activation between 5-7 hr after 1 hr training consolidated STM to LTM. Silencing between 7-10 hr after 6 hr training impaired LTM consolidation. Robustly induced daytime sleep.
ecological_context: Mediates learning-dependent sleep after courtship experience. Distinct from homeostatic sleep regulation.
summary: vFB neurons promote sleep and provide excitatory input to DAN-aSP13 neurons. They are necessary and sufficient to consolidate courtship LTM during post-learning sleep.

paper_title: Neuronal reactivation during post-learning sleep consolidates long-term memory in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson); Thermogenetic activation (TrpA1).
neural_activity: Induced sleep. Did not elicit calcium changes in DAN-aSP13 neurons (R23E10 line).
behavioral_consequence: Activation induced sleep. Silencing between 7-10 hr after training did not affect LTM persistence. Activation did not consolidate STM to LTM.
ecological_context: Regulates homeostatic sleep (daytime and night time) distinct from learning-dependent sleep.
summary: dFB neurons promote sleep but are neither necessary nor sufficient for courtship LTM consolidation. They regulate homeostatic sleep distinct from vFB-mediated learning-dependent sleep.

paper_title: Neural circuit basis of aversive odour processing in Drosophila from sensory input to descending output
experimental_stimulus: Geosmin (1:1000 dilution)
neural_activity: Strong and highly selective responses to geosmin (In vivo electrophysiology); Cholinergic and excitatory
behavioral_consequence: Synaptic silencing (tetanus toxin) completely abolished avoidance behavior in egg-laying two-choice assay
ecological_context: Female Drosophila avoid geosmin produced by toxic moulds when laying eggs
techniques_used: ['In vivo electrophysiology', 'Tetanus toxin silencing', 'Egg-laying two-choice assay', 'Connectomics (EM)']
summary: DA2 PNs are necessary for geosmin sensing and egg-laying aversion, responding selectively to geosmin.

paper_title: Neural circuit basis of aversive odour processing in Drosophila from sensory input to descending output
experimental_stimulus: Geosmin (1:1000), Vinegar, 1-methylindole (1:1000 or 1:10000)
neural_activity: Calcium responses to geosmin (GCaMP3.0 imaging); No response to vinegar; Receive input from multiple aversive PNs (DA2, DL4, DA4l)
behavioral_consequence: Silencing abolishes geosmin and 1-methylindole egg-laying avoidance; Optogenetic activation triggers aversion in four-field assay
ecological_context: Processing aversive odours (toxic moulds and bacterial metabolites) for egg-laying decisions
techniques_used: ['In vivo calcium imaging', 'Tetanus toxin silencing', 'Optogenetic activation', 'Egg-laying two-choice assay', 'Connectomics (EM)']
summary: LHAV1a1 neurons integrate multiple aversive odour channels and are necessary and sufficient for triggering odour avoidance during egg-laying.

paper_title: Neural circuit basis of aversive odour processing in Drosophila from sensory input to descending output
experimental_stimulus: Endogenous geosmin pathway, Optogenetic stimulation
neural_activity: Strongly connected to LHAV1a1 axons; Innervate thoracic neuromeres
behavioral_consequence: Silencing abolishes geosmin avoidance; Optogenetic activation triggers consistent backing up and occasional take-off
ecological_context: Locomotor avoidance behavior to prevent infection or parasitism
summary: DNp42 neurons convey geosmin information from the lateral horn to the ventral nerve cord, where they drive backing up and take-off avoidance behaviors.

paper_title: A GABAergic Feedback Shapes Dopaminergic Input on the Drosophila Mushroom Body to Promote Appetitive Long-Term Memory
experimental_stimulus: Appetitive olfactory conditioning (odor paired with caloric sugar)
neural_activity: In vivo calcium imaging (GCaMP3) showed increased frequency of calcium oscillations after training, normally lasting less than 1 hour; knockdown of D-GABABR1 caused persistent high-frequency oscillations
behavioral_consequence: Inhibition immediately post-training (0-0.75 hr) impaired Long-Term Memory (LTM); Activation 0.5-1 hr post-training impaired LTM; D-GABABR1 knockdown impaired LTM
ecological_context: Consolidation of memory for nutritious food (sugar) reward in starved flies
techniques_used: ['In vivo calcium imaging', 'Thermogenetics (Shits, TrpA1)', 'RNAi', 'Olfactory T-maze behavior']
summary: MP1 neurons show post-training calcium oscillations necessary for LTM; their activity must be temporally restricted and terminated via GABAergic feedback to prevent memory defects.

paper_title: A GABAergic Feedback Shapes Dopaminergic Input on the Drosophila Mushroom Body to Promote Appetitive Long-Term Memory
experimental_stimulus: Appetitive olfactory conditioning (odor paired with caloric sugar)
neural_activity: Requires dopamine signaling via DAMB and dD2R receptors; activity required 0.5-1 hr post-training for LTM; activity immediately post-training (0-0.5 hr) is deleterious
behavioral_consequence: Blocking activity 0.5-1 hr post-training impaired LTM; Activation 0-0.5 hr post-training impaired LTM; Knockdown of DAMB or dD2R in MVP2 impaired LTM
ecological_context: Regulates the temporal window of dopaminergic signaling during food reward memory consolidation in starved flies
summary: MVP2 neurons provide inhibitory feedback to MP1; their activity is required 0.5-1 hr post-training to regulate MP1 oscillations for proper LTM formation.

paper_title: The Organization of Projections from Olfactory Glomeruli onto Higher-Order Neurons.
experimental_stimulus: Two-photon photostimulation of ReaChR in specific glomeruli; In vivo odor delivery (methyl acetate, ethyl hexanoate, methyl laurate, hexanoic acid)
neural_activity: Reliable spiking evoked by photostimulation (verified via whole-cell recording); Calcium transients evoked by odors (GCaMP6f imaging); Responses typically sparse and specific to home glomerulus
ecological_context: Transmit odor signals from antennae to higher brain regions (antennal lobe to lateral horn and mushroom body)
summary: PNs exhibit reliable spiking in response to glomerular photostimulation and calcium transients to specific odors, serving as the primary input to higher olfactory centers.

paper_title: The Organization of Projections from Olfactory Glomeruli onto Higher-Order Neurons.
experimental_stimulus: Photostimulation of specific glomeruli (via PN ReaChR); In vivo odor delivery (hexanoic acid, methyl laurate, methyl acetate, ethyl hexanoate)
neural_activity: Excitatory postsynaptic potentials (EPSPs) and inhibitory postsynaptic potentials (IPSPs) in response to glomerular stimulation; Voltage responses to odorants; Inputs predictably sum from connected glomeruli
ecological_context: Wiring connects glomeruli co-activated by salient odor scenes (e.g., pheromones for social behavior, esters/alcohols for food/yeast, terpenoids for egg-laying sites)
summary: 110 LHNs were classified into 39 morphology types; specific glomerular combinations are over-represented, suggesting a prediction of ecologically relevant odor scenes.

paper_title: The Organization of Projections from Olfactory Glomeruli onto Higher-Order Neurons.
experimental_stimulus: Photostimulation of specific glomeruli (via PN ReaChR)
neural_activity: Regularly spaced EPSPs indicating input from a single PN per glomerulus; Sparse and independent glomerular inputs compared to LHNs
ecological_context: Unbiased sampling of glomerular combinations to maximize diversity of odor representations
summary: KC responses consist of regularly spaced EPSPs, indicating single-PN input per glomerulus, contrasting with the convergent, patterned connectivity of LHNs.

paper_title: Functional architecture of reward learning in mushroom body extrinsic neurons of larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Odor (n-amylacetate) + Fructose (associative learning task); Optogenetic activation (ChR2-XXL, Chrimson)
neural_activity: Not recorded; functional manipulation via silencing (Kir2.1::GFP) or optogenetic activation
behavioral_consequence: Silencing partially impaired odor-FRU association but not odor-ASP or odor-lowsalt association; did not affect innate odor/FRU preference. Optogenetic activation sufficient as internal reward signal for appetitive memory
ecological_context: Mediates reward processing for specific tastants (fructose) in associative learning for tracking food sources
summary: DAN-h1 is required for odor-fructose associative learning and sufficient to mediate an internal reward signal, but dispensable for amino-acid or low-salt reward learning.

paper_title: Functional architecture of reward learning in mushroom body extrinsic neurons of larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (ChR2-XXL, Chrimson) paired with odor
neural_activity: Not recorded; functional manipulation via optogenetic activation
