behavioral_consequence: Activation sufficient for internal reward signal (forward pairing). Timing-dependent valence reversal observed (backward pairing induced aversive memory). Silencing did not impair learning for known taste rewards
ecological_context: Enables timing-dependent valence learning (reward onset vs. termination) for adaptive behavior
summary: DAN-i1 activation is sufficient for reward learning with timing-dependent valence reversal, but its activity is not required for known taste reward association.

paper_title: Functional architecture of reward learning in mushroom body extrinsic neurons of larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Silencing (Kir2.1::GFP)
neural_activity: Not recorded; functional manipulation via silencing
behavioral_consequence: Impaired innate odor preference AND innate fructose preference; did not impair odor-FRU association
ecological_context: Mediates innate preference behaviors for odors and tastes
summary: DAN-k1 activity is required for innate odor and fructose preference but not for odor-fructose associative learning.

paper_title: Functional architecture of reward learning in mushroom body extrinsic neurons of larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Silencing (Kir2.1::GFP)
neural_activity: Not recorded; functional manipulation via silencing
behavioral_consequence: Did not impair association or preference tasks; rendered olfactory behavior more susceptible to non-associative effects of odor-only or FRU-only exposure
ecological_context: Modulates susceptibility to non-associative sensory exposure effects
summary: DAN-g1 is not required for associative learning or preferences but modulates susceptibility to non-associative sensory effects.

paper_title: Functional architecture of reward learning in mushroom body extrinsic neurons of larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Silencing (Kir2.1::GFP)
neural_activity: Not recorded; functional manipulation via silencing
behavioral_consequence: Silencing impaired odor-FRU association; did not affect odor preference or fructose preference
ecological_context: Outputs learned valence signals for associative memory
summary: MBON-j2 is required for odor-fructose associative learning but not for innate preferences.

paper_title: Functional architecture of reward learning in mushroom body extrinsic neurons of larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Silencing (Kir2.1::GFP)
neural_activity: Not recorded; functional manipulation via silencing
behavioral_consequence: Silencing impaired odor-FRU association; did not affect odor preference or fructose preference
ecological_context: Outputs learned valence signals for associative memory
summary: MBON-g1,g2 are required for odor-fructose associative learning but not for innate preferences.

paper_title: Functional architecture of reward learning in mushroom body extrinsic neurons of larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Silencing (Kir2.1::GFP) or Optogenetic activation (ChR2-XXL)
neural_activity: Not recorded; functional manipulation via silencing or activation
behavioral_consequence: Silencing or activation impaired odor-FRU association; did not affect innate odor or fructose preference. Silencing impaired ability to discriminate olfactory foreground from background
ecological_context: Maintains sparse Kenyon cell activity pattern for discriminating olfactory stimuli from contextual background
summary: APL neuron is required for odor-fructose associative learning by maintaining sparse KC activity for olfactory discrimination.

paper_title: Persistent activity in a recurrent circuit underlies courtship memory in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (TrpA1), optogenetic activation (SFOCatCh), courtship training with mated females
neural_activity: Repetitive optogenetic stimulation potentiates synaptic transmission to M6 neurons (observed via calcium imaging in M6 dendrites). Activation suppresses courtship more potently in experienced males than naïve males.
behavioral_consequence: Silencing with tetanus toxin prevents courtship suppression (memory) after training. Thermogenetic activation suppresses courtship in experienced males.
ecological_context: Courtship conditioning: learning to avoid mated females after rejection to conserve reproductive effort.
summary: MBγ Kenyon cells form a recurrent circuit with M6 and aSP13 neurons. Their output to M6 is potentiated by dopamine and experience, mediating courtship suppression memory.

paper_title: Persistent activity in a recurrent circuit underlies courtship memory in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (TrpA1), optogenetic activation (SFOCatCh), dopamine perfusion
neural_activity: Calcium response in dendrites is initially weak but potentiated by exogenous dopamine and repetitive MBγ stimulation. Activation elicits a calcium response in aSP13 neurons (glutamatergic).
behavioral_consequence: Silencing prevents courtship suppression during both training and testing. Optogenetic/thermogenetic activation suppresses courtship with equal potency in naïve and experienced males.
ecological_context: Courtship conditioning: M6 output suppresses courtship behavior, allowing experienced males to avoid mated females.
summary: M6 neurons provide glutamatergic input to aSP13 neurons and are required for both memory acquisition and retrieval. Their synapses with MBγ are potentiated by dopamine during training.

paper_title: Persistent activity in a recurrent circuit underlies courtship memory in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Repetitive MBγ optogenetic activation, M6 optogenetic activation, courtship training
neural_activity: Exhibit persistent calcium response lasting ~2 minutes after M6 or MBγ activation. Response depends on glutamatergic transmission (blocked by AP-5) and cholinergic/dopaminergic inputs.
behavioral_consequence: Activity is required during the training period for memory acquisition. Persistent activity supports short-term memory formation.
ecological_context: Courtship conditioning: Persistent aSP13 activity within the recurrent circuit underlies short-term memory of rejected courtship.
summary: aSP13 dopaminergic neurons show persistent activity following recurrent circuit activation, facilitating synaptic potentiation at MBγ>M6 synapses for short-term memory.

paper_title: Neural circuits for long-term water-reward memory processing in thirsty Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Water deprivation (16 h) paired with odour and water reward in T-maze
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (UAS-GCaMP6) showed robust increase in fluorescence in b01 region upon water intake in thirsty flies
behavioral_consequence: Silencing output during conditioning impaired 24-h long-term memory (LTM) but not short-term memory (STM); activation (UAS-TrpA1) with odour induced LTM but not learning or STM
ecological_context: Thirsty flies associate drinking water with odour for survival
summary: PAM-b01 neurons respond to water intake in thirsty flies and are required for water-reward LTM reinforcement; artificial activation induces LTM.

paper_title: Neural circuits for long-term water-reward memory processing in thirsty Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Water deprivation (16 h) paired with odour and water reward in T-maze
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (UAS-GCaMP6) showed response to water intake in γ region
behavioral_consequence: Silencing output during conditioning impaired STM (3-min/3-h) but not LTM (24-h); activation with odour induced learning and STM but not LTM
ecological_context: Thirsty flies associate drinking water with odour for survival
summary: PAM-γ4 neurons respond to water intake and reinforce STM and learning, but are not required for water-reward LTM.

paper_title: Neural circuits for long-term water-reward memory processing in thirsty Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Water reward conditioning (Odour + Water)
neural_activity: DopR1 receptor expression required; output manipulation tested
behavioral_consequence: Silencing output during consolidation (8 h post-training) impaired LTM (24-h); no effect during acquisition or retrieval; DopR1 expression restored LTM in dumb2 mutants
ecological_context: Thirsty flies associate drinking water with odour for survival
summary: MB α'β' neuron output is required for consolidation of water-reward LTM, dependent on DopR1.

paper_title: Neural circuits for long-term water-reward memory processing in thirsty Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Water reward conditioning (Odour + Water)
neural_activity: Output manipulation tested
behavioral_consequence: Silencing output during retrieval impaired LTM (24-h); no effect during acquisition or consolidation
ecological_context: Thirsty flies associate drinking water with odour for survival
summary: MB γ neuron output is required for retrieval of water-reward LTM.

paper_title: Neural circuits for long-term water-reward memory processing in thirsty Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Water reward conditioning (Odour + Water)
neural_activity: Output manipulation tested
behavioral_consequence: Silencing output during retrieval impaired LTM (24-h); no effect during acquisition or consolidation
ecological_context: Thirsty flies associate drinking water with odour for survival
summary: MB αβ neuron output is required for retrieval of water-reward LTM.

paper_title: Neural circuits for long-term water-reward memory processing in thirsty Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Memory retrieval phase (Testing)
neural_activity: Output manipulation tested; VGlut knockdown tested
behavioral_consequence: Silencing output during retrieval impaired LTM; VGlut knockdown impaired LTM (glutamatergic transmission required)
ecological_context: Thirsty flies associate drinking water with odour for survival
summary: MB-M6 neurons retrieve water-reward LTM from γ neurons via glutamatergic transmission.

paper_title: Neural circuits for long-term water-reward memory processing in thirsty Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Memory retrieval phase (Testing)
neural_activity: Output manipulation tested; ChAT knockdown tested
behavioral_consequence: Silencing output during retrieval impaired LTM; ChAT knockdown impaired LTM (cholinergic transmission required)
ecological_context: Thirsty flies associate drinking water with odour for survival
summary: MB-V3 neurons retrieve water-reward LTM from αβ neurons via cholinergic transmission.

paper_title: Re-evaluation of learned information in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: CS+ odor reactivation 3 h after sugar-rewarded training
neural_activity: Synaptic release blocked using uas-shits1 at 33°C; no change in odor-evoked responses noted in trained flies
behavioral_consequence: Blocking output did not impair extinction of appetitive memory; retained appetitive memory when retraining while blocked
ecological_context: Animals constantly reassess the reliability of learned information to optimize their behavior
summary: Rewarding PAM cluster DANs are not required for extinction of appetitive memory; their output is not necessary for extinction processes triggered by CS+ reactivation.

paper_title: Re-evaluation of learned information in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: CS+ odor reactivation (extinction) or CS- odor reactivation (reconsolidation) 3 h after training
neural_activity: Synaptic release blocked using TH-GAL4 or MB504B-GAL4 driven uas-shits1
behavioral_consequence: Blocking compromised CS+ driven extinction; blocking during CS- exposure reduced 6 h memory performance
ecological_context: Animals constantly reassess the reliability of learned information to optimize their behavior
summary: Aversive PPL1 DANs are required for both extinction of reward memory (via CS+ exposure) and reconsolidation of reward memory (via CS- exposure).

paper_title: Re-evaluation of learned information in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: CS- odor reactivation 3 h after training
neural_activity: Synaptic release blocked using MB296B-GAL4 driven uas-shits1; evoked calcium response when γ2α'1 MBONs activated (GCaMP6f)
behavioral_consequence: Blocking during CS- reactivation abolished 6 h memory (reconsolidation); dispensable for 3 h memory expression
ecological_context: Animals constantly reassess the reliability of learned information to optimize their behavior
techniques_used: ['Genetic silencing (uas-shits1)', 'Behavioral assay (T-maze odor preference)', 'Two-photon calcium imaging (GCaMP6f)']
summary: MB-MV1 DANs are required for CS- triggered memory reconsolidation and are activated by γ2α'1 MBON output, but not for initial memory expression or extinction.

paper_title: Re-evaluation of learned information in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: CS+ odor reactivation 3 h after training
neural_activity: Synaptic release blocked using uas-shits1; light-triggered activation evoked calcium response in PPL1 DANs (GCaMP6f)
behavioral_consequence: Impaired CS+ driven extinction; not involved in CS- reconsolidation; dispensable for 3 h memory expression
ecological_context: Animals constantly reassess the reliability of learned information to optimize their behavior
techniques_used: ['Genetic silencing (uas-shits1)', 'Behavioral assay (T-maze odor preference)', 'Optogenetics and calcium imaging (CsChrimson/GCaMP6f)']
summary: V2 MBONs drive aversively reinforcing DANs to mediate extinction of reward memory upon CS+ reactivation, without affecting reconsolidation or initial memory.

paper_title: Re-evaluation of learned information in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: CS- odor reactivation 3 h after training
neural_activity: Synaptic release blocked using uas-shits1; CS- odor-evoked calcium responses significantly increased after training; evoked calcium responses in MB-MV1 and rewarding DANs upon activation
behavioral_consequence: Blocking during CS- impaired reconsolidation; required for 3 h memory-guided approach behavior
ecological_context: Animals constantly reassess the reliability of learned information to optimize their behavior
techniques_used: ['Genetic silencing (uas-shits1)', 'Behavioral assay (T-maze odor preference)', 'Two-photon functional calcium imaging', 'Optogenetics and calcium imaging (CsChrimson/GCaMP6f)']
summary: γ2α'1 MBONs direct memory-guided behavior and orchestrate CS- triggered reconsolidation by recruiting MB-MV1 and rewarding DANs.

paper_title: Suppression of Dopamine Neurons Mediates Reward
experimental_stimulus: Sugar ingestion (sucrose), Odor pairing (OCT/MCH) with thermoactivation or inhibition, Electric shock
neural_activity: Baseline activity fluctuates without stimulation. Sugar ingestion immediately silences/suppresses baseline activity (measured via two-photon Ca2+ imaging with GCaMP5). Thermoactivation (dTrpA1) depolarizes neurons. Transient blockade (shits1) or light-induced inhibition (eNpHR) suppresses output. Knockdown of DAR-1 attenuates sugar-induced suppression of activity.
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces robust aversive memory (odor avoidance). Blockade impairs sugar-rewarded appetitive memory. Transient inactivation during odor presentation substitutes for reward and induces appetitive memory. Blockade does not impair electric shock-induced aversive memory or innate sucrose preference.
ecological_context: Mediates 'natural reward' processing. Activity suppression is linked to sugar ingestion and satiety signaling via Allatostatin A input.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon Ca2+ imaging', 'Thermogenetics (dTrpA1, shits1)', 'Optogenetics (eNpHR)', 'T-maze olfactory learning assay', 'Sucrose preference assay', 'GRASP synaptic contact assay']
summary: PAM-γ3 dopamine neurons mediate reward signaling by suppressing baseline activity upon sugar ingestion. Activation induces aversive memory, while transient inactivation induces appetitive memory; they are selectively required for sugar reward but not shock punishment.

paper_title: Suppression of Dopamine Neurons Mediates Reward
experimental_stimulus: Thermoactivation, Sugar ingestion during learning (sucrose and arabinose)
neural_activity: Neurons project to PAM-γ3 dendrites (confirmed via GRASP). Activity changes were not directly imaged in this study, but they are identified as a source of inhibitory input to PAM-γ3.
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces immediate appetitive odor memory. Blockade (shits1) or genetic loss (null alleles/RNAi) impairs appetitive memory for sucrose and nonnutritive sugar arabinose. Innate sugar preference remains intact.
ecological_context: AstA is a 'satiety-signaling neuropeptide' that signals satiation and conveys inhibitory input to PAM-γ3 upon ingestion of rewarding substances.
techniques_used: ['Thermogenetics (dTrpA1, shits1)', 'CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis', 'RNA interference (RNAi)', 'T-maze olfactory learning assay', 'Sucrose preference assay']
summary: AstA neurons mediate the reinforcement property of sugar reward. Their activation is sufficient for appetitive memory, and they are necessary for sugar learning, likely by inhibiting PAM-γ3 dopamine neurons.

paper_title: Control of Sleep by Dopaminergic Inputs to the Drosophila Mushroom Body
cell_type: PAM Dopaminergic Neurons (targeting γ5 and β′2 compartments)
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTRPA1, 21.5°C to 28.5°C); Pharmacogenetic activation (P2X2 channel + ATP application); Silencing (Kir2.1 K+ channel)
neural_activity: Activation induces robust transient Ca2+ increases in dendrites of MBON-γ5β′2a/β′2mp/β′2mp_bilateral (measured via GCaMP6m imaging); Response mediated by D1 dopamine receptors (blocked by SCH23390)
behavioral_consequence: Activation suppresses sleep (increases wakefulness); Silencing increases total sleep
ecological_context: Regulation of sleep-wake states and homeostatic rebound; Fly sleep exhibits circadian regulation and features conserved with vertebrate sleep
summary: PAM DANs projecting to γ5 and β′2 compartments are wake-promoting; their activation increases MBON-γ5β′2 Ca2+ levels via D1 receptors and suppresses sleep.

paper_title: Control of Sleep by Dopaminergic Inputs to the Drosophila Mushroom Body
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTRPA1, MB312B split-GAL4 driver)
neural_activity: Axon terminals project to γ4 compartment containing dendrites of MBON-γ4 > γ1γ2
behavioral_consequence: Activation suppresses sleep (increases wakefulness)
ecological_context: Regulation of sleep-wake states and homeostatic rebound
summary: PAM DANs projecting to the γ4 compartment promote wakefulness and suppress sleep.

paper_title: Control of Sleep by Dopaminergic Inputs to the Drosophila Mushroom Body
cell_type: PPL1 Dopaminergic Neurons (targeting γ2 and α′1 compartments)
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTRPA1, MB439B, MB296B split-GAL4 drivers)
neural_activity: Axons project to compartments innervated by sleep-promoting MBON-γ2α′1
behavioral_consequence: Activation does not significantly affect sleep (Figure 1B)
ecological_context: Regulation of sleep-wake states
summary: Thermogenetic activation of specific PPL1 DANs targeting γ2/α′1 compartments does not significantly alter sleep, contrasting with PAM DAN effects.

paper_title: Control of Sleep by Dopaminergic Inputs to the Drosophila Mushroom Body
experimental_stimulus: Indirect activation via ATP-mediated PAM DAN activation
neural_activity: Robust transient Ca2+ increases in dendrites (γ5 and β′2 compartments) measured via GCaMP6m; Response blocked by D1 antagonist (SCH23390)
behavioral_consequence: Associated with wake-promoting circuitry (inferred from DAN activation results)
ecological_context: Sleep-wake regulation and homeostatic rebound
summary: MBON-γ5β′2a/β′2mp/β′2mp_bilateral dendrites receive strong dopaminergic input from wake-promoting PAM DANs via D1 receptors.

paper_title: Control of Sleep by Dopaminergic Inputs to the Drosophila Mushroom Body
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTRPA1, MB434B split-GAL4 driver)
neural_activity: Not directly recorded in this study; activity inferred from DAN targeting
behavioral_consequence: Activation suppresses sleep (increases wakefulness)
ecological_context: Sleep-wake regulation and homeostatic rebound
summary: Direct activation of MBON-γ4 > γ1γ2 neurons suppresses sleep and promotes wakefulness.

paper_title: Control of Sleep by Dopaminergic Inputs to the Drosophila Mushroom Body
experimental_stimulus: Indirect activation via ATP-mediated PAM DAN activation
neural_activity: Ca2+ increases observed, but significantly weaker than MBON-γ5β′2 in response to PAM DAN activation
behavioral_consequence: Described as sleep-promoting in the context of the study (cited from Sitaraman et al., 2015); not directly tested for behavior in this study
ecological_context: Sleep-wake regulation and homeostatic rebound
summary: MBON-γ2α′1 shows significantly lower Ca2+ response to wake-promoting PAM DAN activation compared to wake-promoting MBON-γ5β′2.

paper_title: Activity of Defined Mushroom Body Output Neurons Underlies Learned Olfactory Behavior in Drosophila
cell_type: M4/6 Mushroom Body Output Neurons (M4b/MBON-b2b02a, M4b0/MBON-b02mp, M6/MBON-g5b02a)
experimental_stimulus: Odors (Methylcyclohexanol, Octanol), Sugar reward, Electric shock punishment, Red light (optogenetic activation)
neural_activity: Two-photon functional calcium imaging showed odor-evoked activity in dendrites (b0 lobe) and presynaptic boutons. Appetitive conditioning bidirectionally decreased relative odor drive to CS+ vs CS-; aversive conditioning increased relative odor drive to CS+ vs CS-.
behavioral_consequence: Blocking output impaired appetitive and aversive memory expression. Blocking in naive flies converted odor avoidance into approach (M4b0/M6); M6 alone blunted aversion without reversal. Optogenetic activation induced avoidance behavior.
ecological_context: Innate behavioral responses to odors are redirected toward approach or avoidance by learning (sugar or shock). Naive flies exhibit innate avoidance of high odor concentrations.
techniques_used: ['Immunostaining (DVGlut, DenMark, Syd-1)', 'Two-photon functional calcium imaging', 'Temperature-sensitive shibirets1 blockade', 'Optogenetics (ReaChR)', 'T-maze memory assays', 'T-maze naive odor choice']
summary: M4/6 glutamatergic mushroom body output neurons are required for memory expression. Conditioning bidirectionally alters odor drive to M4b0 neurons. Blocking them mimics appetitive conditioning (avoidance to approach), while activation induces avoidance.

paper_title: Gustatory Learning and Processing in the Drosophila Mushroom Bodies
experimental_stimulus: Aversive taste conditioning: 500 mM sucrose (CS) on tarsi paired with 50 mM quinine (US) on proboscis
neural_activity: Synaptic output blocked using temperature-sensitive shibire (shi ts) in gamma lobe neurons
behavioral_consequence: Blocking gamma lobe output completely abolished conditioned aversion (short-term memory). Alpha/beta lobe output block had no effect.
ecological_context: Modifies innate feeding behavior (proboscis extension) when a taste cue is associated with a noxious stimulus
summary: Gamma lobe Kenyon cell output is required for the formation of short-term aversive taste memory.

paper_title: Gustatory Learning and Processing in the Drosophila Mushroom Bodies
experimental_stimulus: Bitter taste (caffeine/denatonium) on proboscis, Sugar (sucrose) on proboscis, Noxious heat shock to abdomen, Aversive taste conditioning paradigm
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (GCaMP6s) revealed strong responses to bitter stimulation and heat shock, but no response to sugar stimulation
behavioral_consequence: Silencing PPL1 neurons abolished aversive taste memory. Silencing PAM dopaminergic neurons had no effect
ecological_context: Signals aversive punishment (bitter, heat) to modify behavior based on negative experience
summary: PPL1 dopaminergic neurons encode bitter and heat aversive signals and are required for aversive taste memory formation.

paper_title: Gustatory Learning and Processing in the Drosophila Mushroom Bodies
cell_type: Mushroom Body Kenyon Cell Dendrites (Main Calyx)
experimental_stimulus: Tastants (Sucrose, Quinine, Fructose, Glucose, Water) presented to tarsi or proboscis; Odors activated via Orco-dTRPA1 heat activation
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (GCaMP5) showed sparse, taste-specific, and organ-specific activation in claws. Different modalities activated different claws; same modality activated overlapping claws. Some KCs responded to both odors and tastes
behavioral_consequence: Conditioned taste aversion was leg-specific (associations formed only for the trained leg)
ecological_context: Allows taste information to be contextualized as sensory cues (CS) rather than just rewards/punishments
summary: Taste inputs to the main calyx are sparse and organized by modality and organ, enabling independent taste associations.

paper_title: Gustatory Learning and Processing in the Drosophila Mushroom Bodies
experimental_stimulus: Bitter taste, Sugar taste, Noxious heat shock
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (GCaMP6s) showed MB-MP1 neurons respond to bitter but not heat; gamma/prime tip neurons respond to heat but not bitter
behavioral_consequence: None measured directly
ecological_context: Suggests PPL1 encodes a general aversive signal, with individual neurons having specific response properties
summary: Subsets of PPL1 neurons show distinct response properties to different aversive stimuli (bitter vs heat).

paper_title: Gustatory Learning and Processing in the Drosophila Mushroom Bodies
experimental_stimulus: Tastants (Sucrose, Quinine)
neural_activity: Calcium imaging revealed activation by tastants but not olfactory stimulation
behavioral_consequence: Silencing dorsal accessory calyx neurons did not affect aversive taste conditioning
ecological_context: Suggests compartmentalization of sensory inputs into the mushroom body
summary: The dorsal accessory calyx receives gustatory inputs distinct from olfactory cues but is not required for taste conditioning.

paper_title: Gustatory Learning and Processing in the Drosophila Mushroom Bodies
experimental_stimulus: Aversive taste conditioning paradigm
neural_activity: Synaptic output blocked using temperature-sensitive shibire (shi ts)
behavioral_consequence: No memory deficit observed when 5-HT or OA neurons were silenced
ecological_context: N/A
summary: Serotonergic and octopaminergic neurons are not essential for aversive taste memory formation.

paper_title: Sweet Taste and Nutrient Value Subdivide Rewarding Dopaminergic Neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Sweet taste (sucrose, arabinose), Odor + dTrpA1 activation
neural_activity: Not directly measured; activity blocked (UAS-shits1) or activated (UAS-dTrpA1). Identified by R48B04-GAL4 expression in ~55 neurons projecting to beta02 and gamma4 mushroom body regions.
behavioral_consequence: Blocking output impairs Short-Term Memory (STM) reinforced by sweet/non-nutritious arabinose or sucrose. Artificial activation implants STM in both starved and fed flies. Artificial STM is labile (does not persist to 24 hr). Does not affect Long-Term Memory (LTM).
ecological_context: Sweet taste reinforcement is hunger-state independent, allowing immediate recognition of palatable food regardless of internal state.
summary: oambP-GAL4 dopaminergic neurons (beta02am, gamma4) convey sweet taste reinforcement for STM independently of hunger state.

paper_title: Sweet Taste and Nutrient Value Subdivide Rewarding Dopaminergic Neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Nutritious sucrose (saturated), Odor + dTrpA1 activation
neural_activity: Not directly measured; subset identified by intersection with oambP-GAL80 (projecting to gamma5b and beta02m).
behavioral_consequence: Required for natural nutrient-dependent LTM (blocking impairs 24-hr memory). Artificial activation forms LTM only if flies are hungry (satiated flies do not form LTM). Food-relevant memory is suppressed by feeding after training.
ecological_context: Nutrient-dependent memory requires a hungry internal state, ensuring reinforcement of nutrient value only when energy is needed.
summary: 0104-GAL4 neurons projecting to gamma5b are required for nutrient-dependent LTM and require hunger for formation.

paper_title: Sweet Taste and Nutrient Value Subdivide Rewarding Dopaminergic Neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Odor + dTrpA1 activation (artificial reinforcement)
neural_activity: Not directly measured; identified by GAL4 expression patterns (projecting to alpha1, beta1, beta2).
behavioral_consequence: Artificial activation forms LTM. 0273 neurons form LTM in satiated flies (hunger independent) and memory persists after feeding (food independent). 0104, R15A04, 0804, R87D06 neurons require hunger to form LTM and memory is suppressed by feeding (food relevant). 0279 neurons require hunger to form LTM but memory persists after feeding (food independent).
ecological_context: Some dopaminergic neurons encode general reward (0273), while others encode food-specific reward (0104, etc.). Hunger state gates food-relevant memory formation.
summary: Dopaminergic neurons projecting to alpha1, beta1, beta2 are sufficient for artificial LTM; 0273 is hunger-independent, while others (0104, 0279, etc.) are hunger-dependent.

paper_title: Sweet Taste and Nutrient Value Subdivide Rewarding Dopaminergic Neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Sucrose (saturated), Odor conditioning
neural_activity: Lack octopamine (TbhM18 mutant).
behavioral_consequence: Defective STM with 2M sucrose, but LTM (30 min, 3 hr, 24 hr) with saturated sucrose is intact. Demonstrates nutrient-dependent memory is octopamine-independent.
ecological_context: Separates sweet taste signaling (octopamine-dependent) from nutrient value signaling (octopamine-independent) in learning.
summary: TbhM18 mutants lack sweet-taste STM but retain nutrient-dependent LTM, indicating separate pathways for taste and nutrient reinforcement.

paper_title: A single pair of neurons links sleep to memory consolidation in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Acute temperature shift (22°C to 31°C) activating dTrpA1; Temperature shift (18°C to 31°C) activating Shibirets (Shits) or Kir2.1; Bath application of ATP to activate P2X2 receptors; RNAi knockdown of VGAT or Trh.
neural_activity: dTrpA1 activation evokes increases in intracellular calcium, membrane voltage, and vesicle fusion in DPM projections. P2X2-mediated activation causes DPMs to release neurotransmitter. Functional imaging with SuperClomeleon shows DPM activation evokes robust increases in chloride in MB neurons, but no detectable increases in calcium or cAMP. DPMs stain positive for GABA (VGAT, Gad1) and 5HT, but not ChAT or TH.
behavioral_consequence: Acute activation produces immediate and dramatic increases in sleep (both nighttime and daytime). Inhibition of synaptic release, electrical activity, or neurotransmitter synthesis results in decreased nighttime sleep. Sleep loss is not due to locomotor impairment (flies exhibit normal levels of nighttime activity while awake).
ecological_context: Activity is normally recruited following stimulus-rich experiences such as learning; DPMs are active in the first 0–3 hr post-training memory consolidation period.
techniques_used: ['Temperature-shift activation/inhibition (dTrpA1, Shits, Kir2.1)', 'RNAi knockdown', 'Immunohistochemistry (GABA, 5HT, Gad1, VGAT)', 'Functional fluorescence imaging (GCaMP, SuperClomeleon, EPAC, Arclight, SpH)', 'Sleep monitoring (DAM system)']
summary: DPM neurons are sleep-promoting inhibitory neurons that release GABA and 5HT to inhibit MB neurons, with activity normally required during memory consolidation.

paper_title: A single pair of neurons links sleep to memory consolidation in Drosophila melanogaster
cell_type: MB α'/β' neurons (Mushroom Body α'/β' neurons)
experimental_stimulus: Acute temperature shift (22°C to 31°C) activating dTrpA1; RNAi knockdown of GABA receptors (Rdl, GABABR3).
neural_activity: Identified as postsynaptic targets of DPMs. DPM activation evokes chloride increases in these neurons. MB neurons do not respond to DPM activation with calcium or cAMP increases.
behavioral_consequence: Acute activation results in strong decrease in nighttime sleep (wake-promoting phenotype). Knockdown of GABA receptors (Rdl, GABABR3) results in decreased nighttime sleep and fragmentation.
ecological_context: Activity is required during the 0–3 hr memory consolidation period for storage of 24 hr long-term memory; MBs are implicated in attention and arousal.
techniques_used: ['Temperature-shift activation (dTrpA1)', 'RNAi knockdown (Rdl, GABABR3)', 'Sleep monitoring (DAM system)', 'Functional imaging (SuperClomeleon)']
summary: MB α'/β' neurons are wake-promoting postsynaptic targets of DPM-mediated inhibition and are required for memory consolidation.

paper_title: A single pair of neurons links sleep to memory consolidation in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: RNAi knockdown of VGAT.
neural_activity: Not directly measured for activity changes in this study; known to be gap junction coupled to DPMs.
behavioral_consequence: Expression of VGAT-RNAi results in weak nighttime sleep loss, sometimes accompanied by an increase in activity while awake (distinct from DPM RNAi phenotype).
ecological_context: Coupled to DPMs via gap junctions and densely innervate MBs.
summary: APL neurons show weak sleep-promoting effects distinct from DPMs, with RNAi causing weak sleep loss and increased wake activity.

paper_title: Thermosensory processing in the Drosophila brain
experimental_stimulus: Temperature steps (cooling and warming, ~10°C changes; fast ~100 ms and slow ~7 s; small steps <1°C)
neural_activity: Excited by cooling, inhibited by warming. Strong adaptation to sustained cooling. Respond better to fast cooling than slow cooling. Highly sensitive to small cooling steps.
ecological_context: Receives input primarily from cool thermoreceptor cells in the arista of the antenna. Projects to the lateral protocerebrum.
techniques_used: ['In vivo whole-cell current-clamp recordings', 'Gal4-driven GFP expression', 'Arista clipping']
summary: Fast-cool-PNs are projection neurons excited by cooling (fast steps preferred) and inhibited by warming, receiving input from arista cool cells.

paper_title: Thermosensory processing in the Drosophila brain
experimental_stimulus: Temperature steps (cooling and warming)
neural_activity: Excited by cooling, inhibited by warming. Little adaptation to sustained cooling. Fast and slow cooling elicit similar peak firing rates. Less sensitive to small cooling steps than fast-cool-PNs.
ecological_context: Receives input mainly from cool thermoreceptor cells in the sacculus. Projects to the mushroom body.
techniques_used: ['In vivo whole-cell current-clamp recordings', 'Gal4-driven GFP expression', 'Arista clipping']
summary: Slow-cool-PNs are projection neurons excited by cooling with little adaptation, receiving input from sacculus cool cells.

paper_title: Thermosensory processing in the Drosophila brain
experimental_stimulus: Temperature steps (cooling and warming)
neural_activity: Excited by warming, inhibited by cooling. Respond better to fast warming than slow warming. Show adaptation to prolonged increases. Receive input from both warm and cool pathways.
ecological_context: Projects to the lateral protocerebrum. Crossover inhibition from cool pathway may cancel non-thermal noise.
techniques_used: ['In vivo whole-cell current-clamp recordings', 'Gr28b.d mutant analysis', 'Histamine iontophoresis (silencing warm thermoreceptors)', 'Synaptic inhibition blocking']
summary: Warm-PNs are excited by warming via a warm pathway and disinhibition from cool thermoreceptors, inhibited by cooling.

paper_title: Thermosensory processing in the Drosophila brain
experimental_stimulus: Temperature steps (cooling and warming)
neural_activity: Excited by both cooling and warming. Respond to fast but not slow changes. Strong adaptation. Signal rapid temperature shifts regardless of direction.
ecological_context: Projects to the lateral protocerebrum. Receive input from both warm and cool receptor cells.
techniques_used: ['In vivo whole-cell current-clamp recordings', 'Gr28b.d mutant analysis', 'Histamine iontophoresis']
summary: Warm-cool-PNs signal rapid temperature shifts regardless of direction, receiving input from both warm and cool pathways.

paper_title: Thermosensory processing in the Drosophila brain
experimental_stimulus: Temperature steps (cooling and warming)
neural_activity: Tonically active at room temperature. Excited by cooling, inhibited by warming. Found in GABAergic and glutamatergic populations. Provide crossover inhibition to PNs.
ecological_context: Local neurons in the proximal antennal protocerebrum. Mediate disinhibition of warm-PNs during warming.
techniques_used: ['In vivo whole-cell current-clamp recordings', 'Immunohistochemistry (GABA/dVGluT)', 'Gal4-driven GFP expression']
summary: Cool-LNs are inhibitory local neurons tonically active, excited by cooling, and inhibited by warming.

paper_title: Thermosensory processing in the Drosophila brain
experimental_stimulus: Temperature changes
neural_activity: Excited by warming, inhibited by cooling. Express Gr28b.d receptor.
ecological_context: Reside in the arista of the antenna. Provide input to warm-PNs.
summary: Peripheral cells in the arista excited by warming and inhibited by cooling.

paper_title: Thermosensory processing in the Drosophila brain
experimental_stimulus: Temperature changes
neural_activity: Excited by cooling, inhibited by warming. Reside in arista and sacculus.
ecological_context: Peripheral cells in the antenna. Input to cool-PNs and source of crossover inhibition to warm-PNs.
summary: Peripheral cells in the antenna excited by cooling and inhibited by warming.

paper_title: Shared mushroom body circuits underlie visual and olfactory memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual conditioning with sugar reward (appetitive) and electric shock punishment (aversive)
neural_activity: Blocked continuously using temperature-sensitive shibire (shits1)
behavioral_consequence: No significant defect in sucrose reward or shock punishment memory; octopamine blockade did not impair visual memory acquisition
ecological_context: Associated with associative memories allowing animals to predict upcoming events and choose appropriate behavior when encountering reward or harm
summary: TDC2-GAL4 labeled octopamine/tyramine neurons are not required for appetitive or aversive visual memory formation in the tested assay.

paper_title: Shared mushroom body circuits underlie visual and olfactory memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual conditioning with sugar reward and electric shock punishment
neural_activity: Blocked continuously using temperature-sensitive shibire (shits1); output analyzed during training and test
behavioral_consequence: Selectively reduced aversive memory; caused prolonged hyperactivity that indirectly influenced shock avoidance; output necessary during acquisition of aversive memory but dispensable during test
ecological_context: Part of the neurotransmitter system driving both olfactory and visual memories in insects
summary: TH-GAL4 labeled dopamine neurons mediate aversive visual memory acquisition, with output required specifically during training.

paper_title: Shared mushroom body circuits underlie visual and olfactory memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual conditioning with sugar reward and electric shock punishment
neural_activity: Blocked continuously using temperature-sensitive shibire (shits1); output analyzed during training and test
behavioral_consequence: Substantially impaired appetitive memory but not aversive memory; did not affect reflexive choice of sugar; output necessary during training but dispensable during test
ecological_context: Shared requirement with olfactory learning for reinforcement signals
summary: DDC-GAL4 labeled dopamine neurons mediate appetitive visual memory acquisition, with output required specifically during training.

paper_title: Shared mushroom body circuits underlie visual and olfactory memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual conditioning with sugar reward
neural_activity: Blocked with shits1; thermally activated using dTRPA1
behavioral_consequence: Blocking impaired appetitive memories; thermo-activation was sufficient to induce appetitive visual memories (reinforcement replacement)
ecological_context: Signals reward for visual and olfactory memories
summary: R58E02-GAL4 labeled PAM cluster neurons provide appetitive reinforcement signals for visual memories, sufficient to drive memory formation via thermo-activation.

paper_title: Shared mushroom body circuits underlie visual and olfactory memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual conditioning with electric shock punishment
neural_activity: Blocked with shits1; thermally activated using dTRPA1
behavioral_consequence: Blocking impaired aversive memories; thermo-activation was sufficient to induce aversive visual memories; did not affect reflexive choice of sugar and shock
ecological_context: Signals punishment for visual and olfactory memories
summary: MB504B-GAL4 labeled PPL1 cluster neurons provide aversive reinforcement signals for visual memories, sufficient to drive memory formation via thermo-activation.

paper_title: Shared mushroom body circuits underlie visual and olfactory memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual conditioning (appetitive and aversive)
neural_activity: Null mutant (dumb2); rescue with GAL4-dependent expression in Kenyon cells (MB247-GAL4)
behavioral_consequence: dumb2 mutants showed severe defects in appetitive and aversive visual memory; selective expression of DopR+ in KCs significantly rescued the memory defect
ecological_context: Required for associative modulation within the mushroom bodies
summary: DopR expression in Kenyon cells is required for both appetitive and aversive visual memory; rescue in KCs restores memory in dumb2 mutants.

paper_title: Shared mushroom body circuits underlie visual and olfactory memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual conditioning (appetitive and aversive)
neural_activity: Output blocked continuously or transiently using temperature-sensitive shits1
behavioral_consequence: Continuous blockade impaired both appetitive and aversive memories; required for memory acquisition and retrieval; requirement independent of conditioning design (classical vs operant) and context changes
ecological_context: Mushroom bodies are required for associative learning, allowing prediction of events based on sensory cues
summary: General Kenyon cell output is necessary for both acquisition and retrieval of visual memories, regardless of learning protocol or context consistency.

paper_title: Shared mushroom body circuits underlie visual and olfactory memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual conditioning (appetitive and aversive)
neural_activity: Output blocked continuously or transiently using temperature-sensitive shits1
behavioral_consequence: Blocking output impaired both appetitive and aversive visual memories; dispensable during training but necessary for retrieval of aversive visual memory
ecological_context: Converging inputs from different stimuli into this lobe suggest a general role in short-lasting memories
summary: γ lobe Kenyon cells are required for visual memory formation and retrieval, specifically necessary for retrieval of aversive memory.

paper_title: Shared mushroom body circuits underlie visual and olfactory memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual and Olfactory conditioning (aversive)
neural_activity: Output blocked using temperature-sensitive shits1
behavioral_consequence: Blocking output selectively impaired olfactory memory but did not significantly reduce performance in visual memory tasks
ecological_context: Preferential representation of olfactory inputs to these neurons
summary: α′/β′ Kenyon cells are required for olfactory memory but not visual memory in the tested paradigms.

paper_title: Shared mushroom body circuits underlie visual and olfactory memories in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual and Olfactory conditioning (aversive)
neural_activity: Output blocked using temperature-sensitive shits1
behavioral_consequence: Blocking 17D-GAL4 (α/β) did not significantly perturb either memory; MB008B-GAL4/ UAS-shits1 flies showed a tendency toward impairment in visual memory
ecological_context: α/β neurons might play modulatory roles in specific visual memory tasks or long-lasting memories
summary: α/β Kenyon cells are not essential for visual or olfactory memory in these assays, though a tendency toward impairment was observed with specific drivers.

paper_title: Integration of the olfactory code across dendritic claws of single mushroom body neurons
experimental_stimulus: Panel of 10 odorants (1-hexanol, 2-phenylethanol, pentyl acetate, methyl benzoate, trans-2-hexanal, geraniol, apple cider vinegar, peppermint, pine, orange); Optogenetic stimulation of 3 glomerular channels (DA1, DC3, VA1d) via ChR2-YFP; Somatic current injection
neural_activity: Individual dendritic claws exhibit distinct odor response profiles. Somatic calcium signals and spiking responses typically require activation of multiple claws (3-5+ claws). Inputs integrate linearly or sublinearly. Spiking responses are sparse and rare when few claws are active.
summary: Kenyon cells integrate input from multiple glomerular channels with distinct odor tuning via dendritic claws. Linear or sublinear summation of inputs is observed, and reliable spiking requires activation of multiple claws, resulting in sparse response patterns.

paper_title: Integration of the olfactory code across dendritic claws of single mushroom body neurons
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation via ChR2-YFP (blue light, 470 nm)
neural_activity: Light-evoked spiking rates similar to those observed during strong odor responses. Firing dynamics showed a transient peak in firing more prominent with ChR2-based stimulation.
summary: Projection neurons express ChR2-YFP in specific glomeruli (DA1, DC3, VA1d) and exhibit light-evoked spiking rates comparable to strong odor responses.

paper_title: Dopaminergic Neurons Encode a Distributed, Asymmetric Representation of Temperature in Drosophila
cell_type: PPL1 dopaminergic neurons (vertical lobes of mushroom body)
experimental_stimulus: Temperature shifts (cooling from 25°C to 18°C, heating from 25°C to 32°C or 37°C, sustained cooling at 18°C)
neural_activity: Increased calcium activity (GCaMP3) in response to cooling with rapidly adapting bursts; no response to heating. Responses observed in V1, MV1, MP1, and α/α' tip regions. Responses reduced by ablation of antennae and maxillary palps. Responses eliminated by TTX.
behavioral_consequence: Conditional silencing of broad PPL1 population (TH-GAL4) substantially reduced behavioral cold avoidance on a temperature gradient. Silencing specific subsets (V1, MV1, MP1) had no effect on cold or heat avoidance.
ecological_context: Flies behaviorally avoid cold and warm extremes, distributing around 24°C in a temperature gradient. This represents an innate temperature-preference behavior.
techniques_used: ['In vivo calcium imaging', 'Conditional silencing (Kir2.1 / TARGET system)', 'Temperature preference behavior assay', 'Surgical ablation (antennae, maxillary palps, legs)', 'Pharmacological application (TTX)']
summary: PPL1 dopaminergic neurons innervating the vertical lobes respond asymmetrically to cooling (phasic bursts) but not heating. While broadly distributed across V1, MV1, MP1, and α/α' tips, silencing of specific subsets (V1, MV1, MP1) did not affect cold avoidance, whereas broad PPL1 silencing reduced cold avoidance. Responses depend on sensory input from antennae and maxillary palps.

paper_title: Dopaminergic Neurons Encode a Distributed, Asymmetric Representation of Temperature in Drosophila
cell_type: PAM dopaminergic neurons (horizontal lobes of mushroom body)
experimental_stimulus: Temperature shifts (cooling from 25°C to 18°C, heating from 25°C to 32°C)
neural_activity: No significant calcium activity changes (GCaMP3) in response to cooling or heating, observed in neuropil and somata.
behavioral_consequence: Conditional silencing of M3 neurons (NP5272 driver, part of PAM/horizontal lobe group) had no significant effect on heat or cold avoidance.
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed in the context of temperature preference for this specific subset.
techniques_used: ['In vivo calcium imaging', 'Conditional silencing (Kir2.1 / TARGET system)', 'Temperature preference behavior assay']
summary: PAM dopaminergic neurons innervating the horizontal lobes do not respond to temperature shifts (cooling or heating). Silencing the M3 subset of these neurons did not alter temperature preference behavior.

paper_title: Layered reward signaling through octopamine and dopamine in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Arabinose (sweet, non-nutritious) conditioning; Sucrose (sweet, nutritious) conditioning; Artificial heat activation (UAS-dTrpA1, 31 uC)
neural_activity: Synaptic transmission blockade (UAS-shits1, 31 uC); Depolarization via UAS-dTrpA1 (31 uC)
behavioral_consequence: Blocking impairs short-term appetitive memory with arabinose but not sucrose. Activation forms short-term appetitive memory (30 min retention, absent at 3 h). Memory requires octopamine synthesis (blocked in Tbh mutants).
ecological_context: Appetitive olfactory conditioning with food reinforcement (sweet taste vs nutrient value)
summary: Tdc2-GAL4 octopamine neurons mediate short-term appetitive memory reinforcement for sweet taste (arabinose), but are not required for nutrient-based sucrose reinforcement. Artificial activation substitutes for sugar but memory is short-lived.

paper_title: Layered reward signaling through octopamine and dopamine in Drosophila
cell_type: MB-innervating Octopamine subsets (NP7088, 0665, 0891 - OA-VUMa2, OA-VPM3, OA-VPM4)
experimental_stimulus: Artificial heat activation (UAS-dTrpA1); Synaptic blockade (UAS-shits1)
neural_activity: Synaptic transmission blockade; Depolarization via UAS-dTrpA1
behavioral_consequence: Activation did not form appetitive memory. Blocking did not significantly impair arabinose-reinforced memory.
ecological_context: N/A (These specific subsets not sufficient for appetitive conditioning)
summary: Specific MB-innervating octopamine subsets (OA-VUMa2, OA-VPM3, OA-VPM4) are neither sufficient nor essential for conditioned olfactory approach behavior.

paper_title: Layered reward signaling through octopamine and dopamine in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Odour presentation; Artificial heat activation (UAS-dTrpA1); Octopamine application (in vitro); Blocking (UAS-shits1); Arabinose/Sucrose conditioning
neural_activity: Increased intracellular Ca2+ upon octopamine application (GCaMP3.0); Depolarization via UAS-dTrpA1
behavioral_consequence: Activation forms robust appetitive memory (3 min). Blocking abolishes arabinose memory and impairs sucrose memory (3 min and 24 h). Memory requires DopR (dumb1 mutation) and OAMB receptor. Can function independently of octopamine (in Tbh mutants).
ecological_context: Appetitive reinforcement signals for food sources
summary: PAM dopamine neurons (0104/0273-GAL4) are critical for appetitive reinforcement. They increase Ca2+ with octopamine and require OAMB receptors. Their activation is sufficient for memory, and they function downstream of octopamine.

paper_title: Layered reward signaling through octopamine and dopamine in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Artificial octopamine activation (UAS-dTrpA1); Blocking (UAS-shits1); Octb2R RNAi
neural_activity: Synaptic transmission blockade; Octb2R knockdown
behavioral_consequence: Blocking output prevents memory formation during artificial conditioning. Octb2R RNAi impairs memory. Mutant flies (octb2R/+) show impaired artificial learning in satiated flies, restored in hungry flies.
ecological_context: Modulation of appetitive reinforcement via interaction with octopamine
summary: MB-MP1 dopamine neurons require OCTb2R modulation for octopamine-dependent appetitive reinforcement. Blocking these neurons prevents memory formation during artificial conditioning.

paper_title: Three Dopamine Pathways Induce Aversive Odor Memories with Different Stability
experimental_stimulus: Electric shock (12 pulses, 90 V) paired with odor; dTRPA1 thermo-activation (30°C) paired with odor
neural_activity: TH-immunoreactive dopamine neurons projecting to bsp2 and b'a2 subdomains of the mushroom body lobes
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces immediate aversive memory. Inactivation (Shits1) during acquisition preferentially impairs 2-hour memory. Combinatorial activation with MB-MV1 neurons improves 2-hour memory.
ecological_context: Animals acquire predictive values of sensory stimuli through reinforcement to avoid cues that predict negative outcome
techniques_used: ['T-maze olfactory conditioning', 'dTRPA1 thermo-activation', 'Shits1 temperature-sensitive inactivation', 'Immunohistochemistry (TH staining)']
summary: MB-M3 neurons are dopaminergic and project to specific MB subdomains; they induce immediate aversive memory and are required for 2-hour memory retention.

paper_title: Three Dopamine Pathways Induce Aversive Odor Memories with Different Stability
experimental_stimulus: Electric shock (12 pulses, 90 V) paired with odor; dTRPA1 thermo-activation (30°C) paired with odor
neural_activity: TH-immunoreactive dopamine neurons projecting to c1 subdomain and peduncle core
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces immediate aversive memory. Inactivation (Shits1) during acquisition impairs all tested memory phases (2 min, 2 hours, 9 hours).
ecological_context: Animals acquire predictive values of sensory stimuli through reinforcement to avoid cues that predict negative outcome
techniques_used: ['T-maze olfactory conditioning', 'dTRPA1 thermo-activation', 'Shits1 temperature-sensitive inactivation', 'Immunohistochemistry (TH staining)']
summary: MB-MP1 neurons are dopaminergic and project to c1/peduncle; they are required for all phases of aversive memory retention.

paper_title: Three Dopamine Pathways Induce Aversive Odor Memories with Different Stability
experimental_stimulus: Electric shock (12 pulses, 90 V) paired with odor; dTRPA1 thermo-activation (30°C) paired with odor
neural_activity: TH-immunoreactive dopamine neurons projecting to c2 and a'1 subdomains
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces immediate aversive memory. Inactivation (Shits1) during acquisition preferentially impairs 9-hour memory. Combinatorial activation with MB-M3 improves 2-hour memory.
ecological_context: Animals acquire predictive values of sensory stimuli through reinforcement to avoid cues that predict negative outcome
techniques_used: ['T-maze olfactory conditioning', 'dTRPA1 thermo-activation', 'Shits1 temperature-sensitive inactivation', 'Immunohistochemistry (TH staining)']
summary: MB-MV1 neurons are dopaminergic and project to c2/a'1; they induce immediate aversive memory and are required for 9-hour memory retention.

paper_title: Three Dopamine Pathways Induce Aversive Odor Memories with Different Stability
experimental_stimulus: Electric shock (12 pulses, 90 V) paired with odor
neural_activity: TH-immunoreactive dopamine neurons projecting to a2, a'2 subdomains
behavioral_consequence: Activation alone (drivers NP7187/MZ840) did not induce significant aversive memory.
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed
summary: MB-V1 neurons are dopaminergic and project to a2/a'1 but do not induce significant aversive memory when activated alone.

paper_title: Optic Glomeruli and Their Inputs in Drosophila Share an Organizational Ground Pattern with the Antennal Lobes
experimental_stimulus: Slow full-field flicker (0.5 Hz), single bar moving in four directions, square-wave gratings moving in eight directions, sinusoidal gratings moving in four directions, static square-wave gratings at four orientations
neural_activity: Nonspiking; fast and slow membrane potential fluctuations. Single neurons show subtle/variable responses. Ensemble averaging (N=33) reveals significant power increase to light ON/OFF (flicker). Ensemble (N=25) shows significant response to downward (270°) bar motion. No significant response to gratings or static patterns.
ecological_context: Visual processing allows animals to negotiate their environment and direct their behaviors; optic lobes reconstruct salient features of the taxon's visual ecology by processing optic flow and distinguishing static features.
summary: L1CNs are nonspiking neurons that individually encode visual stimuli ambiguously, but ensemble averaging reveals reliable responses to flicker and downward motion, while showing no response to gratings.

paper_title: Optic Glomeruli and Their Inputs in Drosophila Share an Organizational Ground Pattern with the Antennal Lobes
cell_type: Type 2 lobula plate lobula columnar neurons (LPL2CNs)
experimental_stimulus: Looming stimulus (expanding square black block), slow full-field flicker
neural_activity: Nonspiking; subtle responses in single neurons resolved only via power spectrum analysis. Responds to looming stimulus expanding over the retina. Intensity decrements initiate larger depolarization than increments in postsynaptic targets.
ecological_context: Visual processing allows animals to negotiate their environment; optic lobes reconstruct salient features of the taxon's visual ecology by processing optic flow and distinguishing static features.
summary: LPL2CNs are nonspiking output neurons that respond to looming stimuli, though single responses are subtle and require power spectrum analysis for resolution.

paper_title: Optic Glomeruli and Their Inputs in Drosophila Share an Organizational Ground Pattern with the Antennal Lobes
experimental_stimulus: Looming stimulus, slow full-field flicker
neural_activity: Unambiguous and rapidly adapting response to looming stimulus. Intensity decrements initiate larger depolarization than increments. Response shows signal averaging and noise reduction compared to single afferents.
ecological_context: Visual processing allows animals to negotiate their environment; optic glomeruli are involved in further reconstructing the fly's visual world.
summary: LINs in the optic glomerulus reliably code visual primitives (e.g., looming) through signal averaging, providing a noise-free relay to projection neurons.

paper_title: Optic Glomeruli and Their Inputs in Drosophila Share an Organizational Ground Pattern with the Antennal Lobes
experimental_stimulus: Looming stimulus
neural_activity: Corresponding depolarization to looming stimulus. Less rapid adaptation than associated LINs. Projects to the thoracic ganglion.
ecological_context: Visual processing allows animals to negotiate their environment; optic glomeruli are involved in further reconstructing the fly's visual world.
summary: The GF projection neuron receives convergent inputs from LPL2CNs and LINs, showing depolarization to looming stimuli with less adaptation than the LIN.

paper_title: Cellular-Resolution Population Imaging Reveals Robust Sparse Coding in the Drosophila Mushroom Body
experimental_stimulus: Monomolecular odors (e.g., isoamyl acetate, 3-octanol), artificial odor blends, natural odors (yeast, banana, orange, apple cider vinegar), and varying odor concentrations (0.01 to 0.1 dilutions of saturated vapor)
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (GCaMP3) revealed sparse population activity, with approximately 5% of KCs responding to a specific odor. Responses were subadditive for odor blends. Sparseness was largely invariant across concentrations, influenced by stimulus history (adaptation). No spatial organization of responsive neurons was observed based on response probability or odor tuning.
summary: Kenyon cells in the Drosophila mushroom body exhibit robust sparse calcium coding in response to diverse odors, including natural and blended stimuli, with subadditive responses to mixtures and no spatial organization of responsive cells.

paper_title: D1 Dopamine Receptor dDA1 Is Required in the Mushroom Body Neurons for Aversive and Appetitive Learning in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Odor (CS) paired with electric shock (aversive US) or sucrose (appetitive US)
behavioral_consequence: dDA1 absence in MB (dumb mutants) causes negligible aversive learning and moderately impaired appetitive learning. Reinstating dDA1 expression in MB neurons fully rescues learning performance in both paradigms.
ecological_context: Avoiding odors that predict punishment or preferring odors that predict food reward
summary: Mushroom Body neurons expressing the D1 dopamine receptor dDA1 are required for both aversive and appetitive olfactory learning in Drosophila.

paper_title: Hormonal control of postmitotic neuronal identity
cell_type: 84C10-labelled dFB neurons (dorsal fan-shaped body neurons innervating layers 6-7 of the FB)
experimental_stimulus: High-sugar feeding; Post-mitotic depletion of E93
neural_activity: Marked reduction in vesicular glutamate transporter (vGLUT) expression; progressive neuronal loss; ectopic expansion of axonal arborizations across FB layers
behavioral_consequence: Impaired increases in fat-to-lean mass ratios
ecological_context: Nutrient sensing and metabolic adaptation
techniques_used: ['Lineage tracing', 'Clonal analysis', 'Birth dating', 'Post-mitotic depletion of E93']
summary: 84C10-labelled dFB neurons express the late temporal transcription factor E93 in adulthood; post-mitotic depletion of E93 results in neuronal loss, reduced vGLUT expression, and ectopic axonal expansion, leading to impaired fat-to-lean mass ratio increases during high-sugar feeding.

paper_title: A new system for studying neuronal remodeling and its relation to behavior in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation using UAS-CsChrimson driven by MooSEZ-specific split GAL4 drivers (VT020742-p65.AD x R60F09-GAL4.DBD); genetic manipulation of ecdysone signaling using EcR dominant negative variants (EcRDN)
neural_activity: Expression tracked via confocal microscopy (CsChrimson.mVenus/mCherry) throughout development. Neurite pruning observed in VNC dendrites (8-24 hr APF) and SEZ axons (18-48 hr APF). Ecdysone signaling manipulation altered pruning timing and outgrowth morphology.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation drives backward crawling in larvae and backward walking in adults. Unilateral activation causes ipsiversive backward rotation in adults but not larvae. Genetic disruption of remodeling (EcRDN expression) abolishes adult backward walking despite preserved larval backward crawling.
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics (CsChrimson, GtACR2)', 'Confocal microscopy', 'Behavioral tracking (Ctrax)', 'Genetic manipulation (Split-GAL4, EcRDN, Immortalization, SPARC)', 'Three-dimensional reconstruction']
summary: MooSEZ neurons persist from larva to adult, driving backward locomotion. Adult-specific rotational functionality emerges after metamorphosis. Cell-autonomous ecdysone signaling is required for neurite outgrowth and is causally linked to adult backward walking behavior.

paper_title: A new system for studying neuronal remodeling and its relation to behavior in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic inhibition using UAS-GtACR2 or silencing using Tetanus Toxin light chain (TNT) in GH146II-GAL4 neurons (MooSEZ) background
neural_activity: Identified as postsynaptic partners of MooSEZ via anatomical analysis and functional manipulation.
behavioral_consequence: Inhibition of MDN activity decreased but did not eliminate backward walking evoked by MooSEZ activation, suggesting MooSEZ utilizes both MDN-dependent and MDN-independent pathways.
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics (GtACR2)', 'Tetanus Toxin silencing', 'Optogenetic activation of upstream partners', 'Behavioral tracking']
summary: MDNs are downstream partners of MooSEZ. Inhibiting MDNs reduces backward walking evoked by MooSEZ, indicating MooSEZ drives backward locomotion via both MDN-dependent and independent pathways.

paper_title: A new system for studying neuronal remodeling and its relation to behavior in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation using AMB split GAL4 driver line (R11E07-p65.AD x R60F09-GAL4.DBD)
neural_activity: Expression patterns analyzed via anatomical imaging in larvae.
behavioral_consequence: Activation elicits robust backward crawling in larvae. Does not drive backward walking in adult flies, indicating AMBs are larval-specific or do not persist as functional backward-driving neurons in adults.
summary: AMBs are identified as larval equivalents of MooSEZ neurons, driving backward crawling in larvae but failing to drive backward locomotion in adults.

paper_title: Rewiring an olfactory circuit by altering cell-surface combinatorial code
experimental_stimulus: cVA (11-cis-vaccenyl acetate)
neural_activity: Activated by cVA (endogenous stimulus)
behavioral_consequence: In wild-type, cVA inhibits male-male courtship. In rewired flies, loss of connection to DA1-PNs contributes to increased male-male courtship activity.
ecological_context: cVA is only produced in males and acts to inhibit the courtship of males towards other males or recently mated females.
summary: DA1-ORNs are activated by the male pheromone cVA. In wild-type flies, this pathway inhibits male-male courtship, but rewiring DA1-ORNs to VA1v-PNs removes this inhibition.

paper_title: Rewiring an olfactory circuit by altering cell-surface combinatorial code
experimental_stimulus: cVA (via DA1-ORNs)
neural_activity: Receives synaptic connection from DA1-ORNs in wild-type flies. Connection is lost in rewired flies.
behavioral_consequence: Loss of connection to DA1-PNs in rewired males contributes to increased male-male courtship activity.
ecological_context: Part of the neural circuit that normally inhibits male-male courtship.
summary: DA1-PNs normally receive input from DA1-ORNs. Genetic rewiring disconnects these neurons, reducing male-male courtship inhibition.

paper_title: Rewiring an olfactory circuit by altering cell-surface combinatorial code
experimental_stimulus: PA (palmitoleic acid)
neural_activity: Activated by PA (endogenous stimulus)
behavioral_consequence: PA promotes courtship in males. Connections to VA1v-PNs remain intact in rewired flies.
ecological_context: PA is a fly cuticular pheromone that promotes courtship.
summary: VA1v-ORNs are activated by PA and promote male courtship. Their connections to VA1v-PNs remain unaffected by DA1-ORN rewiring.

paper_title: Rewiring an olfactory circuit by altering cell-surface combinatorial code
experimental_stimulus: PA, cVA (rewired flies)
neural_activity: Wild-type: Activity increases to PA, decreases to cVA. Rewired: Activity increases to both PA and cVA due to DA1-ORN input.
behavioral_consequence: Rewiring leads to markedly increased male-male courtship. Rewired males exhibited unilateral wing extensions towards wild-type partner males significantly more frequently.
ecological_context: This PN type promotes courtship. Rewiring allows the male pheromone cVA to activate this courtship-promoting pathway.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon Ca2+ imaging (GCaMP7b)', 'Behavioral assays (courtship wing extension, chasing)']
summary: VA1v-PNs respond to PA and cVA. In rewired flies, they respond to cVA via DA1-ORN input, driving increased male-male courtship.

paper_title: Rewiring an olfactory circuit by altering cell-surface combinatorial code
experimental_stimulus: MP (methyl palmitate), MM (methyl myristate)
neural_activity: Activated by MP and MM (endogenous stimuli)
behavioral_consequence: Rewiring changes odour responses of downstream PNs. No specific behavioral assay reported for VA1d rewiring in this text.
ecological_context: MP and MM are fly pheromones detected by these neurons.
summary: VA1d-ORNs respond to pheromones MP and MM. Rewiring their axons alters the odour response profile of their new postsynaptic partners.

paper_title: Rewiring an olfactory circuit by altering cell-surface combinatorial code
experimental_stimulus: Farnesol (endogenous), MP/MM (rewired)
neural_activity: In rewired flies, dendrites showed stronger response to VA1d-ORN-specific odours (MP/MM) compared with wild-type flies.
behavioral_consequence: No specific behavioral assay reported for DC3-PN rewiring in this text.
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed in relation to natural behavior.
summary: DC3-PNs gain response to VA1d-ORN odours (MP/MM) when VA1d-ORNs are rewired to synapse with them.

paper_title: Rewiring an olfactory circuit by altering cell-surface combinatorial code
experimental_stimulus: Farnesol (control), MP/MM (rewired)
neural_activity: In rewired flies, dendrites gained response to VA1d-ORN odours (MP/MM), though response magnitude was smaller than in other rewiring cases.
behavioral_consequence: No specific behavioral assay reported for DL3-PN rewiring in this text.
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed in relation to natural behavior.
summary: DL3-PNs gain response to VA1d-ORN odours (MP/MM) when VA1d-ORNs are rewired to synapse with them.

paper_title: Sex peptide targets distinct higher order processing neurons in the brain to induce the female post-mating response
experimental_stimulus: membrane-tethered sex peptide (mSP) expression, temperature-induced TrpA1 activation, tetanus toxin (TNT) inhibition
neural_activity: mSP expression and TrpA1 activation induce functional response; TNT inhibition reduces receptivity; Retro-Tango shows input from higher order interneurons
behavioral_consequence: Significant reduction of female receptivity to males; significant induction of egg laying when expressed in brain
ecological_context: Part of post-mating response (PMR) to male sex peptide; allows adjustment of reproductive behavior to physiological state and environmental conditions (e.g., predation risk)
techniques_used: ['split-GAL4 intersection', 'mSP expression', 'TrpA1 activation', 'TNT inhibition', 'Retro-Tango', 'Trans-Tango', 'Oviposition assay', 'Receptivity assay']
summary: SPR8 ∩ dsx interneurons in the brain and VNC induce post-mating responses including reduced receptivity and increased egg laying when activated by SP or TrpA1.

paper_title: Sex peptide targets distinct higher order processing neurons in the brain to induce the female post-mating response
experimental_stimulus: mSP expression, TrpA1 activation, TNT inhibition
neural_activity: mSP expression and TrpA1 activation induce functional response; TNT inhibition reduces receptivity; Retro-Tango shows input from higher order interneurons
behavioral_consequence: Significant reduction of female receptivity; partial induction of egg laying
ecological_context: Part of post-mating response to male sex peptide
techniques_used: ['split-GAL4 intersection', 'mSP expression', 'TrpA1 activation', 'TNT inhibition', 'Oviposition assay', 'Receptivity assay']
summary: fru11/12 ∩ dsx interneurons reduce receptivity when activated, contributing to the female post-mating response.

paper_title: Sex peptide targets distinct higher order processing neurons in the brain to induce the female post-mating response
experimental_stimulus: mSP expression, TrpA1 activation, TNT inhibition
neural_activity: mSP expression and TrpA1 activation induce functional response; TNT inhibition reduces receptivity
behavioral_consequence: Significant reduction of female receptivity; partial induction of egg laying
ecological_context: Part of post-mating response to male sex peptide
techniques_used: ['split-GAL4 intersection', 'mSP expression', 'TrpA1 activation', 'TNT inhibition', 'Oviposition assay', 'Receptivity assay']
summary: SPR8 ∩ fru11/12 interneurons reduce receptivity and partially induce egg laying upon activation.

paper_title: Sex peptide targets distinct higher order processing neurons in the brain to induce the female post-mating response
experimental_stimulus: mSP expression, TNT inhibition
neural_activity: mSP expression induces functional response; TNT inhibition reduces receptivity
behavioral_consequence: Significant reduction of female receptivity
ecological_context: Part of post-mating response to male sex peptide
summary: SPR8 ∩ FD6 interneurons reduce female receptivity when activated.

paper_title: Sex peptide targets distinct higher order processing neurons in the brain to induce the female post-mating response
experimental_stimulus: mSP expression, TrpA1 activation, TNT inhibition
neural_activity: mSP expression and TrpA1 activation induce partial functional response; TNT inhibition reduces receptivity partially
behavioral_consequence: Partial reduction of female receptivity; no induction of egg laying
ecological_context: Part of post-mating response to male sex peptide
techniques_used: ['split-GAL4 intersection', 'mSP expression', 'TrpA1 activation', 'TNT inhibition', 'Oviposition assay', 'Receptivity assay']
summary: fru11/12 ∩ FD6 interneurons partially reduce receptivity but do not induce egg laying.

paper_title: Sex peptide targets distinct higher order processing neurons in the brain to induce the female post-mating response
experimental_stimulus: mSP expression
neural_activity: mSP expression induces functional response
behavioral_consequence: Induction of egg laying; no reduction of receptivity
ecological_context: Regulation of egg laying independently of receptivity
summary: SPR12 neurons induce egg laying without affecting receptivity.

paper_title: Sex peptide targets distinct higher order processing neurons in the brain to induce the female post-mating response
experimental_stimulus: mSP expression
neural_activity: mSP expression induces functional response
behavioral_consequence: Reduction of receptivity and increase in egg laying
ecological_context: Control of sexual receptivity and egg laying
summary: FD6 SAG neurons induce both reduced receptivity and increased egg laying.

paper_title: Network synchrony creates neural filters promoting quiescence in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) at 1 Hz; visual stimuli (green light); air puffs
neural_activity: Induces rhythmic activity patterns and slow-wave activity (SWA, 0.5-1.5 Hz); optogenetic activation leads to net hyperpolarization of downstream EPG neurons
behavioral_consequence: Reduces locomotor activity and promotes quiescence (rest, grooming); reduces responsiveness to visual stimuli (green light) during quiescence; reduces arousal to air puffs when synchronized with helicon
ecological_context: Promotes quiescent behavior during night and high sleep need; creates neural filter to suppress sensory processing during sleep-like states
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics (CsChrimson)', 'Voltage imaging (ArcLight)', 'Whole-cell patch clamp', 'Behavioral tracking in circular arena']
summary: R5 neurons generate coherent SWA at 1 Hz that reduces locomotion and sensory responsiveness, promoting quiescence and suppressing visual inputs via EPG hyperpolarization.

paper_title: Network synchrony creates neural filters promoting quiescence in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) at 1 Hz; Optogenetic inhibition (GtACR1)
neural_activity: Exhibits spontaneous SWA at night; optogenetic activation depolarizes downstream EPG neurons
behavioral_consequence: Activation increases arousal and locomotion (opposing R5); inhibition induces quiescence; synchronized activation with R5 enhances sensory filtering
ecological_context: Part of locomotion-promoting network; antagonizes R5 to regulate sensory gating and wakefulness
summary: Helicon cells exhibit nocturnal SWA and drive locomotion via EPG depolarization; optogenetic activation promotes arousal while inhibition promotes quiescence.

paper_title: Network synchrony creates neural filters promoting quiescence in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson); Optogenetic inhibition (GtACR1)
neural_activity: Night-time specific SWA (0.5-1.5 Hz); activation amplifies R5 presynaptic SWA; inhibition reduces R5 network oscillatory power
behavioral_consequence: Indirectly regulates quiescence by modulating R5 SWA coherence
ecological_context: Circadian and homeostatic control of sleep need; facilitates coherent oscillations between networks at night
summary: dFSB exhibits nocturnal SWA and functionally modulates R5 network oscillations, influencing the coherence of neural filters for sleep regulation.

paper_title: Network synchrony creates neural filters promoting quiescence in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual stimulation (retina activation); Optogenetic activation (1 Hz and 10 Hz); R5/Helicon activation (downstream)
neural_activity: Spontaneous depolarizations decrease at night; optogenetic R5 activation causes hyperpolarization; Helicon activation causes depolarization; visually evoked depolarization attenuated at night
behavioral_consequence: 10 Hz activation alters locomotor activity and turning behavior; 1 Hz activation has no effect on locomotion; visual responsiveness reduced at night correlates with EPG suppression
ecological_context: Head-direction system controlling navigation; receives antagonistic inputs to regulate movement based on sleep state
techniques_used: ['Voltage imaging (ArcLight)', 'Optogenetics', 'Ex vivo compound recordings', 'In vivo focal illumination']
summary: EPG neurons receive antagonistic R5 (inhibitory) and Helicon (excitatory) inputs; visual responsiveness is gated at night via R5-mediated hyperpolarization.

paper_title: Rewiring an olfactory circuit by altering the combinatorial code of cell-surface proteins
experimental_stimulus: Male pheromone
neural_activity: Senses a male pheromone
behavioral_consequence: Original circuit inhibits male-male courtship; rewiring connection markedly increases male-male courtship
ecological_context: Male-male courtship inhibition (natural); Courtship promotion (rewired)
techniques_used: ['Systematically altering the combination of differentially expressed cell-surface proteins (CSPs)']
summary: A male pheromone-sensing ORN type normally inhibits male-male courtship; altering its CSPs switched its connection to a new PN type, markedly increasing male-male courtship.

paper_title: Rewiring an olfactory circuit by altering the combinatorial code of cell-surface proteins
experimental_stimulus: Male pheromone (via ORN input)
neural_activity: Odor response changed after anatomical switch
behavioral_consequence: Promotes courtship; rewiring ORN to this PN markedly increased male-male courtship
ecological_context: Courtship promotion context
summary: The new PN partner promotes courtship; the anatomical switch to this PN changed the odor response and markedly increased male-male courtship.

paper_title: Rewiring an olfactory circuit by altering the combinatorial code of cell-surface proteins
experimental_stimulus: Not explicitly described
neural_activity: Not explicitly described
behavioral_consequence: Successfully rewired to multiple distinct PN types
ecological_context: Not explicitly described
techniques_used: ['Three manipulation strategies (increasing repulsion with old partner, decreasing repulsion with new partner, matching attraction with new partner)']
summary: A second ORN type was successfully rewired to multiple distinct PN types using three manipulation strategies.

paper_title: Divergent synaptic dynamics originate parallel pathways for computation and behavior in an olfactory circuit.
