experimental_stimulus: Noni odour (methyl hexanoate), pulsed noni odours, optogenetic light stimulation
neural_activity: Light-evoked spiking confirmed via single-sensillum recordings. Calcium imaging showed higher odour sensitivity in D. sechellia compared to D. melanogaster. Optogenetic activation mimicked odour-evoked spiking.
behavioral_consequence: Loss of Or22a abolished attraction to noni odour in tethered fly and wind tunnel assays. Optogenetic activation of Or22a OSNs induced attractive behaviour (positive delta wing beat amplitude).
ecological_context: Essential for long- and short-range odour-guided behaviours towards Morinda citrifolia noni fruit, the host fruit for the ecological specialist D. sechellia.
techniques_used: ['single-sensillum recording', 'two-photon calcium imaging', 'tethered fly assay', 'wind tunnel tracking', 'optogenetics']
summary: Or22a OSNs are required for attraction to noni fruit. In D. sechellia, these neurons exhibit higher sensitivity and their activation is sufficient to drive attraction, supporting persistent odour tracking.

paper_title: Olfactory sensory neuron population expansions influence projection neuron adaptation and enhance odour tracking
experimental_stimulus: 2-heptanone, pulsed noni odours
neural_activity: Calcium imaging revealed no sensitivity differences between D. sechellia and D. melanogaster. Population size is approximately three-fold increased in D. sechellia.
behavioral_consequence: Mutants show less persistent attraction to noni odour but retain transient turning. Optogenetic activation of Or22a (not Or85b specifically for behavior in this text) showed persistence depends on OSN number.
ecological_context: Required for long- and/or short-range odour-guided behaviours towards Morinda citrifolia noni fruit in D. sechellia.
summary: Or85b OSNs are required for persistent noni attraction. Their population is expanded in D. sechellia, though sensitivity per neuron is not different from D. melanogaster.

paper_title: Olfactory sensory neuron population expansions influence projection neuron adaptation and enhance odour tracking
experimental_stimulus: Noni odour
neural_activity: Population size approximately three-fold increased in D. sechellia compared to D. melanogaster and D. simulans (quantified via RNA FISH).
behavioral_consequence: Mutants show less persistent attraction to noni odour but retain transient turning towards the stimulus.
ecological_context: Required for long- and/or short-range odour-guided behaviours towards Morinda citrifolia noni fruit in D. sechellia.
summary: Ir75b OSN populations are expanded in the noni-specialist D. sechellia and contribute to persistent attraction to the host fruit odour.

paper_title: Olfactory sensory neuron population expansions influence projection neuron adaptation and enhance odour tracking
experimental_stimulus: 2-heptanone (single and pulsed odour stimuli), pharmacological blockers (GABA, cholinergic)
neural_activity: Whole-cell patch clamp showed ~2-fold lower input resistance in D. sechellia. Calcium imaging showed reduced decay (adaptation) during repeated odour pulses in D. sechellia compared to D. melanogaster. Pharmacological inhibition of LNs decreased adaptation in D. melanogaster but not D. sechellia.
behavioral_consequence: Not directly tested via activation/inactivation; physiological findings (reduced adaptation) are linked to enhanced odour tracking behaviour of the pathway.
ecological_context: Part of the noni-sensing pathway; reduced adaptation likely enables sustained odour representation for long-distance localisation of host fruit.
summary: VM5d PNs in D. sechellia show reduced adaptation to repeated odour stimuli compared to D. melanogaster, associated with the expanded input from Or85b OSNs.

paper_title: Olfactory sensory neuron population expansions influence projection neuron adaptation and enhance odour tracking
experimental_stimulus: Methyl hexanoate (noni odour) pulses
neural_activity: Calcium imaging showed D. melanogaster DM2 PN responses decreased over time, while D. sechellia DM2 PN responses were unchanged in magnitude (reduced decay).
behavioral_consequence: Not directly tested via activation/inactivation; pathway activity linked to persistent tracking.
ecological_context: Partner to Or22a OSNs in the noni-sensing pathway.
summary: DM2 PNs in D. sechellia exhibit reduced adaptation to pulsed noni odours compared to D. melanogaster, correlating with the expanded Or22a OSN population.

paper_title: Olfactory sensory neuron population expansions influence projection neuron adaptation and enhance odour tracking
experimental_stimulus: Odour pulses
neural_activity: Calcium imaging revealed PNs in both D. sechellia and D. melanogaster displayed decreasing responses over the course of stimulations (adaptation occurred).
behavioral_consequence: Not directly tested.
ecological_context: Control pathways where the number of cognate OSNs is conserved between species.
summary: Control PNs (DM4, VA2) show adaptation in both species, contrasting with the reduced adaptation observed in expanded noni-sensing pathways (VM5d, DM2) in D. sechellia.

paper_title: The fruit fly, Drosophila melanogaster, as a micro-robotics platform
experimental_stimulus: Red (625 nm) and blue (455 nm) light stimulation via painted antennas
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation using CsChrimson (red-shifted) and ChR2 (blue-shifted) channelrhodopsin under orco-Gal4 control
behavioral_consequence: Red light triggers right turns, blue light triggers left turns; enables guiding flies to arbitrary spatial positions with ~80% fidelity; asymmetric antenna stimulation produces lateralized turning behavior
ecological_context: Osmotropotaxis - natural behavior where flies turn toward the more strongly stimulated antenna in response to olfactory gradients

paper_title: The fruit fly, Drosophila melanogaster, as a micro-robotics platform
experimental_stimulus: Red light optogenetic activation during olfactory-guided walking
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation using CsChrimson
behavioral_consequence: Fidelity scores approached 94% (comparable to visually induced turning); maintained high performance throughout experiment; increased occupancy in walking area between target regions
ecological_context: Previously shown to induce attraction in olfaction context; positive olfactory valence

paper_title: The fruit fly, Drosophila melanogaster, as a micro-robotics platform
experimental_stimulus: Red and blue light optogenetic activation during olfactory-guided walking
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation using CsChrimson
behavioral_consequence: Improved fidelity scores in olfactory-guided turning compared to control
ecological_context: Previously shown to induce attraction in olfaction context; positive olfactory valence

paper_title: The fruit fly, Drosophila melanogaster, as a micro-robotics platform
experimental_stimulus: Red and blue light optogenetic activation during olfactory-guided walking
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation using CsChrimson
behavioral_consequence: Improved fidelity scores in olfactory-guided turning compared to control
ecological_context: Previously shown to induce attraction in olfaction context; positive olfactory valence

paper_title: The fruit fly, Drosophila melanogaster, as a micro-robotics platform
experimental_stimulus: Red and blue light optogenetic activation during olfactory-guided walking
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation using CsChrimson
behavioral_consequence: Improved fidelity scores in olfactory-guided turning compared to control
ecological_context: Previously implicated in olfactory odor tracking

experimental_stimulus: Red and blue light optogenetic activation during olfactory-guided walking
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation using CsChrimson
behavioral_consequence: Improved fidelity scores in olfactory-guided turning compared to control
ecological_context: Previously implicated in olfactory odor tracking

paper_title: The fruit fly, Drosophila melanogaster, as a micro-robotics platform
experimental_stimulus: Red laser optogenetic activation using dual galvanometer mirror system
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation using CsChrimson
behavioral_consequence: Flies stop walking; used to immobilize flies at spatial targets for formation control
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed in ecological context

paper_title: ninna nanna links circadian and homeostatic sleep drive in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Sleep deprivation (12 hours), Natural circadian time points (ZT0 and ZT12)
neural_activity: Nanna protein levels increased after sleep deprivation and at ZT12 (high sleep pressure) compared to ZT0. CaLexA fluorescence signal (indicating neural activity) decreased after sleep deprivation compared to rested controls.
behavioral_consequence: Direct neuronal activation or inactivation was not tested; however, disruption of the *ninna nanna* locus (EP21723) resulted in reduced homeostatic daytime sleep.
ecological_context: Neurons track homeostatic sleep pressure accumulated during waking periods, associated with daytime sleep (siesta) and night sleep.
techniques_used: ['qRT-PCR', 'Immunofluorescence', 'CaLexA system', 'Sleep monitoring (Drosophila Activity Monitors)', 'GRASP (GFP Reconstitution Across Synaptic Partners)']
summary: Nanna-expressing ICLI neurons show increased protein levels and decreased neural activity with higher sleep pressure; disruption of the *ninna nanna* locus reduces daytime sleep.

paper_title: ninna nanna links circadian and homeostatic sleep drive in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Not explicitly tested for sensory or circadian stimulus response; identified via anatomical expression
neural_activity: Not measured in this study
behavioral_consequence: Direct neuronal activation or inactivation was not tested; specific disruption of the *ninna* locus (MB06512) did not produce a sleep phenotype, whereas combined *ninna nanna* disruption reduced daytime sleep.
ecological_context: l-LNvs are circadian pacemaker neurons involved in wake-promotion and light-sensing
summary: Ninna protein is expressed in l-LNvs; specific *ninna* gene disruption did not alter sleep behavior, though combined locus disruption reduced daytime sleep.

paper_title: The caloric value of food intake structurally adjusts a neuronal mushroom body circuit mediating olfactory learning in Drosophila
cell_type: MB188B-Gal4-positive dopaminergic neurons (PAM cluster, γ3/γ4/β'1 compartments)
experimental_stimulus: 7 days hypocaloric food exposure; optogenetic induction of cAMP (bPAC + white light) or depolarization (CsChrimson + red light); electric shock (aversive learning paradigm)
neural_activity: Decreased structural synaptic connectivity with Kenyon cells (KCs) measured by splitGFP reconstitution (GRASP); reduced functional synaptic transmission confirmed by functional GRASP; increased neuronal activity detected via CaLexA under hypocaloric conditions; optogenetic cAMP induction mimicked connectivity decrease
behavioral_consequence: Reduced aversive (punishment-induced) olfactory learning; appetitive (reward-based) olfactory learning unaffected; blocking synaptic transmission impairs aversive learning; RNAi knockdown of AstA1 receptor in these neurons prevents connectivity decrease and increases sucrose uptake
ecological_context: Adjusts learning valence based on nutritional state; prolonged low-calorie experience shifts balance between positive and negative reinforcing signals, diminishing ability to learn to avoid odor cues signaling negative outcomes
techniques_used: ['GRASP (splitGFP reconstitution)', 'Optogenetics (bPAC, CsChrimson)', 'Temperature-sensitive shibire (synaptic transmission block)', 'Olfactory conditioning (T-maze)', 'CaLexA activity reporter', 'RNAi (knockdown)', 'CAFE assay (food uptake)']
summary: MB188B-Gal4-positive DANs structurally reduce synaptic connectivity with KCs after low-caloric food exposure via cAMP-dependent mechanisms involving AstA receptors; this plasticity specifically impairs aversive learning while sparing appetitive learning.

paper_title: The caloric value of food intake structurally adjusts a neuronal mushroom body circuit mediating olfactory learning in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Hypo-, iso-, and hypercaloric food exposure (7 days)
neural_activity: No change in splitGFP reconstitution with Kenyon cells (KCs) under different feeding conditions
behavioral_consequence: Not explicitly tested for behavior in isolation; used as control for connectivity specificity
ecological_context: Serves as control indicating specificity of caloric plasticity to MB188B-Gal4+ subset
summary: Connectivity between R58E02-Gal4+ DANs and KCs remains stable regardless of caloric food intake.

paper_title: The caloric value of food intake structurally adjusts a neuronal mushroom body circuit mediating olfactory learning in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Hypo-, iso-, and hypercaloric food exposure (7 days)
neural_activity: Increased splitGFP reconstitution with Kenyon cells (KCs) under hypercaloric conditions
behavioral_consequence: Not explicitly tested for behavior in isolation; used as control for connectivity specificity
ecological_context: Serves as control indicating differential plasticity in PPL1 vs PAM clusters
summary: Connectivity between TH-Gal4+ DANs and KCs is enhanced under hypercaloric conditions.

paper_title: The caloric value of food intake structurally adjusts a neuronal mushroom body circuit mediating olfactory learning in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Electric shock (aversive unconditioned stimulus); 7 days hypo-, iso-, or hypercaloric food exposure
neural_activity: Increased cAMP levels in γ2 and γ3 compartments upon electric shock (G-Flamp1 sensor); no morphological changes in axons, dendrites, or boutons under food conditions (MARCM)
behavioral_consequence: Required for associative olfactory learning; learning outcomes shift depending on DAN-KC connectivity changes
ecological_context: Circuit integration of sensory and reward/punishment signals for adaptive behavioral decisions
techniques_used: ['Two-photon cAMP imaging (G-Flamp1)', 'MARCM (single-cell analysis)', 'GRASP (synaptic contact quantification)', 'Olfactory conditioning (T-maze)']
summary: KC cAMP response to electric shock is observed in γ2/γ3 compartments; KC anatomy remains stable under dietary changes, though synaptic input from specific DANs is altered.

paper_title: Drosophila learning and memory centers and the actions of drugs of abuse
experimental_stimulus: Ethanol exposure (acute, rapid tolerance, associative memory)
neural_activity: Mef2 signaling required in α/β lobes for rapid tolerance. Sirt1 required in α/β lobes for rapid tolerance and ethanol sensitivity. Neuronal activity required for rapid tolerance (blocking transmission with tetanus toxin or hyperpolarizing with Kir2.1 reduces tolerance).
behavioral_consequence: Decreased ethanol sensitivity when Mef2 or Sirt1 inhibited. Reduced rapid tolerance when neuronal activity blocked. Required for retrieval of alcohol-associative preference memory.
ecological_context: Ethanol sensitivity and tolerance are relevant to adaptation to fermenting environments; ethanol preference is linked to egg-laying on fermentation substrates.
summary: α/β Kenyon cells utilize Mef2 and Sirt1 signaling to regulate ethanol sensitivity and rapid tolerance, and their activity is required for the retrieval of alcohol-associative memory.

paper_title: Drosophila learning and memory centers and the actions of drugs of abuse
experimental_stimulus: Ethanol exposure (oviposition, associative memory)
neural_activity: Blocking presynaptic release decreases oviposition preference. Required for consolidation of alcohol-associative preference memory.
behavioral_consequence: Decreased preference for laying eggs on ethanol substrates. Impaired consolidation of alcohol-associative preference.
ecological_context: Female flies prefer to lay eggs on ethanol-containing substrates to protect young and provide food source in fermentation sites.
summary: α′/β′ Kenyon cells are necessary for oviposition preference on ethanol substrates and for the consolidation of alcohol-associative memories.

paper_title: Drosophila learning and memory centers and the actions of drugs of abuse
experimental_stimulus: Ethanol exposure (associative memory, habituation)
neural_activity: Activity required for acquisition of alcohol-associative preference. Rutabaga adenylyl cyclase in MBs required for habituation of ethanol odor startle response.
behavioral_consequence: Deficits in acquiring alcohol-associative preference. Failure to habituate to ethanol odor startle response.
ecological_context: Habituation to drug-related cues reduces the impact of environmental information during drug use.
summary: γ Kenyon cells are required for the acquisition of alcohol-associative preference, while general MB Rutabaga function mediates habituation to ethanol odor.

paper_title: Drosophila learning and memory centers and the actions of drugs of abuse
experimental_stimulus: Ethanol (associative memory retrieval)
neural_activity: Activity promotes retrieval of ethanol reward memories.
behavioral_consequence: Promotes retrieval of ethanol reward memories.
ecological_context: Supports cue-induced ethanol seeking behavior.
summary: β2β′2a MBON activity promotes the retrieval of ethanol reward memories.

paper_title: Drosophila learning and memory centers and the actions of drugs of abuse
experimental_stimulus: Ethanol (associative memory consolidation)
neural_activity: Activity inhibits ethanol reward memory formation during consolidation.
behavioral_consequence: Inhibits ethanol reward memory formation during consolidation.
ecological_context: Regulates memory strength and formation during drug experience.
summary: β′2mp MBON activity inhibits ethanol reward memory formation during the consolidation phase.

paper_title: Drosophila learning and memory centers and the actions of drugs of abuse
experimental_stimulus: Ethanol (associative memory, preference)
neural_activity: Activity required during acquisition and retrieval of alcohol-associative preference. Innervate β′2a and α′2 zones.
behavioral_consequence: Required for acquisition and retrieval of alcohol-associative preference.
ecological_context: Encodes reward associated with ethanol cues.
summary: PAM cluster dopamine neurons are required for the acquisition and retrieval of alcohol-associative preference memories.

paper_title: Drosophila learning and memory centers and the actions of drugs of abuse
experimental_stimulus: Ethanol (associative memory, oviposition)
neural_activity: Other dopamine neurons required specifically during retrieval of alcohol-associative preference. Positive and negative valence DANs promote and inhibit oviposition choice, respectively.
behavioral_consequence: Required for retrieval of aversion/reward memory. Promote or inhibit egg-laying preference on ethanol substrates.
ecological_context: Valence assignment to ethanol cues influences reproductive choices in fermentation environments.
summary: Non-PAM dopamine neurons are required for retrieval of alcohol-associative preference, and valence-specific dopamine neurons regulate ethanol oviposition choice.

paper_title: Drosophila learning and memory centers and the actions of drugs of abuse
experimental_stimulus: Ethanol (rapid tolerance)
neural_activity: Octopamine-deficient mutant TbhM18 shows deficit in rapid tolerance.
behavioral_consequence: Deficit in developing rapid ethanol tolerance.
ecological_context: Tolerance affects response to environmental ethanol levels.
summary: Octopamine signaling via tyramine β-hydroxylase is required for the development of rapid ethanol tolerance.

paper_title: Drosophila learning and memory centers and the actions of drugs of abuse
experimental_stimulus: Ethanol odor (innate attraction)
neural_activity: Neurons expressing serotonin regulate ethanol odor attraction. Single pair counteracts serotonergic inhibition of attraction.
behavioral_consequence: Regulates innate olfactory attraction to ethanol.
ecological_context: Innate attraction to fermentation odors guides flies to food and mates.
summary: Serotonin neurons regulate innate olfactory attraction to ethanol, with specific pairs counteracting inhibition of this behavior.

paper_title: Drosophila learning and memory centers and the actions of drugs of abuse
experimental_stimulus: Ethanol (tolerance, memory)
neural_activity: GABAergic signals from DPMs and octopaminergic signals from APLs implicated in ethanol tolerance. ASM and ARM encoded by APL, DPM, and KC circuitry.
behavioral_consequence: Required for ethanol tolerance and memory states (ASM/ARM).
ecological_context: Support learning and memory mechanisms involved in drug experience.
summary: APL and DPM interneurons provide signals to Kenyon cells that are implicated in ethanol tolerance and memory encoding.

paper_title: Cyclic nucleotide‐induced bidirectional long‐term synaptic plasticity in Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation paired with focal dopamine injection (1 mM), forskolin injection (cAMP pathway activator), or BAY 41–2272 injection (cGMP pathway agonist); paired-pulse light stimulation.
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation evoked EPSCs in MBON-γ1pedc. Pairing with dopamine or forskolin induced presynaptic Long-Term Depression (LTD) with increased paired-pulse ratio (PPR). Pairing with BAY 41–2272 induced presynaptic Long-Term Potentiation (LTP) with decreased PPR. Two-photon cAMP imaging showed no synergistic cAMP increase between KC activity and dopamine/forskolin.
ecological_context: Olfactory associative learning and memory formation.
techniques_used: ['Whole-cell voltage-clamp electrophysiology', 'Optogenetics', 'Two-photon cAMP imaging', 'Focal drug application', 'Pharmacological inhibition']
summary: γ KC-to-MBON synapses exhibit bidirectional plasticity: dopamine or cAMP pathway activation paired with KC activity induces presynaptic LTD, while cGMP pathway activation paired with KC activity induces presynaptic LTP. cAMP increase alone is insufficient for LTD; concurrent KC activity is required. cAMP levels do not show synergy between KC activity and dopamine input.

paper_title: Cyclic nucleotide‐induced bidirectional long‐term synaptic plasticity in Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation paired with focal dopamine injection (1 mM), forskolin injection, or BAY 41–2272 injection.
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation evoked EPSCs in MBON-γ1pedc. Pairing with dopamine induced transient presynaptic LTD (recovery within ~10 min). Pairing with BAY 41–2272 induced presynaptic LTP. Forskolin alone failed to induce consistent LTD without KC activity.
ecological_context: Olfactory associative learning and memory formation.
summary: α/β KC-to-MBON synapses show similar bidirectional plasticity mechanisms to γ KCs, but dopamine-induced LTD is transient compared to the long-lasting LTD in γ KCs. cGMP-induced LTP duration is similar to γ KCs.

paper_title: Cyclic nucleotide‐induced bidirectional long‐term synaptic plasticity in Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Whole-cell recordings while receiving input from optogenetically stimulated KCs; focal application of dopamine, forskolin, or BAY 41–2272.
neural_activity: EPSC amplitudes and paired-pulse ratios (PPR) were measured. LTD was associated with increased PPR; LTP was associated with decreased PPR. LTD induction depended on PKA activity but not CaMKII activity.
ecological_context: Olfactory associative learning and memory formation.
summary: MBON-γ1pedc receives input from KCs where plasticity is regulated by cyclic nucleotide pathways. Dopamine-induced LTD is PKA-dependent and presynaptic, while cGMP-induced LTP is also presynaptic and requires concurrent KC activation.

paper_title: Dopamine-Dependent Plasticity Is Heterogeneously Expressed by Presynaptic Calcium Activity across Individual Boutons of the Drosophila Mushroom Body
experimental_stimulus: 1-second odor pulses (MCH, OCT) paired with optogenetic activation of dopaminergic neurons (DANs)
neural_activity: Bulk Ca2+ activity in KC axon bundles remained unchanged after pairing. Acetylcholine (ACh) release in the γ5 compartment was depressed. Individual presynaptic boutons showed heterogeneous Ca2+ plasticity: strongly responding boutons underwent depression, while weakly responding boutons underwent potentiation.
techniques_used: ['In vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging', 'ACh3.0 sensor imaging', 'Optogenetics']
summary: Odor-DAN pairing induced compartment-specific depression of ACh release in KC axons, while bulk Ca2+ activity remained unchanged. Individual bouton imaging revealed heterogeneous Ca2+ plasticity where strong responders depressed and weak responders potentiated.

paper_title: Dopamine-Dependent Plasticity Is Heterogeneously Expressed by Presynaptic Calcium Activity across Individual Boutons of the Drosophila Mushroom Body
experimental_stimulus: 1-second odor pulses (MCH, OCT) paired with optogenetic activation of corresponding DANs
neural_activity: Odor-evoked Ca2+ activity in MBON-γ5β'2a and MBON-γ1pedc dendrites was depressed for the paired odor and potentiated for the reversal odor.
summary: Odor-DAN pairing induced odor-specific depression of Ca2+ activity in MBON dendrites of γ5 and γ1pedc compartments.

paper_title: Dopamine-Dependent Plasticity Is Heterogeneously Expressed by Presynaptic Calcium Activity across Individual Boutons of the Drosophila Mushroom Body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (625 nm light, 4 pulses at 2 Hz) timed with odor presentation
neural_activity: Axonal activation in specific MB compartments (γ5, β'2, γ1pedc) via Chrimson expression.
summary: Optogenetic activation of PAM and PPL1 DAN subsets during odor presentation successfully induced compartment-specific synaptic plasticity in KCs and MBONs.

paper_title: Independent insulin signaling modulators govern hot avoidance under different feeding states
experimental_stimulus: Hot stimulus (thermal)
neural_activity: Calcium response (GCaMP7s) to hot stimulus; activity level was the same in sated and hungry flies
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic silencing (GtACR2) or ChAT knockdown reduced Hot Avoidance Behavior (HAB) in both sated and hungry states
ecological_context: Convey hot signals from the antenna lobe to the mushroom body calyx for thermoregulation and temperature avoidance
summary: mALT neurons transmit hot stimuli to the mushroom body via cholinergic transmission and are required for HAB in both feeding states.

paper_title: Independent insulin signaling modulators govern hot avoidance under different feeding states
experimental_stimulus: Hot stimulus (thermal)
neural_activity: Increased calcium response in sated state; significantly lower calcium response in hungry state compared to sated
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic silencing inhibited HAB in sated state but not in hungry state
ecological_context: Part of the mushroom body circuit involved in thermosensation, though less critical than α0β0 MBn during hunger
summary: γ MBn activity increases to hot stimuli in sated flies but is diminished in hungry flies; silencing affects HAB only in sated state.

paper_title: Independent insulin signaling modulators govern hot avoidance under different feeding states
experimental_stimulus: Hot stimulus (thermal)
neural_activity: Increased calcium response in sated state; significantly lower calcium response in hungry state compared to sated
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic silencing inhibited HAB in sated state but not in hungry state
ecological_context: Contribute to thermosensation and HAB regulation primarily in the sated state
summary: αβ MBn activity increases to hot stimuli in sated flies but is diminished in hungry flies; silencing affects HAB only in sated state.

paper_title: Independent insulin signaling modulators govern hot avoidance under different feeding states
experimental_stimulus: Hot stimulus (thermal)
neural_activity: Calcium response significantly higher in hungry state than sated state; InR activation reduced response, InR DN increased response
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic silencing inhibited HAB in both states; activation increased HAB in both states; Dilp2/PI3K/AKT regulates activity in sated state; Dilp6/Ras/ERK regulates activity in hungry state
ecological_context: Integrates nutritional status and hot stimulus to regulate hot avoidance behavior for thermoregulation
summary: α0β0 MBn activity is essential for HAB in both feeding states, with distinct insulin signaling pathways (Dilp2/PI3K/AKT in satiety, Dilp6/Ras/ERK in hunger) modulating their response.

paper_title: Independent insulin signaling modulators govern hot avoidance under different feeding states
experimental_stimulus: Hot stimulus (thermal)
neural_activity: Calcium response to hot stimulus; response increased in hungry state compared to sated state
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic silencing disrupted HAB in both sated and hungry states
ecological_context: Output neuron transmitting integrated hot avoidance information from α0β0 MBn
summary: MBON-α03 responds to hot stimuli and is necessary for executing Hot Avoidance Behavior in both feeding states.

paper_title: Independent insulin signaling modulators govern hot avoidance under different feeding states
experimental_stimulus: Hot stimulus (thermal)
neural_activity: Calcium response to hot stimulus; response increased in hungry state compared to sated state
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic silencing disrupted HAB in both sated and hungry states
ecological_context: Output neuron transmitting integrated hot avoidance information from α0β0 MBn
summary: MBON-β01 responds to hot stimuli and is necessary for executing Hot Avoidance Behavior in both feeding states.

paper_title: Independent insulin signaling modulators govern hot avoidance under different feeding states
experimental_stimulus: Nutritional status (sated vs hungry); Hot stimulus (tested for responsiveness)
neural_activity: Dilp2 accumulation observed in IPCs during hungry state (immunostaining); IPCs were not responsive to hot stimulus (calcium imaging)
behavioral_consequence: Silencing dilp2 in IPCs increased HAB in sated flies but not in hungry flies
ecological_context: Regulate satiety signals via Dilp2 secretion to modulate thermosensation in the brain
summary: IPCs secrete Dilp2 to regulate α0β0 MBn activity and HAB specifically during the sated state, independent of hot stimulus input.

paper_title: Cyclic nucleotide-induced bidirectional long-term synaptic plasticity in Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) paired with focal dopamine, forskolin (cAMP pathway), or BAY 41–2272 (cGMP pathway) application
neural_activity: Optogenetic stimulation evoked EPSCs in MBON-γ1pedc. Two-photon cAMP imaging (G-Flamp1) showed no additive effect of KC activity on forskolin- or dopamine-induced cAMP elevation. KC activation alone did not evoke cAMP increase. Pairing with dopamine or forskolin induced LTD (EPSC depression, PPR increase). Pairing with BAY 41–2272 induced LTP (EPSC potentiation, PPR decrease).
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this study
ecological_context: Underlying olfactory associative learning and aversive learning (Abstract)
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics', 'Whole-cell voltage-clamp recordings', 'Two-photon cAMP imaging', 'Focal drug injection']
summary: γ KC activation paired with cAMP pathway activation induces presynaptic LTD requiring PKA. cGMP pathway activation paired with γ KC activation induces slowly developing LTP.

paper_title: Cyclic nucleotide-induced bidirectional long-term synaptic plasticity in Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) paired with focal dopamine or forskolin application
neural_activity: Optogenetic stimulation evoked EPSCs in MBON-γ1pedc. Pairing with dopamine or forskolin induced presynaptic LTD (EPSC depression, PPR increase) that was transient (recovered within ~10 min), unlike the longer lasting LTD in γ KCs. cGMP pathway activation paired with α/β KC activation induced LTP similar to γ KCs.
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this study
ecological_context: Underlying olfactory associative learning (Introduction)
summary: α/β KCs exhibit distinct plasticity duration compared to γ KCs; dopamine/forskolin-induced LTD at α/β KC synapses is transient, while cGMP-induced LTP is similar to γ KCs.

paper_title: Cyclic nucleotide-induced bidirectional long-term synaptic plasticity in Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Presynaptic KC activation (optogenetic) paired with focal dopamine, forskolin, or BAY 41–2272 application
neural_activity: Whole-cell voltage-clamp recordings from soma showed changes in EPSC amplitude and paired-pulse ratio (PPR). Dopamine/forskolin+KC pairing induced presynaptic LTD (decreased EPSC, increased PPR). BAY 41–2272+KC pairing induced presynaptic LTP (increased EPSC, decreased PPR).
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this study
ecological_context: Output neurons of the mushroom body involved in olfactory learning (Introduction)
summary: MBON-γ1pedc synapses receive bidirectional plasticity inputs: cAMP pathway induces LTD, cGMP pathway induces LTP, both requiring concurrent KC activity.

paper_title: Cyclic nucleotide-induced bidirectional long-term synaptic plasticity in Drosophila mushroom body
experimental_stimulus: Focal application of dopamine (1 mM) mimicking DAN input
neural_activity: Dopamine application induced presynaptic cAMP elevation (two-photon imaging). Paired with KC activation, it induced presynaptic LTD dependent on D1-like dopamine receptors and PKA. Dopamine alone did not induce plasticity.
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this study
ecological_context: Aversive learning signals; partner cell types of MBONs show matching innervation patterns (Abstract, Figure 4)
techniques_used: ['Focal drug injection', 'Whole-cell voltage-clamp recordings', 'Two-photon cAMP imaging', 'Pharmacological inhibition']
summary: Dopaminergic input activates cAMP/PKA pathway in KCs; pairing with KC activity induces presynaptic LTD via D1-like receptors.

paper_title: LIM-kinase 1 effects on memory abilities and male courtship song in Drosophila depend on the neuronal type
experimental_stimulus: Conditioned courtship suppression paradigm (CCSP)
neural_activity: Not measured; LIMK1 protein levels observed to be low in MB lobes via confocal microscopy
behavioral_consequence: Increased 3-h short-term memory (STM); steeper STM forgetting curve (0–30 min); no significant effect on long-term memory
ecological_context: CCSP described as 'more natural' than olfactory aversive learning; memory formation relevant to courtship experience
techniques_used: ['GAL4 x UAS RNAi', 'Confocal microscopy', 'Conditioned courtship suppression paradigm']
summary: limk1 RNA interference in Mushroom Body lobes and glia increased 3-h STM but did not affect long-term memory.

paper_title: LIM-kinase 1 effects on memory abilities and male courtship song in Drosophila depend on the neuronal type
experimental_stimulus: Conditioned courtship suppression paradigm (CCSP)
neural_activity: Not measured; LIMK1 expression suppressed via RNAi
behavioral_consequence: Impaired STM (decreased learning index within 60 min); courtship song changes included decreased pulse song index/frequency and increased period/intertrain interval/duration
ecological_context: Male courtship song recorded during interaction with fertilized female
techniques_used: ['GAL4 x UAS RNAi', 'Conditioned courtship suppression paradigm', 'Courtship song analysis']
summary: limk1 interference in cholinergic neurons impaired STM and altered courtship song parameters.

paper_title: LIM-kinase 1 effects on memory abilities and male courtship song in Drosophila depend on the neuronal type
experimental_stimulus: Conditioned courtship suppression paradigm (CCSP)
neural_activity: Not measured; LIMK1 expression suppressed via RNAi
behavioral_consequence: Dramatically impaired learning ability (learning index did not differ from zero); negative learning index at 24 h
ecological_context: Courtship memory formation dependent on these neuronal clusters
techniques_used: ['GAL4 x UAS RNAi', 'Conditioned courtship suppression paradigm']
summary: limk1 interference in DAN and SRN neurons dramatically impaired learning ability in CCSP.

paper_title: LIM-kinase 1 effects on memory abilities and male courtship song in Drosophila depend on the neuronal type
experimental_stimulus: Conditioned courtship suppression paradigm (CCSP)
neural_activity: Not measured; LIMK1 expression suppressed via RNAi
behavioral_consequence: Increased 15–60 min STM (decreased forgetting speed); courtship song changes included increased pulse song frequency and decreased period/intertrain interval/sine song parameters
ecological_context: FRN regulate mating behavior and courtship song; song parameters analyzed during female interaction

paper_title: Multisensory learning binds neurons into a cross-modal memory engram
experimental_stimulus: Multisensory training (Colour + Odour); Odour (CS+/CS-) and Colour (Blue/Green) presentation during imaging
neural_activity: After multisensory appetitive training, CS+ odour evokes depolarization (excitation) in $\gamma$5 axon compartments (two-photon voltage imaging). After aversive training, CS+ odour excites $\gamma$1 and $\gamma$5 compartments. Naive flies show odour-evoked inhibition.
behavioral_consequence: Blocking output during testing abolishes visual enhancement of memory performance and removes interference from incongruent cues. Blocking output impairs odour-only memory retrieval in multisensory trained flies but not in odour-only trained flies (UAS-Shits1).
ecological_context: Improves object recognition and memory performance in multisensory experiences.
summary: $\gamma$d KCs acquire odour-evoked excitation in axonal compartments following multisensory learning and are required for enhanced memory performance of both visual and olfactory cues.

paper_title: Multisensory learning binds neurons into a cross-modal memory engram
experimental_stimulus: Multisensory training (Colour + Odour)
neural_activity: Received predominantly visual information via dendrites in accessory calyces.
behavioral_consequence: Blocking output during testing abolishes visual enhancement of performance in the congruent protocol and removes interference of incongruence (UAS-Shits1).
ecological_context: Improves object recognition and memory performance in multisensory experiences.
summary: $\alpha\beta$p KCs are required for the visual enhancement of memory performance following multisensory learning.

paper_title: Multisensory learning binds neurons into a cross-modal memory engram
experimental_stimulus: Multisensory training (Colour + Odour); Colour presentation during imaging
neural_activity: After multisensory appetitive training, $\gamma$5 axon compartments show pronounced gain of excitation by CS+ colour (two-photon voltage imaging).
behavioral_consequence: Blocking $\gamma$-KC output during training impairs olfactory and visual retrieval after multisensory learning (UAS-Shits1).
ecological_context: Improves object recognition and memory performance in multisensory experiences.
summary: $\gamma$m KCs acquire colour-evoked excitation in $\gamma$5 axonal compartments following multisensory training.

paper_title: Multisensory learning binds neurons into a cross-modal memory engram
experimental_stimulus: Multisensory training (Colour + Odour)
neural_activity: Increased CS+ odour voltage responses in $\gamma$5 projections 6h after training (two-photon voltage imaging). Anatomical bridge between $\gamma$m and $\gamma$d KCs in $\gamma$-lobe (EM connectome).
behavioral_consequence: Blocking output during acquisition or retrieval impairs multisensory training enhancement of odour and visual retrieval memory (UAS-Shits1). No effect on unisensory odour memory.
ecological_context: Improves object recognition and memory performance in multisensory experiences.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon voltage imaging', 'Electron microscopy connectome', 'Temperature-sensitive synaptic blockade (UAS-Shits1)']
summary: DPM neurons form compartment-specific serotonergic bridges between olfactory and visual KCs, required for multisensory memory binding and retrieval.

paper_title: Multisensory learning binds neurons into a cross-modal memory engram
experimental_stimulus: Multisensory training
neural_activity: Makes synapses along DPM branches in the $\gamma$-lobe. Expresses Dop2R dopamine receptor.
behavioral_consequence: Knocking down Dop2R in APL neurons abolishes multisensory enhancement of olfactory retrieval performance. Mild defect for odour memory following olfactory appetitive conditioning.
ecological_context: Improves object recognition and memory performance in multisensory experiences.
summary: APL neurons provide GABAergic inhibition to DPM neurons; dopamine-inhibited APL function permits DPM-mediated binding of KC streams.

paper_title: Mushroom body output neurons MBON-a1/a2 define an odor intensity channel that regulates behavioral odor discrimination learning in larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Ethyl acetate (EA) odor pulses at varying dilutions (1/10 to 1/2000); optogenetic activation using CsChrimson and amber light during conditioning
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (jRCaMP1b) showed responses to EA concentrations higher than 1/200 dilution; response magnitude decreased with increasing dilution (concentration-dependent). Responses were odor-concentration dependent compared to concentration-invariant KCs.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation during conditioning impaired odor discrimination learning (fructose-reward assay) compared to controls at both high and low odor concentrations tested.
ecological_context: Behavioral odor discrimination learning in larval Drosophila.
techniques_used: ['Calcium imaging (jRCaMP1b)', 'Optogenetics (CsChrimson)', 'Behavioral assay (fructose-reward odor-choice learning)', 'Anatomical analysis (GRASP, CATMAID)']
summary: MBON-a1/a2 neurons exhibit concentration-dependent calcium responses to odors (active at >1/200 dilution) and optogenetic activation impairs odor discrimination learning in larvae.

paper_title: Mushroom body output neurons MBON-a1/a2 define an odor intensity channel that regulates behavioral odor discrimination learning in larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Ethyl acetate (EA) odor pulses at varying dilutions (1/10 to 1/10000)
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (jRCaMP1b) showed concentration-invariant responses; detected at dilutions as low as 10^-4. Peak responses were similar across dilutions 1/10 to 1/1000.
behavioral_consequence: Not tested.
ecological_context: Not tested.
summary: KCs show concentration-invariant calcium responses to ethyl acetate, contrasting with the concentration-dependent responses of MBON-a1/a2.

paper_title: Mushroom body output neurons MBON-a1/a2 define an odor intensity channel that regulates behavioral odor discrimination learning in larval Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Blue light (470 nm) to activate ChR2-XXL; combined with odor pulse (EA 1/10 dilution)
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation of Tdc2-LexA neurons showed an additive effect on MBON-a1/a2 calcium responses but did not significantly modulate or enhance odor-evoked activity.
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this paper (behavioral role cited from previous work).
ecological_context: Not tested.
summary: Optogenetic activation of calyx-innervating octopaminergic neurons (sVUM1) did not show a detectable effect on MBON-a1/a2 odor responses, suggesting no significant modulation under tested conditions.

paper_title: Acetylcholine deficit causes dysfunctional inhibitory control in an aging-dependent manner
experimental_stimulus: Strong airflow (10 L/min) in Go/No-Go task
neural_activity: ChAT expression reduced via UAS-ChAT RNAi (MB009B or NP1131 drivers); ChAT immunoreactivity quantified via immunohistochemistry
behavioral_consequence: Aging-dependent increase in Loss of Inhibition Events (LIEs); reduced movement suppression compared to controls
ecological_context: Inhibitory control described as critical for fitness and survival
summary: MB γ neurons are a chief neural site for acetylcholine's contribution to aging-associated loss of inhibitory control; ChAT knockdown in these neurons increases loss of inhibition events with aging.

paper_title: Acetylcholine deficit causes dysfunctional inhibitory control in an aging-dependent manner

ecological_context: Sustained movement suppression
summary: MBON-γ2α’1 cholinergic transmission is critical for sustained movement suppression; ChAT knockdown increases loss of inhibition events at all ages.

paper_title: Acetylcholine deficit causes dysfunctional inhibitory control in an aging-dependent manner

ecological_context: N/A

paper_title: Acetylcholine deficit causes dysfunctional inhibitory control in an aging-dependent manner

behavioral_consequence: Strong rolling behavior (C-shape curl, body axis turn); observed in over 50% of larvae during 5s stimulus

paper_title: High-throughput automated methods for classical and operant conditioning of Drosophila larvae

paper_title: High-throughput automated methods for classical and operant conditioning of Drosophila larvae

paper_title: Age‐related memory vulnerability to interfering stimuli is caused by gradual loss of MAPK‐dependent protection in Drosophila
cell_type: Mushroom Body (MB) neurons (specifically MB γ neurons)
experimental_stimulus: Aversive olfactory classical conditioning (odor paired with electric shock); post-learning interfering stimuli (electric shock, cooling)
neural_activity: Learning-induced phosphorylated MAPK (P-MAPK) levels increased within 15 min after learning in young flies (2-3 days), but declined with age and were absent in 25-day-old flies (Western blot/Immunofluorescence)
behavioral_consequence: 1h memory performance (T-maze avoidance) impaired in aged flies; acute Raf-GOF expression in MB neurons restored 1h memory and suppressed age-related memory impairment (AMI) in aged flies (Behavioral assay)
summary: MB γ neurons exhibit learning-induced MAPK activation in young flies that declines with age; restoring MAPK signaling via Raf-GOF expression in MB neurons suppresses age-related memory impairment and vulnerability to interfering stimuli.

paper_title: Age‐related memory vulnerability to interfering stimuli is caused by gradual loss of MAPK‐dependent protection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory conditioning (CS+ OCT + Electric Shock; CS- MCH)
neural_activity: Calcium activity (GCaMP6f) in dendritic region decreased for CS+ relative to CS- 5 min and 1h after training in young flies; this 1h memory trace was absent in aged flies (19-21 days); trace restored by Raf-GOF expression (Two-photon functional calcium imaging)
behavioral_consequence: Calcium trace correlates with labile aversive olfactory memory; age-related loss of trace coincides with 1h memory impairment
summary: MBON-γ1pedc>α/β neurons show a learning-induced calcium depression trace correlating with memory; this trace is impaired in aged flies and restored by acute MAPK pathway activation.

paper_title: Temporally and Spatially Localized PKA Activity within Learning and Memory Circuitry Regulated by Network Feedback
experimental_stimulus: Developmental age (0 vs 7 days post-eclosion), Sex (Male vs Female), Genetic manipulations (FMRP/Rugose loss, Meng-Po overexpression/RNAi, shibire/TNT block), Temperature (20, 25, 33°C for shibire)
neural_activity: PKA activity measured via PKA-SPARK::GFP puncta in axon lobes; low at 0 dpe, high at 7 dpe in a'1 and b'1 fields; higher in females than males at 7 dpe; reduced by FMRP/Rugose loss; enhanced by Meng-Po overexpression (including ectopic g3 activity); spatially expanded by synaptic output block (shibire/TNT)
ecological_context: Activity increases during postcritical period adulthood when sensory input experience remodels MB circuitry
summary: PKA activity in Mushroom Body Kenyon Cell axon lobes is age-dependent (increasing from 0 to 7 dpe) and sex-dependent (higher in females), regulated by FMRP/Rugose and Meng-Po, and spatially restricted by synaptic output feedback.

paper_title: insomniac links the development and function of a sleep-regulatory circuit
cell_type: Mushroom Body (MB) neurons (c253, c309, pan-MB split-Gal4)
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTrpA1, 28.5°C shift)
neural_activity: In wild-type, activation promotes sleep; in inc mutants, sleep-promoting function is abolished (no significant change in sleep compared to controls)
behavioral_consequence: Wild-type: Strong increase in sleep. inc mutants: No significant change in sleep. inc mutants show baseline short sleep phenotype
ecological_context: MB integrates sensory stimuli and regulates sleep; may filter sensory stimuli to promote sleep in appropriate environmental conditions
summary: MB neurons promote sleep upon activation in wild-type flies, but this function is lost in inc mutants due to developmental anatomical defects.

paper_title: insomniac links the development and function of a sleep-regulatory circuit
cell_type: Mushroom Body neuron subtypes (alpha'/beta' and alpha/beta c)
experimental_stimulus: Developmental analysis (Immunohistochemistry in control vs. inc mutants)
neural_activity: alpha'/beta' neurons increased 58% in number with axonal defects (10% missing lobes); alpha/beta c neurons doubled in number with severe axonal defects (86% failed to project into lobes)
behavioral_consequence: Anatomical defects in late-born MB neurons correlate with loss of sleep-promoting function in adults
ecological_context: Late-born MB neurons develop during the critical period (late 3rd instar/pupal) when inc acts to influence adult sleep
summary: Late-born MB neuron subtypes are overproduced and have axonal projection defects in inc mutants, impairing circuit assembly.

paper_title: insomniac links the development and function of a sleep-regulatory circuit
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic activation (dTrpA1, 28.5°C shift)
neural_activity: Activation increases sleep
behavioral_consequence: Increased sleep in both wild-type and inc mutants
ecological_context: Sleep regulation
summary: EB R5 neurons promote sleep, and this function remains intact in inc mutants.

paper_title: insomniac links the development and function of a sleep-regulatory circuit

paper_title: Olfactory sensory preconditioning in Drosophila: role of memory forgetting in gating S1/S2 associations
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory stimuli: 4-methylcyclohexanol (MCH), 3-octanol (OCT), penthyl acetate (PA), ethyl lactate (EL). Stimuli included preconditioning phases (S1-S2 pairing with varying interstimulus intervals) and aversive conditioning phases (S1 paired with electric shock).
neural_activity: Calcium responses measured via in vivo confocal imaging (GCaMP6f). Responses to trained odor S1 were depressed. In control flies with 1-s ISI preconditioning, responses to non-trained pre-paired odor S2 were also significantly depressed. In Rac1N17 KC flies, depression to S2 was observed with 30-s ISI (not in controls). 5-min ISI showed no depression to S2 in either group.
behavioral_consequence: Flies avoided S2 odor in a T-maze behavioral assay after 10 repeated preconditioning pairings (Control with 1-s ISI; Rac1N17 KC flies with 30-s ISI). A single pairing induced neural depression but no behavioral aversive memory to S2.
ecological_context: The paper states learning and memory are fundamental for animal survival in dynamic and noisy environments. The association between neutral stimuli increases predictive power of moment-by-moment experiences, allowing inference of value to non-reinforced odors based on associative structure.
techniques_used: ['In vivo calcium imaging (confocal microscopy)', 'T-maze behavioral assay', 'Genetic manipulation (Rac1N17 in KC)']
summary: MBON-γ1pedc>α/β neurons show decreased calcium responses to a trained odor (S1) and, under specific conditions (short ISI or Rac1 inhibition), to a pre-paired non-shocked odor (S2). Behavioral aversion to S2 requires repeated pairings despite single-pairing neural plasticity.

paper_title: Olfactory sensory preconditioning in Drosophila: role of memory forgetting in gating S1/S2 associations
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory stimuli: MCH, OCT, PA, EL presented as preconditioning pairs (S1-S2) followed by aversive conditioning of S1 with electric shock.
neural_activity: Calcium response dynamics in KC γ lobes showed no change when Rac1N17 was expressed (recorded at 28 Hz). Inhibition of Rac1 in KC facilitated the association between S1/S2 odors.
behavioral_consequence: Expression of dominant-negative Rac1 (Rac1N17) in adult KC allowed flies to exhibit aversive memory to the non-shocked pre-paired odor (S2) with a 30-s interstimulus interval, whereas control flies did not show this memory at that interval.
ecological_context: Inhibition of Rac1 lengthens the olfactory 'sensory buffer', allowing the establishment of associations between odors presented in sequence, which supports inferred value learning in dynamic environments.
techniques_used: ['Genetic manipulation (Rac1N17 in KC using TARGET system)', 'In vivo calcium imaging (GCaMP6f)', 'T-maze behavioral assay']
summary: Rac1 inhibition in Kenyon Cells facilitates the formation of associative links between sequentially presented odors, evidenced by behavioral memory to a non-shocked pre-paired odor at longer time intervals.

paper_title: Serotonin Signals Modulate Mushroom Body Output Neurons for Sustaining Water-Reward Long-Term Memory in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Water reward training (odor-water pairing) in water-deprived flies
neural_activity: Neurotransmission required for consolidation; 5HT biosynthesis inhibited via RNAi (Trh/Ddc); neurotransmission blocked via temperature-sensitive shits
behavioral_consequence: Disrupted 24-h water-reward long-term memory (wLTM) if inhibition occurred during 0-4 h post-training; no effect if blocked during acquisition or retrieval; normal odor avoidance and water preference
ecological_context: Thirst-dependent water memory allows thirsty animals to behave appropriately when facing life-supporting experienced events again
summary: DPM neurons release serotonin required for wLTM consolidation during the 0-4 h period after training in thirsty flies.

paper_title: Serotonin Signals Modulate Mushroom Body Output Neurons for Sustaining Water-Reward Long-Term Memory in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Water reward training (odor-water pairing) in water-deprived flies
neural_activity: Neurotransmission required during 0-2 h post-training; 5HT receptors (5HT1A, 5HT2A, 5HT7) silenced via RNAi; GAD knockdown disrupted memory
behavioral_consequence: Blocking neurotransmission or silencing 5HT receptors during consolidation disrupted wLTM; no requirement for acquisition or retrieval; normal odor avoidance and water preference
ecological_context: Thirst-dependent water memory for life-supporting events
summary: MBON-γ3β′1 neurotransmission is required during the 0-2 h post-training window for wLTM consolidation, modulated by DPM serotonin.

behavioral_consequence: Blocking neurotransmission or silencing 5HT receptors during consolidation disrupted wLTM; no requirement for acquisition or retrieval; normal odor avoidance and water preference

paper_title: Serotonin Signals Modulate Mushroom Body Output Neurons for Sustaining Water-Reward Long-Term Memory in Drosophila
