behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation triggered bilateral wing flicks (agonistic) in isolated males and drove pursuit/aggression towards wingless males; silencing suppressed wing flicks in triads without affecting courtship. Activation towards D. simulans females elicited aggression only when perfumed with cVA.
ecological_context: Gates aggressive motor programs during male-male competition in complex social landscapes; utilizes song and pheromone cues to identify rivals.
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics (CsChrimson, GtACR1)', 'Two-photon functional imaging', 'Free behavioral assays', 'Tethered behavioral assays']
summary: pC1x neurons coordinate agonistic actions in competitive courtship; they are recruited by rival cues to drive wing flicks and aggression while courtship continues.

paper_title: Male-male interactions shape mate selection in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Red light (optogenetics)
neural_activity: Immunohistochemistry showed sexually dimorphic neurons in the ventral nerve cord.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation evoked robust bilateral wing flicks and produced an audible agonistic song; silencing suppressed wing flicks in triads while preserving courtship song.
ecological_context: Generation of agonistic wing flicks used to repel rivals and jam female auditory perception during mating competition.
summary: R85A12-GAL4 neurons in the VNC mediate bilateral wing flicks; their activation generates an agonistic song that interferes with female perception of rivals.

paper_title: Male-male interactions shape mate selection in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Playback of MF courtship song (pulse song), MMF triad recordings, Agonistic song
neural_activity: Two-photon imaging in tethered females showed robust response to MF courtship song; activity far weaker in response to MMF triads or pure bouts of agonistic song.
behavioral_consequence: Activity shifts the balance of excitation and inhibition onto vpoDNs; reduced activity in triads correlates with attenuated vaginal plate opening responses to rivals.
ecological_context: Selective tuning for conspecific courtship song IPI to assure sexual receptivity only to conspecifics; vulnerable to acoustic interference from rivals.
summary: vpoEN neurons are excitatory auditory input to vpoDNs tuned to courtship pulse song; their activity is suppressed by agonistic song interference in triads.

paper_title: Male-male interactions shape mate selection in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Playback of MF courtship song (pulse song), MMF triad recordings, Agonistic song
neural_activity: Two-photon imaging in tethered females showed preferential activation by complex acoustic environment of triads and agonistic song; weaker responses to courtship song.
behavioral_consequence: Activity shifts the balance of excitation and inhibition onto vpoDNs towards inhibition during triad playback, obscuring female perception of courtship song.
ecological_context: Detection of complex acoustic environments and interference signals during male-male competition.
summary: vpoIN neurons are inhibitory auditory input to vpoDNs sensitive to broader spectra; they are preferentially activated by agonistic song and triad noise to inhibit receptivity.

paper_title: Male-male interactions shape mate selection in Drosophila.
experimental_stimulus: Red light (optogenetics)
neural_activity: Not explicitly imaged in results, but identified as mediating pulse song production.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic inhibition suppressed unilateral wing extensions (courtship song) in courting pairs but had no effect on vigorous flicks evoked by song playback in pairs of males.
ecological_context: Mediates production of courtship song; distinct from motor pathways for agonistic wing flicks.
summary: pIP10 neurons mediate courtship song production; their inhibition affects singing but not agonistic wing flicks.

paper_title: Activity of nested neural circuits drives different courtship songs in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) in solitary males; Optogenetic stimulation of song driver (R22D03-LexA) during calcium imaging; Optogenetic activation of pIP10
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (VNC): Signals increased during pulse song transitions (sine to pulse) and decreased during sine song transitions (pulse to sine). Activity high during pulse song, low during sine song. Active during pulse song induced by pIP10 activation.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation elicited pulse but not sine song in solitary males. In paired flies, activation increased pulse song and decreased sine song. Affected song-type switch probabilities (sine-to-pulse and pulse-to-sine).
ecological_context: Part of the courtship motor circuit; pulse song influences female receptivity; involved in context-dependent song selection (e.g., distance to females).
summary: dPR1 neurons are active during pulse song but not sine song. Their optogenetic activation drives pulse song production and increases pulse song probability while decreasing sine song probability during courtship.

paper_title: Activity of nested neural circuits drives different courtship songs in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) in solitary males; Optogenetic stimulation of song driver (R22D03-LexA) during calcium imaging; Optogenetic activation of pIP10
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (VNC): Active during both pulse and sine song, with preference for sine. Signals increased during pulse-to-sine transitions; decreased only slightly during sine-to-pulse transitions. Active during pulse song induced by pIP10.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation induced sine but not pulse song in solitary males. In paired flies, activation increased sine song without changing pulse song. Specifically modified pulse-to-sine transition probability.
ecological_context: Part of the courtship motor circuit; sine song influences female receptivity; active during both song types suggesting shared motor functions with pulse song.
summary: TN1A-2 neurons are active during both pulse and sine song with a preference for sine. Their activation drives sine song and increases sine song probability during courtship.

paper_title: Activity of nested neural circuits drives different courtship songs in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) in solitary males; Optogenetic stimulation of song driver during calcium imaging; Optogenetic activation in paired flies
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (Brain): Increased calcium signals during song driver stimulation. Similar signal levels during pulse and sine song (weak preference for pulse).
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation increased both pulse and sine song in solitary and paired flies. Did not significantly affect song-type switch probabilities.
ecological_context: Descending pathway required for singing; encodes whether to sing rather than specifying song type; active during both pulse and sine song in natural courtship.
summary: pIP10 descending neurons are active during both pulse and sine song and promote overall singing behavior without strongly biasing song type switching.

paper_title: Activity of nested neural circuits drives different courtship songs in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) in solitary males; Optogenetic stimulation of song driver during calcium imaging; Optogenetic activation in paired flies
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (Brain): Strongly selective for pulse song. Signals increased during pulse song and decreased during sine song.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation induced pulse but not sine song in solitary males. In paired flies, increased pulse and decreased sine song. Affected song-type switch probabilities.
ecological_context: Descending pathway plays a key role in context-dependent song selection; provides primary input to pulse-promoting VNC neurons.
summary: pMP2 descending neurons are selectively active during pulse song and drive pulse song production while suppressing sine song during courtship.

paper_title: Activity of nested neural circuits drives different courtship songs in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic stimulation of song driver during calcium imaging
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (VNC): Both pulse-suppressing and sine-suppressing populations were active during both pulse and sine song with a weak preference for pulse. No clear reciprocal activity pattern observed.
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this paper (activation effects referenced from prior work).
ecological_context: Previously implicated in switching between pulse and sine song, but activity patterns here suggest they alone are unlikely to drive the switch.
summary: vPR9 neurons are active during both song types without a clear reciprocal activity pattern, suggesting they do not solely drive song-type switching.

paper_title: Activity of nested neural circuits drives different courtship songs in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic stimulation of song driver; Optogenetic activation of pIP10
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (VNC): Majority preferred sine song. Pulse-preferring neurons decreased activity during non-preferred song; sine-preferring neurons did not decrease significantly during pulse song. Virtually all TN1 neurons showed increased calcium signals during pulse song (pIP10 activation).
behavioral_consequence: Not directly tested in this paper.
ecological_context: Male-specific, dsx-expressing VNC neurons involved in song production.
summary: TN1 neurons exhibit nested activation patterns where both pulse- and sine-preferring subsets are active during pulse song, while sine-preferring neurons are also active during sine song.

paper_title: Activity of nested neural circuits drives different courtship songs in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic stimulation of song driver during calcium imaging
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (VNC): Some voxels active during both song types; others modulated by song type (stronger during pulse). Suppression of jGCaMP7f in fru neurons reduced pulse-selective voxels in pan-neuronal imaging.
behavioral_consequence: Not directly tested in this paper.
ecological_context: Sex determination gene regulates sexually dimorphic behaviors including courtship; fru neurons contribute to pulse-selective signals.
summary: A population of fru-expressing neurons contributes to pulse-selective signals in the song motor circuit, with only a few percent of VNC neurons having a disproportionate impact on these signals.

paper_title: Drosophila males require the longitudinal stretch receptors to tremulate their abdomen and produce substrate-borne signals during courtship
experimental_stimulus: Courtship behavior (male courting female)
behavioral_consequence: Inhibition (Clh8-Gal4>UAS-shits) increased abdominal bobbing (~15/min vs ~3/min), reduced tremulation occurrence (~6.5% vs ~21%), shortened tremulation bouts (~0.7s vs ~3s), and reduced female immobility. TRPA1 knockdown increased bobbing. Piezo knockdown/KO showed no phenotype.
ecological_context: Courtship communication: substrate-borne vibrations immobilise females to facilitate copulation.
techniques_used: ['Behavioral courtship assays (video recording)', 'Gal4/UAS (UAS-shits, RNAi)', 'Confocal microscopy/Immunohistochemistry', 'Laser vibrometry']
summary: dbd neurons, part of the Longitudinal Stretch Receptor (LSR), regulate abdominal tremulation during courtship; their inhibition causes arhythmic bobbing and reduces female immobility response.

paper_title: Drosophila males require the longitudinal stretch receptors to tremulate their abdomen and produce substrate-borne signals during courtship
experimental_stimulus: Courtship behavior (male courting female)
behavioral_consequence: Inhibition (7-33-Gal4>UAS-shits) did not lead to an increase in bobbing.
ecological_context: Abdominal body wall sensory neurons.
summary: ddaC neurons do not appear to contribute to the regulation of abdominal tremulations or bobbing during courtship.

paper_title: Male-male interactions shape mate selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) or silencing (GtACR1/Kir2.1); synthetic D. melanogaster pulse song playback (tethered); presence of wingless male target
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (jGCaMP7s) showed robust activation as males switched from courtship to agonistic displays during song playback; activity sustained throughout song playback but near baseline during courtship tracking
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation triggered bilateral wing flicks, pursuit, and lunging at wingless male targets; silencing suppressed wing flicks in male-male-female triads but did not affect courtship of females (unilateral wing extensions)
ecological_context: Wild Drosophila meet on fermenting fruits in complex social environments where females are pursued by multiple males; pC1x enables flexible alternation of courtship and aggression to repel rivals
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics (CsChrimson, GtACR1, Kir2.1)', 'Two-photon calcium imaging', 'Tethered behavioral assays', 'Free behavioral assays (MMF triads)']
summary: pC1x neurons are recruited during male-male competition to drive agonistic behaviors like wing flicks; they are silenced to suppress flicks in competition without impairing courtship.

paper_title: Male-male interactions shape mate selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) or silencing; synthetic D. melanogaster pulse song playback (tethered)
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (jGCaMP7f) showed activity correlated with onset of courtship pursuit; activity continued and elevated during agonistic displays (not suppressed)
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation drove persistent courtship pursuit (unilateral wing extensions) even in absence of food; activity maintained during interleaving of courtship and aggression
ecological_context: Sustains sexual arousal during competitive social environments where males must persist in pursuit despite rival interference
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics', 'Two-photon calcium imaging', 'Tethered behavioral assays', 'Free behavioral assays']
summary: P1a neurons promote persistent courtship pursuit and remain active during transitions to aggression, supporting sustained male sexual arousal in competition.

paper_title: Male-male interactions shape mate selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson) or silencing (Kir2.1); sound playback
neural_activity: Morphology localized to ventral nerve cord (VNC); no imaging data reported
behavioral_consequence: Activation evoked robust bilateral wing flicks and produced audible agonistic song (higher frequency, longer IPI); silencing suppressed flicks in triads while preserving courtship song production
ecological_context: Agonistic wing flicks serve to defend resources and jam rival courtship song during competition for mates on fermenting fruits
summary: R85A12 neurons in the VNC drive bilateral wing flicks and produce agonistic song, which is suppressed during silencing, impairing competitive success.

paper_title: Male-male interactions shape mate selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Playback of MF courtship song, MMF triad recordings, synthetic pulse song, agonistic song
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (jGCaMP7f) in females showed robust response to MF pulse song; far weaker response to MMF triads or pure agonistic song
behavioral_consequence: Excitatory input to vpoDNs controls vaginal plate openings; activity shifts towards inhibition during MMF playback due to interference
ecological_context: Female sexual receptivity relies on conspecific pulse song; susceptibility to interference during male competition obscures perception of rivals
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging (females)', 'Sound playback', 'Behavioral assays (vaginal plate opening/abdomen length)']
summary: vpoEN neurons respond to conspecific pulse song to drive receptivity but are suppressed by complex acoustic environments generated during male competition.

paper_title: Male-male interactions shape mate selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Playback of MF courtship song, MMF triad recordings, synthetic pulse song, agonistic song
neural_activity: Two-photon calcium imaging (jGCaMP7s) in females showed preferential activation by MMF triad environment and agonistic song; weaker response to courtship song
behavioral_consequence: Inhibitory input to vpoDNs; balance of vpoEN/vpoIN input shifts towards inhibition during MMF playback, hindering receptivity
ecological_context: Female auditory pathways are vulnerable to acoustic interference from rival males during competitive courtship
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging (females)', 'Sound playback', 'Behavioral assays (vaginal plate opening)']
summary: vpoIN neurons are activated by agonistic song and triad environments, shifting the excitation/inhibition balance to reduce female receptivity during competition.

paper_title: Male-male interactions shape mate selection in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic silencing (GtACR1); song playback
neural_activity: Not directly imaged in this study
behavioral_consequence: Silencing suppressed unilateral wing extensions (courtship song) but had no effect on wing flicks evoked by song playback
ecological_context: Produces species-specific courtship song necessary for female sexual receptivity
summary: pIP10 neurons mediate courtship song production (unilateral wing extensions) independently of agonistic flick generation.

paper_title: Two brain pathways initiate distinct forward walking programs in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson); Optogenetic activation of upstream neurons (pC1, LC9) during calcium imaging
neural_activity: Calcium transients in P9 soma upon pC1 or LC9 activation; Low or no activity during spontaneous walking
behavioral_consequence: Activation initiates forward walking with ipsilateral turning (unilateral activation causes turn to active side); Inactivation (TeTx) reduces copulation success and female following index during courtship
ecological_context: Courtship (object-directed walking towards a female during mate pursuit)
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics', 'Calcium imaging (GCaMP6s)', 'EM connectomics', 'Behavioral assays (grooming, copulation, courtship)', 'Leg kinematics (DeepLabCut)']
summary: P9 neurons initiate forward walking with ipsilateral turning. They receive functional and anatomical inputs from courtship-promoting (pC1) and visual projection (LC9) neurons. P9 is necessary for males to track females during courtship.

paper_title: Two brain pathways initiate distinct forward walking programs in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson); Green light stimulation; Spontaneous walking during in vivo imaging
neural_activity: Calcium activity correlates with high-velocity, long-duration, straight walking bouts; Low activity during turning or low-speed walking
behavioral_consequence: Activation initiates straight forward walking without turning (velocity correlates with activation strength); Inactivation reduces walking duration and velocity, eliminating fast/long walking bouts
ecological_context: Fast, straight, long forward walking bouts (discussed as potential role in exploration or escape)
techniques_used: ['Optogenetics', 'Calcium imaging (in vivo ball walking)', 'Behavioral assays (grooming, green light induced walking)', 'Leg kinematics']
summary: BPN neurons initiate straight forward walking. Their activity specifically increases during fast, long-duration straight walks. They are required for high-velocity walking but are dispensable for courtship.

paper_title: Recurrent Circuitry Sustains Drosophila Courtship Drive While Priming Itself for Satiety.
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic stimulation (TrpA1, 4.5 hours), Optogenetic stimulation/silencing (CsChrimson/GtACR1, spaced or continuous), Natural mating
neural_activity: Activation causes hyperpolarization of NPF projections (voltage imaging/ASAP1). Activation causes decreased calcium activity in dopaminergic projections to SMPa (GCaMP6s). Activity necessary during copulation to induce satiety.
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces artificial satiety (dramatic reduction in mating behaviors). Silencing prevents satiety (males become largely insatiable over 4.5-hour assay). Spaced stimulation induces stronger satiety than collapsed stimulation.
ecological_context: Report copulation status to mating drive circuitry; detect matings to induce satiety reflecting reproductive history.
summary: CRNs (R42G02-Gal4 subset in abdominal ganglion) detect copulation. Their activation inhibits NPF and dopaminergic neurons to induce satiety, while silencing prevents satiety.

paper_title: Recurrent Circuitry Sustains Drosophila Courtship Drive While Priming Itself for Satiety.
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic stimulation (TrpA1, 12 hours), Optogenetic stimulation/silencing (CsChrimson/Ort), CREB2 manipulation
neural_activity: Calcium activity in SMPa projections tracks mating drive (high in naive, low in satiated). CREB2 activity correlates with mating drive (luciferase reporter). Task7 transcript levels correlate with CREB2 activity (smFISH).
behavioral_consequence: Prolonged stimulation accelerates recovery from satiety. Long-term silencing decreases courtship. Acute stimulation does not immediately induce courtship from satiated males.
ecological_context: Part of recurrent loop sustaining mating drive; involved in recovery from satiety linked to replenishment of reproductive fluids.
summary: pCd neurons form a recurrent loop with NPF neurons. Their sustained activity and CREB2-mediated transcription of Task7 sustain mating drive and enable long-term recovery from satiety.

paper_title: Recurrent Circuitry Sustains Drosophila Courtship Drive While Priming Itself for Satiety.
experimental_stimulus: Thermogenetic stimulation (TrpA1, overnight/acute), Optogenetic stimulation/silencing (CsChrimson/Ort), CRN stimulation
neural_activity: Calcium activity in SMPa projections tracks mating drive. Stimulation increases calcium in dopaminergic projections. Hyperpolarized by CRN stimulation (voltage imaging).
behavioral_consequence: Overnight stimulation accelerates recovery from satiety. Acute stimulation reverts satiety but effect is time-locked. Silencing reduces baseline calcium activity in dopaminergic projections.
ecological_context: Output to dopaminergic neurons to promote mating drive; inhibited during mating to reduce drive.
summary: Fru+ NPF neurons promote mating drive via dopaminergic neurons. They form a recurrent loop with pCd neurons and are inhibited by CRNs during copulation.

paper_title: Recurrent Circuitry Sustains Drosophila Courtship Drive While Priming Itself for Satiety.
experimental_stimulus: Natural mating, NPF stimulation, CRN stimulation, DopR2 mutation
neural_activity: Dopaminergic calcium activity in SMPa decreases after repeated matings. Activity increases with NPF stimulation. Activity decreases with CRN stimulation.
behavioral_consequence: DopR2 mutant males cannot process motivating dopamine signal (reduced courtship drive). Dopamine tone biases P1 response to sensory input.
ecological_context: Motivating dopamine signal reflects male’s recent mating history and reproductive potency; adjusts probability of courtship for each encounter.
summary: Dopaminergic neurons projecting to SMPa encode mating drive. Their activity is reduced by mating (via CRNs) and increased by drive-promoting stimulation (via NPF/pCd).

paper_title: Unsupervised identification of the internal states that shape natural behavior
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (csChrimson + 627 nm LED, 0.46 mW/mm2, 500 ms) in solitary or courtship contexts
behavioral_consequence: In solitary males, induces production of all three song types (Pfast, Pslow, sine). In courtship, drives a general increase in song production. Does not significantly alter internal state switching probabilities (Close, Chasing, Whatever).
ecological_context: Courtship drive and acoustic signaling during mating interactions
summary: P1a activation induces song production in solitary males and increases song during courtship without altering internal state switching.

paper_title: Unsupervised identification of the internal states that shape natural behavior
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (csChrimson + 627 nm LED, 0.46 mW/mm2, 500 ms) in solitary or courtship contexts
behavioral_consequence: In solitary males, induces production of all three song types. In courtship, drives song production (increases pulse song). Biases males toward the 'Close' internal state (increases probability of entering 'Close' state). Alters feedback cue relationships to match 'Close' state filters.
ecological_context: Acoustic communication during courtship, modulating sensorimotor strategies and feedback processing
summary: pIP10 activation drives song production in solitary males and biases males toward the 'Close' internal state during courtship, altering feedback-to-song mappings.

paper_title: Unsupervised identification of the internal states that shape natural behavior
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (csChrimson + 627 nm LED, 0.46 mW/mm2, 500 ms) in solitary or courtship contexts
behavioral_consequence: In solitary males, induces only pulse song (Pfast, Pslow). In courtship, drives song production during stimulation. Does not significantly alter internal state switching probabilities.
ecological_context: Song production during courtship
summary: vPR6 activation induces pulse song in solitary males and drives song production in courtship without altering internal state switching.

paper_title: Discovery of a New Song Mode in Drosophila Reveals Hidden Structure in the Sensory and Neural Drivers of Behavior
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (csChrimson) using R71G01 and P1a drivers in solitary males
neural_activity: Not directly measured; activity manipulated via optogenetic activation
behavioral_consequence: Activation biases males toward producing sine song; Pfast and Pslow are largely suppressed. High duty cycle P1a activation leads to pulse song dominance.
ecological_context: Suggested to encode female proximity; males naturally bias toward sine song and Pslow when close to the female
summary: Optogenetic activation of P1 neurons biases males toward sine song production.

paper_title: Discovery of a New Song Mode in Drosophila Reveals Hidden Structure in the Sensory and Neural Drivers of Behavior
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (csChrimson) in solitary males; Inactivation (Kir2.1 or TNT) during courtship
neural_activity: Not directly measured; activity manipulated via optogenetics/chemical silencing
behavioral_consequence: Activation drives mainly Pfast. Inactivation decreases Pfast production (increases Pslow fraction) and disrupts distance-dependent switching. Disruption reduces copulation success.
ecological_context: Modulates pulse type choice (Pfast vs Pslow) based on distance to the female
summary: pIP10 activation drives Pfast, while inactivation reduces Pfast production and mating success.

paper_title: Discovery of a New Song Mode in Drosophila Reveals Hidden Structure in the Sensory and Neural Drivers of Behavior
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (csChrimson) in solitary males
neural_activity: Not directly measured; activity manipulated via optogenetic activation
behavioral_consequence: Drives only pulses (no sine). Pfast dominates early during stimulation; Pslow dominates late.
ecological_context: Part of the central pattern generator for pulse song
summary: Optogenetic activation of vPR6 drives pulse song with distinct temporal dynamics for Pfast and Pslow.

paper_title: Discovery of a New Song Mode in Drosophila Reveals Hidden Structure in the Sensory and Neural Drivers of Behavior
experimental_stimulus: Inactivation (Tetanus toxin, TNT) during courtship
neural_activity: Not directly measured; activity manipulated via chemical silencing
behavioral_consequence: Does not abolish Pfast, but strongly affects switching with distance (males continue singing Pslow when far). Reduces amplitude of both pulse types.
ecological_context: Contributes to distance-dependent pulse choice and amplitude control
summary: Inactivation of ps1 motor neurons disrupts distance-dependent pulse switching and reduces song amplitude.

paper_title: Shared Song Detector Neurons in Drosophila Male and Female Brains Drive Sex-Specific Behaviors
experimental_stimulus: Pulse song trains (varying IPI, carrier frequency, pulse duration, train duration), sine song (150 Hz), broadband noise
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (GCaMP6m) showed strong responses to pulse song, weak responses to sine song and noise. Tuned to conspecific pulse song features (IPI ~36 ms). Somatic and neurite responses in Lateral Junction (LJ) correlated with behavioral tuning.
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation (csChrimson) drove pulse song production in males but not females. Inactivation (TNT) in males increased sine song production. Inactivation in females altered locomotor tuning to IPI (acceleration for longer IPIs). Activation in FLyTRAP evoked sex-specific locomotor responses.
ecological_context: Natural courtship communication where males produce pulse song to court females and females respond to song during mating decisions. Social experience (group housing) sharpens pC2 tuning and behavioral selectivity in males.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging', 'Optogenetics (CsChrimson)', 'Genetic silencing (Tetanus toxin)', 'FLyTRAP behavioral assay', 'Courtship song recording']
summary: pC2l neurons serve as shared pulse song detectors in both sexes, with tuning matching behavioral tuning for pulse song features. Their activation drives sex-specific behaviors (singing in males, locomotion in both), and inactivation alters song production bias and female locomotor tuning.

paper_title: Shared Song Detector Neurons in Drosophila Male and Female Brains Drive Sex-Specific Behaviors
experimental_stimulus: Pulse song and sine song stimuli
neural_activity: Calcium imaging detected auditory responses in a subset of somas (2-3 in females, none in males). Responses in Lateral Junction (LJ) largely reflected pC2 activity.
behavioral_consequence: No behavioral manipulation (activation/inactivation) performed on pC1 neurons in this study.
ecological_context: Not explicitly tested in this study.
summary: pC1 neurons in the Lateral Junction show limited auditory calcium responses, primarily in females, but were not found to be the primary source of LJ activity or behaviorally manipulated in this paper.

paper_title: Shared Song Detector Neurons in Drosophila Male and Female Brains Drive Sex-Specific Behaviors
experimental_stimulus: Pulse song and sine song stimuli
neural_activity: Calcium imaging detected auditory responses in this female-specific ventral nerve cord-projecting neuron.
behavioral_consequence: No behavioral manipulation (activation/inactivation) performed on pMN2 neurons in this study.
ecological_context: Not explicitly tested in this study.
summary: pMN2 neurons are female-specific and exhibit auditory calcium responses in the Lateral Junction, though their contribution to behavior was not tested in this paper.

paper_title: Optogenetic Neuronal Silencing in Drosophila during Visual Processing
cell_type: Glutamatergic neurons (including motor neurons) in larvae
experimental_stimulus: Light illumination (457, 527, 640 nm)
neural_activity: Silencing confirmed by behavioral readout (cessation of crawling); body length increased due to presumed relaxation of body wall musculature
behavioral_consequence: Immediate cessation of crawling activity upon light onset; crawling resumed shortly after light offset
ecological_context: None explicitly discussed for larvae
summary: Optogenetic silencing of glutamatergic neurons in larvae using GtACR1/2 causes immediate cessation of crawling and body elongation.

paper_title: Optogenetic Neuronal Silencing in Drosophila during Visual Processing
experimental_stimulus: Light illumination (535, 565, 615 nm); Visual motion (gratings, edges)
neural_activity: Strong hyperpolarization of up to 22 mV upon optogenetic illumination (onset latency 2–3 ms); visual motion evokes graded potential changes (depolarization for preferred, hyperpolarization for null direction)
behavioral_consequence: None directly tested in behavior assay (physiological readout only)
ecological_context: Visual motion processing in the optic lobe
summary: GtACR1 expression in LPTCs mediates fast, light-sensitive hyperpolarization (up to 22 mV), enabling reversible silencing without marked interference from visual stimulation.

paper_title: Optogenetic Neuronal Silencing in Drosophila during Visual Processing
experimental_stimulus: Light illumination (615 nm); Visual motion (gratings, ON/OFF edges)
neural_activity: Silencing reduces or eliminates downstream LPTC visual responses (graded potentials) during illumination; visual responses recover after light offset
behavioral_consequence: Marked reduction or abolition of optomotor turning response (tethered walking on air-suspended ball) during illumination
ecological_context: Visual course control during navigation
techniques_used: ['Whole-cell patch-clamp recording (downstream LPTCs)', 'Tethered walking behavior assay', 'Optogenetics (GtACR1)']
summary: Optogenetic silencing of T4/T5 neurons reduces downstream LPTC responses to visual motion and abolishes the optomotor turning response in tethered flies.

paper_title: Optogenetic Neuronal Silencing in Drosophila during Visual Processing
experimental_stimulus: Light illumination (615 nm); Moving ON and OFF edges
neural_activity: Silencing almost completely abolishes downstream LPTC responses to ON edges while OFF responses remain comparable to wild type
behavioral_consequence: None directly tested in behavior assay (physiological readout only)
ecological_context: ON-motion pathway processing
summary: Selective silencing of T4 cells abolishes ON-edge responses in downstream tangential cells while preserving OFF-edge responses, demonstrating pathway specificity.

paper_title: Substrate-Borne Vibratory Communication during Courtship in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Heat activation (26-27°C for mild; 29.5°C for strong) of TRPA1 channel; Genetic loss of function (fru- mutation)
neural_activity: Not directly recorded
behavioral_consequence: Mild activation triggered quivering in solitary males and other courtship behaviors; strong activation induced quivering in females; fru- mutant males quivered less and caused wild-type females to stop less often
ecological_context: Courtship communication; substrate-borne vibrations trigger female receptivity (stopping); behavior conserved in other Drosophila species
summary: Activation of fru-expressing neurons triggers male abdominal quivering and female receptivity; loss of function reduces both quivering and female stopping.

paper_title: Substrate-Borne Vibratory Communication during Courtship in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Heat activation (26-27°C) of TRPA1 channel; Genetic loss of function (dsx- mutation)
neural_activity: Not directly recorded
behavioral_consequence: Mild activation triggered quivering in solitary males; dsx- mutant males quivered less and caused wild-type females to stop less often
ecological_context: Courtship communication; substrate-borne vibrations trigger female receptivity (stopping); behavior conserved in other Drosophila species
summary: Activation of dsx-expressing neurons triggers male abdominal quivering; loss of function reduces quivering and female receptivity.

paper_title: Substrate-Borne Vibratory Communication during Courtship in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: RNA interference reducing transformer (tra) expression in the female nervous system
neural_activity: Not directly recorded
behavioral_consequence: Masculinized females exhibited abdominal quivering; wild-type female partners tended to become immobile when paired with these males
ecological_context: Courtship communication; quivering alone (without male pheromones) sufficient to trigger female stopping; implies latent circuitry in females
summary: Neuronally masculinized females quiver and induce immobility in wild-type female partners, indicating quivering is the effective signal for receptivity.

paper_title: Automated real-time quantification of group locomotor activity in Drosophila melanogaster
cell_type: R2/R4 ring neurons of the ellipsoid body (4-67-GAL4)
experimental_stimulus: Ethanol vapor exposure (various concentrations: 40:75 to 110:05 ratio); Thermogenetic inactivation at 30-31°C; Optogenetic activation (680 nm light, 40 Hz, 25 ms pulses)
neural_activity: Not directly measured; activity manipulated via thermogenetic inactivation (shibirets1) and optogenetic activation (Chrimson)
behavioral_consequence: Inactivation during ethanol exposure significantly reduced locomotor activity and caused premature sedation (within 5 minutes). Optogenetic activation alone produced a sustained, modest increase in group activity. Activation in the context of low ethanol exposure produced a robust increase in group activity compared to controls or stimulation alone
summary: R2/R4 ellipsoid body neurons modulate ethanol-induced locomotor activity; inactivation reduces activity and causes sedation during ethanol exposure, while optogenetic activation increases group activity, particularly in the presence of ethanol.

paper_title: A rapid and dynamic role for FMRP in the plasticity of adult neurons
experimental_stimulus: Temperature shift (19°C to 30°C) to induce UAS-Fmr1 or UAS-shFmr1 expression at Zeitgeber times ZT10 or ZT22; Light:Dark cycles (12:12 LD)
neural_activity: 3D spread of s-LNv projections (structural plasticity) expanded at dawn (ZT2) and retracted at dusk (ZT14) in controls. FMRP overexpression blocked expansion at dawn. FMRP knockdown prevented retraction at dusk. Hr38-Tomato (activity reporter) levels were unchanged.
ecological_context: s-LNvs are circadian pacemaker neurons that control 24-hour rhythms in adult flies
summary: FMRP acutely regulates s-LNv structural plasticity by repressing sif mRNA translation, blocking expansion at dawn when overexpressed and preventing retraction at dusk when knocked down.

paper_title: Temporally and spatially partitioned neuropeptide release from individual clock neurons
cell_type: s-LNv clock neurons (small ventral lateral neurons)
experimental_stimulus: Entrainment to 12L:12D light-dark cycles; application of Ca2+ channel blocker Cd2+; application of IP3 receptor blocker Xestospongin C; genetic expression of IP3 sponge.
neural_activity: Synaptic neuropeptide release peaks at ZT3 (3 hours after sunrise) and requires Ca2+ influx (inhibited by Cd2+). Somatic neuropeptide release peaks at ZT23 (late night) and requires IP3 signaling (inhibited by Xestospongin C/IP3 sponge) but is independent of extracellular Ca2+ influx (unaffected by Cd2+). Measured using FAP-based neuropeptide release imaging.
behavioral_consequence: Disruption of somatic IP3 signaling in the LNv neuron population (which includes s-LNv) reduced the number of flies maintaining rhythmic locomotor behavior, increased daily activity, reduced total sleep time, and shortened sleep bout length. Morning anticipation and locomotor period remained unaffected.
ecological_context: Regulation of daily locomotor activity and sleep-wake balance in circadian rhythms.
techniques_used: ['FAP-based neuropeptide release imaging', 'Pharmacology (Cd2+, Xestospongin C)', 'Behavioral activity monitoring (DAM2)', 'Genetic manipulation (IP3 sponge)']
summary: s-LNv clock neurons exhibit temporally partitioned neuropeptide release: terminals release at ZT3 via Ca2+ influx, while the soma releases at ZT23 via IP3 signaling. Disruption of somatic release reduces locomotor rhythmicity and sleep consolidation.

paper_title: Temporally and spatially partitioned neuropeptide release from individual clock neurons
cell_type: l-LNv clock neurons (large ventral lateral neurons)
experimental_stimulus: Entrainment to 12L:12D light-dark cycles; application of Ca2+ channel blocker Cd2+; application of IP3 receptor blocker Xestospongin C; genetic expression of IP3 sponge.
neural_activity: Synaptic neuropeptide release peaks at ZT23 (late night) and requires Ca2+ influx (inhibited by Cd2+). Somatic neuropeptide release is elevated between ZT18 and ZT23 and requires IP3 signaling (inhibited by Xestospongin C/IP3 sponge) but is independent of extracellular Ca2+ influx (unaffected by Cd2+). Measured using FAP-based neuropeptide release imaging.
behavioral_consequence: Disruption of somatic IP3 signaling in the LNv neuron population (which includes l-LNv) reduced the number of flies maintaining rhythmic locomotor behavior, increased daily activity, reduced total sleep time, and shortened sleep bout length. Morning anticipation and locomotor period remained unaffected.
ecological_context: Regulation of daily locomotor activity and sleep-wake balance in circadian rhythms.
techniques_used: ['FAP-based neuropeptide release imaging', 'Pharmacology (Cd2+, Xestospongin C)', 'Behavioral activity monitoring (DAM2)', 'Genetic manipulation (IP3 sponge)']
summary: l-LNv clock neurons exhibit temporally partitioned neuropeptide release: terminals release at ZT23 via Ca2+ influx, while the soma releases between ZT18-ZT23 via IP3 signaling. Disruption of somatic release reduces locomotor rhythmicity and sleep consolidation.

paper_title: The CCHamide1 Neuropeptide Expressed in the Anterior Dorsal Neuron 1 Conveys a Circadian Signal to the Ventral Lateral Neurons in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Circadian light-dark (LD) and constant darkness (DD) cycles; CCHa1 knockdown/knockout manipulation
neural_activity: Rhythmic expression of CCHa1 neuropeptide in cell bodies (peak at ZT17 in LD). CCHa1 terminals project along s-LNv dorsal projections. Knockdown confirmed by immunostaining.
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown or knockout leads to generally low activity levels with a special reduction of morning activity. Morning activity bout is narrower with earlier offset. Flies exhibit longer siesta duration. Activity is phase-advanced in LD and phase-delayed in DD compared to controls.
summary: DN1a neurons express rhythmic CCHa1 neuropeptide; loss of CCHa1 reduces morning activity, shortens the morning activity bout, and increases siesta duration in circadian conditions.

paper_title: The CCHamide1 Neuropeptide Expressed in the Anterior Dorsal Neuron 1 Conveys a Circadian Signal to the Ventral Lateral Neurons in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Exogenous CCHa1 peptide application (10^-5 M); Endogenous CCHa1 signaling from DN1a; CCHa1-R knockdown
neural_activity: Significant increase in intracellular cAMP levels upon CCHa1 application (Live cAMP imaging). Response reduced when CCHa1-R is knocked down. PDP1 oscillations phase-advanced in morning (LD) and phase-delayed (DD) when CCHa1 is knocked down. PDF levels in terminals are higher at ZT11 in CCHa1 mutants.
behavioral_consequence: Changes in PDP1 oscillations and PDF levels correlate with reduced morning activity and altered activity timing (phase advance/delay) observed in CCHa1 knockdown flies.
summary: s-LNv neurons express CCHa1 receptor and increase cAMP upon CCHa1 application; CCHa1 signaling modulates PDP1 oscillation phase and PDF levels in s-LNv to regulate morning activity timing.

paper_title: The CCHamide1 Neuropeptide Expressed in the Anterior Dorsal Neuron 1 Conveys a Circadian Signal to the Ventral Lateral Neurons in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Exogenous CCHa1 peptide application (10^-5 M)
neural_activity: Express CCHa1-R strongly but do not show significant increase in cAMP levels upon CCHa1 application. Show very low levels of PDP1 protein.
summary: l-LNv neurons express CCHa1 receptor but do not show cAMP increase upon CCHa1 peptide application despite strong receptor expression.

paper_title: Drosophila Free-Running Rhythms Require Intercellular Communication
experimental_stimulus: Light-Dark (LD) cycles (12h:12h) and Constant Darkness (DD)
neural_activity: tim mRNA oscillates with trough at ZT3 and peak at ZT15 in LD; oscillations persist in DD for s-LNvs (in situ hybridization). In Pdf^01 mutants, molecular oscillations damp in DD. Biotinylated PDF peptide binds to the surface of LNvs (in vitro peptide binding assay, confocal microscopy).
behavioral_consequence: Rescuing LNv molecular clocks alone in cyc^01 mutants fails to restore locomotor activity rhythms (flies arrhythmic in LD and DD, locomotor activity monitoring). Pdf^01 mutants lose locomotor rhythmicity gradually in DD.
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed in paper (experimental focus on free-running conditions)
techniques_used: ['pdf-GAL4 driver', 'UAS-CYC transgene', 'whole-mount in situ hybridization', 'locomotor activity monitoring', 'in vitro peptide binding assay']
summary: LNvs exhibit robust tim mRNA oscillations in LD and DD. However, LNvs alone are insufficient to support locomotor activity rhythms. PDF peptide binds to LNv surface, and loss of PDF (Pdf^01 mutant) causes molecular oscillations in LNvs to damp in DD, paralleling behavioral damping.

paper_title: Drosophila Free-Running Rhythms Require Intercellular Communication
cell_type: Dorsal Neurons (DNs) and Dorsal Lateral Neurons (LNds)
experimental_stimulus: Light-Dark (LD) cycles and Constant Darkness (DD) (up to 8 days)
neural_activity: tim and cry mRNA oscillate robustly in all brain clock neurons including DNs and LNds in DD (in situ hybridization). In Pdf^01 mutants, molecular oscillations in these neurons damp in DD. Biotinylated PDF peptide binds to DN3 and other clock neurons (in vitro peptide binding assay).
behavioral_consequence: These neurons work cooperatively with LNvs to support circadian locomotor activity rhythms (inferred from network requirement in DD).
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed in paper (experimental focus on free-running conditions)
techniques_used: ['whole-mount in situ hybridization', 'locomotor activity monitoring', 'in vitro peptide binding assay']
summary: All brain clock neurons including DNs and LNds manifest robust molecular oscillations in DD. PDF peptide binds to these neurons. Molecular oscillations in these neurons damp in Pdf^01 mutants in DD, paralleling behavioral damping.

paper_title: Homeostatic synaptic plasticity rescues neural coding reliability
experimental_stimulus: Physiological odor stimuli (Isopentyl acetate, linalool, 2-heptanone, ethyl acetate, isobutyl acetate) at high (5 × 10−2) and low (5 × 10−4) concentrations
neural_activity: Knockdown of Cacophony (Cac) or Synapsin (Syn) reduced Ca2+ signals in ORN terminals (two-photon imaging) and decreased synaptic vesicle release probability at ORN-PN and ORN-iLN synapses (electrophysiology)
behavioral_consequence: Reduced ability to correctly classify a shock-paired odor (isopentyl acetate) in behavioral assays (ball assay and linear chamber); specifically impaired at high odor concentrations in day 4 flies and low concentrations in day 0 flies; learning index remained unchanged
ecological_context: To survive, animals must recognize reoccurring stimuli; when flies navigate in their natural habitat, olfactory stimuli are often brief and repetitive
techniques_used: ['RNAi (Orco-GAL4/UAS-cacRNAi)', 'Two-photon Ca2+ imaging', 'In vivo whole-cell patch-clamp recordings', 'Behavioral assays (ball assay, linear chamber)']
summary: ORNs transmit odor information where synaptic vesicle release probability is critical for reliable neural coding; reducing Cacophony in ORNs disrupts coding reliability and impairs behavioral odor classification in ecologically relevant brief/repetitive stimuli contexts.

paper_title: Homeostatic synaptic plasticity rescues neural coding reliability
experimental_stimulus: Physiological odor stimuli (Isopentyl acetate, linalool, 2-heptanone, ethyl acetate, isobutyl acetate) at high and low concentrations
neural_activity: ORN Cac knockdown reduced PN odor responses and increased variability of firing rates, delayed onset, and increased jitter of first spike; homeostatic plasticity (increase in AZ numbers) rescued eEPSC amplitudes and reliability at low stimulus intensities within a day but failed at high intensities
behavioral_consequence: Impaired reliability of odor classification correlated with reduced neural coding reliability; homeostatic rescue at day 4 restored behavioral reliability at low concentrations but not high
ecological_context: Reliable stimulus representation is an essential requirement for repeated identification of stimuli; homeostatic plasticity maintains stable neural coding and reliable behavior
techniques_used: ['In vivo whole-cell patch-clamp recordings (current/voltage clamp)', 'Confocal microscopy (Brp punctae)', 'Behavioral assays']
summary: PNs receive ORN input and exhibit homeostatic synaptic plasticity that increases active zone numbers to rescue coding reliability; this plasticity maintains reliable neural coding and enables consistent classification of physiological stimuli.

paper_title: Homeostatic synaptic plasticity rescues neural coding reliability
experimental_stimulus: Physiological odor stimuli (linalool, isopentyl acetate, 2-heptanone, isobutyl acetate)
neural_activity: Reducing Cac protein levels in ORNs resulted in an almost complete abolishment of iLN odor responses in both 0 and 2-4-day-old flies; no homeostatic rescue of iLN responses or AZ numbers observed unlike PNs
behavioral_consequence: Not directly tested via iLN manipulation; however, their reduced recruitment contributes to PN reliability deficits which impair behavioral odor classification
ecological_context: iLNs respond linearly to ORN activity and respond most strongly to high ORN firing rates triggered by high odor intensities
techniques_used: ['In vivo whole-cell patch-clamp recordings (449-QF driver)', 'Confocal microscopy']
summary: iLNs provide circuit modulation to PNs; their recruitment is highly sensitive to ORN release probability and lacks the target-specific homeostatic compensation seen at ORN-PN synapses.

paper_title: Homeostatic Synaptic Plasticity Rescues Neural Coding Reliability
experimental_stimulus: Physiological odors (isopentyl acetate, linalool, 2-heptanone, ethyl acetate, isobutyl acetate) at high and low concentrations; shock-paired isopentyl acetate in behavioral assays
neural_activity: Knockdown of Cacophony (Cac) reduced Ca2+ signals in terminals and synaptic vesicle (SV) release probability, increasing synaptic delay and jitter. Homeostatic synaptic plasticity resulted in a target-specific increase in the number of active zone (AZ) release sites (Brp punctae) within a day, rescuing eEPSC amplitudes at ORN-PN synapses.
behavioral_consequence: Cac knockdown reduced the behavioral ability to correctly classify a shock-paired olfactory stimulus (isopentyl acetate) in repetitive short trials (ball assay). Learning index in long-exposure paradigms was unaffected.
ecological_context: To survive, animals must recognize reoccurring stimuli; in the natural habitat, olfactory stimuli are often brief and repetitive, requiring reliable classification.
techniques_used: ['RNA interference (RNAi)', 'In vivo whole-cell patch-clamp recordings', 'Two-photon Ca2+ imaging', 'Immunohistochemistry (Brp, Cac)', 'Behavioral assays (learning chamber, ball assay)']
summary: ORNs expressing Cacophony show reduced SV release probability upon knockdown, which initially disrupts coding reliability. Homeostatic plasticity rescues AZ numbers and neural coding reliability at low odor intensities within a day, maintaining behavioral classification accuracy.

paper_title: Homeostatic Synaptic Plasticity Rescues Neural Coding Reliability
experimental_stimulus: Physiological odors at high and low concentrations
neural_activity: Coding reliability (temporal correlation, firing rate variability, latency, jitter) was reduced when ORN Cac was knocked down at high odor concentrations in 2-4-day-old flies. Reliability was rescued at low concentrations by homeostatic synaptic plasticity (AZ increase).
behavioral_consequence: Reduced PN coding reliability correlated with reduced behavioral reliability (accuracy of odor classification) in the ball assay.
ecological_context: Reliable stimulus representation by the neural code is necessary for the repeated identification of stimuli, a requirement for survival.
techniques_used: ['In vivo whole-cell patch-clamp recordings (current and voltage clamp)', 'Correlation and variance analysis of spike trains']
summary: PNs exhibit reduced neural coding reliability when ORN release probability is disrupted, particularly at high firing rates. Homeostatic plasticity rescues this reliability at low stimulus intensities.

paper_title: Homeostatic Synaptic Plasticity Rescues Neural Coding Reliability
experimental_stimulus: Physiological odors (high concentration)
neural_activity: Cac knockdown in ORNs resulted in an almost complete abolishment of iLN odor responses. No homeostatic increase in AZ numbers was observed at ORN-iLN synapses. Pharmacological blockade of GABA receptors in PNs increased PN activity variability.
behavioral_consequence: Not directly tested for iLN manipulation behavior, but blocking inhibitory circuitry (GABA receptors) altered PN coding variability.
ecological_context: iLNs modulate the olfactory circuit and recruitment affects the reliability of the neural code, relevant for processing high-firing rate inputs from strong odors.
techniques_used: ['In vivo whole-cell patch-clamp recordings', 'Pharmacology (GABA receptor antagonists)', 'Immunohistochemistry (Brp)']
summary: iLN recruitment is highly sensitive to reduced ORN release probability and shows no homeostatic rescue of AZ numbers, affecting PN coding variability.

paper_title: Functional Interaction Between Drosophila Olfactory Sensory Neurons and Their Support Cells
experimental_stimulus: VUAA1 (synthetic Orco agonist), 14 ecologically relevant odorants (e.g., ethyl acetate, CO2)
neural_activity: K+ efflux upon VUAA1 stimulation (live cation imaging). Increased spike frequency in response to diagnostic odorants (single sensillum recording). Resting activity largely unchanged in thecogen-ablated flies.
ecological_context: Discrimination of odors in the turbulent sensory landscape of insect flight
summary: Orco+ OSNs exhibit K+ efflux during odor stimulation and increased spike frequency to diagnostic odorants, with resting activity remaining stable despite thecogen ablation.

paper_title: Functional Interaction Between Drosophila Olfactory Sensory Neurons and Their Support Cells
experimental_stimulus: VUAA1, balsamic vinegar
neural_activity: Acute cytoplasmic Ca2+ influx in a subpopulation (~42%) following VUAA1 stimulation. No K+ flux. No Ca2+ response to balsamic vinegar.
summary: Tormogen cells show acute Ca2+ influx in a subpopulation during odor presentation but exhibit no net K+ flux.

paper_title: Functional Interaction Between Drosophila Olfactory Sensory Neurons and Their Support Cells
experimental_stimulus: VUAA1, varying extracellular K+ concentrations (1-150 mM), heatshock (inducible ablation)
neural_activity: Cytoplasmic K+ influx upon VUAA1 stimulation (enhanced at low extracellular K+). No Ca2+ flux. Ablation resulted in gain of mechanosensitivity in OSNs (SSR) and loss of odor specificity in ab1 sensilla.
ecological_context: Important for the ability of insect olfactory systems to dynamically and sensitively discriminate between odors
summary: Thecogen cells buffer K+ influx during odor stimulation; their ablation increases OSN mechanosensitivity and impairs odor specificity without changing resting activity.

paper_title: PKC98E Regulates Odorant Responses in Drosophila melanogaster
cell_type: ORCO-expressing olfactory neurons (including at1 neurons sensing cVA; ai2a neurons sensing farnesol)
experimental_stimulus: cVA pheromone (0.01% to 100% dilution); Farnesol (10^-4 to pure); Prolonged odor exposure (1 hour cVA pre-exposure)
neural_activity: Single sensillum recordings (SSRs) showed reduced peak spike frequency in PKC98E RNAi and conditional mutants compared to controls. Immunofluorescence showed reduced ORCO phosphorylation at S289 in PKC98E mutants. Expression of constitutively active PKC98ECAAX restored peak responses but was defective for slow desensitization (failed to reduce responsiveness after prolonged odor exposure).
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this study (chemotaxis defects referenced from prior work Guo et al., 2017).
ecological_context: Discussion suggests this modulation mechanism is important for the ability to identify food sources in the presence of a pervasive background food odor.
techniques_used: ['Single sensillum recordings (SSRs)', 'Immunofluorescence/Immunostaining', 'RNA-seq', 'CRISPR/Cas9 conditional mutagenesis', 'Genetic rescue with transgenic flies']
summary: PKC98E regulates ORCO phosphorylation at S289 in ORCO-expressing olfactory neurons. Loss of PKC98E reduces peak odorant responses and phospho-ORCO S289 levels. A constitutively active kinase rescues naive sensitivity but impairs slow desensitization following prolonged odor exposure.

paper_title: Calmodulin regulates the olfactory performance in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: VUAA1 (synthetic Orco agonist), Vinegar (10^-2 dilution), Grape juice (pure and dilutions)
neural_activity: Ca2+ imaging (ex-vivo antenna) showed reduced maximum fluorescence response (Max ∆F/F0) in K339N mutants compared to wild type upon stimulation. Sensitization to repeated subthreshold stimuli was abolished in K339N mutants.
behavioral_consequence: Wind tunnel bioassay showed severely diminished odor localization. Only 12% of OrX/OrcoK339N flies reached the vinegar source compared to 51% of wild type. Only 10% reached grape juice compared to 62% of wild type.
ecological_context: Long-range odor-guided behavior for finding food sources (vinegar and grape juice represent rotting fruit). Vital for detecting volatile chemical cues in nature.
summary: OrX/Orco OSNs show reduced Ca2+ responses and abolished sensitization with the OrcoK339N mutation, leading to significantly impaired odor localization in wind tunnel behavior.

paper_title: Calmodulin regulates the olfactory performance in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Ethyl hexanoate (natural ligand), Vinegar, Grape juice
neural_activity: Ca2+ imaging showed reduced maximum fluorescence response and decreased sensitivity (EC50 shift) to ethyl hexanoate in K339N mutants compared to wild type. Sensitization to repeated ethyl hexanoate stimuli was abolished in K339N mutants.
behavioral_consequence: Wind tunnel bioassay showed that flies expressing only this receptor type had low attraction to vinegar (1%) and grape juice (7%) even in wild type. Comparison of the K339N mutation effect was prevented by this low baseline attraction.
ecological_context: Detection of fruit odors (ethyl hexanoate is a fruit odor). Part of the olfactory system for finding food and oviposition sites.
summary: Or22a/Orco OSNs show reduced sensitivity and abolished sensitization with the OrcoK339N mutation, though behavioral contribution of this single population is limited compared to the full OSN array.

paper_title: Calmodulin Affects Sensitization of Drosophila melanogaster Odorant Receptors
experimental_stimulus: Repeated submicromolar VUAA1 (synthetic agonist) or ethyl hexanoate (odor) stimulation at 100 s intervals
neural_activity: Calcium imaging revealed sensitization (increased response to 2nd stimulus) in outer dendritic segment (ODS), inner dendritic segment (IDS), and soma under control conditions; CaM inhibition with W7 abolished sensitization in ODS but sensitization persisted in IDS and soma for both VUAA1 and ethyl hexanoate
ecological_context: Flying insects navigate in highly diluted and dispersed odor plumes; sensitization allows variable sensitivity to detect faint and turbulent odor traces and regulate receptor sensitivity according to requirements
techniques_used: ['Calcium imaging (ex vivo antenna preparation)', 'Pharmacological inhibition (W7, CPZ)', 'Electrophysiology (patch clamp in CHO cells)']
summary: Repeated stimulation sensitizes Or22a-expressing OSNs; CaM inhibition abolishes this sensitization in the outer dendrites but not in the inner dendrites or soma, suggesting distinct mechanisms in different compartments.

paper_title: Spontaneous and Evoked Release Are Independently Regulated at Individual Active Zones
experimental_stimulus: Electrical stimulation of motor nerves (evoked); resting conditions (spontaneous)
neural_activity: Individual active zones show variable release probability. 22.3% of active zones are dedicated to spontaneous release only, 36.3% to evoked only, and 41.1% participate in both. Spontaneous and evoked release probabilities are not correlated. Ca2+ influx signals visualized via postsynaptic myrGCaMP5; miniature EPSPs recorded via sharp electrode electrophysiology.
summary: Motor neurons at the Drosophila larval muscle 4 NMJ exhibit independent regulation of spontaneous and evoked release at individual active zones, with a significant subset of zones dedicated exclusively to spontaneous fusion.

paper_title: Phosphorylation via PKC Regulates the Function of the Drosophila Odorant Co-Receptor
experimental_stimulus: Ethyl butyrate odor pulses (0.5 s duration)
neural_activity: Microinjection of PLC inhibitor (U73122) or PKC inhibitor (Gö6976) into sensilla reduced the OSN spike frequency response to odor. Microinjection of PKC activators (OAG or PMA) enhanced the odor response (spike frequency).
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this study (only neural activity measured via electrophysiology)
ecological_context: Ethyl butyrate is identified in the Introduction as a fruit volatile emitted by e.g., pineapple (cited from Stensmyr et al., 2003; Hallem and Carlson, 2006)
techniques_used: ['Extracellular single sensillum electrophysiology', 'Microinjection of chemical agents into sensilla']
summary: Or22a-expressing OSNs in large basiconic sensilla show increased firing upon ethyl butyrate stimulation; PLC and PKC inhibition reduces this response, while PKC activation enhances it.

paper_title: Transient photocurrents in a subthreshold evidence accumulator accelerate perceptual decisions
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory discrimination task (4-methylcyclohexanol); optogenetic stimulation (25-ms red light pulses) triggered upon entry into central decision zone
neural_activity: Dendritically targeted CsChrimson produced subthreshold depolarizations (1–4 mV) at the soma in response to light; EPSP-like photocurrents generated; subthreshold voltage dynamics form an accumulator memory
behavioral_consequence: Dendritic optogenetic depolarization shortened reaction times, particularly in difficult discriminations, at a marginal cost to accuracy; untargeted (spiking) stimulation drastically reduced reaction times and compressed accuracy to near chance
techniques_used: ['closed-loop optogenetics', 'whole-cell patch-clamp electrophysiology', 'olfactory discrimination behavior', 'confocal fluorescence imaging', 'Bayesian model comparison']
summary: Dendritically targeted αβc KCs integrate sensory evidence via subthreshold voltage changes; optogenetic depolarization accelerates decisions by lowering the decision bound.

paper_title: Transient photocurrents in a subthreshold evidence accumulator accelerate perceptual decisions
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory discrimination task (4-methylcyclohexanol); optogenetic stimulation (25-ms red light pulses)
neural_activity: Identified as naturally poor temporal integrators
behavioral_consequence: Photostimulation did not alter reaction times or choice accuracies compared to unstimulated conditions
summary: α'β' KCs are not involved in the evidence accumulation step of odor discrimination.

paper_title: Electrophysiological Validation of Monosynaptic Connectivity between Premotor Interneurons and the aCC Motoneuron in the Drosophila Larval CNS
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic stimulation (470 nm LED, 1 s) of ChR and NaChBac driven by R36G02-Gal4
neural_activity: Monosynaptically connected to aCC motoneuron; induced inward synaptic current in aCC present in TTX; robust RCaMP calcium signal in aCC in TTX
ecological_context: Part of the central pattern generator that regulates locomotion in Drosophila larvae
summary: A27h is confirmed as a cholinergic premotor interneuron monosynaptically connected to aCC, providing excitatory drive validated by electrophysiology and calcium imaging in the presence of TTX.

paper_title: Electrophysiological Validation of Monosynaptic Connectivity between Premotor Interneurons and the aCC Motoneuron in the Drosophila Larval CNS
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic stimulation (470 nm LED) of ChR and NaChBac driven by R15B07-Gal4 (CLI2-Gal4)
neural_activity: Monosynaptically connected to aCC motoneuron; induced inward synaptic current in aCC present in TTX
ecological_context: Part of the central pattern generator that regulates locomotion in Drosophila larvae
summary: A18a is confirmed as a cholinergic premotor interneuron monosynaptically connected to aCC, providing excitatory synaptic drive.

paper_title: Electrophysiological Validation of Monosynaptic Connectivity between Premotor Interneurons and the aCC Motoneuron in the Drosophila Larval CNS
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic stimulation (470 nm LED) of ChR and NaChBac driven by tsh-Gal80; R47E12-Gal4, cha3.3-Gal80
neural_activity: NOT monosynaptically connected to aCC motoneuron; no synaptic current in aCC in absence or presence of TTX; GRASP imaging showed cell-cell contact but no functional monosynaptic connection
ecological_context: Part of the central pattern generator that regulates locomotion in Drosophila larvae
summary: Despite GRASP suggesting contact, electrophysiological validation shows A18b3 is not monosynaptically connected to aCC.

paper_title: Electrophysiological Validation of Monosynaptic Connectivity between Premotor Interneurons and the aCC Motoneuron in the Drosophila Larval CNS
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic stimulation (470 nm LED) of ChR driven by SS04495-Gal4 (R41G07-AD; R78F07-DBD)
neural_activity: Monosynaptically connected to aCC motoneuron; induced hyperpolarizing drive and reduced aCC firing; inhibitory response blocked by PTX and gabazine
ecological_context: Part of the central pattern generator that regulates locomotion in Drosophila larvae
summary: A23a is confirmed as a GABAergic premotor interneuron monosynaptically connected to aCC, providing reliable inhibitory drive.

paper_title: Electrophysiological Validation of Monosynaptic Connectivity between Premotor Interneurons and the aCC Motoneuron in the Drosophila Larval CNS
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic stimulation (470 nm LED) of ChR driven by SS04399-Gal4 (R20A03-AD; R93B07-DBD)
neural_activity: Monosynaptically connected to aCC motoneuron; induced hyperpolarizing drive and reduced aCC firing; inhibitory response blocked by PTX and gabazine
ecological_context: Part of the central pattern generator that regulates locomotion in Drosophila larvae
summary: A31k is confirmed as a GABAergic premotor interneuron monosynaptically connected to aCC, providing robust inhibitory drive.

paper_title: Mechanisms underlying homeostatic plasticity in the Drosophila mushroom body in vivo
experimental_stimulus: Artificial activation via temperature-sensitive channel dTRPA1 (31°C for 4 days); Odor presentation (isoamyl acetate, δ-decalactone)
neural_activity: Acute dTRPA1 activation suppresses Kenyon cell odor responses. After 4-day preactivation, odor-evoked Ca2+ influx in APL is decreased compared to controls. Acute activation efficacy during imaging is unchanged by preactivation.
ecological_context: Provides feedback inhibition to regulate Kenyon cell activity for sparse olfactory coding, which enhances learned odor discrimination and associative memory capacity.
summary: APL neurons show decreased odor-evoked activity after prolonged artificial activation, contributing to homeostatic compensation in the mushroom body.

paper_title: Mechanisms underlying homeostatic plasticity in the Drosophila mushroom body in vivo
experimental_stimulus: Odor pulses (isoamyl acetate, δ-decalactone); Artificial APL blockade (Tetanus toxin, histamine-gated channel Ort); Prolonged APL activation
neural_activity: Blocking APL increases KC odor responses with little compensation. Prolonged APL activation leads to increased KC odor responses after activation stops. αβ KCs compensation persists when APL is acutely blocked (non-inhibitory plasticity). γ KCs compensation disappears when APL is acutely blocked (reduced APL activity). Odor responses become less sparse in adapted flies.
ecological_context: Sparse coding of sensory stimuli maximizes associative memory capacity and separates population representations of different stimuli to enhance learned discrimination.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon imaging (GCaMP6f, GCaMP3)', 'Genetic manipulation (TNT, dTRPA1, Ort)', 'Histamine bath application']
summary: Kenyon cells homeostatically compensate for excess inhibition via increased excitation/intrinsic excitability in αβ KCs and reduced APL activity in γ KCs.

paper_title: Ca2+-Activated K+ Channels Reduce Network Excitability, Improving Adaptability and Energetics for Transmitting and Perceiving Sensory Information
experimental_stimulus: Light flashes (dim, medium, bright) and naturalistic light intensity time series
neural_activity: In vivo intracellular recordings showed distinct response dynamics in mutants: dSK− and double mutants peaked faster and decayed earlier than wild-type (WT), while dSlo− photoreceptors showed decelerated dynamics (peaked later, lasted longer). Mutants had more depolarized resting potentials and lower membrane input resistance in darkness. Information transfer rates were broadly WT-like after adaptation, but synaptic feedback load was higher in mutants, leading to increased ATP consumption per bit of information (13.1% to 42.7% higher than WT).
behavioral_consequence: Optomotor behavior in a flight simulator showed genotype-specific sensitivities: dSK− mutants responded strongly to fast coarse field rotations and exhibited fast body saccades; dSlo− mutants were most sensitive to slow hyperacute field rotations; double mutants showed slower response rise and decay consistent with slower LMC dynamics.
ecological_context: Visual processing for motion perception during flight navigation and adaptation to changing light conditions.
techniques_used: ['In vivo intracellular recordings', 'Whole-cell recordings (dissociated ommatidia)', 'Electroretinograms (ERGs)', 'Flight simulator (optomotor behavior)', 'Biophysical modeling']
summary: R1–R6 photoreceptors maintain information transfer rates despite Ca2+-activated K+ channel loss but exhibit altered adaptation dynamics, response speeds, and synaptic feedback loads, resulting in increased energy costs and distorted motion perception behavior.

paper_title: Ca2+-Activated K+ Channels Reduce Network Excitability, Improving Adaptability and Energetics for Transmitting and Perceiving Sensory Information
experimental_stimulus: Brightening light flashes (4-log intensity range) and naturalistic light intensity time series (inferred via ERG)
neural_activity: In vivo intracellular recordings revealed that mutant LMCs displayed oscillating responses (ringing) around specific frequencies not seen in WT. dSK− LMCs showed accelerated dynamics (fastest time-to-peak) and oscillation bursts to dim flashes. dSlo− and double mutant LMCs showed slower dynamics (slower repolarization, peaked later) and sluggish ringing. ERG oscillations in mutants matched intracellular LMC oscillations.
behavioral_consequence: Altered LMC response dynamics correlated with distorted motion perception: accelerated dSK− LMC responses drove hypersaccadic optomotor responses, while decelerated dSlo− LMC responses sensitized vision to slow scene rotations.
ecological_context: Processing visual motion signals for the optomotor response required for course control during flight.
summary: LMCs in Ca2+-activated K+ channel mutants show mutation-specific response dynamics (accelerated in dSK−, decelerated in dSlo−) and oscillations that directly influence optomotor behavior and motion perception limits.

paper_title: Functional neuroanatomy of Drosophila olfactory memory formation
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory conditioning (odor + electric shock or sucrose reward)
neural_activity: Functional cellular imaging showed synaptic plasticity for ~5 min after conditioning; increased translation of CaMKII in Pn synapses after spaced conditioning
behavioral_consequence: Expression of rut cDNA in Pn rescues appetitive memory deficit but not aversive; RNAi knockdown of PQBP1 in Pn impairs aversive memory
summary: Pn are actively involved in memory formation; rut expression rescues appetitive memory, and PQBP1 knockdown impairs aversive memory.

paper_title: Functional neuroanatomy of Drosophila olfactory memory formation
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory conditioning (odor + shock or reward)
neural_activity: G-CaMP imaging showed neuronal plasticity and calcium response to odors occurs primarily in γ MBn; presynaptic activity inhibited by G(o) signaling
behavioral_consequence: Expression of dDA1 receptor in γ MBn restores all memory phases in dumb mutants; blocking synaptic output at 15 min impares aversive and appetitive memory; rut expression provides partial to complete rescue
techniques_used: ['functional cellular imaging (G-CaMP, synapto-pHluorin)', 'thermogenetic activation', 'rescue experiments', 'synaptic block (shits)']
summary: γ MBn are primary sites for coincidence detection and early memory acquisition/expression.

paper_title: Functional neuroanatomy of Drosophila olfactory memory formation
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory conditioning (odor + shock or reward)
neural_activity: G-CaMP imaging showed a memory trace forms in vertical branches 9–24 h after spaced conditioning
behavioral_consequence: Blocking synaptic output diminishes performance at all times (15 min to 24 h); essential for PSD-LTM expression; required for memory retrieval
summary: α/β MBn are required for the expression of all temporal forms of memory, particularly PSD-LTM.

paper_title: Functional neuroanatomy of Drosophila olfactory memory formation
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory conditioning (odor + shock or reward)
neural_activity: Functional imaging of odor-evoked Ca2+ responses showed an early memory trace persists for at least 1 h
behavioral_consequence: Blocking synaptic output impairs memory up to 90 min; abolished appetitive memory expression up to 3 h; partially impairs aversive memory to 90 min
summary: α'/β' MBn hold early memories and are absolutely required for appetitive memory expression up to 3 h.

paper_title: Functional neuroanatomy of Drosophila olfactory memory formation
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory conditioning (odor + shock or reward)
neural_activity: Memory trace forms between 30–70 min after aversive conditioning in vertical lobe neurites (increased calcium influx); appetitive trace persists to ~150 min
behavioral_consequence: Blocking neurotransmission after learning impairs memory expression tested 1 or 3 h later; required for ARM formation
summary: DPMn modulate memory consolidation via interactions with MBn, forming delayed memory traces.

paper_title: Functional neuroanatomy of Drosophila olfactory memory formation
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory conditioning
neural_activity: Suppress olfactory learning by activating GABAA receptor Rdl on MBn; learning suppresses APLn Ca2+ responses
behavioral_consequence: Blocking synaptic transmission after training impairs 3 h memory
summary: APLn suppress olfactory learning and maintain sparse odor coding; blocking them impairs labile memory.

paper_title: Functional neuroanatomy of Drosophila olfactory memory formation
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory conditioning; Optogenetic stimulation paired with odor
neural_activity: Ongoing activity observed in DAn innervating MV1 and MP1 regions; release of DA onto MBn
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic stimulation drives learning/avoidance; blocking PPL1 output enhances 3 h memory; activating DAn abolishes ASM and ARM; dDA1 mutants impaired in both aversive and appetitive memory
summary: DAn mediate punishment and reward reinforcement; also involved in forgetting through chronic activity.

paper_title: Functional neuroanatomy of Drosophila olfactory memory formation
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory conditioning
neural_activity: Synaptic activity required for retrieval of PSD-LTM
behavioral_consequence: Protein synthesis and synaptic activity in DALn required for PSD-LTM; not required for acquisition or consolidation
summary: DALn provide input to MBn dendrites and are required for retrieval of PSD-LTM.

paper_title: Functional neuroanatomy of Drosophila olfactory memory formation
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory conditioning
neural_activity: Synaptic activity required for retrieval of PSD-LTM
behavioral_consequence: Blocking MB-V2n output impairs aversive short-term and PSD-LTM; blocking MB-V3n impairs retrieval of appetitive PSD-LTM
summary: MB-V2n and MB-V3n connect MB lobes to other brain regions and are required for retrieval of long-term memory.

paper_title: A single pair of interneurons commands the Drosophila feeding motor program
experimental_stimulus: Temperature elevation (31-37°C) via TrpA1 channel; 400 mM sucrose applied to labellar lobes; Two-photon laser irradiation of cell body
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (GCaMP3.0) shows large increase in fluorescence in starved flies upon sucrose stimulation; no response in satiated flies; no response in adjacent LPE neurons; heat/laser activation induces activity
behavioral_consequence: Activation induces complete feeding motor program (proboscis extension, labellar opening, pharyngeal pumping) in food-free environment; single neuron activation causes asymmetric proboscis extension and pumping; suppression or ablation of both neurons abolishes sucrose-induced feeding; ablation of one neuron distorts response to be asymmetric
ecological_context: Response is contingent on the metabolic state of the animal (starved vs satiated); operates at the top of the feeding motor hierarchy to execute the decision to feed, integrating gustatory and metabolic cues
techniques_used: ['Thermogenetics (TrpA1)', 'Calcium imaging (GCaMP3.0)', 'Laser ablation', 'Laser activation', 'Genetic suppression (Kir2.1)', 'Flip-out Gal80 screening']
summary: Fdg-neurons are a single pair of interneurons that generate the feeding motor program; they respond to sucrose only in starved flies and activation is necessary and sufficient for proboscis extension and pharyngeal pumping.

paper_title: A single pair of interneurons commands the Drosophila feeding motor program
experimental_stimulus: 400 mM sucrose application; Two-photon laser irradiation of cell body; Laser ablation
