experimental_stimulus: Ethyl butyrate concentration series; Panel of 8 odors (10^-2 dilution)
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (G-CaMP) showed LNs respond only to high ethyl butyrate concentrations. Activation requires summed input from at least two OSNs (Or42a + Or42b); no activation with single OSN input.
behavioral_consequence: No behavioral consequence determined. Attempts to silence LNs (diphtheria toxin, tetanus toxin) resulted in dead or sluggish larvae, precluding behavioral analysis.
ecological_context: Proposed role as a gain-control mechanism to maintain consistent attraction to food odors (rotting fruit/yeast) across large concentration ranges in the natural environment.
summary: LN2 interneurons are recruited by summed OSN input at high odor concentrations, suggesting a role in circuit gain control, though direct behavioral effects could not be tested.

paper_title: A circuit supporting concentration-invariant odor perception in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Ethyl butyrate concentration series; Panel of 8 odors (10^-2 dilution)
neural_activity: Calcium imaging (G-CaMP) at mushroom body terminals. Responses were significantly weaker when summed input from two OSNs (Or42a + Or42b) impinged compared to single OSN input.
behavioral_consequence: No behavioral consequence determined (not tested).
ecological_context: Relay odor information to higher brain centers (mushroom body) for perception and memory.
summary: PN responses to ethyl butyrate are reduced when multiple OSNs are active compared to single OSN activation, consistent with inhibitory modulation by local interneurons.

paper_title: Atypical Membrane Topology and Heteromeric Function of Drosophila Odorant Receptors In Vivo
experimental_stimulus: Genetic manipulation of OR83b expression (wild-type, mutant, TARGET induction)
neural_activity: OR22a/b protein localization to sensory cilia is dependent on OR83b; OR22a/b is retained in the cell body and endoplasmic reticulum in Or83b mutants. OR83b is continuously required to maintain OR22a/b in cilia.
summary: OR22a/b-expressing OSNs require OR83b for the trafficking and maintenance of OR22a/b protein within the sensory cilia.

paper_title: Atypical Membrane Topology and Heteromeric Function of Drosophila Odorant Receptors In Vivo
experimental_stimulus: CO2 (endogenous); Cyclohexanol, Benzaldehyde, Hexanol, etc. (ectopic OR43a + OR83b expression)
neural_activity: Endogenous neurons show calcium increases in response to CO2. Ectopic expression of OR43a + OR83b reconstitutes functional odor-evoked calcium responses to OR43a ligands (e.g., cyclohexanol) in these neurons.
summary: Gr21a OSNs normally respond to CO2; co-expression of OR43a and OR83b is sufficient to reconstitute odor-evoked calcium signaling to other ligands in these neurons.

paper_title: Atypical Membrane Topology and Heteromeric Function of Drosophila Odorant Receptors In Vivo
experimental_stimulus: OR83b expression levels (wild-type, mutant, heterologous expression)
neural_activity: OR83b localizes to sensory cilia independently of conventional ORs but is destabilized without them. OR83b forms homomeric and heteromeric complexes with conventional ORs (e.g., OR43a) in the sensory cilia, confirmed by protein-fragment complementation assay (PCA).
ecological_context: The OR/OR83b complex is identified as a target for developing insect repellents to disrupt host-seeking behaviors in insect disease vectors.
summary: OR83b is essential for the stability and ciliary localization of conventional ORs and forms functional heteromeric complexes with them in sensory cilia.

paper_title: Atypical Membrane Topology and Heteromeric Function of Drosophila Odorant Receptors In Vivo
experimental_stimulus: Ectopic expression of OR43a + OR83b
neural_activity: Co-expression of OR43a and OR83b promotes localization of both proteins to the ciliated dendrites of these neurons, demonstrating OR83b couples to a general ciliary trafficking pathway.
summary: OR43a and OR83b localize to cilia in mechanosensory neurons when co-expressed, indicating OR83b mediates OR trafficking to cilia generally.

paper_title: Odor Coding in a Model Olfactory Organ: TheDrosophila Maxillary Palp
experimental_stimulus: Ethyl acetate, ethyl propionate, aliphatic esters, E2-hexenal, isoamyl acetate, cis-vaccenyl acetate
neural_activity: Excitatory responses (increased firing rate). Dose-dependent sigmoid response curves reaching saturation. Adaptation to prolonged stimulation (response reduced to 60% after 25 sec). Cross-adaptation to structurally similar esters. Prolonged firing continues past end of odor delivery for some stimuli.
ecological_context: Esters are common volatiles; cis-vaccenyl acetate is a sex pheromone in Drosophila, though this neuron responds more strongly to other odors.
summary: pb1A neurons respond with increased firing to ethyl acetate and esters, showing dose-dependent saturation, adaptation, and cross-adaptation.

paper_title: Odor Coding in a Model Olfactory Organ: TheDrosophila Maxillary Palp
experimental_stimulus: 4-methylphenol, 4-methylcyclohexanol
neural_activity: Strong excitatory response to 4-methylphenol (178 spikes/sec increase). Weak response to 4-methylcyclohexanol. Functionally independent from pb1A neuron in the same sensillum.
ecological_context: 4-methylphenol is present in cattle urine and attracts tsetse flies.
summary: pb1B neurons are strongly excited by 4-methylphenol and function independently of the paired pb1A neuron.

paper_title: Odor Coding in a Model Olfactory Organ: TheDrosophila Maxillary Palp
experimental_stimulus: Benzaldehyde
neural_activity: Excitatory response to benzaldehyde. Spontaneous firing rate distinct from pb2B.
summary: pb2A neurons are excited by benzaldehyde and are paired with pb2B neurons in pb2 sensilla.

paper_title: Odor Coding in a Model Olfactory Organ: TheDrosophila Maxillary Palp
experimental_stimulus: 4-methylphenol, 3-octanol, benzaldehyde, isoamyl acetate
neural_activity: Excitatory response to 4-methylphenol. Inhibitory response to 3-octanol (80-100% reduction in high spontaneous firing rate of ~30 spikes/sec) and benzaldehyde. Functionally independent from pb2A neuron.
summary: pb2B neurons respond with excitation to 4-methylphenol but show inhibition to 3-octanol and benzaldehyde.

paper_title: Odor Coding in a Model Olfactory Organ: TheDrosophila Maxillary Palp
experimental_stimulus: 3-octanol, isoamyl acetate, aliphatic esters
neural_activity: Excitatory response to 3-octanol and isoamyl acetate. Response magnitude to esters is lower than pb3B. Molecule length correlates with potency (6 carbons most potent).
summary: pb3A neurons are excited by 3-octanol and esters, showing distinct response profiles compared to pb3B.

paper_title: Odor Coding in a Model Olfactory Organ: TheDrosophila Maxillary Palp
experimental_stimulus: 3-octanol, isoamyl acetate, aliphatic esters
neural_activity: Excitatory response to 3-octanol and isoamyl acetate. Stronger response to esters than pb3A. Relative responses vary with ester chain length compared to pb3A.
summary: pb3B neurons are excited by 3-octanol and esters, with stronger response to esters than the paired pb3A neurons.

paper_title: Satiation state-dependent dopaminergic control of foraging in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Fed state vs 16-20 hr food-deprived state; food source introduced in open arena
neural_activity: Acute inactivation (Shibirets) and activation (TrpA1) tested
behavioral_consequence: Inactivation decreased food occupancy in fed flies but not food-deprived flies; activation increased food occupancy in fed flies
ecological_context: Promotes pre-ingestive food seeking in well-fed flies
techniques_used: ['genetic inactivation', 'genetic activation', 'open field food occupancy assay']
summary: TH-Gal4 dopamine neurons promote food occupancy in fed flies but are dispensable in food-deprived flies.

paper_title: Satiation state-dependent dopaminergic control of foraging in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Fed state vs 16-20 hr food-deprived state; food source introduced
neural_activity: Acute inactivation (Shibirets) and activation (TrpA1) tested
behavioral_consequence: Inactivation decreased food occupancy in food-deprived flies but not fed flies; activation decreased food occupancy in fed flies
ecological_context: Promotes hunger-driven foraging in food-deprived flies
techniques_used: ['genetic inactivation', 'genetic activation', 'open field food occupancy assay']
summary: PAM dopamine neurons promote food occupancy in food-deprived flies and can inhibit foraging in fed flies.

paper_title: Satiation state-dependent dopaminergic control of foraging in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Fed state
neural_activity: Acute activation (TrpA1) tested
behavioral_consequence: Activation increased food occupancy in fed flies
ecological_context: Promotes pre-ingestive food seeking in well-fed flies
summary: TH-C' dopamine neurons (including PPL2ab, PPM2, PAL) are sufficient to promote food occupancy in fed flies.

paper_title: Satiation state-dependent dopaminergic control of foraging in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Fed state
neural_activity: Acute inactivation (Shibirets) tested
behavioral_consequence: Inactivation decreased food occupancy in fed flies
ecological_context: Permissive for foraging in well-fed flies
summary: PPL1-γ1pedc neurons are necessary for promoting food occupancy in fed flies.

paper_title: Satiation state-dependent dopaminergic control of foraging in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Fed state vs food-deprived state; food source introduced
neural_activity: Acute blockade (Shibirets) and activation tested; genetic rescue of Dop1R1 mutant
behavioral_consequence: Inactivation decreased food occupancy in both fed and food-deprived flies; activation increased food occupancy in fed flies
ecological_context: Promotes foraging independent of satiation state
techniques_used: ['genetic inactivation', 'genetic activation', 'genetic rescue', 'open field food occupancy assay']
summary: Dop1R1-expressing mushroom body neurons promote foraging in both fed and food-deprived states.

paper_title: Satiation state-dependent dopaminergic control of foraging in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Food odor in open arena
neural_activity: Genetic ablation (Orco1) and surgical ablation (antennectomy)
behavioral_consequence: No effect on food occupancy alone; effect revealed only when combined with taste ablation
ecological_context: Olfaction acts in concert with taste for food location
techniques_used: ['genetic mutants', 'surgical ablation', 'open field food occupancy assay']
summary: Olfaction is not crucial for food seeking alone but coordinates with taste cues for occupancy.

paper_title: Satiation state-dependent dopaminergic control of foraging in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Sucrose taste in open arena (Light and Dark conditions)
neural_activity: Genetic mutation (Gr5a-, Gr64a-)
behavioral_consequence: Normal food occupancy in light; reduced occupancy in darkness or with olfactory ablation
ecological_context: Taste cues coordinate with olfaction and vision for food occupancy
summary: Taste receptors are required for food occupancy in darkness and in combination with olfaction.

paper_title: Dynamics of memory-guided choice behavior in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Conditioned odor choice test (appetitive memory after sugar reward conditioning); innate odor avoidance
neural_activity: Not directly measured; activity controlled via thermo-genetic blockade (shi at 32°C) or activation (dTrpA1 at 29.5°C)
behavioral_consequence: Transient blockade slowed choice speed (increased thalf) and reduced initial performance (1-10 sec) but left eventual performance intact. Transient activation impaired eventual performance but left choice speed and initial performance intact. Blockade did not affect innate odor avoidance speed.
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed in the context of natural ecology
techniques_used: ['Thermo-genetic manipulation (shibirets1, dTrpA1)', 'Multiplexed T-maze video recording', 'Automatic fly counting']
summary: Serotonin neurons modulate the speed-accuracy trade-off in memory-guided choice behavior; blockade slows choice speed without affecting accuracy, while activation impairs accuracy without affecting speed.

paper_title: Dynamics of memory-guided choice behavior in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Conditioned odor choice test (aversive memory via electric shock for TH-GAL4; appetitive memory via sugar reward for R58E02-GAL4)
neural_activity: Not directly measured; activity controlled via thermo-genetic blockade (shi at 32°C)
behavioral_consequence: Transient blockade of these reinforcement neurons impaired 2-hour memory for both aversive and appetitive conditioning.
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed in the context of natural ecology
techniques_used: ['Thermo-genetic manipulation (shibirets1)', 'Multiplexed T-maze video recording', 'Automatic fly counting']
summary: Dopamine neurons labeled by TH-GAL4 and R58E02-GAL4 are required for 2-hour memory retention in both aversive and appetitive olfactory conditioning.

paper_title: Ir76b is a Co-receptor for Amine Responses in Drosophila Olfactory Neurons
experimental_stimulus: Amines (1,4-diaminobutane, pyrrolidine)
neural_activity: Excitatory spiking responses eliminated in Ir25a2 and Ir76b1 mutants; not reduced in Ir8a1 mutant
summary: Amine-sensing ORNs in ac2 sensilla require both Ir25a and Ir76b co-receptors for spiking responses.

paper_title: Ir76b is a Co-receptor for Amine Responses in Drosophila Olfactory Neurons
experimental_stimulus: Amines (phenethylamine, ammonia, pyrrolidine)
neural_activity: Excitatory spiking responses lost in Ir25a2 and Ir76b1 mutants; not reduced in Ir8a1 mutant
summary: Amine-sensing ORNs in ac4 sensilla require both Ir25a and Ir76b co-receptors for spiking responses.

paper_title: Ir76b is a Co-receptor for Amine Responses in Drosophila Olfactory Neurons
experimental_stimulus: Amines (trimethylamine, 1,4-diaminobutane, dimethylamine)
neural_activity: Spiking responses retained in single mutants (Ir25a2, Ir76b1, Ir8a1); reduced by 66-82% in Ir25a2;Ir76b1 double mutant
summary: Ir92a+ ORNs in ac1 sensilla show redundant co-receptor dependence, requiring both Ir25a and Ir76b for full response magnitude.

paper_title: Ir76b is a Co-receptor for Amine Responses in Drosophila Olfactory Neurons
experimental_stimulus: Ammonia
neural_activity: Spiking responses only slightly reduced (~23%) in Ir25a2;Ir76b1 double mutant
summary: Ammonia-sensing ORNs expressing Amt function largely independently of Ir25a and Ir76b co-receptors.

paper_title: Ir76b is a Co-receptor for Amine Responses in Drosophila Olfactory Neurons
experimental_stimulus: Amines (amylamine, phenethylamine), Hexanol
neural_activity: Amine responses lost in Ir25a2 and Ir76b1 mutants; Hexanol responses unaffected by IR co-receptor loss but dependent on Orco
summary: ac3B ORN utilizes distinct pathways: Ir76b/Ir25a-dependent IR responses for amines and Orco-dependent OR responses for hexanol.

paper_title: Ir76b is a Co-receptor for Amine Responses in Drosophila Olfactory Neurons
experimental_stimulus: Acids (propionic acid, 2-oxovaleric acid, phenylacetic acid, phenylacetaldehyde)
neural_activity: Responses eliminated in Ir8a1 mutants; no requirement for Ir76b or Ir25a
summary: Acid-sensing ORNs across ac1, ac2, ac3, and ac4 sensilla rely on Ir8a but not Ir76b or Ir25a for odor detection.

paper_title: Re-Classification of Drosophila melanogaster Trichoid and Intermediate Sensilla Using Fluorescence-Guided Single Sensillum Recording
cell_type: ab2A/ab2B/ab3A/ab3B Basiconic ORNs (Or59b, Or85a, Or22a, Or85b)
experimental_stimulus: 10 standard odorants (Ethyl acetate, Pentyl acetate, Ethyl butyrate, etc.), mineral oil, cVA (for at1)
neural_activity: Extracellular action potentials recorded via SSR. No significant differences in firing patterns or spontaneous activity compared to wild-type, with the exception of ab2A response to pentyl acetate which was decreased in FgSSR.
summary: GFP expression in these basiconic olfactory receptor neurons does not significantly alter odor response profiles or spontaneous activity compared to wild-type.

paper_title: Re-Classification of Drosophila melanogaster Trichoid and Intermediate Sensilla Using Fluorescence-Guided Single Sensillum Recording
experimental_stimulus: cVA (cis-vaccenyl acetate), Red light (627nm)
neural_activity: SSR responses to cVA similar to wild-type. Single amplitude spike photo-activated by red light via CsChrimson.
summary: Verified identity and cVA sensitivity; confirmed light-activated activity in the at1 neuron.

paper_title: Re-Classification of Drosophila melanogaster Trichoid and Intermediate Sensilla Using Fluorescence-Guided Single Sensillum Recording
experimental_stimulus: Red light (627nm)
neural_activity: Single amplitude spike photo-activated by red light via CsChrimson.
summary: Verified identity as at4C neuron via light-evoked activity.

paper_title: Re-Classification of Drosophila melanogaster Trichoid and Intermediate Sensilla Using Fluorescence-Guided Single Sensillum Recording
experimental_stimulus: Red light (627nm)
neural_activity: Single amplitude spike photo-activated by red light via CsChrimson. Confirmed single neuron in sensillum.
summary: Verified single-neuron composition and identity in the ai1 sensillum using optogenetics.

paper_title: Re-Classification of Drosophila melanogaster Trichoid and Intermediate Sensilla Using Fluorescence-Guided Single Sensillum Recording
experimental_stimulus: None (Morphological analysis)
neural_activity: N/A
summary: Reclassified from trichoid to intermediate based on significantly shorter sensillar length (10.38 ± 0.17μm) compared to trichoid types (~18-19μm).

paper_title: Re-Classification of Drosophila melanogaster Trichoid and Intermediate Sensilla Using Fluorescence-Guided Single Sensillum Recording
experimental_stimulus: None (Morphological analysis)
neural_activity: N/A
summary: Reclassified from trichoid to intermediate based on significantly shorter sensillar length (8.49 ± 0.17μm) compared to trichoid types (~18-19μm).

paper_title: Feeding regulates sex pheromone attraction and courtship in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: cis-vaccenyl acetate (cVA), vinegar, cVA + vinegar blend
neural_activity: DA1 glomerulus responded specifically to cVA, not vinegar alone. cVA response in DA1 was stronger in females than males. Starvation did not significantly affect cVA response. Adding vinegar to cVA significantly decreased DA1 response in starved females but had no effect in fed females.
behavioral_consequence: Knocking down insulin receptors (InR) in these OSNs almost entirely suppressed cVA preference in fed females in a Y-tube olfactometer. InR knockdown did not affect female sexual receptivity to male courtship.
ecological_context: cVA is a male sex pheromone that increases female receptivity. Fed females (sexually receptive) are attracted to cVA + food odour blends, whereas starved females prioritize food odour.
techniques_used: ['Functional imaging (GCaMP)', 'InR RNAi knockdown', 'Y-tube olfactometer', 'Female sexual receptivity assay']
summary: Or67d-expressing OSNs in the DA1 glomerulus respond specifically to cVA, and their sensitivity to cVA in fed females depends on insulin signalling, regulating attraction to cVA + food odour.

paper_title: Feeding regulates sex pheromone attraction and courtship in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: cis-vaccenyl acetate (cVA), vinegar, cVA + vinegar blend
neural_activity: DM2 glomerulus responded to both cVA and vinegar. Response was modulated by starvation and sex, particularly with cVA + vinegar blend.
behavioral_consequence: Knocking down InR in these OSNs had no significant effect on cVA preference in fed females compared to control lines.
ecological_context: Integration of food and sex pheromone signals occurs in this pathway, though insulin signalling in these cells did not regulate the observed attraction behavior.
summary: Or22a-expressing OSNs in the DM2 glomerulus respond to cVA and vinegar, but insulin signalling in these neurons was not required for cVA attraction in fed females.

paper_title: Feeding regulates sex pheromone attraction and courtship in Drosophila females
experimental_stimulus: cis-vaccenyl acetate (cVA), vinegar, cVA + vinegar blend
neural_activity: VM2 glomerulus responded to cVA and vinegar. Nutritional state had a sexually dimorphic effect on cVA response in this glomerulus. Response pattern was observed with cVA + vinegar blend, not vinegar alone.
behavioral_consequence: Knocking down InR in these OSNs resulted in no significant preference for cVA, but behavior did not significantly differ from control parental lines, so a role in cVA attraction regulation could not be confirmed.
ecological_context: Participates in the integration of food and sex signals, potentially modulating attraction based on nutritional state.
summary: Or43b-expressing OSNs in the VM2 glomerulus respond to cVA and vinegar with starvation-dependent modulation, though insulin signalling in these cells did not clearly regulate cVA attraction behavior.

paper_title: A gustatory receptor paralog controls rapid warmth avoidance in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Warmth (>25°C); steep thermal gradient (~31.0°C vs ~25.5°C)
neural_activity: Warmth-responsive thermoreceptors; Gr28b(D) expression required for warmth sensitivity; Gr28b(D) confers warmth-responsive inward currents when expressed in heterologous cells (motor neurons, chemosensors)
behavioral_consequence: Controls rapid negative thermotaxis (warmth avoidance); inhibition (TNT) or mutation (Gr28b) eliminates behavior; rescue by HC-specific Gr28b(D) expression restores behavior
ecological_context: Experiences ambient temperature fluctuations in advance of core changes, eliciting rapid avoidance; critical for tiny ectotherms where ambient and core temperatures equalize rapidly
techniques_used: ['Gal4/UAS expression', 'two-temperature choice behavior assay', 'electrophysiology (patch clamp in heterologous cells)', 'RT-PCR', 'mutant rescue']
summary: HC neurons are peripheral warmth sensors in the arista that drive rapid negative thermotaxis via Gr28b(D); they function to detect ambient temperature fluctuations for rapid avoidance.

paper_title: A gustatory receptor paralog controls rapid warmth avoidance in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Warmth; shallow thermal gradient (18–32°C)
neural_activity: Internal warmth sensors; warmth-activated via TRPA1 channel
behavioral_consequence: Controls slowly developing thermal preference (long-term body temperature selection); inhibition or mutation does not affect rapid negative thermotaxis
ecological_context: Controls responses to sustained temperature elevations reaching the core; distinguishes from peripheral sensors for different contexts
summary: AC neurons are internal warmth sensors using TRPA1 to control long-term thermal preference, distinct from the rapid avoidance mediated by peripheral HC neurons.

paper_title: Coordinated control of neuronal differentiation and wiring by sustained transcription factors
summary: Tm neurons are defined by unique combinations of continuously expressed transcription factors (pdm3 for Tm2, Drgx for Tm1, SoxN for Tm6). Targeted loss or gain of these factors induces predictable morphological and transcriptional fate conversions (e.g., pdm3 loss converts Tm2 to Tm4-like morphology and axonal targeting). Tm4 is the default fate overridden by specific selectors.

paper_title: Coordinated control of neuronal differentiation and wiring by sustained transcription factors
summary: Mi15 (aminergic and cholinergic) and Dm2 (cholinergic) neurons are distinguished by Vsx1/2 expression. Ectopic Vsx1/2 expression in Mi15 neurons converts them to Dm2 morphology and reduces Vmat protein levels, altering neurotransmitter identity and receptor expression.

paper_title: Coordinated control of neuronal differentiation and wiring by sustained transcription factors
summary: Lamina neurons (L1-L5) were used to benchmark gene regulatory network models. Hr3 knockdown delays the downregulation of Blimp-1, indicating ecdysone signaling regulates the timing of downstream effector expression during development.

paper_title: Inhibitory circuits control leg movements during Drosophila grooming
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson); Optogenetic silencing (Kir/GtACR1); Pulsed activation (70ms on/off light pulses)
neural_activity: Not directly recorded; functional effects inferred from optogenetic manipulation and behavioral outcomes
behavioral_consequence: Silencing locks front legs in flexion, reduces grooming time, and decreases extension-flexion cycle frequency. Activation reduces grooming time, induces leg extension, and increases frequency variability. Pulsed activation (70ms on/off) induces rhythmic grooming and walking movements in clean flies with normal frequency and angular velocity.
ecological_context: Drosophila grooming involves coordinated, rhythmic leg movements to sweep the body and remove debris (dust).
summary: 13A inhibitory neurons are essential for rhythmic leg coordination during grooming; silencing locks legs in flexion while activation induces rhythmic movements.

paper_title: Inhibitory circuits control leg movements during Drosophila grooming
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (CsChrimson); Optogenetic silencing (GtACR1)
neural_activity: Not directly recorded; functional effects inferred from optogenetic manipulation and behavioral outcomes
behavioral_consequence: Silencing reduces grooming, decreases proximal inter-leg distance, and strongly decreases extension-flexion cycle frequency. Activation reduces grooming and causes joint positioning defects.
ecological_context: Drosophila grooming involves coordinated, rhythmic leg movements to sweep the body and remove debris (dust).
summary: 13B inhibitory neurons contribute to spatial and temporal coordination during grooming; silencing reduces inter-leg distance and movement frequency.

paper_title: Distinct sensory representations of wind and near-field sound in the Drosophila brain
cell_type: Wind-sensitive Johnston's Organ neurons (Zones C and E)
experimental_stimulus: Airflow/wind (0.7–1.6 m/s; 0.3–0.9 m/s for recordings); directional wind (front, rear, side); unidirectional arista deflections
neural_activity: Tonically activated by unidirectional static deflections of larger magnitude (>0.04 mm); Zone E activated by front airflow (medial AMMC); Zone C activated by rear airflow (lateral AMMC); direction-sensitive response
behavioral_consequence: Genetic ablation (UAS-Ricin/eye-FLP) abolishes Wind-Induced Suppression of Locomotion (WISL); ablation does not impair hearing (copulation latency normal) or phototaxis
ecological_context: Wind-induced suppression of locomotion (WISL) in natural habitats; may control population dispersal, maintain genetic homogeneity, or protect from injury/prevent dispersal from food resources
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging (GCaMP-1.3)', 'Genetic ablation (UAS-Ricin, eye-FLP)', 'Extracellular electrophysiology', 'WISL locomotion tracking assay', 'Courtship copulation latency assay', 'Phototaxis assay']
summary: Wind-sensitive JO neurons (Zones C/E) are tonically activated by unidirectional airflow and directionally sensitive; their ablation abolishes WISL behavior without impairing hearing.

paper_title: Distinct sensory representations of wind and near-field sound in the Drosophila brain
cell_type: Sound-sensitive Johnston's Organ neurons (Zones A and B)
experimental_stimulus: Near-field sound (courtship song, 280–400 Hz, 75–100 dB); small bi-directional arista displacements (0.01 mm)
neural_activity: Phasically activated (transient response at onset/offset); low displacement threshold (<0.01 mm); activated by sound but not wind; bidirectional movement sensitivity
behavioral_consequence: Not explicitly tested for ablation in this paper; CE neuron ablation does not impair hearing-dependent courtship behavior
ecological_context: Detection of near-field sound (courtship song) for mating
summary: Sound-sensitive JO neurons (Zones A/B) are phasically activated by small bi-directional displacements and sound stimuli, contrasting with the tonic wind-sensitive response.

paper_title: Bilateral interactions of optic-flow sensitive neurons coordinate course control in flies
cell_type: HS cells (Horizontal System cells: HSN, HSE, HSS)
experimental_stimulus: Full-field rotation, unilateral Back-to-Front (BtF) and Front-to-Back (FtB) motion, local moving spot scanning visual field
neural_activity: Graded potential changes excited by FtB motion. Loss of gap junctions (Flp-shakB mutant) reduced receptive field size (loss of contralateral sensitivity in HSE), enhanced hyperpolarizing responses to null direction motion, resting membrane fluctuations/oscillations
behavioral_consequence: Silencing HS/VS reduced optomotor response strength and eliminated anti-saccadic turns to FtB motion. ShakB disruption caused flies to turn smoothly with FtB motion instead of anti-saccades, resulting in linear summation of binocular cues rather than non-linear integration
ecological_context: Navigating natural environments where distinguishing global displacement (e.g., wind gusts) from local motion cues (e.g., approaching objects for collision avoidance) is critical
techniques_used: ['Behavioral arena (freely walking)', 'In vivo whole-cell patch-clamp', 'Genetic silencing (Kir2.1, shibirets)', 'FlpStop shakB mutant generation', 'Neurobiotin filling and confocal imaging', 'Immunohistochemistry']
summary: HS cells encode optic-flow and generate anti-saccadic turning to unilateral FtB motion via bilateral electrical coupling; disruption of gap junctions linearizes course control and alters turning dynamics.

paper_title: Bilateral interactions of optic-flow sensitive neurons coordinate course control in flies
experimental_stimulus: Back-to-Front (BtF) motion (inferred from connectivity), unilateral motion stimuli during behavioral assays
neural_activity: Spiking neurons increasing firing rate to BtF motion. Electrically coupled to contralateral HSE cells via gap junctions
behavioral_consequence: Blocking H2 synaptic transmission reversed anti-saccadic responses to FtB motion, similar to HS/VS silencing, indicating involvement in proper course control
ecological_context: Part of the bilateral motion cue integration network used for navigation and course control
techniques_used: ['Genetic silencing (shibirets)', 'Neurobiotin filling and confocal imaging', 'In vivo patch-clamp (connectivity confirmation)']
summary: H2 cells form electrical connections with contralateral HS cells and their synaptic transmission is required for anti-saccadic turning responses during visual course control.

paper_title: Bilateral interactions of optic-flow sensitive neurons coordinate course control in flies
experimental_stimulus: Full-field and unilateral optic-flow motion patterns (as part of HS/VS silencing group)
neural_activity: Silenced via Kir2.1 expression in combination with HS cells
behavioral_consequence: Silencing HS and VS neurons did not abolish classic optomotor response but reduced overall strength and eliminated anti-saccades to FtB motion
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed separately from HS cells in this paper
summary: VS cells, when silenced alongside HS cells, reduce optomotor response strength but the specific behavioral phenotype is largely attributed to the HS-H2 network disruption.

paper_title: FlpStop, a tool for conditional gene control in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: 25 ms light flashes off of a gray background
neural_activity: Visually evoked calcium signals measured via GCaMP6f (two-photon imaging). Loss of cac reduced calcium responses in cell body, Lo1, and Lo4 arbors. Loss of cac increased calcium responses in M5 arbor. No effect on M1, M8, or M10 arbors.
behavioral_consequence: Not tested in this study. Background literature cited states Tm3 is required for response to fast moving ON edges.
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed.
summary: In Tm3 neurons, FlpStop-mediated loss of cac selectively altered visually evoked calcium signals across subcellular compartments, reducing signals in axonal regions (Lo1, Lo4) and cell body, while increasing signals in dendritic region M5.

paper_title: FlpStop, a tool for conditional gene control in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: None (tool validation)
neural_activity: tdTomato expression used to quantify FlpStop cassette inversion efficiency. 90% (Mi1), 93% (Tm1), and 70% (T4) of Gal4-expressing cells were tdTomato-positive.
behavioral_consequence: Not tested.
ecological_context: Not discussed.
summary: FlpStop cassette inversion was efficient in Mi1, Tm1, and T4 neurons, indicated by tdTomato reporter expression in 70-93% of target cells.

paper_title: Elevated serotonin receptor 2A signaling restores learning and memory in a Fragile X syndrome model
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory aversive conditioning (CS+ odor paired with electric shock vs. CS- odor without shock)
neural_activity: Immunocytochemistry quantified 5-HT and 5-HT2AR protein levels in Mushroom Body lobes; Neuronal 5-HT2AR levels significantly reduced in dfmr1 mutants compared to controls
behavioral_consequence: Neuronal 5-HT2AR knockdown impaired learning and memory (phenocopied dfmr1); Neuronal 5-HT2AR overexpression restored learning and memory in dfmr1 mutants; Neuronal Trhn overexpression and SERT knockdown restored learning and memory in dfmr1 mutants
summary: Neuronal manipulation of serotonin synthesis (Trhn), uptake (SERT), or receptor levels (5-HT2AR) in the Mushroom Body circuit modulates associative learning and memory performance in Drosophila Fragile X model.

paper_title: Elevated serotonin receptor 2A signaling restores learning and memory in a Fragile X syndrome model
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory aversive conditioning (CS+ odor paired with electric shock vs. CS- odor without shock)
neural_activity: Immunocytochemistry quantified 5-HT and 5-HT2AR protein levels in Mushroom Body lobes; Glial manipulation affected 5-HT levels
behavioral_consequence: Glial Trhn overexpression and SERT knockdown restored learning and memory in dfmr1 mutants; Glial 5-HT2AR overexpression restored learning and memory; Glial 5-HT2AR knockdown did not significantly contribute to learning deficits compared to ubiquitous knockdown
summary: Glial manipulation of serotonin synthesis and uptake in the Mushroom Body circuit is sufficient to restore learning and memory in the Fragile X model, though glial 5-HT2AR function appears less critical for knockdown phenotypes than neurons.

paper_title: Neuroligin 3 highlights sexually dimorphic circuitry in Drosophila social spacing
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic hyperactivation (TrpA1 at 27°C) and silencing (Shits at 29°C); RNAi knockdown of choline acetyltransferase (ChAT) and dopamine receptors (Dop1R1, Dop1R2, Dop2R, DopEcR)
neural_activity: Increased or decreased neural transmission; reduced acetylcholine biosynthesis; reduced dopamine receptor expression
behavioral_consequence: Hyperactivation or silencing increased social space (decreased number of flies within 4BL) in both males and females. Reducing acetylcholine release decreased social space (increased flies within 4BL) specifically in females. Knocking down dopamine receptors increased social space (decreased flies within 4BL) specifically in males.
ecological_context: Social spacing within a group context, relevant to social interactions
summary: MB neurons regulate social space in a sex-specific manner; acetylcholine release from MB affects females, while dopaminergic inputs/receptors in MB affect males.

paper_title: Neuroligin 3 highlights sexually dimorphic circuitry in Drosophila social spacing
experimental_stimulus: nlg3 RNAi knockdown
neural_activity: Reduced Neuroligin 3 protein expression
behavioral_consequence: Increased social space (decreased number of flies within 4BL), with the effect mostly driven by males
ecological_context: Visual processing linked to social spacing behavior
summary: Nlg3 expression in OL is required to maintain normal social spacing, particularly in males.

paper_title: Neuroligin 3 highlights sexually dimorphic circuitry in Drosophila social spacing
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic hyperactivation (TrpA1) and silencing (Shits); nlg3 RNAi knockdown
neural_activity: Increased or decreased neural transmission; reduced Neuroligin 3 expression
behavioral_consequence: Hyperactivation had no effect. Silencing PB neurons increased social space specifically in females. nlg3 knockdown had no effect on social space.
ecological_context: Social spacing within a group context
summary: PB neuronal transmission influences female social space, though nlg3 knockdown here alone does not alter behavior.

paper_title: Neuroligin 3 highlights sexually dimorphic circuitry in Drosophila social spacing
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic hyperactivation (TrpA1) and silencing (Shits); RNAi knockdown of nlg3, ChAT, GAD1, and Tyrosine hydroxylase (TH)
neural_activity: Increased or decreased neural transmission; reduced nlg3, acetylcholine, or GABA synthesis; reduced dopamine synthesis
behavioral_consequence: Hyperactivation decreased social space (increased flies within 4BL) in both sexes. Silencing increased social space (decreased flies within 4BL) in males only. Acetylcholine reduction increased social space in both sexes. GABA reduction increased social space in females only. Dopamine reduction did not change social space.
ecological_context: Sexually dimorphic social spacing behavior
summary: fruP1 neurons regulate social spacing with sexually dimorphic effects; acetylcholine and GABA release are involved, but dopamine release is not.

paper_title: Autism-like behaviors regulated by the serotonin receptor 5-HT2B in the dorsal fan-shaped body neurons of Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Ubiquitous knockdown of tryptophan hydroxylase (Trh) via RNAi; Trh01 mutant; 5-HTP feeding
behavioral_consequence: Trh knockdown and Trh01 mutants showed significantly increased social space distance (social avoidance) and increased repetitive grooming episodes. 5-HTP feeding restored social interaction and reduced grooming frequency.
ecological_context: Fruit flies are social animals that communicate to compete for resources (food, mates) and synchronize daily activities; serotonin levels affect these social behaviors.
summary: Serotonergic neurons regulate social interaction and repetitive behavior; serotonin depletion increases social distance and grooming.

paper_title: Autism-like behaviors regulated by the serotonin receptor 5-HT2B in the dorsal fan-shaped body neurons of Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Specific knockdown of 5-HT2B receptor using 23E10-Gal4 driver (dFB-specific)
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown resulted in significantly increased social space distance and elevated grooming numbers. No climbing defects observed.
ecological_context: Regulation of social spacing and stereotyped behaviors within a social group.
techniques_used: ['RNAi', 'Confocal imaging', 'Social space assay', 'Grooming assay', 'Negative geotaxis assay']
summary: 5-HT2B receptors in dFB neurons are essential for normal social interaction and repetitive behavior.

paper_title: Autism-like behaviors regulated by the serotonin receptor 5-HT2B in the dorsal fan-shaped body neurons of Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Specific knockdown of 5-HT2B receptor using Or83b-Gal4 driver
behavioral_consequence: Showed normal grooming numbers and intermediate nearest-neighboring distances; not responsible for social behaviors.
ecological_context: Olfactory processing (social avoidance), though 5-HT2B here did not mediate the tested behaviors.
summary: 5-HT2B receptors in olfactory neurons do not regulate the social interaction and repetitive behaviors tested.

paper_title: Autism-like behaviors regulated by the serotonin receptor 5-HT2B in the dorsal fan-shaped body neurons of Drosophila melanogaster
cell_type: Large Ventral Lateral Clock Neurons (lLNVs / Pdf-Gal4)
experimental_stimulus: Specific knockdown of 5-HT2B receptor using Pdf-Gal4 driver
behavioral_consequence: Increased distance to nearest neighbor compared to controls, but no repetitive behavior defects detected.
ecological_context: Circadian rhythm and arousal regulation; social behavior function found distinct from circadian clock.
summary: 5-HT2B receptors in lLNVs affect social distance but not repetitive grooming behavior.

paper_title: Autism-like behaviors regulated by the serotonin receptor 5-HT2B in the dorsal fan-shaped body neurons of Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Specific knockdown of 5-HT2B receptor using per-Gal4 driver
behavioral_consequence: Displayed closer distance between flies compared to dFB knockdown; significant locomotion defects observed in negative geotaxis assay.
ecological_context: Circadian rhythm regulation; social behavior defects linked to locomotor impairments.
summary: 5-HT2B knockdown in clock neurons affects locomotion and social distance differently than dFB knockdown.

paper_title: Drosophila ßHeavy-Spectrin is required in polarized ensheathing glia that form a diffusion-barrier around the neuropil
experimental_stimulus: Genetic ablation (UAS-rpr/hid), karst knockdown (RNAi), and diffusion barrier challenge (10 kDa dextran injection)
neural_activity: Polarized distribution of PIP2 (enriched at apical neuropil-facing membrane) and PIP3/Nrv2 (enriched at basolateral membrane); loss of polarity in karst mutants with delocalized PIP2 and Nrv2; forms internal diffusion barrier around neuropil
behavioral_consequence: Reduced crawling velocity and reduced peristalsis efficiency in larvae; reduced adult longevity (~40% reduction) upon ablation; abnormal locomotor behavior in karst mutants and knockdowns
ecological_context: Required for normal locomotor control and general nervous system function; not explicitly linked to specific natural ecological contexts in experimental findings
techniques_used: ['Split-Gal4 ablation', 'FIM locomotion analysis', 'Dye injection assay (10 kDa dextran)', 'Confocal microscopy (lipid/protein localization)', 'Serial section transmission electron microscopy (ssTEM)']
summary: Ensheathing glia are polarized cells that form a diffusion barrier around the neuropil; their loss or polarity disruption reduces larval locomotion velocity and peristalsis efficiency and decreases adult longevity.

paper_title: Drosophila ßHeavy-Spectrin is required in polarized ensheathing glia that form a diffusion-barrier around the neuropil
experimental_stimulus: Ablation of ensheathing glia
neural_activity: Compensatory growth of processes encasing the neuropil; 20% increase in cell number in adult flies
behavioral_consequence: None directly tested for astrocytes alone; observed in the context of ensheathing glia ablation
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed
summary: Astrocyte-like glia show compensatory growth and increased proliferation following ensheathing glia ablation, though they do not fully restore the diffusion barrier.

paper_title: Circadian and feeding cues integrate to drive rhythms of physiology in Drosophila insulin-producing cells
experimental_stimulus: 12:12 Light:Dark cycles (Morning ZT 0-4, Evening ZT 8-12, Night ZT 16-20), Constant Darkness (DD), *per01* circadian mutant condition, Restricted night feeding (starvation followed by feeding ZT 12-14), DN1 neuron stimulation (ATP application to P2X2 receptors)
neural_activity: Patch clamp electrophysiology revealed higher firing event frequency and proportion of bursting cells in the morning compared to night. Rhythms in firing frequency and burst firing were lost in *per01* mutants and in Constant Darkness. Restricted night feeding increased firing frequency and bursting proportion (morning-like phenotype), but tonic action potential half-width remained widened (night-like phenotype). GCaMP6m imaging showed ~60% increase in fluorescence in IPCs upon DN1 stimulation.
behavioral_consequence: None tested in this study (behavioral roles referenced from prior literature).
ecological_context: Coordination of metabolism with natural feeding rhythms (morning/evening peaks) and anticipation of environmental changes to maximize metabolic efficiency.
techniques_used: ['Whole-cell patch clamp electrophysiology', 'GCaMP6m imaging', 'GRASP (Genetic Reconstitution Across Synaptic Partners)', 'qRT-PCR']
summary: IPCs in the pars intercerebralis display circadian rhythms in firing frequency and burst proportion controlled by the central clock and feeding cues, receiving functional input from DN1 neurons.

paper_title: Circadian and feeding cues integrate to drive rhythms of physiology in Drosophila insulin-producing cells
experimental_stimulus: ATP application to P2X2 receptors expressed in DN1 neurons
neural_activity: Stimulation induced depolarization leading to increased GCaMP fluorescence in downstream IPCs (~60% above baseline), confirming functional connectivity.
behavioral_consequence: None tested in this study.
ecological_context: Part of central circadian clock circuit (specifically noted as target of PDF and regulator of circadian behavior in general introduction).
summary: DN1 neurons functionally connect to IPCs, driving calcium responses in IPCs upon stimulation.

paper_title: Fast near-whole–brain imaging in adult Drosophila during responses to stimuli and behavior
experimental_stimulus: Natural behavior (walking, resting, grooming); no external sensory stimuli reported for this driver
neural_activity: Strong large-scale increase in GCaMP6f activity tightly locked with walking; little activity during resting or grooming
behavioral_consequence: Not tested (study was correlational; activity correlated with walking)
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed
techniques_used: ['Light field microscopy', 'GCaMP6 calcium imaging', 'TH-Gal4 driver line']
summary: Dopamine neurons (TH-Gal4) show a strong global increase in calcium activity tightly locked with walking behavior, with minimal activity during rest or grooming.

paper_title: Fast near-whole–brain imaging in adult Drosophila during responses to stimuli and behavior
experimental_stimulus: Natural behavior (walking, turning left/right); no external sensory stimuli reported for this driver
neural_activity: Global increase in GCaMP6f activity during walking; components in posterior slope and lateral protocerebrum correlated with turning left or right
behavioral_consequence: Not tested (study was correlational; activity correlated with walking and turning)
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed
techniques_used: ['Light field microscopy', 'GCaMP6 calcium imaging', 'Cha-Gal4 driver line', 'PCA/ICA analysis']
summary: Cholinergic neurons (Cha-Gal4) exhibit global activity increases during walking, and specific components in the posterior slope correlate with turning direction.

paper_title: Fast near-whole–brain imaging in adult Drosophila during responses to stimuli and behavior
experimental_stimulus: Odor puffs (apple cider vinegar, ethanol); natural behavior
neural_activity: Spontaneous activity in absence of odor; some increased activity upon odor presentation; maps matched antennal lobe, calyx, and lateral horn
behavioral_consequence: Not tested (study was correlational; activity correlated with odor perception)
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed
summary: Components matching antennal lobe projection neuron anatomy respond to odor puffs with increased activity and show spontaneous activity in the absence of odor.

paper_title: Fast near-whole–brain imaging in adult Drosophila during responses to stimuli and behavior
experimental_stimulus: Odor puffs; natural behavior
neural_activity: Components spanning alpha/beta, alpha'/beta', or gamma mushroom body lobes identified via PCA/ICA
behavioral_consequence: Not tested (study was correlational)
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed
summary: Functional components matching Kenyon cell axons in mushroom body lobes were identified, though specific behavioral correlates were not detailed beyond general odor response.

paper_title: Fast near-whole–brain imaging in adult Drosophila during responses to stimuli and behavior
experimental_stimulus: Natural behavior (walking, turning)
neural_activity: Components in protocerebral bridge and ellipsoid body (radial parts); correlated with turning left or right
behavioral_consequence: Not tested (study was correlational; activity correlated with turning)
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed
summary: Components matching tile or wedge neuron anatomy in the protocerebral bridge and ellipsoid body were identified, with some correlating with turning behavior.

paper_title: Fast near-whole–brain imaging in adult Drosophila during responses to stimuli and behavior
experimental_stimulus: UV light flashes; odor puffs; natural behavior
neural_activity: Spike-like signals and graded potentials detected in optic lobe, antennal lobe, mushroom body, and lateral horn; spontaneous up/down switches in nodulus and protocerebral bridge
behavioral_consequence: Not tested (study was correlational; activity correlated with behavior/stimuli)
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed
summary: Pan-neuronal voltage imaging with ArcLight detected spike-like signals and spontaneous switching activity in specific neuropils, confirming voltage probe utility in whole-brain imaging.

paper_title: Flight Initiation and Maintenance Deficits in Flies with Genetically Altered Biogenic Amine Levels
behavioral_consequence: Genetic ablation (dTdc2-Gal4 > UAS-reaper) or TαH null mutation (lacking octopamine, high tyramine) resulted in reduced flight initiation (fewer responses to stimulation), reduced initial flight duration, reduced average flight duration per stimulation, and reduced total flight duration. Wing-beat frequencies, amplitudes, and muscle structure were unaffected. Deficits were rescued by feeding octopamine (partial rescue of maintenance) or blocking tyramine receptors with yohimbine (full rescue of initiation and maintenance).
ecological_context: Insect flight is described as one of the fastest, most intense, and most energy-demanding motor behaviors. These neurons regulate flight initiation and maintenance.
techniques_used: ['Genetic ablation (dTdc2-Gal4 > UAS-reaper)', 'TαH null mutation (genetic)', 'Pharmacological feeding (Octopamine, Yohimbine)', 'Tethered flight assay', 'High-speed video recording (1000 fps)', 'Immunohistochemistry (GFP, Bruchpilot, Phalloidin)']
summary: Ablation of dTdc2-expressing octopaminergic and tyraminergic neurons impairs flight initiation and maintenance without affecting wing-beat kinematics. Flight deficits are rescued by blocking tyramine receptors or supplementing octopamine, indicating these amines modulate flight motor behavior.

paper_title: Learning and memory deficits upon TAU accumulation in Drosophila mushroom body neurons.
experimental_stimulus: Olfactory associative conditioning (aversive odors benzaldehyde/3-octanol paired with electric shock 90V); Sensory controls (avoidance of odors, shock, osmotaxis to geraniol)
behavioral_consequence: Directed expression of TAU (bovine, human, Drosophila) in adult MB neurons caused 25-30% impairment in associative olfactory learning and memory retrieval/stability. Olfactory conditioning-relevant osmotactic and mechanosensory responses remained intact. No detectable neurodegeneration or premature death in 3-21 day old flies.
ecological_context: Described as centers for olfactory learning and memory in Drosophila.
techniques_used: ['GAL4/UAS directed expression', 'Immunohistochemistry', 'Behavioral assays (olfactory learning/memory, odor trap, shock avoidance)', 'Western blot', 'RT-PCR', 'Survival analysis']
summary: Accumulation of TAU in mushroom body neurons impaired associative olfactory learning and memory without affecting sensory perception or causing neurodegeneration in young adults.

paper_title: Learning and memory deficits upon TAU accumulation in Drosophila mushroom body neurons.
experimental_stimulus: Sensory tests (avoidance of odors benzaldehyde/octanol, shock avoidance, osmotaxis to geraniol)
behavioral_consequence: TAU accumulation in these neurons (driven by c492 GAL4) did not result in deficits in sensory modalities requisite for olfactory conditioning.
ecological_context: Functioned as sensory reception sites for olfactory conditioning stimuli.
summary: TAU accumulation in antennal lobe intrinsic neurons did not impair sensory behaviors such as odor avoidance or shock avoidance.

paper_title: Descending neuron population dynamics during odor-evoked and spontaneous limb-dependent behaviors
experimental_stimulus: Odor (odor-evoked contexts) and spontaneous limb-dependent behaviors in tethered flies
neural_activity: Largest fraction of recorded DNs encode walking; large fraction of walk-encoding DNs encode turning; fewer weakly encode speed; fewer active during head grooming and resting; a few DNs encode odors rather than behaviors
behavioral_consequence: Neural activity correlates with walking, turning, speed modulation, head grooming, and resting behaviors; odor context does not determine which behavior-encoding DNs are recruited
ecological_context: Natural behaviors and sensory context
techniques_used: ['Population recording of nearly 100 DNs in tethered flies']
summary: The study recorded populations of nearly 100 DNs in tethered flies, finding that most encode walking (with turning encoding in many), fewer encode grooming or resting, and a few encode odors; odor context does not dictate recruitment of behavior-encoding DNs.

paper_title: Olfactory and neuromodulatory signals reverse visual object avoidance to approach in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Appetitive odors (apple cider vinegar, ethanol), Aversive odor (benzaldehyde), Visual stimuli (small object, vertical bar), Optogenetic activation (685nm LED)
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation drives calcium accumulation in T4/T5 motion detecting neurons (two-photon imaging)
behavioral_consequence: Optogenetic activation reverses visual valence from object avoidance to approach. Enhances steering toward vertical bar. Fails to reverse valence in TH-Gal4, PAM-Gal4, or TRH-Gal4 lines
ecological_context: Small moving object likely represents a threat (predator); vertical bar likely represents a landscape feature (plant stalk). Reversal allows food approach while navigating landscape features and avoiding threats
summary: Tdc2-Gal4 labeled neurons mediate visual valence reversal; optogenetic activation mimics appetitive odor effects by exciting T4/T5 motion detectors.

paper_title: Olfactory and neuromodulatory signals reverse visual object avoidance to approach in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Visual stimuli (small object, vertical bar), Optogenetic activation (685nm LED at 0.010 mW/mm2 or 0.040 mW/mm2)
neural_activity: Calcium accumulation observed when Tdc2 neurons are activated; increased synaptic drive upon mild optogenetic excitation
behavioral_consequence: Mild optogenetic activation reverses object avoidance to approach and enhances bar tracking. High intensity activation weakens optomotor responses to both stimuli
ecological_context: Supports bar tracking behavior (landscape features) and visual motion processing during flight
summary: T4/T5 neurons support motion vision; optogenetic activation induces visual valence reversal and enhances bar approach behavior.

paper_title: Olfactory and neuromodulatory signals reverse visual object avoidance to approach in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (685nm LED)
neural_activity: Not measured
behavioral_consequence: Failed to evoke visual valence reversal; responses to small object and bar similar to wild-type control
ecological_context: Not explicitly linked to valence reversal in this study
summary: Optogenetic activation of broadly labeled dopaminergic neurons is insufficient to induce visual valence reversal.

paper_title: Olfactory and neuromodulatory signals reverse visual object avoidance to approach in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (685nm LED)
neural_activity: Not measured
behavioral_consequence: Failed to reverse visual valence; merely weakened small object avoidance without inducing approach
ecological_context: Not explicitly linked to valence reversal in this study
summary: Optogenetic activation of mushroom body dopaminergic neurons does not phenocopy odor-induced visual valence reversal.

paper_title: Olfactory and neuromodulatory signals reverse visual object avoidance to approach in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Optogenetic activation (685nm LED)
neural_activity: Not measured
behavioral_consequence: Failed to evoke visual valence reversal
ecological_context: Not explicitly linked to valence reversal in this study
summary: Optogenetic activation of serotonergic neurons does not induce visual valence reversal.

paper_title: Imaging neural activity in the ventral nerve cord of behaving adult Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Natural behavior (walking, grooming, spherical treadmill rotations)
neural_activity: Transient increases in GCaMP6s fluorescence ('events') in axons within the thoracic cervical connective. Left and right dMAN activity strongly correlated.
behavioral_consequence: dMAN events were associated with rapid bimodal anterior-posterior rotations of the spherical treadmill, corresponding to flies extending all six legs to push down on the treadmill.
ecological_context: Locomotion mechanics and limb pushdown behaviors during walking.
techniques_used: ['2-photon functional imaging', 'coronal section imaging of cervical connective', 'spherical treadmill videography']
summary: dMAN neurons show activity events correlated with rapid limb pushdown behaviors (treadmill rotations) during walking.

paper_title: Imaging neural activity in the ventral nerve cord of behaving adult Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Natural behavior (walking)
neural_activity: Transient increases in GCaMP6s fluorescence in axons within the thoracic cervical connective. Left and right MDN activity strongly correlated.
behavioral_consequence: MDN activity became active prior to anterior rotations of the spherical treadmill, corresponding to brief episodes of backward walking.
ecological_context: Backward locomotion control.
techniques_used: ['2-photon functional imaging', 'coronal section imaging of cervical connective', 'spherical treadmill videography']
summary: MDN neurons are active during backward walking episodes, correlating with anterior treadmill rotations.

paper_title: Imaging neural activity in the ventral nerve cord of behaving adult Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Natural behavior (walking)
neural_activity: Transient increases in GCaMP6s fluorescence. Left and right A1 activities were not highly correlated.
behavioral_consequence: Left A1 activity correlated with negative medial-lateral and yaw rotations (leftward turning). Right A1 activity correlated with positive medial-lateral and yaw rotations (rightward turning). Both linked to forward walking.
ecological_context: Steering and turning during forward locomotion.
techniques_used: ['2-photon functional imaging', 'coronal section imaging of cervical connective', 'spherical treadmill videography']
summary: A1 descending neurons show activity linked to forward walking and steering, with left neurons correlating to left turns and right neurons to right turns.

paper_title: PiVR: An affordable and versatile closed-loop platform to study unrestrained sensorimotor behavior
experimental_stimulus: Virtual odor gradients (Gaussian and volcano-shaped) created by optogenetic activation of Or42a OSNs using red light (CsChrimson). Real odor (isoamyl acetate) gradients tested for comparison.
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation using CsChrimson (light-gated ion channel). Direct neural recording (firing/calcium) was not performed.
behavioral_consequence: Positive chemotaxis towards the virtual odor source. Modulation of locomotion including runs, reorientation maneuvers (head casts), and tail speed changes during peristalsis. Larvae navigated along the rim of volcano-shaped gradients.
ecological_context: Innate positive chemotaxis behavior for navigating odor gradients.
summary: Optogenetic activation of Or42a olfactory sensory neurons in Drosophila larvae elicits positive chemotaxis towards virtual odor sources, modulating running and turning maneuvers.

paper_title: PiVR: An affordable and versatile closed-loop platform to study unrestrained sensorimotor behavior
experimental_stimulus: Virtual bitter taste simulated by optogenetic activation of Gr66a neurons in light 'ON' squares of a checkerboard pattern. Light 'OFF' squares served as control.
neural_activity: Optogenetic activation using CsChrimson. Direct neural recording was not performed.
behavioral_consequence: Avoidance of light 'ON' squares (virtual bitter taste). Increased locomotion speed in lit squares and frequent stopping in dark squares.
ecological_context: Innate aversion to bitter taste, aiding in avoidance of noxious substances.
summary: Optogenetic activation of Gr66a bitter-sensing neurons in adult Drosophila triggers avoidance behavior, characterized by speed modulation and preference for dark squares over light squares.

paper_title: Different spectral sensitivities of ON- and OFF-motion pathways enhance the detection of approaching color objects in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Expanding UV discs, competing ON/OFF motion edges (UV-green)
neural_activity: Drive the luminance channel; colorblind flies (norpA36) with R1-6 rescue show isoluminance levels where disc and background appear matched in luminance
behavioral_consequence: Necessary for motion vision; silencing makes flies motion blind; rescuing function in colorblind flies enables responses to isoluminant UV discs
ecological_context: General motion vision required for navigation; paper notes Drosophila forages on fruits/flowers with UV reflectance distinct from foliage
summary: R1-6 photoreceptors drive the luminance channel; rescuing their function in colorblind flies restores motion vision responses to isoluminant UV discs.

paper_title: Different spectral sensitivities of ON- and OFF-motion pathways enhance the detection of approaching color objects in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Expanding UV discs, competing ON/OFF motion edges
neural_activity: Rescuing R7 (with R1-6) restores the difference in isoluminance levels between ON and OFF motion pathways
behavioral_consequence: Critical for UV-sensitivity of ON-motion; silencing R7 or rescuing without R7 abolishes the behavioral difference between ON and OFF motion isoluminance
ecological_context: Contributes to detection of approaching color objects in natural foraging contexts (fruits, flowers, water droplets)
summary: R7 photoreceptors are required for the increased UV sensitivity of ON-motion behavioral responses compared to OFF-motion.

paper_title: Different spectral sensitivities of ON- and OFF-motion pathways enhance the detection of approaching color objects in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Expanding UV discs, competing ON/OFF motion edges
neural_activity: Rescuing R8 without R7 partners has no significant effect on isoluminance differences
behavioral_consequence: Not primarily responsible for the ON/OFF motion UV-sensitivity difference compared to R7
ecological_context: Not explicitly linked to specific ecological events beyond general color vision
summary: R8 photoreceptors alone do not support the behavioral difference in UV sensitivity between ON and OFF motion pathways.

paper_title: Different spectral sensitivities of ON- and OFF-motion pathways enhance the detection of approaching color objects in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Expanding UV discs, directional green/UV edges
neural_activity: Preferentially respond to ON-motion edges; two-photon calcium imaging shows lower UV isoluminance level (5.2) than T5
behavioral_consequence: Required for turning away from expanding UV discs; silencing abolishes responses to expanding discs
ecological_context: Enhances detection of approaching UV objects; mechanism allows detection of specific colored objects against backgrounds
summary: T4 cells are directionally selective for ON-motion, show lower UV isoluminance than T5, and are required for avoidance of expanding UV discs.

paper_title: Different spectral sensitivities of ON- and OFF-motion pathways enhance the detection of approaching color objects in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Expanding UV discs, directional green/UV edges
neural_activity: Preferentially respond to OFF-motion edges; two-photon calcium imaging shows higher UV isoluminance level (7.8) than T4
behavioral_consequence: Required for turning away from expanding UV discs; silencing abolishes responses to expanding discs
ecological_context: Enhances detection of approaching UV objects via OFF-motion pathway
summary: T5 cells are directionally selective for OFF-motion, show higher UV isoluminance than T4, and are required for avoidance of expanding UV discs.

paper_title: Different spectral sensitivities of ON- and OFF-motion pathways enhance the detection of approaching color objects in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Expanding UV discs
neural_activity: Have lower UV-green isoluminance level than L1, L2, L4 (more UV sensitive); provide input to Mi9 and T4/T5
behavioral_consequence: Silencing L3 increases ON-motion isoluminance level (ION), reducing the difference between ON and OFF motion isoluminance
ecological_context: Contributes to the divergence in spectral sensitivity between ON and OFF motion pathways
summary: L3 cells are more UV sensitive than L1/L2; silencing them reduces the behavioral difference between ON and OFF motion UV sensitivity.

paper_title: Different spectral sensitivities of ON- and OFF-motion pathways enhance the detection of approaching color objects in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Expanding UV discs
neural_activity: Have higher UV-green isoluminance level than L1/L2/L4 (less UV sensitive); calcium response decreases at low UV
behavioral_consequence: Silencing L5 increases ON-motion isoluminance level (ION)
ecological_context: Contributes to the divergence in spectral sensitivity between ON and OFF motion pathways
summary: L5 cells are less UV sensitive than L1/L2; silencing them increases ON-motion isoluminance, affecting the ON/OFF UV sensitivity gap.

paper_title: Different spectral sensitivities of ON- and OFF-motion pathways enhance the detection of approaching color objects in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Expanding UV discs
neural_activity: Share similar tuning to L1 and T4; principal excitatory inputs to T4
behavioral_consequence: Silencing Mi1 or Tm3 increases ON-motion isoluminance level (ION) without affecting OFF-motion
ecological_context: Part of the circuit linking R7-8 inputs to T4 for UV-sensitive ON-motion
summary: Mi1 and Tm3 cells drive T4 and share its UV sensitivity; silencing them alters behavioral ON-motion isoluminance.

paper_title: Different spectral sensitivities of ON- and OFF-motion pathways enhance the detection of approaching color objects in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Expanding UV discs
neural_activity: Provide inhibitory inputs to T4; Mi4 shares tuning with L5 (higher isoluminance), Mi9 with L3 (lower isoluminance)
behavioral_consequence: Silencing Mi4 or Mi9 reduces the difference between ON and OFF motion isoluminance levels
ecological_context: Contribute to the spectral divergence in ON-motion processing
summary: Mi4 and Mi9 are inhibitory inputs to T4 with divergent UV sensitivity; silencing them reduces the behavioral difference between ON and OFF motion isoluminance.

paper_title: Different spectral sensitivities of ON- and OFF-motion pathways enhance the detection of approaching color objects in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Expanding UV discs
neural_activity: Have lower UV-green isoluminance level than lamina and T4 pathway cells; slow calcium response
behavioral_consequence: Not explicitly tested for behavioral consequence via silencing in this study
ecological_context: Prominent target of R7 photoreceptors
summary: Dm9 cells are R7 targets with low UV isoluminance and slow response dynamics, though behavioral role not explicitly tested.

paper_title: Artificial sweeteners differentially activate sweet and bitter gustatory neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Sucralose (10-500 mM), Rebaudioside A (0.001-10 mM), Aspartame (0.001-10 mM), Water (control), Sucrose (1 M positive control)
neural_activity: In vivo calcium imaging (GCaMP6f) showed significant increase in activity for 100-500 mM sucralose and Rebaudioside A; 10 mM sucralose elicited response in at least one fly. No cellular response observed for Aspartame. Response magnitude to sucralose was moderate compared to 1 M sucrose.
behavioral_consequence: Activation signals feeding attraction at low concentrations (50 mM). Enhanced PER to subsequent neutral water stimulus after 50 mM sucralose. Optogenetic (GtACR1) and genetic (Kir2.1) silencing attenuated PER to 50 mM sucralose and reduced FLIC preference for 10-100 mM sucralose.
ecological_context: Mediates flexible feeding responses to unique compounds; balance with aversive signals likely enables flies to respond to chemicals beneficial to some extent but harmful in high quantities.
techniques_used: ['In vivo calcium imaging', 'Proboscis extension response (PER) assay', 'Fly Liquid Interaction Counter (FLIC) assay', 'Optogenetics (GtACR1)', 'Genetic silencing (Kir2.1)']
summary: Sweet GRNs (Gr64f+) are activated by sucralose and Reb A but not aspartame, signaling feeding attraction at low concentrations which is required for PER and FLIC preference.

paper_title: Artificial sweeteners differentially activate sweet and bitter gustatory neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Sucralose (100-500 mM), Aspartame (0.001-10 mM), Rebaudioside A (0.001-10 mM), Water (control), Caffeine (100 mM positive control)
neural_activity: In vivo calcium imaging (GCaMP6f) showed significant activation by 100-500 mM sucralose (high magnitude comparable to caffeine) and significant activation by aspartame (including OFF peak). Mild activation observed for Rebaudioside A.
behavioral_consequence: 500 mM sucralose activation signals feeding aversion, attenuating PER to subsequent repeated sucralose stimulation. Silencing bitter GRNs (optogenetic or genetic) reduced this aversive signal (restored PER to repeated stimulus) and significantly increased FLIC preference and interactions for 100 mM sucralose. No effect on aspartame or Reb A behavior.
ecological_context: Mediates flexible feeding responses to unique compounds; balance with appetitive signals likely enables flies to respond to chemicals beneficial to some extent but harmful in high quantities.
techniques_used: ['In vivo calcium imaging', 'Proboscis extension response (PER) assay', 'Fly Liquid Interaction Counter (FLIC) assay', 'Optogenetics (GtACR1)', 'Genetic silencing (Kir2.1)']
summary: Bitter GRNs (Gr66a+) are activated by high concentration sucralose and aspartame, signaling feeding aversion and limiting intake, which is confirmed by increased preference for sucralose when these cells are silenced.

paper_title: Odors drive feeding through gustatory receptor neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Volatile odors (e.g., ethyl butyrate, benzaldehyde, banana), Sucrose
neural_activity: Robust, concentration-dependent calcium responses (GCaMP6s) in axons within the subesophageal zone. Responses persisted even with olfactory organs covered.
behavioral_consequence: Silencing (Kir2.1) or mutation (Gr5a) significantly reduced odor-evoked Proboscis Extension Reflex (PER). Co-application of odor and sucrose synergistically enhanced PER compared to sucrose alone.
ecological_context: During foraging, GRNs on the labella sense odors to enhance PER probability before physical contact with food, acting as a non-contact chemo-sensing strategy.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging', 'Proboscis Extension Reflex (PER) assay', 'Kir2.1 suppression', 'Genetic mutants (Gr5a)']
summary: Gr5a GRNs directly respond to odors via calcium signaling. They are necessary for odor-evoked PER and mediate synergistic enhancement of PER when odors and sucrose are co-applied.

paper_title: Odors drive feeding through gustatory receptor neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Volatile odors
neural_activity: Robust, concentration-dependent calcium responses in axons within the subesophageal zone.
behavioral_consequence: Silencing in starved flies had little effect on odor-evoked PER. Silencing in fed flies increased odor-evoked PER, indicating Gr66a GRNs normally inhibit PER.
ecological_context: Gr66a GRNs counteract PER, potentially modulating feeding decisions in a state-dependent manner (starved vs fed).
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging', 'Proboscis Extension Reflex (PER) assay', 'Kir2.1 suppression']
summary: Gr66a GRNs respond to odors with calcium activity and function to inhibit odor-evoked PER, particularly in fed flies.

paper_title: Odors drive feeding through gustatory receptor neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Volatile odors
neural_activity: No calcium responses observed in axons within the subesophageal zone.
behavioral_consequence: Silencing (Kir2.1) had no effect on odor-evoked PER.
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed in relation to odor-evoked PER in this paper.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging', 'Proboscis Extension Reflex (PER) assay', 'Kir2.1 suppression']
summary: Ppk28 GRNs do not respond to odors and are not required for odor-evoked PER.

paper_title: Odors drive feeding through gustatory receptor neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Volatile odors
neural_activity: No calcium responses observed in axons within the subesophageal zone.
behavioral_consequence: Silencing (Kir2.1) had no effect on odor-evoked PER.
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed in relation to odor-evoked PER in this paper.
techniques_used: ['Two-photon calcium imaging', 'Proboscis Extension Reflex (PER) assay', 'Kir2.1 suppression']
summary: Ir94e GRNs do not respond to odors and are not required for odor-evoked PER.

paper_title: Odors drive feeding through gustatory receptor neurons in Drosophila
experimental_stimulus: Volatile odors
neural_activity: Not directly imaged for odor response in this study.
behavioral_consequence: Removal of olfactory organs (antennae/palps) or silencing (Orco-Gal4 > Kir2.1) attenuated but did not eliminate odor-evoked PER.
ecological_context: ORNs guide the animal to palatable food sources during foraging in the air.
techniques_used: ['Proboscis Extension Reflex (PER) assay', 'Surgical removal of sensory organs', 'Kir2.1 suppression']
summary: Olfactory system modulates but is not required for odor-evoked PER, as gustatory receptors in GRNs can detect odors independently.

paper_title: Protein-responsive gut hormone tachykinin directs food choice and impacts lifespan
experimental_stimulus: Protein-rich diet (10% yeast), amino acid feeding, H2O2 (ROS), starvation (control)
neural_activity: Calcium activity increases (CaLexA, GCaMP6s) upon protein feeding; intracellular Tk peptide levels decrease indicating release
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown reduces sugar intake and increases yeast intake; activation inhibits protein intake; loss increases midday sleep on yeast diet; loss extends lifespan
ecological_context: Regulates protein-specific satiety to balance macronutrient intake; protein intake signals shorten lifespan while inhibition (mimicking restriction) promotes longevity
techniques_used: ['CaLexA imaging', 'GCaMP6s calcium imaging', 'RNA interference (RNAi)', 'Thermoactivation of TrpA1', 'Dye-feeding assays', 'FLIC (Fly Liquid-Food Interaction Counter)', 'FlyPAD', 'Sleep monitoring', 'Lifespan assays']
summary: Tk+ midgut EECs sense dietary protein and amino acids via Slif, TOR, and TrpA1, increasing calcium activity and releasing Tk peptide, which suppresses protein appetite, promotes sugar hunger, modulates midday sleep, and regulates lifespan.

paper_title: Protein-responsive gut hormone tachykinin directs food choice and impacts lifespan
experimental_stimulus: Protein-rich diet (yeast feeding), gut Tk release, TkR99D knockdown
neural_activity: Intracellular AKH peptide levels decrease (indicating secretion) upon yeast feeding; requires TkR99D receptor
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown (Akh or TkR99D) reduces sugar intake and increases yeast intake; loss increases midday sleep on yeast diet; loss extends lifespan
ecological_context: Glucagon-like hormone signaling mobilizes energy stores and aligns behavior with nutritional needs; starvation-induced AKH release suppresses sleep to prioritize foraging
summary: APCs release AKH in response to protein intake via TkR99D signaling, which regulates food choice (suppressing protein, promoting sugar), sleep, and lifespan.

paper_title: Protein-responsive gut hormone tachykinin directs food choice and impacts lifespan
experimental_stimulus: AKH signaling (inferred), AkhR knockdown
neural_activity: Express AkhR (confirmed by single-cell RNA-seq and RT-PCR)
behavioral_consequence: Knockdown reduces sugar intake and increases yeast intake, mimicking pan-neuronal AkhR knockdown
ecological_context: Mediates neuronal AKH signaling required for food choice adaptations to dietary protein
summary: Cholinergic neurons expressing the AKH receptor mediate the food choice effects of AKH signaling, specifically regulating sugar versus yeast preference.

paper_title: Protein-responsive gut hormone tachykinin directs food choice and impacts lifespan
experimental_stimulus: High-protein diet, TOR inhibition (Torin1), TRPA1 agonists/antagonists
neural_activity: Tac1 expression and Substance P (SP) levels change (release); TRPA1 activation increases SP production
behavioral_consequence: Not tested behaviorally in mice; plasma SP levels increase with high-protein diet
ecological_context: Conserved protein-responsive gut hormone mechanism across species
summary: Mouse EC cells express Tac1 (Tk ortholog) and release Substance P in a protein-dependent manner regulated by TOR and TRPA1, showing evolutionary conservation of the gut hormone response.

paper_title: Cholesterol taste avoidance in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Cholesterol (low levels, high levels); Bitter substances
neural_activity: Responsive to cholesterol; subset also responds to bitter substances. Detection depends on five ionotropic receptor (IR) family members.
behavioral_consequence: Flies indifferent to low levels of cholesterol and avoid higher levels. Disrupting IR genes impairs ability to avoid cholesterol.
ecological_context: Not explicitly discussed in abstract.
summary: Cholesterol-responsive GRNs detect cholesterol via five IR family members; flies avoid high cholesterol but are indifferent to low levels, and gene disruption impairs this avoidance.

paper_title: Cholesterol taste avoidance in Drosophila melanogaster
experimental_stimulus: Cholesterol (via ectopic receptor expression)
neural_activity: Ectopic expression of cholesterol receptors reveals two classes of cholesterol receptors (each with three shared IRs and one unique subunit).
